blocks:
Sūrat al-Baqarah

(Entirely Medinan, except for one verse, which is (وَاتَّقُوا يَوْماً تُرْجَعُونَ) [2:281]. It consists of two hundred and eighty-six verses) بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful) Its exegesis has passed.

(١) الم (Alif. Lām. Mīm.) In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الم (Alif. Lām. Mīm.) is a letter from the letters of Allah's Greatest Name (اسم الله الأعظم) which is disjointed (المقطّع) in the Quran, which the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم or the Imam puts together (يؤلفه). If He is called upon with it, the prayer is answered. I say: This indicates that the disjointed letters (الحروف المقطّعات) are secrets between Allah, the Exalted, and His Messenger, and are symbols (رموز) not intended to be understood by anyone other than him and those firmly rooted in knowledge (الرّاسخين في العلم) from his progeny. Communication through single letters is the tradition (سنّة) of the beloved ones in the ways (سنن) of love. Thus, it is the secret of the Beloved with the beloved, such that the observer (الرقيب) does not become aware of it:

Between the lovers is a secret that neither

speech nor pen reveals to creation. The proof for this is also from the Quran, His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَأُخَرُ مُتَشابِهاتٌ (and others are ambiguous) [3:7], up to His saying: وَما يَعْلَمُ تَأْوِيلَهُ إِلَّا اللهُ وَالرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ (and no one knows its interpretation except Allah and those firmly rooted in knowledge) [3:7]. And from the Hadith is what al-Ayyashi narrated from Abu Labid al-Makhzumi, who said: Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: "O Abu Labid, twelve men from the progeny of al-Abbas will rule. Four of them will be killed after the eighth. One of them will be afflicted by the plague (الذبحة), and he will be slaughtered by a faction whose lives are short and whose conduct is wicked. Among them is the little wicked one (الفويسق) nicknamed al-Hadi (الهادي), al-Natiq (الناطق), and al-Ghawi (الغاوي). O Abu Labid, I possess abundant knowledge concerning the disjointed letters (حروف القرآن المقطعة) of the Quran. Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed الم ذلِكَ الْكِتابُ (Alif. Lām. Mīm. That is the Book) [2:1-2]. Muhammad arose until His light appeared and His word was established. He was born when one hundred and three years had passed from the seventh millennium." Then He said: "And its clarification is in the Book of Allah, in the disjointed letters (الحروف المقطعة), if you count them without repetition. There is no disjointed letter whose days expire except that one of Banu Hashim arises upon its expiration." Then He said: "The Alif (الألف) is one, the Lām (اللام) is thirty, the Mīm (الميم) is forty, and the Sād (الصاد) is ninety. That makes one hundred and sixty-one. Then the rising of al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon them both) revolved around الم (Alif. Lām. Mīm.) Allah. When its term reached its end, a Qā’im (قائم) from the progeny of al-Abbas arose at المص (Alif. Lām. Mīm. Sād.) [7:1]. And our Qā’im (قائمنا) will arise at the expiration of المر (Alif. Lām. Mīm. Rā.) [13:1]. So understand that, count it, and conceal it." And in Tafsir al-Imam: The meaning of الم (Alif. Lām. Mīm.) is that this Book which I have sent down is the disjointed letters (الحروف المقطعة) from which Alif Lām Mīm is derived, and it is in your language and your alphabet (حروف هجائكم). So bring the like of it إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (if you are truthful) [2:23]. I say: This also indicates that they are among the symbols (الرموز) that require this explanation, thus returning to the first interpretation, as do all other narrations concerning their interpretation, which are numerous. And in al-Majma‘, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), that He said: "Every book has an essence (صفوة), and the essence of this Book is the letters of the alphabet (حروف التهجي)." I say: And among the strange secrets (الأسرار الغريبة) in these disjointed letters (المقطعات) is that after combining them and removing the repetitions, they form [the phrase]: "Ali is a path of truth that we hold fast to (عليّ صراط حقّ نمسكه)" or "The path of Ali is a truth that we hold fast to (صراط علي حقّ نمسكه)."

(٢) ذلِكَ الْكِتابُ (2) (That is the Book)

In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from the Imam (peace be upon him): It means the Quran, which begins with الم (Alif. Lām. Mīm.), is ذلِكَ الْكِتابُ (that is the Book) about which I informed Moses (peace be upon him) and the Prophets after him, and they informed the Children of Israel that I would send it down upon you, O Muhammad. ** لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ (there is no doubt in it): There is no doubt in it because of its clarity (لِظُهُورِهِ) to them.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: The Book of Ali (كتاب علي) لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ (there is no doubt in it).

I say: That is its exegesis (تفسيره), and this is its esoteric interpretation (تأويله). The attribution of the Book (الكتاب) to Ali is explanatory (بيانيّة), meaning that ذلِكَ (that) is a reference to Ali, and الْكِتابُ (the Book) is an expression referring to him. The meaning is that ذلِكَ الْكِتابُ (that is the Book), who is Ali, has no doubt (لا مرية) in him. This is because his perfections (كمالاته) are witnessed through his conduct (سيرته), and his merits (فضائله) are explicitly stated (منصوص عليها) by Allah and His Messenger. Furthermore, applying the term الكتاب (the Book) to the Perfect Man (الإنسان الكامل) is common in the custom (عرف) of the people of Allah (أهل الله) and His special saints (خواص أوليائه).

The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said:

Your remedy is within you, yet you do not perceive; And your ailment is from you, yet you do not see. And you are the Manifest Book (الكتاب المبين) By whose letters the hidden (المُضْمَرُ) is revealed. And you claim that you are a small body (جِرْمٌ صغير) Yet within you is folded the Greater World (العالَم الأكبرُ).

And al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: The human form (الصورة الانسانية) is the greatest proof (حجّة) of Allah upon His creation, and it is the Book (الكتاب) which Allah wrote with His Hand.

هُدىً (a guidance): A clarification (بيان) from misguidance (الضلالة).

لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (for the Godwary): Those who guard against destructive sins (الموبقات) and guard against allowing foolishness (السّفه) to dominate their souls, such that when they know what they must know, they act upon that which necessitates the pleasure (رضاء) of their Lord for them.

In al-Ma'ani and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Godwary (المتّقون) are our followers (شيعتنا).

I say: He specified the Godwary (المتّقين) with guidance through it because they are the ones who benefit from it, and that is because piety (التقوى) is a prerequisite (شرط) for attaining true knowledge (المعرفة الحقّة).

(٣) الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْغَيْبِ (3) those who believe in the Unseen, [They believe] in what is hidden from their senses, such as the Oneness of Allah, the prophethood of the Prophets, the rising of the Riser (قيام القائم), the Return (الرجعة), the Resurrection (البعث), the Reckoning (الحساب), Paradise (الجنّة), and the Fire (النار), and all other matters in which belief is obligatory upon them, which are not known through observation but are only known through the proofs which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has established for them. وَيُقِيمُونَ الصَّلاةَ and maintain the prayer, By completing its bowing (ركوعها) and prostration (سجودها), preserving its appointed times (مواقيتها) and its limits (حدودها), and safeguarding it from what invalidates it or diminishes it. وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ and spend out of what We have provided them. [They spend] from the wealth, strengths (القوى), bodies (الأبدان), status (الجاه), and knowledge (العلم) that We have provided them. يُنْفِقُونَ They give charity (يتصدقون), bear the burden of all [who need help], render the rights (الحقوق) to their rightful owners (لأهاليها) (1), lend [money], assist in needs, take the hands of the weak, guide those in distress (الضرائر), save them from destruction (المهالك), carry their belongings for them, mount pedestrians upon their beasts, prefer those who are superior to them in faith over themselves with wealth and soul, treat those who are of the same rank as them in faith equally with both [wealth and soul], teach knowledge to its people, and narrate the virtues of the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them) to their lovers and to those whose guidance they hope for. In al-Ma'ani, al-Majma', and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "And they disseminate from what We have taught them."

footnote (١) The *lām* [in *لأهاليها*] relates to *الحقوق* (rights), not to *يؤدون* (render/perform). From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(٤) وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْكَ (4) And those who believe in what has been sent down to you : from the Quran and the Law (الشّريعة). وَما أُنْزِلَ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ and what was sent down before you, : from the Torah (التوراة), the Gospel (الإنجيل), the Psalms (الزّبور), the Scrolls of Abraham (صحف إبراهيم), and all the other revealed Books of Allah (سائر كتب الله المنزلة). وَبِالْآخِرَةِ and are certain of the Hereafter. meaning the Abode (الدار) which is after this world (الدنيا), in which is the recompense (جزاء) for righteous deeds (الأعمال الصالحة) with better than what they performed, and the punishment (عقاب) for evil deeds (الأعمال السيئة) with the like of what they earned. هُمْ يُوقِنُونَ : they do not doubt (لا يشكونَ).

(٥) أُولئِكَ عَلى هُدىً مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ (It is they who are guided by their Lord) [They are] upon clarity (بيان), correctness (صواب), and knowledge (علم) regarding what He commanded them with. وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ (and it is they who are the felicitous.) [They are] the saved ones (النّاجون) from that which they fear (يوجلون), the triumphant ones (الفائزون) with that which they hope for (يؤملون). (٦) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (Indeed, those who are faithless) [Those who disbelieved] in Allah, and in that which these believers have believed in. سَواءٌ عَلَيْهِمْ أَأَنْذَرْتَهُمْ ** (it is all the same to them whether you warn them) [Whether you warn them]: You frighten them (خوّفتهم). أَمْ لَمْ تُنْذِرْهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ (or do not warn them, they will not have faith.) He informed [you] of His knowledge concerning them. (٧) خَتَمَ اللهُ عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ وَعَلى سَمْعِهِمْ (Allah has set a seal upon their hearts and their hearing) He marked them with a sign (بسمة) which He wills that His angels and His saints (أوليائه) recognize when they look at it, [indicating] that they are those who will not believe. In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: The sealing (الختم) is the stamping (الطبع) upon the hearts of the disbelievers as a punishment for their disbelief, just as He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: (بَلْ طَبَعَ اللهُ عَلَيْها بِكُفْرِهِمْ فَلا يُؤْمِنُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً). (Rather, Allah has set a seal on them for their faithlessness, so they will not have faith, except a few.) وَعَلى أَبْصارِهِمْ غِشاوَةٌ (and there is a covering on their eyes) [A covering (غطاء)]. That is because when they turned away from looking into what they were obligated [to look into] (كلّفوه) and fell short in what was desired of them, they became ignorant of what was incumbent upon them to believe in. Thus, they became like one who has a covering (غطاء) over his eyes and does not see what is before him. For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is far exalted above causing corruption (العيث والفساد) and above demanding from His servants that which He has forcibly prevented them from [attaining]. وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ (and there is a great punishment for them.) Meaning, in the Hereafter, the punishment prepared for the disbelievers. And in the world (الدنيا) also, [it is] for whomever He wills to reform (يستصلحه) through the punishment of reformation (عذاب الاستصلاح) that He sends down upon him, so as to alert him to His obedience; or [it is] the punishment of eradication (عذاب الاصطلام), so that He may bring him to His justice and His wisdom. I say: The eradication (الاصطلام)—with the two undotted letters (بالمهملتين)—is the uprooting (الاستيصال). As for reformation (الاستصلاح), it is only valid for one whose seal (ختمه) and covering (غشاؤه) have not become firmly established, and who is among those for whom goodness is still hoped for. This is an alert (تنبيه) from Allah to him and a completion of the proof (إتمام للحجة), even if he himself does not benefit from it.

(8) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَقُولُ آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَبِالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ. (And among the people are those who say, 'We believe in Allah and the Last Day,') I mean those like Ibn Ubayy and his companions, and like the first and the second and their likes among the hypocrites (المنافقين) who exceeded the disbelief (الكفر) that necessitates the sealing (الختم), the covering (الغشاوة), and hypocrisy (النفاق), especially at the time of the appointment of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) for the Caliphate (الخلافة) and the Imamate (الإمامة). I say: And included in this is everyone who practices hypocrisy (ينافق) in religion until the Day of Resurrection, even if he is lesser than them in hypocrisy (النفاق), just as al-Baqir (peace be upon him) said concerning Hakam ibn Utaybah, that he is among the people referred to in this Ayah (1). And in Tafsir al-Imam, the summary of which is that: When the Companions were commanded on the Day of Ghadir to pledge allegiance to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) with the commandership of the believers (إمرة المؤمنين), Abu Bakr and Umar stood up, along with nine of the Muhajirun and the Ansar, and pledged allegiance to him with it. He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) confirmed upon them the covenants and the solemn pledges, and Umar came with the Bakhbakhah (2) and they dispersed. A group of their rebels (متمرديهم) and tyrants (جبابرتهم) conspired among themselves (saying) that if something were to happen to Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), they would surely push this matter away from Ali (peace be upon him) and would not leave it for him. And they used to come to the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) and say: "You have indeed appointed over us the most beloved of creation to Allah and to you, and through him, you have spared us the burden of the oppressors (الظلمة) against us and those who act unjustly (الجائرين) in our governance (سياستنا)." But Allah Almighty knew the opposite of that from their hearts, and that they were persisting in enmity and pushing the right away from those who deserved it. So Allah informed about them through this Ayah. وَما هُمْ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ : (and they are not believers.) Rather, they conspired to destroy you and to destroy those who love you and whom you love, if they were able, and to rebel against the rulings of Allah, especially the caliphate (خلافة) of the one Whom You appointed by the command of Allah over Your nation after You, due to their denial of his caliphate (خلافته) and his commandership (إمارته) over them, out of envy (حَسَداً) and transgression (عتواً). It is said: He excluded their very selves from the count of the believers, as an exaggeration in negating faith (الإيمان) from them entirely.

(1) Mujahid said: Four verses from the beginning of the Surah were revealed concerning the believers, two verses after them were revealed concerning the disbelievers, and thirteen verses after them were revealed concerning the hypocrites (المنافقين). From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(2) Al-Bakhbakhah: His saying, "Bravo, bravo (بخ بخ) to you, O Abu al-Hasan! You have become my master and the master of every believing man and woman." From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(٩) يُخادِعُونَ اللهَ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (9) They seek to deceive Allah and those who have faith, They seek to deceive the Messenger of Allah by showing him the opposite of what is in their hearts (جوانحهم). I say: The deception of the Messenger was attributed to Allah only because deceiving him reverts to deceiving Allah, as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: مَنْ يُطِعِ الرَّسُولَ فَقَدْ أَطاعَ اللهَ (Whoever obeys the Apostle certainly obeys Allah.) And He said: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبايِعُونَكَ إِنَّما يُبايِعُونَ اللهَ (Indeed, those who swear allegiance to you, swear allegiance only to Allah.) And He said: وَما رَمَيْتَ إِذْ رَمَيْتَ وَلكِنَّ اللهَ رَمى (And you did not cast when you cast, but Allah cast.) And you may say that its meaning is that they treat Allah with the treatment of a deceiver, as is indicated by what al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked: "In what lies salvation tomorrow?" He said: "Salvation lies only in that you do not deceive Allah, lest He deceive you. For whoever deceives Allah, He deceives him and strips faith (الإيمان) from him, and he deceives himself, if he but perceives." He was asked: "And how does one deceive Allah?" He said: "He performs what He, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded, and then intends something other than Him by it. So fear Allah and hypocrisy (الرياء), for it is associating partners (شرك) with Allah." وَما يَخْدَعُونَ but they deceive And they do not harm [anyone] by that deception. And it was read (قُرِئ) as يُخادِعُونَ. إِلَّا أَنْفُسَهُمْ only themselves, For Allah is independent of them and of their support, and were it not for His respite (إمهاله) to them, they would not be able to commit any of their wickedness (فجورهم) or transgression (طغيانهم). وَما يَشْعُرُونَ and they are not aware. That the matter is thus, and that Allah informs His Prophet of their hypocrisy (نفاقهم), their lies (كذبهم), and their disbelief (كفرهم), and commands him to curse them among the curse of the wrongdoers (الظالمين).

(١٠) فِي قُلُوبِهِمْ مَرَضٌ (There is a sickness in their hearts) It is said (that the sickness is) hypocrisy (نفاق) and doubt (شكّ). That is because their hearts boil against the Prophet, the Successor (الوصيّ), and the believers, with malice (حقداً), envy (حَسَداً), rage (غيظاً), and resentment (حنقاً). The indefiniteness (تنكير) of the word al-marad (المرض) (sickness) and the presentation of the sentence as an adverbial phrase (ظرفية) is an indication of its permanence and deep-rootedness (استقراره ورسوخه); otherwise, He would have said: "Their hearts are sick (قلوبهم مرضى)." فَزادَهُمُ اللهُ مَرَضاً (so Allah has increased their sickness) To the extent that their hearts became bewildered (تاهت) by it. وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ بِما كانُوا يَكْذِبُونَ (and there is a painful punishment for them because of what they used to lie about.) I say: Meaning, a painful punishment (عذاب مؤلم) whose infliction of pain reaches the utmost degree (غاية البلوغ) due to their lying (كذبهم) or their denial/rejection (تكذيبهم), according to the variant reading (اختلاف القراءة). For indeed, the description of the punishment as "painful" (الأليم) is only for the sake of emphasis (للمبالغة). This is the punishment prepared for the hypocrites (المنافقين), and it is more severe than the punishment of the disbelievers (الكافرين), because the hypocrites are in the lowest depth (الدّرك الأسفل) of the Fire.

(11) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ (11) And when they are told, 'Do not cause corruption on the earth,' by manifesting hypocrisy towards the weak servants of Allah, thereby confusing their religion for them and making them bewildered in their ways (مذاهبهم). قالُوا إِنَّما نَحْنُ مُصْلِحُونَ they say, 'We are only reformers.' because we do not believe in a religion, so we satisfy Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم outwardly, and we free ourselves from his servitude (رقّه) inwardly, and in this is the rectification (صلاح) of our condition.

(١٢) أَلا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ الْمُفْسِدُونَ (12) Look! It is indeed they who are the agents of corruption. by what they do in the affairs of their own souls (أنفسهم), because Allah informs His Prophet of their hypocrisy. So He curses them and commands the Muslims to curse them. And the enemies of the believers also do not trust them, because they think that they are also being hypocritical towards them, just as they are hypocritical towards the believers. Thus, they do not attain a high status with them. And for this reason, He refuted them with the most eloquent refutation (1). وَلكِنْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ. but they are not aware.

(13) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ (13) And when they are told The best among the believers said to them: آمَنُوا 'Believe,' It is said that this is part of the completeness of sincere advice (النّصح) and guidance (الإرشاد), for the perfection of faith (الإيمان) is only achieved by turning away from what is improper—which is intended by His saying: لا تُفْسِدُوا (Do not cause corruption)—and by performing what is proper, which is requested by His saying: آمِنُوا كَما آمَنَ النَّاسُ 'Believe just as the people have believed,' meaning the believers like Salman, al-Miqdad, Abu Dharr, and Ammar. And it is said, meaning those who are perfect in humanity (الإنسانية) and act according to the dictates of reason (العقل). That is, believe with a faith (إيماناً) coupled with sincerity (الإخلاص), purified from the impurities (شوائب) of hypocrisy (النّفاق). قالُوا they say in response to those to whom they confide, not to these believers, for they do not dare to reveal this answer openly to them, أَنُؤْمِنُ كَما آمَنَ السُّفَهاءُ 'Shall we believe as the fools have believed?' those who have humbled themselves (المذلّون) before Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so that when his affair declines, his enemies will destroy them. أَلا إِنَّهُمْ هُمُ السُّفَهاءُ Beware! It is indeed they who are the fools, those whose intellects (العقول) and opinions (الآراء) are light (الأخفّاء), who have not looked with true insight (حقّ النّظر) so as to recognize his prophethood (نبوّته), the firmness of his cause (ثبات أمره), and the soundness of what He entrusted to His successor (وصيّه) concerning the affairs of religion (الدين) and the world (الدنيا). Thus, they remain fearful of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his companions, and of their opponents, and they are not safe, wondering which of the two sides will prevail, causing them to perish with him, for each of the two parties assumes that their hypocrisy with him is like their hypocrisy with the other. وَلكِنْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ (but they do not know) that the matter is such, and that Allah informs His Prophet of their secrets, thereby disgracing them (فيخسئهم) and causing them to fall (ويسقطهم). وَإِذا لَقُوا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا قالُوا آمَنَّا And when they meet the faithful, they say, 'We believe,' This is a clarification of their dealings with the believers and the disbelievers, following the explanation of their doctrine (مذهبهم) and the establishment of their hypocrisy (نفاقهم). For they used to show faith (الإيمان) to Salman, Abu Dharr, Miqdad, and Ammar.

(1) And what the general populace (العامّة) narrated: that Salman رضي الله عنه said: "The people of this verse have not yet come." Perhaps he meant that the primary ones intended by it are those falsely named as the successors (خلفاء) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and they had not yet begun their corruption (إفسادهم). This saying of his was before the passing of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Otherwise, he meant by it that the people of this verse are not only those who existed then, but also those who will come after, or anyone whose condition is like theirs—from him, may his secret be sanctified (قدّس سرّه).

(١٤) وَإِذا خَلَوْا إِلى شَياطِينِهِمْ (14) And when they are alone with their devils, their close friends (أخدانهم) among the hypocrites who share with them in denying the Messenger (الرسول). قالُوا إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ they say, 'We are indeed with you, meaning, in the religion (الدين) and the belief (الاعتقاد), just as we were. ** إِنَّما نَحْنُ مُسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** we are only mocking.' the believers (بالمؤمنين).

(١٥) اللهُ يَسْتَهْزِئُ بِهِمْ (Allah derides them) He recompenses them with the recompense of one who is derided. As for in this world (الدنيا), it is by applying the rulings of the Muslims upon them, and by His command to the Messenger to allude to them, so that the one intended by that allusion is not hidden. And as for in the Hereafter (الآخرة), it is according to what has been narrated: that a door to Paradise (الجنّة) will be opened for them while they are in the Fire (النار), so they hasten towards it, but when they reach it, the door is shut upon them. And that is His saying, the Almighty: ** فَالْيَوْمَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنَ الْكُفَّارِ يَضْحَكُونَ (So today the faithful will laugh at the faithless) This was narrated by the general populace (العامّة). And in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him) there is what is close to its meaning in a long narration. ** وَيَمُدُّهُمْ (and He lets them go on) He grants them respite, and He deals with them gently, and He calls them to repentance (التوبة), and He promises them forgiveness (المغفرة) if they turn back. ** فِي طُغْيانِهِمْ ** (in their transgression) It is said: in their exceeding the limit (حدّهم) upon which they ought to have been. ** يَعْمَهُونَ ** (wandering bewildered) They do not refrain from anything ugly (قبيح) nor do they cease harming Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. It is said: He blinds their hearts (قلوبهم). And al-ʿamah (العمه) is the blindness of the heart (عمى القلب), which is bewilderment (التحيّر) in the matter.

** أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الضَّلالَةَ بِالْهُدى : They sold the religion of Allah and took disbelief in Allah in exchange for it. فَما رَبِحَتْ تِجارَتُهُمْ They did not profit in their trade in the Hereafter, because they bought the Fire and its various kinds of torment in exchange for the Paradise that had been prepared for them had they believed. ** وَما كانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ to the truth (الحقّ) and the correctness (الصّوَاب).

I say: And nor to the ways of trade, because the purpose of trade is the safety of the capital (رأس المال) and profit, but these people wasted their capital, which is the sound innate disposition (الفطرة السليمة), through the errors (الضلالات) they believed in, and they did not profit.

** أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الضَّلالَةَ بِالْهُدى فَما رَبِحَتْ تِجارَتُهُمْ وَما كانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ** (16) They are the ones who have bought error for guidance; so their trade has brought them no profit, nor are they guided.

(١٧) مَثَلُهُمْ (Their parable) means their strange condition (حالهم العجيبة). It is said that Allah only sets forth parables (الأمثال) for people in His Books for the sake of increased clarification (التوضيح) and confirmation (التقرير), for they are more impactful upon the heart and more suppressive of the most stubborn opponent (الخصم الألدّ), because they show the imagined (المتخيّل) as realized (محقّقاً) and the intelligible (المعقول) as sensible (محسوساً). كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ ناراً (١) (is like the parable of someone who kindled a fire) He sought the fire's blaze (سطوع النار) so that he might see by it what was around him. فَلَمَّا أَضاءَتْ ما حَوْلَهُ (When it illuminated all around him). It is said: meaning, the fire illuminated what was around the kindler, or the things around him became illuminated, if you consider أضاءت (illuminated) to be intransitive (لازمةً). ذَهَبَ اللهُ بِنُورِهِمْ (Allah took away their light) by sending a wind or rain that extinguished it. And that is because they perceived the truth (الحق) and guidance (الهدى) through the outward appearance of faith (ظاهر الإيمان), and they were granted the rulings of the Muslims (أحكام المسلمين), such as the protection of blood (حقن الدم) and the safety of wealth (سلامة المال). So when their outward faith (إيمانهم الظاهر) illuminated what was around them, Allah caused them to die, and they became in the darknesses (ظلمات) of Allah's punishment in the Hereafter, seeing no exit from it and finding no escape (محيصاً) from it. وَتَرَكَهُمْ فِي ظُلُماتٍ لا يُبْصِرُونَ (and left them in darknesses, unable to see.) In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): That Allah is not described by abandonment (الترك) as His creation is described, but rather, whenever He knows that they will not return from disbelief (الكفر) and misguidance (الضلال), He withholds assistance (المعاونة) and grace (اللطف) from them, and leaves them to their own choice (اختيارهم).

(18) ** صُمٌّ بُكْمٌ عُمْيٌ ** (18) (They are) deaf, dumb, and blind.

Meaning, in the Hereafter, as the Almighty and Majestic said: وَنَحْشُرُهُمْ (And We shall gather them) عَلى وُجُوهِهِمْ عُمْياً وَبُكْماً وَصُمًّا. (on their faces, blind, dumb, and deaf.)

I say: And in this world too, concerning the sciences (العلوم) and gnosis (المعارف) related to the Hereafter. And for that reason, they will be gathered on that Day in the same state. Allah the Almighty said:

لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ لا يَفْقَهُونَ بِها وَلَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ لا يُبْصِرُونَ بِها وَلَهُمْ آذانٌ لا يَسْمَعُونَ بِها (They have hearts with which they do not understand, and they have eyes with which they do not see, and they have ears with which they do not hear.) Meaning, the affairs (أمور) of the Hereafter in this world. And He also said: فَإِنَّها لا تَعْمَى الْأَبْصارُ وَلكِنْ تَعْمَى الْقُلُوبُ الَّتِي فِي الصُّدُورِ (For indeed it is not the eyes that are blind, but the hearts that are in the breasts that are blind.) And He also said: وَتَراهُمْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ لا يُبْصِرُونَ (And you see them looking at you, but they do not perceive.) ** فَهُمْ لا يَرْجِعُونَ (So they will not return) from error (الضلالة) to guidance (الهدى).

(1) It is said that it means the light of those who kindle the fire (المستوقدين) if you make it the answer (جواب) to لما (when), and the light of the hypocrites (المنافقين) if you make it a new sentence (مستأنفاً) or an appositive (بدلاً). Or, the answer to لما might be omitted for brevity (للإيجاز) and to avoid ambiguity (الالتباس), as in His saying, the Almighty: فَلَمَّا ذَهَبُوا بِهِ (When they took him away). And He did not say "by their fire" according to the first interpretation because the purpose of kindling it is the light (النور). From him (منه), may his secret be sanctified (قدّس سرّه).

** أَوْ كَصَيِّبٍ (1) (19) Or like a downpour It is said that it means: or like that with which they were addressed, consisting of truth (الحق) and guidance (الهدى), which is like rain, since by it there is life for the hearts, just as by rain there is life for the earth. ** مِنَ السَّماءِ ** from the sky from the high altitude (العلوّ). فِيهِ ظُلُماتٌ wherein are shadows a similitude for the ambiguities (الشّبهات) and the calamities (المصيبات) related to it. ** وَرَعْدٌ وَبَرْقٌ and thunder and lightning. a similitude for the intimidation (التخويف), the threat (الوعيد), and the dazzling signs (الآيات الباهرة) which contain enlightenment (التبصير) and guidance (التسديد). ** يَجْعَلُونَ أَصابِعَهُمْ فِي آذانِهِمْ مِنَ الصَّواعِقِ حَذَرَ الْمَوْتِ They put their fingers in their ears because of the thunderclaps, apprehensive of death. so that the thunder does not tear out (2) their hearts, or the lightning descends upon them with a thunderbolt (الصاعقة) causing them to die. For these hypocrites (المنافقين), in the state of disbelief (الكفر) and hypocrisy (النفاق) they were in, feared that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم might discover their disbelief and hypocrisy, and thus kill them or eradicate them. So, when they heard from him a curse (لعناً) or a threat (وعيداً) against those who broke the pledge (البيعة), they put their fingers in their ears so that they would not hear, lest their colors change, and the believers recognize that they are the ones meant by that [threat]. وَاللهُ مُحِيطٌ بِالْكافِرِينَ But Allah encompasses the faithless. He is powerful over them; had He willed, He would have revealed to you the hypocrisy (نفاق) of their hypocrites (منافقيهم), disclosed their secrets (أسرارهم) to you, and commanded you to kill them.

(20) يَكادُ الْبَرْقُ يَخْطَفُ أَبْصارَهُمْ ** (The lightning almost snatches away their sight) He takes it away (يذهب بها). That is because this is the similitude of a people afflicted by lightning, who looked at the lightning itself and did not lower their gaze from it, nor did they shield their faces from it so that their eyes might be safe from its glare, nor did they look at the path through which they wished to escape by the light of the lightning. Thus, these hypocrites—the decisive verses (الآيات المحكمة) in the Quran, which point to the truthfulness of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, which they witness but do not gain insight from, and in which they deny the truth—almost invalidate for them all the other things they have done which they know to be true. For whoever denies one truth, that denial leads him to deny every truth. So, the denier, in the invalidation of all other truths upon him, becomes like the one who looks at the body (جرم) of the sun, resulting in the loss of the light of his sight. ** كُلَّما أَضاءَ لَهُمْ (Whenever it lights up for them) What they believed to be the proof (الحجّة) appeared to them, [and] they walked in it (مَشَوْا فِيهِ). And these hypocrites, when they see what they love in their worldly life (دنياهم), they rejoice in their pledge (بيعتهم) and express their wishes by demonstrating their obedience. ** وَإِذا أَظْلَمَ عَلَيْهِمْ قامُوا (and when it darkens upon them, they stand still) They stopped (وقفوا) and were bewildered (وتحيّروا). And these hypocrites, when they see what they dislike in their worldly life (دنياهم), they stop and become pessimistic about the pledge (بيعتهم) they made. It is said that the similitude of their eagerness for any guidance (رشد) that flashes for them, or any aid (رفد) toward which their eyes look, is their walking in the path of the lightning's light whenever it lights up for them. And the similitude of their bewilderment and stopping in the matter when a doubt (شبهة) presents itself to them or a calamity (مصيبة) befalls them, is their stopping when it darkens upon them. He only used كلما (whenever) with illumination and إذا (when) with darkness because they are keen to walk, and whenever they find an opportunity for it, they seize it, but stopping is not like that. ** وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَذَهَبَ بِسَمْعِهِمْ وَأَبْصارِهِمْ (Had Allah willed, He would have taken away their hearing and their sight.) So that it would not be possible for them to guard against you and your companions discovering their disbelief (كفرهم), which would necessitate their execution (قتلهم). ** إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ** (Indeed Allah has power over all things.) Nothing incapacitates Him.

(1) Sayyib (صيب): Is derived from the form fayʿal (فيعل) from al-sawb (الصوب), meaning descent (النزول). It is said of rain and clouds. From him (the author), may his secret be sanctified (قدّس سرّه).

(2) Al-Sāʿiqah (الصاعقة): Is a terrifying clap of thunder accompanied by fire that does not pass by anything except that it burns it, derived from al-saʿq (الصعق), which is the intensity of sound. "From him (the author)."

(٢١) ** يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ** (21) O mankind! Worship your Lord, who created you and those who were before you, so that you may be Godwary.

It is said that when He enumerated the categories (فِرَق) of the accountable (المُكَلَّفِين) and mentioned their characteristics (خَوَاص) and the outcomes (مَصَارِف) of their affairs, He turned to them with the address (الخِطَاب) by way of iltifāt (الالتفات)—to stir the listener, to invigorate him, to emphasize the matter of worship, to magnify its status, to compensate for the burden (كُلْفَة) of worship, and to emphasize the pleasure (لَذَّة) of being addressed.

In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from the Imam (peace be upon him): It has two aspects (وَجْهَان). The first is: He created you and created those who were before you so that you may be Godwary (لِتَتَّقُوا), just as He said: وَما خَلَقْتُ الْجِنَّ وَالْإِنْسَ إِلَّا لِيَعْبُدُونِ (56) I did not create the jinn and the humans except that they may worship Me. The other aspect is: اعْبُدُوا رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ وَالَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ (Worship your Lord, who created you and those who were before you)—meaning, worship Him so that you may be Godwary of the Fire (النَّار). And the word la‘alla (لَعَلَّ) (1) coming from Allah is binding (وَاجِب), because He is too Generous (أَكْرَم) to concern His servant without benefit (مَنْفَعَة), give him hope in His favor (فَضْل), and then disappoint him.

I say: ** لَعَلَّكُمْ (so that you may) in the first aspect relates to ** خَلَقَكُمْ (He created you), and al-taqwā (التَّقْوَى) is intended to mean worship (العِبَادَة). In the second aspect, it relates to اعْبُدُوا (Worship), and al-taqwā (التَّقْوَى) is intended to mean caution (الحَذَر). He (peace be upon him) drew attention, by His saying: لها وجهان (It has two aspects), to the fact that the Quran possesses multiple aspects (ذُو وُجُوه), and that carrying it upon a combination (الجَمْع) is sound. Similar instances (نَظَائِر) of this occur in their sayings (peace be upon them), and the fact that the discourse possesses multiple aspects is among the things that increase its eloquence (بَلَاغَتِه) and subtlety (لَطَافَتِه).

(1) La‘alla (لَعَلَّ), ‘asā (عَسَى), and sawfa (سَوْفَ) in the promises (مَوَاعِيد) of kings are considered equivalent to certainty (الجَزْم). They are only used to show their dignity (وَقَارِهِم) and to indicate that a hint (الرَّمْز) from them is like an explicit statement (الصَّرِيح) from others. The promise (وَعْد) and threat (وَعِيد) of Allah (may He be sanctified) proceed upon this principle.

(٢٢) الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الْأَرْضَ فِراشاً (22) The One Who made the earth a resting place for you He made it suitable for your natures (طبائعكم), agreeable to your bodies, compliant for your cultivation (حرثكم), your buildings (أبنيتكم), and the burial of your dead. He did not make it intensely hot and feverish (الحمى والحرارة) so that it would burn you, nor intensely cold so that it would freeze you, nor intensely fragrant (طيّب الرّيح) so that it would split your heads (هاماتكم), nor intensely foul-smelling (النتن) so that it would destroy you, nor intensely soft like water so that it would drown you, nor intensely hard so that it would resist you in your cultivation, your buildings, and the burial of your dead. Rather, He placed within it a firmness (المتانة) by which you benefit and hold together, and upon which your bodies and your structures hold together. And He placed within it a softness (اللين) by which it yields for your dwellings (دوركم), your graves (قبوركم), and many of your benefits. ** وَالسَّماءَ بِناءً and the sky a canopy A preserved roof (سقفاً محفوظاً) above you, wherein He causes its sun, its moon, and its stars to revolve for your benefits. ** وَأَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ ماءً and sent down water from the sky Meaning the rain (المطر). He sends it down from above (من علىً) so that it reaches the peaks (قلل) of your mountains, your hills (تلالكم), your plateaus (هضابكم) (1), and your lowlands (أوهادكم). Then He dispersed it as fine spray (رذاذاً), heavy rain (وابلاً), continuous downpour (هطلاً), and drizzle (طلًّا), so that your lands may absorb it. He did not cause it to descend upon you as a single mass (قطعة واحدة) lest it ruin your lands, your trees, your crops, and your fruits. And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that he said: "With every drop, an angel descends and places it in the location which his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded him." ** فَأَخْرَجَ بِهِ مِنَ الثَّمَراتِ رِزْقاً لَكُمْ. and with it brought forth fruits for your sustenance. I say: For your food (مطعمكم), your drink (مشربكم), your clothing (ملبسكم), and the rest of your benefits: فَلا تَجْعَلُوا لِلَّهِ أَنْداداً ** So do not set up equals to Allah Similar things (أشباهاً) and likenesses (أمثالاً) from the idols (الأصنام) which neither reason (تعقل), nor hear (تسمع), nor see (تبصر), nor have power over anything. ** وَأَنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (٢) while you know. (2) That they have no power over any of these magnificent blessings (النّعَمْ الجليلة) with which your Lord has favored you.

(٢٣) ** وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ فِي رَيْبٍ مِمَّا نَزَّلْنا عَلى عَبْدِنا** (23) And if you are in doubt concerning what We have sent down to Our servant,

... so that you deny that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله is the Messenger of Allah, and that this revelation sent down upon him is My speech, despite My manifestation upon him in Mecca of the dazzling signs, such as the cloud that shaded him and the inanimate objects that greeted him with peace (التسليم), and other than that.

** فَأْتُوا بِسُورَةٍ مِنْ مِثْلِهِ ** then bring a surah like it,

... like Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, a man from among you who neither reads nor writes, nor studies a book, nor frequented a scholar, nor learned from anyone, and you know him in his travels and his residence. He remained thus for forty years, then he was granted the comprehensive knowledge (جوامع العلم) until he knew the knowledge of the first and the last. Or, [bring a surah] like (١) this Qur'an, from the previous books (الكتب السّالفة) in eloquence (البلاغة) and composition (النّظم).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), the meaning of which is: When oratory (الخطب) and speech (الكلام) were dominant among the people of his era, Allah brought them from His admonitions (مواعظه) and His rulings (أحكامه) that by which He invalidated their speech and established the proof (الحجّة) against them. Just as He brought to the people of Moses (peace be upon him) that by which He invalidated their magic (سحرهم), since magic was dominant among them; and to the people of Jesus (peace be upon him), medicine (الطّبّ), the revival of the dead (إحياء الموتى), and the healing of the born-blind (الأكمه) and the leper (الأبرص), since chronic diseases (الزّمانات) were dominant among them.

** وَادْعُوا شُهَداءَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ** and call your witnesses besides Allah,

... [meaning] your idols (أصنامكم) which you worship, O polytheists (المشركون); and your devils (شياطينكم), O Jews and Christians (اليهود والنّصارى); and your atheist companions (قرناءكم الملحدين) (٢), O hypocrites (منافقي المسلمين) among those who harbor enmity (النصَّاب) towards the pure family of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Those who, according to your claim, testify that you are rightful, and whom you claim are your witnesses before the Lord of the Worlds concerning your worship, and who intercede for you with Him, so that they may testify for you that what you have brought is similar to it. It was said: Or [call them] to help you against opposing it, as in the Almighty's saying: (قُلْ لَئِنِ اجْتَمَعَتِ الْإِنْسُ وَالْجِنُّ عَلى أَنْ يَأْتُوا بِمِثْلِ هذَا الْقُرْآنِ لا يَأْتُونَ بِمِثْلِهِ وَلَوْ كانَ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ ظَهِيراً) (Say: 'Should humans and jinn rally to bring the like of this Qur’an, they will not bring the like of it, though they were to support each other.') For the word al-Shahid (الشهيد) has come to mean the Imam (الامام), the helper (النّاصر), and the one who stands by the testimony (القائم بالشهادة), and the structure (التركيب) is for presence, whether sensory or imaginary.

** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ** if you are truthful.

... that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله fabricated it from his own accord, and Allah did not send it down upon him.

(١) Al-Hadbah (الهضبة) is what opposes al-Wahdah (الوهدة), al-Ridhādh (الرذاذ) is light rain, al-Wābil (الوابل) is heavy rain, al-Hatl (الهطل) is continuous rain, and al-Tall (الطل) is the weakest rain. From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(٢) It was said: And the meaning is that you are people of knowledge (العلم) and consideration (النظر). From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(٢٤) فَإِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا (24) But if you do not do it This (refers to) that which I challenged you with, O you who are struck by the argument (حجّة) of the Lord of the worlds. ** وَلَنْ تَفْعَلُوا and you will not do it And this will never be from you, and you will never be able to do it. So fear the Fire whose fuel (حطبها) is ** النَّاسُ وَالْحِجارَةُ ** (men and stones) (Meaning) the stones of sulfur (الكبريت) because they are the hottest of all things.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): We passed with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by a mountain, and behold, tears were flowing from a part of it. He said: "What makes you weep, O mountain?" It said: "O Messenger of Allah, the Messiah (المسيح) passed by me, and He was warning people of a Fire ** وَقُودُهَا النَّاسُ وَالْحِجارَةُ (whose fuel is men and stones). So I fear that I might be one of those stones." He (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: "(Do not fear, those are the stones of sulfur (الكبريت)." So the mountain settled, became calm, and was tranquil.

And it was said that what is meant by them (الحجارة) are the idols (الأصنام) which they carved and associated themselves with, and worshipped them hoping for their intercession (شفاعتها), as in the Almighty's saying: إِنَّكُمْ وَما تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ حَصَبُ جَهَنَّمَ. (Indeed, you and what you worship besides Allah are the fuel of hell.)

Al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), said: "Indeed, this fire of yours is one part out of seventy parts of the Fire of Hell (جهنّم). It has been extinguished seventy times with water and then flared up. Were it not for that, no human being would be able to extinguish it. And indeed, it (the Fire of Hell) will be brought forth on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) until it is placed upon the (worldly) fire, and it will scream a scream such that no close angel (ملك مقرّب) nor sent prophet (نبيّ مرسل) remains except that he falls upon his knees out of terror from its scream." ** أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكافِرِينَ** it has been prepared for the faithless (Meaning) those who deny His word and His Prophet.

footnote (١) هذا الترديد في التفسير أيضاً ممّا ينبه على أن القرآن ذو وجوه وان حمله على جميع الوجوه صحيح حقّ وليس من قبيل التردد كما يقع في كلام سائر المفسرين ، حاشاهم عن ذلك. منه قدّس سرّه.

footnote (٢) هذا ممّا لفق من موضعين من تفسير الإمام عليه السلام. منه قدّس سرّه.

(٢٥) وَبَشِّرِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ أَنَّ لَهُمْ جَنَّاتٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا: from beneath their trees and dwellings. ** الْأَنْهارُ It is narrated: that this [Ayah] was revealed concerning Ali, Hamzah, Ja'far, and Ubaydah ibn al-Harith ibn Abd al-Muttalib. (25) And give good news to those who have faith and do righteous deeds, that for them there will be gardens with streams running in them. I say: This does not negate the generality of its ruling, as you have learned. ** كُلَّما رُزِقُوا مِنْها from those gardens ** مِنْ ثَمَرَةٍ from its fruits ** رِزْقاً قالُوا هذَا الَّذِي رُزِقْنا مِنْ قَبْلُ in the world (الدنيا). Thus, its names are like its names [the names of the worldly fruits], but they are of the utmost goodness, not transforming into that which the fruits of the world transform into, such as excrement (العذرة), yellow bile (الصفراء), black bile (السوداء), and blood, except for the sweat (العرق) that flows over their bodies (أعراضهم), which is sweeter in fragrance than musk. Whenever they are provided with a fruit from it for a provision, they will say, 'This is what we were provided with before,' I say: al-ʿIrd (العِرض) with a kasrah (vowel i) is the body (الجسد). وَأُتُوا بِهِ مُتَشابِهاً some of it resembles others, in that all of it is excellent (خيار), with no refuse (رذل) in it, and in that every type of it is of the utmost goodness (الطيب) and pleasure (اللذة). They are not like the fruits of the world (الدنيا), some of which are unripe (نيّ) and some of which exceed the limit of ripeness (النّضج) and maturity (الإدراك) to the point of spoilage, such as sourness (حموضة), bitterness (مرارة), and other kinds of unpleasantness (المكاره). They are also similar (متشابهات), having consistent colors (متفقات الألوان) but differing tastes (مختلفات الطعوم). and they will be brought its like. I say: Since knowledge (المعرفة) in the world (الدنيا) is the seed of witnessing (المشاهدة) in the Hereafter (الآخرة), it is permissible that the saying ** هذَا الَّذِي رُزِقْنا مِنْ قَبْلُ refers, for the people of knowledge (أهل المعرفة), to the fruit of their sciences (علومهم) and gnosis (معارفهم) which have become reality (عيناً) and manifest observation (عياناً). وَلَهُمْ فِيها أَزْواجٌ مُطَهَّرَةٌ purified from menstruation (الحيض), post-natal bleeding (النّفاس), and all other types of filth (الأقذار) and indecencies (الفواحش). They are not ولّاجات (frequently entering), nor خرّاجات (frequently exiting), nor دخّالات (deceitful infiltrators), nor ختّالات (deceivers), nor متغايرات (jealous), nor فركات (hateful) towards their husbands, nor صخّابات (1) (loud-voiced), nor عيابات (fault-finders), nor نخّاسات (pushy/shoving), and they are innocent (بريئات) of all defects (العيوب) and unpleasantness (المكاره). And for them there will be pure mates in them, I say: The ولّاجات (الولّاجات) and خرّاجات (الخرّاجات) are those who frequently engage in frivolity (الظّرف) and choice (الاختيار). The دخّالات (الدخّالات) are the deceitful (الغاشّات). The ختّالات (الختّالات) are the tricksters (الخداعات). The متغايرات (المتغايرات) are those who are affected by jealousy (الغيرة). The فركات (فركات) are the hateful (مبغضات). The صخّابات (الصّخابات) are the loud-shouters (الصيّاحات). The عيّابات (العيّابات) are those who find fault (العيب). And the نخّاسات (النّخاسات) are the pushers (الدفّاعات). In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They do not menstruate (يحضن) nor do they excrete (يحدثن). وَهُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ: because their intentions (نيّاتهم) in the world (الدنيا) were that if they were to remain in it, they would obey Allah forever. Thus, they were made eternal (خلّدوا) by their intentions (النيّات). This is mentioned in al-ʿIlal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). and they will abide in it forever.**

(٢٦) إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَسْتَحْيِي أَنْ يَضْرِبَ مَثَلاً (26) Indeed, Allah is not ashamed to draw a parable for the truth, which He clarifies by it for His believing servants, whatever the parable may be. I say: Meaning, whatever parable it may be, for the mā (ما) is for increasing the ambiguity (الإبهام) and commonality (الشيوع) in the indefinite noun (النّكرة). a gnat, or [even] something above it which is the fly (الذّباب). By this, He refuted those who criticized His drawing of parables using the fly (الذّباب), the spider (العنكبوت), the kindling of fire (مستوقد النّار), and the rain cloud (الصّيّب) in His Book. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah only drew the parable of the gnat because, despite its small size, Allah created within it everything that He created in the elephant, despite its large size, plus two additional organs. Thus, Allah intended by this to alert the believers to His subtle creation (لطيف خلقه) and His wondrous making (عجيب صنعه). فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ But as for those who have faith, they know that it is the truth from their Lord. It is the drawn parable, the truth from their Lord. He intended by it the truth, its clarification (إبانته), its disclosure (الكشف عنه), and its elucidation (إيضاحه). I say: Meaning, they know that what is considered essential in the parable (المثل) is that it conforms to the thing being represented (الممثل له) in terms of smallness and greatness, baseness (الخسّة) and nobility (الشّرف), so that He may clarify and elucidate it until it takes the form of the observed, tangible thing (المشاهد المحسوس), rather than the thing being represented (الممثل). وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا فَيَقُولُونَ ما ذا أَرادَ اللهُ بِهذا مَثَلاً But as for the faithless, they say, 'What did Allah mean by this parable?' What thing did He intend by it from the perspective of the parable? ** يُضِلُّ بِهِ كَثِيراً وَيَهْدِي بِهِ كَثِيراً ** He leads many astray thereby, and guides many thereby. It is said that this is the answer to "What did He mean?" (ما ذا), meaning: the misguidance of many is due to their denial of it, and the guidance of many is due to their acceptance of it. Thus, it serves as a clarification (البيان) for the two preceding sentences, meaning that both groups are described by the attribute of multitude (الكثرة), and because He is the cause (سببيّته) of both outcomes for them, they are attributed to Him. In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from the Imam (peace be upon him): Meaning, those who disbelieve say: "The parable has no meaning, because even if it benefits those whom He guides, it harms those whom He leads astray." So Allah refuted their saying and said: ** وَما يُضِلُّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْفاسِقِينَ ** and He does not lead astray thereby anyone except the transgressors (the ones who deviate (الخارجين) from the religion of Allah, those who commit wrong against themselves (الجانين على أنفسهم) by abandoning reflection upon it and by placing it contrary to how Allah commanded it to be placed).

(1) With the undotted letter (المهملة), then the dotted letter (المعجمة), then the single-dotted letter (الموحدة). From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(٢٧) الَّذِينَ يَنْقُضُونَ عَهْدَ اللهِ ** (the ones who break Allah’s covenant) The covenant taken upon them for Allah regarding Lordship (الرّبوبيّة), and for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding Prophethood (النبوة), and for Ali عليه السلام regarding Imamate (الإمامة), and for their followers (شيعتهما) regarding honor (الكرامة), مِنْ بَعْدِ مِيثاقِهِ (after its confirmation), which is its strengthening (إحكامه) and making it severe (تغليظه). وَيَقْطَعُونَ ما أَمَرَ اللهُ بِهِ أَنْ يُوصَلَ (and sever what Allah has commanded to be joined) such as the wombs (الأرحام) and relatives (القرابات), that they should care for them (يتعاهدوهم) and fulfill their rights (حقوقهم). The best kinship (رحم) and the one whose right is most obligatory is the kinship of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for their right is due to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, just as the right of a person's relatives is due to his father and mother. And Muhammad is greater in right than one's parents, and likewise, the right of His kinship (رحمه) is greater, and severing it (قطيعته) is more cutting and more disgraceful (أفضح).

I say: The verse also includes differentiating between the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Books (الكتب) in affirmation (التصديق), abandoning the guardianship (موالاة) of the believers, abandoning the obligatory Friday and congregational prayers (الجمعة والجماعات المفروضة), and all other matters that involve rejecting good (رفض خير) or committing evil (تعاطي شرّ). For indeed, this severs the connection (الوصلة) between Allah and the servant, which is the connection intended essentially (المقصودة بالذّات) by every joining (وصل) and separation (فصل). ** وَيُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ (and cause corruption in the earth.) due to severing that which, in its joining (وصله), lies the order (نظام) and rectification (صلاحه) of the world. ** أُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ (It is they who are the losers.) who have lost their souls by being destined for the Fires (النيران) and being deprived of the Gardens (الجنان). Oh, what a loss that has bound them to eternal punishment (عذاب الأبد) and deprived them of eternal bliss (نعيم الأبد).

(٢٨) كَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِاللهِ (28) How can you disbelieve in Allah, The address is to the disbelievers of Quraysh and the Jews. ** وَكُنْتُمْ أَمْواتاً when you were dead, in the loins of your fathers and the wombs of your mothers. ** فَأَحْياكُمْ and He gave you life? He caused the spirit (الروح) to flow within you and brought you forth alive. ** ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُمْ** Then He will make you die, in this world and will bury you. ** ثُمَّ يُحْيِيكُمْ ** then He will bring you to life. in the graves, and therein He will grant bliss to the believers and punish the disbelievers. ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ Then to Him you shall be returned. in the Hereafter, by dying in the graves after being brought to life, then being brought to life for the Resurrection on the Day of Judgment. You shall return to what He promised you of reward for acts of obedience if you were those who performed them, and of punishment for acts of disobedience if you were those who committed them.

(٢٩) ** هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ لَكُمْ ما فِي الْأَرْضِ جَمِيعاً (29) He it is who created for you all that is in the earth. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: He created it for you so that you may reflect upon it, and through it attain His pleasure, and guard yourselves against the torment of His fires. ** ثُمَّ اسْتَوى (١) إِلَى السَّماءِ Then He turned to the sky, He began its creation and perfection. ** فَسَوَّاهُنَ and fashioned them And it is said: He made them balanced (عدّلهن), protected from crookedness (العوج) and weakness (الفتور). And the pronoun (الضمير) is ambiguous (مبهم); what follows clarifies it. ** سَبْعَ سَماواتٍ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ as seven heavens, and He has knowledge of all things. And for this reason, He created what He created, just as He created it for your benefit (لصالحكم) according to what wisdom (الحكمة) necessitated.

(٣٠) وَإِذْ قالَ رَبُّكَ لِلْمَلائِكَةِ (30) And when your Lord said to the angels who were on the Earth with Iblis, and they had expelled the Jinn, the sons of al-Jann, from it, and the worship (العبادة) was lightened (2).

And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Iblis was among the angels, worshipping Allah in the heaven, and the angels thought he was one of them, but he was not. That was because Allah created a creation before Adam, and Iblis was a ruler among them. They caused corruption on Earth, acted insolently (عَتَوْا), and shed blood unjustly. So Allah sent the angels against them, and they killed them and captured Iblis and raised him with them to the heaven. He remained with the angels, worshipping Allah, until Allah created Adam. When Allah commanded the angels to prostrate to Adam, and what Iblis harbored of envy (حَسَد) toward him and his arrogance (استكباره) became manifest, the angels knew that he was not one of them. And He said that he only entered into the matter because he was related to them by allegiance (بالولاء), but he was not of their species (جنسهم).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about Iblis: Was he one of the angels, or did he manage any affair of the heaven? He said: He was not one of the angels, nor did he manage any affair of the heaven. He was one of the Jinn, and he was with the angels, and the angels thought he was one of them, but Allah knew that He was not. When the command to prostrate was given, what happened, happened.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The like of it, up to His saying: "nor did he manage any affair of the heaven," and he added after it: "nor any honor (كرامة)."

إِنِّي جاعِلٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةً I am going to set a vicegerent on the earth (I am going to set) a successor (خَلِيفَةً) in your stead and raise you from it. This was difficult for them because worship, upon their return to the heaven, would be heavier upon them.

And in a narration: خَلِيفَةً who will be a proof (حجّة) for Me on My Earth against My creation, as will follow. قالُوا أَتَجْعَلُ فِيها مَنْ يُفْسِدُ فِيها وَيَسْفِكُ الدِّماءَ as was done by the Jinn, the sons of al-Jann, whom we have already expelled from this Earth? وَنَحْنُ نُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِكَ (meaning: we declare You free from attributes that are unfitting for You) وَنُقَدِّسُ لَكَ (meaning: we purify Your Earth from those who disobey You). قالَ إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ (This refers to) the righteousness (الصلاح) hidden within him (Adam) and the disbelief (الكفر) hidden within the one who is among you, who is Iblis, may Allah curse him.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them), and he also narrated it in al-Ilal from him (peace be upon him), with variations in its wording. He said: When Allah intended to create a creation with His Hand, this was after seven thousand years had passed since the Jinn and the Nisnas (a pre-Adamite race) were on Earth. So the Glorified One lifted the veil of the heavens and commanded the angels to look at the inhabitants of the Earth from the Jinn and the Nisnas. When they saw the acts of disobedience, the shedding of blood, and the corruption on Earth unjustly, this greatly distressed them, and they became angry for Allah the Almighty and grieved for the Earth. They could not contain their anger and said: "Our Lord, You are the Almighty, the Capable, the One of Great Status, and this is Your creation, abject and contemptible, turning over in Your bounty, enjoying Your well-being, held captive in Your grasp, yet they disobey You with such sins and cause corruption on Earth, and You do not become angry nor take vengeance for Yourself, while You hear and see. This has greatly distressed us and we have magnified it for You." So He, majestic is His glory, said: إِنِّي جاعِلٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةً who will be a proof (حجّة) for Me on My Earth against My creation. The angels said: أَتَجْعَلُ فِيها مَنْ يُفْسِدُ فِيها as these have caused corruption, وَيَسْفِكُ الدِّماءَ as these have done, and they will envy one another and hate one another? So make that vicegerent from among us, for we do not envy one another, nor hate one another, nor shed blood, وَنَحْنُ نُسَبِّحُ بِحَمْدِكَ وَنُقَدِّسُ لَكَ ، قالَ blessed and exalted is He: إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ I desire to create a creation with My Hand and make from his progeny the Prophets and the Messengers, and the righteous servants of Allah, and the rightly guided Imams (أئمّة مهديّين), and make them My vicegerents over My creation on My Earth, guiding them to My obedience and forbidding them from My disobedience. And I will make them a proof (حجّة) for Me against them, as an excuse (عذراً) and a warning (نذراً). And I will banish the Nisnas from My Earth and purify it from them, and I will remove the rebellious and disobedient Jinn from My creation and My chosen ones, and settle them in the air and in the desolate parts of the Earth, so they do not neighbor My creation. And I will place a barrier (حجاباً) between the Jinn and the progeny of My creation. And those of the progeny of My creation whom I have chosen, if they disobey Me, I will settle them in the dwelling place of the disobedient and lead them to their destinations. So the angels said: سُبْحانَكَ لا عِلْمَ لَنا إِلَّا ما عَلَّمْتَنا He said: So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, distanced them from the Throne (العرش) by a distance of five hundred years. So they sought refuge in the Throne and pointed with their fingers. Then the Lord, majestic is His glory, looked upon them, and mercy descended. He placed for them al-Bayt al-Ma'mur (the Frequented House) and said: "Circumambulate it and leave the Throne, for that is My pleasure." So they circumambulated it, and it is the House which seventy thousand angels enter every day, never to return to it again. And Allah established al-Bayt al-Ma'mur as a repentance (توبة) for the inhabitants of the heaven, and the Ka'bah as a repentance for the inhabitants of the Earth. Then Allah, blessed and exalted is He, said: إِنِّي خالِقٌ (1) بَشَراً مِنْ صَلْصالٍ مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُونٍ He said: And that was a prelude from Allah the Almighty regarding Adam before He created him, and a proof (احتجاجاً) from Him against them. He said: So He, majestic is His glory, scooped up a handful of the sweet, fresh water (الماء العذب الفرات) with His Right Hand—and both His Hands are Right—and He solidified it (فَصَلْصَلَهَا) and it froze. And Allah, majestic is His glory, said: (From you I shall create the Prophets and the Messengers, and My righteous servants, and the rightly guided Imams (الأئمّة المهديّين), the callers to Paradise, and their followers until the Day of Resurrection. And I am not questioned concerning what I do, but they will be questioned). Then He scooped up a handful of the salty, bitter water (الماء المالح الأجاج) and solidified it (فَصَلْصَلَهَا) and it froze. Then the Almighty said: (And from you I shall create the Pharaohs and the tyrants, and the brothers of the devils, and the insolent ones, and the callers to the Fire, and their followers until the Day of Resurrection. And I am not questioned concerning what I do, but they will be questioned). He said: And He stipulated al-Bada' (divine alteration of destiny) concerning them, but He did not stipulate it concerning the people of the right (أصحاب اليمين). Then He mixed the two waters together in His palm and solidified them (فَصَلْصَلَهَما). Then He poured them out before His Throne, and they were a progeny (سلالة) of clay. Then He commanded the angels of the directions—the North (الشّمال), the South (الجنوب), the East wind (الصّبا), and the West wind (الدّبور)—to circulate over this progeny of clay. So they perfected it and originated it. Then they divided it and separated it, and they introduced into it the four natures (الطبَائع الأربع): the two biles (المرّتين) (1), the blood (الدمّ), and the phlegm (البلغم). So the angels circulated over it and introduced into it the four natures. The blood (الدمّ) came from the direction of the East wind (الصّبَا), the phlegm (البلغم) from the direction of the North (الشمال), the yellow bile (المرّة الصّفراء) from the direction of the South (الجنوب), and the black bile (المرّة السّوداء) from the direction of the West wind (الدّبور). Thus, the soul (النّسمة) became independent and the body was completed. From the direction of the wind (الرِّيح), he was afflicted with the love of women, prolonged hope, and greed (الحرص). From the direction of the phlegm (البلغم), he was afflicted with the love of food and drink, kindness (البرّ), forbearance (الحلم), and gentleness (الرّفق). From the direction of the bile (المرّة), he was afflicted with anger (الغضَبَ), foolishness (السّفه), devilishness (الشّيطنة), tyranny (التجبّر), rebellion (التمرّد), and haste (العجلة). And from the direction of the blood (الدمّ), he was afflicted with the love of corruption (الفساد), pleasures (اللّذات), committing forbidden acts (ركوب المحارم), and desires (الشهوات).

Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: We found this in the book of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And al-Qummi added in his narration: So Allah created Adam (peace be upon him), and he remained formed for forty years. The accursed Iblis would pass by him and say: "You were created for some great purpose." Al-Alim (the knowledgeable one) (peace be upon him) said: Iblis then said: "If Allah commands me to prostrate to this, I shall disobey Him." He said: Then He breathed the spirit into him, and when it reached his brain, he sneezed a sneeze and sat up straight from it, saying: "All praise belongs to Allah (الحمد لله)." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, answered him: "May your Lord have mercy upon you, O Adam." The Imam (peace be upon him) said: Thus, mercy from Allah preceded for him.

I say: Most of what this narration contains has been transmitted in many reports from them (peace be upon them).

And in the narration of al-Ayyashi: The angels conferred a favor upon Allah through their worship of Him, so He turned away from them. And they said to themselves during their prostration: "We did not think that Allah would create a creation more honored by Him than us. We are the treasurers of Allah, His neighbors, and the closest of creation to Him." When they raised their heads, Allah said: وَأَعْلَمُ ما تُبْدُونَ (referring to your rejection of My command) وَما كُنْتُمْ تَكْتُمُونَ (referring to your assumption that I would not create a creation more honored by Me than you). When the angels realized that they had fallen into an error, they sought refuge in the Throne (العرش). And it was a group (عصابة) of the angels, not all of them. (The rest of) the narration.

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That was due to partisanship (تعصّباً) on their part, so He veiled Himself from them for seven years. They sought refuge in the Throne, saying: "Here we are, O Possessor of the Ascending Ways (ذا المعارج), here we are," until He accepted their repentance. Then, when Adam committed the sin, he circumambulated the House until Allah accepted his repentance.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): So Allah became angry with them. Then they asked Him for repentance, and He commanded them to circumambulate al-Dirah (الضّراح) (1), which is al-Bayt al-Ma'mur. They remained circumambulating it for seven years, seeking Allah's forgiveness for what they had said. Then Allah accepted their repentance after that and was pleased with them. This was the origin of the circumambulation (الطّواف). Then Allah made al-Bayt al-Haram (the Sacred House) directly below al-Dirah (الضّراح), as a repentance for those among the Children of Adam who sinned, and a purification for them.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): So He veiled them from His light for seven thousand years. They sought refuge in the Throne for seven thousand years, so He had mercy on them and accepted their repentance. He made for them al-Bayt al-Ma'mur, which is in the fourth heaven, making it a place of return (مثابة) and security for them. And He placed al-Bayt al-Haram beneath al-Bayt al-Ma'mur, making it a place of return (مثابةً) and security for mankind. Thus, the circumambulation became seven circuits (أشواط), which He made obligatory upon the servants, one circuit for every thousand years.

I say: There is no contradiction between the seven years and the seven thousand years, because the duration of years and days differs according to the difference in realms (النّشئات) and worlds (العوالم). Allah the Almighty said: فِي يَوْمٍ كانَ مِقْدارُهُ خَمْسِينَ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ (in a day whose measure is fifty thousand years.) And He said: وَإِنَّ يَوْماً عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ (Indeed a day with your Lord is like a thousand years of what you count.) So it is permissible that sometimes He counted it by the years of one realm (نشأة) and other times by the years of another realm.

** وَعَلَّمَ آدَمَ الْأَسْماءَ كُلَّها ثُمَ (٣١) (And He taught Adam all the Names) In al-Qummi, he (peace be upon him) said: The names of the mountains, the seas, the valleys (الأودية), the plants, and the animals. In al-Majma' and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked what He taught him. He said: The lands, the mountains, the ravines (الشّعاب), and the valleys (الأدوية). Then he looked at a mat beneath him and said: "And this mat is among what He taught him." In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): He taught him the names of everything. And in it also: The names of the Prophets of Allah, His vicegerents (أوليائه), and His rebellious enemies (عتاة أعدائه). I say: Verifying this station (المقام) and reconciling the two narrations of the Imam requires an expansion of speech and the mention of some secrets. So we say, and success is through Allah: The intent behind teaching the Names is not merely teaching the words (الألفاظ) and indicating their meanings (معانيها). How could it be, when that would revert to teaching language, and it is not a knowledge suitable for boasting over the angels and being favored above them? Rather, the intent behind the Names is the realities of the created beings (حقائق المخلوقات) existing in the realm of Omnipotence (عالم الجَبَروت), which are called by one group the Words (الكلمات), by another group the Names (الأسماء), and by yet others the Intellects (العقول). In summary: [They are] the causes of the existence of creatures (أسباب وجود الخلائق) and the Lords (أرباب) of their species, by which they were created, by which they subsist, and by which they are provided sustenance. For these are the Names of Allah the Almighty, because they indicate Allah through their manifestation in the loci of appearance (المظاهر), just as the name indicates the named (المسمّى). For indication (الدلالة), just as it occurs through words (الألفاظ), likewise occurs through essences (الذّوات), without any difference between them regarding what leads to the meaning. And the Names of Allah do not resemble the names of His creation. They were attributed in the Hadith sometimes to all creatures because all of them are their loci of appearance (مظاهرها) in which His attributes appeared separately, and at other times to the vicegerents (الأولياء) and the enemies (الأعداء) because they are their loci of appearance in which His attributes appeared collectively—meaning that all the attributes of Grace (اللّطف) appeared in the vicegerents, and all the attributes of Might (القهر) appeared in the enemies. This is alluded to in the Hadith Qudsi which will be mentioned in the interpretation of the verse regarding the angels' prostration to Adam (peace be upon him), from His saying, glory be to Him: "O Adam, these are the forms (أشباح) of the best of My creatures and My creations. This is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and I am al-Hamid (the Praiseworthy), the Praised in My actions; I derived a Name for him from My Name. And this is Ali, and I am al-'Ali (the High), al-'Azim (the Great); I derived a Name for him from My Name," until the end of what was mentioned of this kind. For the meaning of derivation (الاشتقاق) in such a context reverts to the manifestation of the attributes (ظهور الصّفات) and the announcement by the locus of appearance (المظهر) about the One manifested therein (الظّاهر فيه), or they are two causes for the derivation, or two effects resulting from it. Only he who does not understand the identity (العينيّة) speaks of causality (السّببيّة). The intent behind teaching Adam all the Names is His creation of him from different parts (أجزاء) and distinct powers (قوىً متباينة) until he became prepared to perceive all types of perceptibles (المدركات)—from intelligibles (المعقولات), sensibles (المحسوسات), imaginables (المتخيّلات), and estimables (الموهومات)—and His inspiring him with the knowledge of the essences of things (ذوات الأشياء), their properties (خواصّها), the fundamentals of knowledge (أصول العلم), the laws of crafts (قوانين الصّناعات), the manner of their tools (آلاتها), and the distinction between the vicegerents of Allah and His enemies. Thus, his knowledge of all that results from his being the locus of manifestation (مظهريّته) for all the Beautiful Names of Allah (أسماء الله الحسنى), his attainment of the station of the Singularity of Collection (أحديّة الجمع) by which he surpassed all other types of existing beings, and his return to his original station from which he came, becoming selected for the Great Book of Allah, which is the Greater World (العالم الأكبر), as the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "And within you is folded the Greater World." If you say: We do not understand much of what you are saying. Grant that the intent behind the Names is the realities (الحقائق), then what is the connection (مناسبة) between teaching Adam the names of the creatures and His creation of him with different powers and parts, and His inspiring him with the knowledge of the essences of things and the distinction between the vicegerents and the enemies? Do you have any clarification (تبيان) or can you bring forth an authority (سلطان) regarding this, by which this riddle (اللّغز) and obscurity (المعمىّ) may be solved, or by which the blindness (العَمَهَ) and obscurity (العمى) may be removed from the insights (البصائر)? I say: Perhaps you have forgotten what we verified in the Fourth Introduction concerning the meaning of the ambiguous (المتشابه) and its interpretation (تأويله), or you were unable to apply it to the matter we are currently discussing. So, let us present that to you in another formulation (تقرير آخر) which will be clearer for you in our current context than what we established there. So we say: And success is through Allah: The Name (الاسم) is that which indicates the Named (المسمّى) and serves as a sign (علامة) for understanding Him. Some Names require consideration of an attribute (صفة) that exists in the Named, and based on that consideration, the Name is applied to Him. Others do not require that consideration. The former indicates the Essence (الذّات) described by a specific attribute, such as the word al-Rahman (الرّحمن), for it indicates an Essence characterized by Mercy (الرّحمة), and the word al-Qahhar (القهّار), for it indicates an Essence possessing Might (القهر), and so forth. The Name may also be applied in this sense to the loci of manifestation (مظاهر) of the attribute of the Essence, based on their being characterized by that attribute, such as the Prophet, who is the locus of manifestation of Allah's guidance, glory be to Him. For he is the Name of Allah, al-Hadi (the Guide), to His servants. And the uttered names (الأسماء الملفوظة) in this consideration are the names of the Names (أسماء الأسماء). And our Master al-Rida (peace be upon him) was asked: "What is the Name (الاسم)?" He said: "An attribute (صفة) for the described (موصوف)." This word (اللّفظ) admits both meanings: the word and the locus of manifestation (المظهر), although it is clearer in the locus of manifestation. The Name may also be applied to what is understood from the word, meaning the mental concept (المعنى الذّهني). Based on this, the saying of al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was reported: "Whoever worships Allah through imagination (التّوهّم) has disbelieved (كفر). And whoever worships the Name (الاسم) and the Meaning (المعنى) has associated partners (أشرك). And whoever worships the Meaning (المعنى) by applying the Names to Him with the attributes by which He described Himself, and binds his heart to Him and speaks of Him with his tongue, in his secrets and his public acts—those are the true believers." For the intent behind the Name (الاسم) here is what is understood from the word, not the word itself, since the word is not worshipped. And the Meaning (المعنى) is that to which the word applies. Thus, the Name is a mental concept (معنى ذهنيّ), and the Meaning is an actual existing entity (موجود عيني), which is the Named (المسمّى). And the Name is other than the Named, because, for example, the human being in the mind (في الذِّهن) is not a human being, nor does he possess corporeality (جسميّة), life, sensation, movement, speech, or any of the properties of humanity. Reflect upon this, and you will understand the meaning of the Hadith, and assistance is from Allah. When this has been established, know that every Name among the Divine Names of Allah has a locus of manifestation (مظهراً) among existing beings, based on the predominance of the manifestation of the attribute contained within that Name therein. And that [locus of manifestation] is a Name, based on its indication of Allah through its characterization by that attribute. This is because Allah, glory be to Him, only creates and governs every species of creatures by one of His Names, and that Name is the Lord (ربّ) of that species, and Allah, glory be to Him, is the Lord of Lords (ربّ الأرباب).
