This is alluded to in the speech of the People of the House (peace be upon them) in their supplications (peace be upon them) when they say: "And by the Name by which You created the Throne (العرش), and by the Name by which You created the Footstool (الكرسيّ), and by the Name by which You created the souls (الأرواح)." And other examples of this pattern. And narrated from our Master al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "We, by Allah, are the Beautiful Names (الأسماء الحسنى) by which Allah accepts no deed from His servants except through knowledge of us." This is because they (peace be upon them) are the means (وسائل) for knowing His Essence, the intermediaries (وسائط) for the manifestation of His attributes, and the Lords (أرباب) of the species of His creation. And no one attains knowledge of all the Names unless he is a locus of manifestation for all of them, and he is not a locus of manifestation for all of them unless his nature (جبلّته) possesses the readiness to accept all of that, which is what we have mentioned. So understand, and you will be rightly guided, if Allah wills. ثُمَّ عَرَضَهُمْ عَلَى الْمَلائِكَةِ : (Then He presented them to the angels) I say: Meaning, He presented the forms (أشباح) of the creatures one by one in the realm of Dominion (عالم الملكوت), which some call the realm of spiritual beings (عالم الرّوحانيّات), indicated by the mention of the Names, since they are the loci of manifestation for all or some of the Names. For this reason, He used the pronoun for rational beings (ذوي العقول), because they are all possessors of intellect. And in the last narration, meaning He presented their forms (أشباحهم) while they were lights in the shadows (الأظلّة), which is explicit regarding what we have said. فَقالَ أَنْبِئُونِي بِأَسْماءِ هؤُلاءِ : (and said, 'Inform Me of the names of these) I say: Meaning, the Names of Allah by which these forms (الأشباح) were created. For they, in their entirety, were concealed from the earthly angels, except for one type for each class of them, just as they are concealed from the rest of the creatures, save for the Prophets and the vicegerents (الأولياء). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ : (if you are truthful.') [Meaning:] that you are more deserving of the vicegerency (الخلافة) than Adam, and that all of you glorify and sanctify [Him], and that leaving you here is more appropriate than bringing forth those who come after you. That is, just as you did not know the unseen nature (غيب) of those among you whose persons (أشخاصها) you see, it is all the more fitting that you should not know the unseen that has not yet come into being.

(٣٢) قالُوا سُبْحانَكَ لا عِلْمَ لَنا إِلَّا ما عَلَّمْتَنا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَلِيمُ (32) They said, 'Immaculate are You! We have no knowledge except what You have taught us. Indeed You are the All-knowing, the All-wise.' the All-knowing of everything, the All-wise (الْحَكِيمُ), the One Who acts rightly in every deed (المصيب بكلّ فعل).

I say: They only admitted incapacity and deficiency because of what had become clear to them regarding the merit of Adam, and the wisdom in his creation became apparent to them. Thus, their state diminished in their own eyes, and their deeds seemed few to them, and the ship of their might (جبروت) was broken, so they drowned in the sea of incapacity and entrusted knowledge and wisdom to Allah. And they only did not know the realities of all things due to their differences and disparities, and their being singular in attribute (وحدانيّة الصّفة), since there is no mixture (خلط) or composition (تركيب) in their nature. For this reason, every category (صنف) among them performs only one action. So the one among them who bows (الرّاكع) is bowing forever, and the one among them who prostrates (السّاجد) is prostrating forever, and the one among them who stands (القائم) is standing forever, as Allah related about them in His saying: (وَما مِنَّا إِلَّا لَهُ مَقامٌ مَعْلُومٌ) (And there is none of us but has a known station.) And for this reason, they have neither rivalry (تنافس) nor mutual hatred (تباغض). Rather, their example is the example of the senses, for sight does not compete with hearing in perceiving sounds, nor does smell compete with them both, nor do they both compete with smell. Therefore, they are naturally disposed (مجبولون) toward obedience, and there is no scope for disobedience concerning them. ** لا يَعْصُونَ اللهَ ما أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ ما يُؤْمَرُونَ يُسَبِّحُونَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ لا يَفْتُرُونَ (They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they carry out what they are commanded. They glorify [Him] night and day, and they do not flag.) So every category (صنف) among them is a manifestation (مظهر) of one Name among the Divine Names, which it does not exceed. Thus, Adam surpassed them by virtue of his complete knowledge and his comprehensive manifestation (مظهريته الشاملة).

(٣٣) قالَ يا آدَمُ أَنْبِئْهُمْ بِأَسْمائِهِمْ. (33) He said, 'O Adam, inform them of their names.' I say: meaning, inform them of the realities hidden from them and the knowledges concealed from them, so that they may know your comprehensiveness (جامعيّتك) of them, and the power of Allah, the Exalted, to combine between the disparate attributes and the contradictory names and their manifestations, including the opposition (التضاد) within them, in a single creature. As it was said: It is not strange for Allah to gather the universe in one (individual). فَلَمَّا أَنْبَأَهُمْ بِأَسْمائِهِمْ When he informed them of their names, so they knew them. He took covenants and solemn pledges from them regarding the Prophets and the Vicegerents (الأولياء), concerning belief in them and preferring them over themselves. Thereupon ** قالَ أَلَمْ أَقُلْ لَكُمْ إِنِّي أَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (He said, 'Did I not tell you that I know the unseen of the heavens and the earth?)—their secret— وَأَعْلَمُ ما تُبْدُونَ ** (and I know what you disclose)—which is your rejection of Me— وَما كُنْتُمْ تَكْتُمُونَ ** (and what you used to conceal?)—which is your belief that no one would come who would be superior to you, and the resolve of Iblis to refuse (الاباء) regarding Adam when he was commanded to obey him. So He made Adam a proof (حجّة) against them.

(٣٤) ** وَإِذْ قُلْنا لِلْمَلائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ (34) And when We said to the angels, 'Prostrate before Adam,'**

And that was because of the lights (الأنوار) of our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his infallible Household عليهم السلام that were in his loins. And they (the Household) had been favored over the angels due to their enduring harm (الأذى) for the sake of Allah. Thus, the prostration was an act of veneration (تعظيماً) and honoring (إكراماً) for them, an act of servitude (عبودية) for Allah, the Glorified, and an act of obedience (طاعة) to Adam عليه السلام.

Ali ibn al-Husayn said: My father narrated to me, from his father عليهم السلام, from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who said: "O servants of Allah, when Adam عليه السلام saw the light shining forth from his loins—for Allah had transferred our forms (أشباحنا) from the summit (ذروة) of the Throne (العرش) to his back—he saw the light but did not discern the forms (الأشباح). So he said: 'O Lord, what are these lights?' He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: '(They are) lights and forms which I transferred from the most honorable places of My Throne to your back. And for that reason, I commanded the angels to prostrate to you, since you were the vessel (وعاءً) for those forms.' Adam said: 'O Lord, if only You would make them visible to me.' Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'Look, O Adam, at the summit of the Throne.' So the images of the lights of our forms (أشباحنا) that were in his back were imprinted upon it, just as the face of a person is imprinted upon a clear mirror. Then he saw our forms (أشباحنا) and said: 'What are these forms, O Lord?' Allah said: 'O Adam, these are the forms of the best of My creation (خلائقي) and My creatures (بريّاتي). This is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and I am al-Hamid (the Praiseworthy), al-Mahmud (the Highly Praised) in My actions, and I derived a Name for him from My Name. And this is Ali, and I am al-Ali (the Exalted), al-Azim (the Magnificent), and I derived a Name for him from My Name. And this is Fatimah, and I am Fatir (the Originator) of the heavens and the earth, the One Who cuts off (فاطم) My enemies from My Mercy on the Day My judgment is decided, and the One Who cuts off (فاطم) My allies from that which disgraces and shames them, so I derived a Name for her from My Name. And this is al-Hasan and this is al-Husayn, and I am al-Muhsin (the Benefactor), al-Mujmil (the Beautifier), and I derived their two names from My Name. These are the chosen ones (خيار) of My creation and the noble ones (كرام) of My creatures. Through them I seize, through them I give, through them I punish, and through them I reward. So seek intercession (توسّل) through them to Me, O Adam, when a calamity (داهية) befalls you, and make them your intercessors (شفعاءك) to Me. For I have sworn a true oath upon Myself that I will not disappoint a hope through them, nor will I turn away a petitioner through them.' For this reason, when the error (الخطيئة) slipped from him, he called upon Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, through them, and He accepted his repentance (تِيبَ عَلَيْهِ) and forgave him."

فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ So they prostrated, but not Iblis.

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Rida عليه السلام: His name was al-Harith (الحارث). He was named Iblis (إبليس) because he despaired (أبلس) of the Mercy of Allah.

** أَبى وَاسْتَكْبَرَ He refused and acted arrogantly**

He brought forth the envy (الحسدِ) that was in his heart.

** وَكانَ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ. and he was one of the faithless.**

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: He was the first one who disbelieved (كفر) and initiated disbelief (الكفر).

And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it from al-Sadiq عليه السلام.

And al-Qummi narrated from him عليه السلام: Arrogance (الاستكبار) is the first disobedience (معصية) by which Allah was disobeyed. He عليه السلام said: Iblis said: "O Lord, exempt me from prostrating to Adam, and I will worship You with a worship that no close angel (ملك مقرّب) nor sent prophet (نبيّ مرسل) has ever worshipped You with." He, Whose Majesty is Glorious, said: "I have no need for your worship. My worship is only accepted from where I desire, not from where you desire."

(35) ** وَقُلْنا يا آدَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنْتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الْجَنَّةَ (And We said, 'O Adam, dwell with your spouse in paradise) In al-Kafi, al-Ilal, and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was one of the gardens (جنان) of the world (الدنيا) upon which the sun and the moon rose. Had it been one of the gardens of eternity (جنان الخلد), he would never have left it. Al-Qummi added: And Iblis did not enter it.

** وَكُلا مِنْها رَغَداً ** (and eat thereof freely) abundantly (واسعاً).

** حَيْثُ شِئْتُما ** (wherever you wish,) without toil.

** وَلا تَقْرَبا هذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ (but do not approach this tree,) Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means, "Do not eat from it." It was said: The prohibition was only attached to the proximity (القرب), which is one of the preliminaries to consumption (التناول), as an exaggeration in its prohibition and the necessity of avoiding it, and as an alert that proximity to a thing generates an inclination (داعية) and a tendency (ميلاناً) that takes hold of the core of the heart and distracts it from what is required by reason (العقل) and the Sacred Law (الشرع).

In Tafsir al-Imam: It is the tree of the knowledge (علم) of Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace). Allah the Almighty favored them with it over the rest of His creation. No one consumes from it by the command of Allah except them. And from it is what the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace), Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them) consumed after feeding the needy (المسكين), the orphan (اليتيم), and the captive (الأسير), until they no longer felt hunger, thirst, toil, or fatigue. This tree was distinguished from all other trees in that while every other tree bears only one type of fruit, this tree and its species bore wheat (البرّ), grapes (العنب), figs (التين), jujubes (العنّاب), and all other types of fruits, produce, and foods. Therefore, those who narrated its description differed: some said it was a grain of wheat (برّة), others said it was a grape (عنبة), and others said it was a jujube (عنّابة). It is the tree that whoever consumes from it by the permission of Allah is inspired with the knowledge (علم) of the ancients and the moderns without learning, and whoever consumes without the permission of Allah fails in his desire and disobeys his Lord.

I say: And in one narration: It is the tree of envy (شجرة الحسد). And in another narration: It is the camphor tree (شجرة الكافور).

In al-Uyun, with his chain of transmission, narrated from Abd al-Salam ibn Salih al-Harawi, who said: I said to Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): "O son of the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace), inform me about the tree from which Adam and Eve ate. What was it? People have differed concerning it. Some narrate that it was wheat (الحنطة), some narrate that it was grapes (العنب), and some narrate that it was the tree of envy (شجرة الحسد)." He said: "All of that is true." I said: "Then what is the meaning of these differing aspects?" He said: "O Aba al-Salt, the tree of Paradise bears various types [of fruit]. It was a wheat tree, and it contained grapes, unlike the trees of the world (الدنيا). When Allah, may His mention be exalted, honored Adam by making His angels prostrate to him and by admitting him into Paradise, Adam said to himself, 'Has Allah created any human being better than me?' Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, knew what occurred in his soul, so He called out to him: 'Raise your head, O Adam, and look at the leg of My Throne (ساق عرشي).' Adam raised his head and looked at the leg of the Throne, and he found written upon it: 'There is no god but Allah, Muhammad is the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace), and Ali ibn Abi Talib is the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين), and his spouse Fatimah is the Mistress of the women of the worlds (سيدة نساء العالمين), and al-Hasan and al-Husayn are the Masters of the youth of the people of Paradise (سيدا شباب أهل الجنّة).' Adam said: 'O Lord, who are these?' He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: 'These are from your progeny, and they are better than you and better than all My creation. Were it not for them, I would not have created you, nor would I have created Paradise or the Fire, nor the heavens or the earth. Beware lest you look at them with the eye of envy (الحسد), lest I expel you from My proximity (جواري).' So, he looked at them with the eye of envy and wished for their station, and Satan gained dominance over him until he ate from the tree which he was forbidden. And Satan gained dominance over Eve because of her looking at Fatimah with the eye of envy, until she ate from the tree just as Adam ate. So, Allah the Almighty expelled them both from His Paradise and sent them down from His proximity to the earth."

I say: Just as the human body has nourishment from grains and fruits, so too does his soul (روح) have nourishment from sciences (العلوم) and gnosis (المعارف). And just as the nourishment of his body has trees that bear it, so too does his soul have trees that bear it. And for every category of it, there is suitable nourishment. For among humans, there are those in whom the rule (حكم) of the body prevails over the rule of the soul, and among them are those for whom the reverse is true. And they have degrees (درجات) in this, by which some are favored over others. And the people of the highest degree possess everything that the people of the lower degree possess, and more. And every fruit in the physical world (العالم الجسماني) has a corresponding example (مثال) in the spiritual world (العالم الروحاني) suitable for it, as was previously indicated in the Fourth Introduction (المقدّمة الرابعة).

For this reason, the tree (الشَّجَرَةَ) was interpreted sometimes as the tree of fruits and other times as the tree of sciences (شجرة العلوم). And the tree of the knowledge (علم) of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his Family and grant them peace) was an indication of the perfect belovedness (المحبوبية الكاملة) that bears all human perfections (الكمالات الإنسانية) necessitating the Muhammadan Monotheism (التوحيد المحمدي), which is annihilation in Allah (الفناء في الله) and subsistence through Allah (البقاء بالله), referred to by his saying (peace be upon him): "I have a time with Allah in which neither a near-stationed angel (ملك مقرّب) nor a sent prophet (نبيّ مرسل) can encompass me." For in it are the fruits of all gnosis (المعارف). And the camphor tree (شجرة الكافور) is an indication of the coolness of certainty (برد اليقين) which necessitates perfect tranquility (الطمأنينة الكاملة) and entails the magnificent character (الخلق العظيم) that belonged to our Prophet (peace be upon him) and, to a lesser degree, to his Household (peace be upon them). Thus, there is no contradiction between the narrations, nor between them and what the people of esoteric interpretation (أهل التأويل) said: that it is the tree of caprice (الهوى) and nature (الطبيعة), because approaching it only occurs through caprice (الهوى) and natural desire (الشهوة الطبيعية). This is the meaning of what was narrated: that it is the tree of envy (شجرة الحسد), for envy only originates from it.

** فَتَكُونا مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ (lest you become wrongdoers.') by your disobedience and your seeking a station (درجة) with which others have been favored, if you aim for it without the ruling (حكم) of Allah.

(٣٦) The Almighty's saying: فَأَزَلَّهُمَا (Then Satan caused them to slip). It was also read (وقرئ): فأزالهما (Then he removed them): الشَّيْطانُ عَنْها (from it) by his whispering (بوسوسته), his deception (وخديعته), his illusion (وإيهامه), his enmity (وعداوته), and his delusion (وغروره), by starting with Adam: وَقالَ مَا نَهَاكُمَا رَبُّكُمَا عَنْ هَٰذِهِ الشَّجَرَةِ إِلَّا أَن تَكُونَا مَلَكَيْنِ (and said, 'Your Lord did not forbid you from this tree except lest you become angels). If you consume from it, you will know the unseen (الغيب) and be able to do what He Whom Allah the Almighty has singled out with power is able to do, أَوْ تَكُونَا مِنَ الْخَالِدِينَ (or lest you become immortal). You will never die. وَقَاسَمَهُمَا (And he swore to them both) he swore an oath to them both: إِنِّي لَكُمَا لَمِنَ النَّاصِحِينَ ('I am indeed your well-wisher').

Then Satan caused them to slip from it (36) and said, 'Your Lord did not forbid you from this tree except lest you become angels or lest you become immortal.' And he swore to them both, 'I am indeed your well-wisher.'

And Iblis was between the jaws (لحيي) of the serpent (الحيّة); she had brought him into Paradise. Adam thought that it was the serpent addressing him, and he did not know that Iblis had hidden himself between its jaws. So Adam replied to the serpent: "O serpent, this is from the delusion (غرور) of Iblis! How could our Lord betray us? Or how can you glorify Allah by swearing by Him while you attribute treachery and ill-consideration to Him, when He is the Most Generous of the generous? Or how can I seek to attain what my Lord has forbidden me and pursue it without His command?" When Iblis despaired of Adam (peace be upon him) accepting from him, he returned a second time between the jaws of the serpent and addressed Eve, making her believe that it was the serpent addressing her. He said: "O Eve, have you seen this tree which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), had forbidden you both? He has now permitted it to you both after forbidding it, because He recognized the excellence of your obedience to Him and your reverence for Him. This is because the angels entrusted with the tree, who have spears (الحراب) to ward off the other animals of Paradise from it, will not ward you off if you approach it. Know by this that He has permitted it to you. And rejoice, for if you consume from it before Adam (peace be upon him), you will be the one dominant over him, commanding and forbidding above him." Eve said: "I shall try this." So she approached the tree. The angels intended to ward her off with their spears, but Allah revealed to them: "You only ward off with your spears those who have no intellect (عقل) to restrain them. But as for the one Whom I have made capable (متمكّناً), discerning (مميزاً), and choosing (مختاراً), entrust him to his intellect (عقله) which I have made a proof (حجّة) against him. If he obeys, he deserves My reward (ثوابي), and if he disobeys and violates My command, he deserves My punishment (عقابي) and recompense (جزائي)." So they left her and did not interfere with her after they had intended to prevent her with their spears. She thought that Allah had forbidden them from preventing her because He had permitted it after He had forbidden it. So she said: "The serpent spoke the truth," thinking that the one addressing her was the serpent. She consumed from it and did not perceive any change in herself. She said to Adam (peace be upon him): "Did you not know that the forbidden tree has been permitted to us? I consumed from it, and its angels did not prevent me, nor did I perceive any change in my state." Because of this, Adam (peace be upon him) was deceived (اغتر) and erred (غلط), and he consumed.

فَأَخْرَجَهُمَا مِمَّا كَانَا فِيهِ (So He expelled them from what they were in) of blessings. وَقُلْنَا (And We said): O Adam, O Eve, O serpent, and O Iblis, اهْبِطُوا بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ (Get down, being enemies of one another). So Adam, Eve, and their progeny are enemies to the serpent and Iblis, and Iblis and the serpent and their progeny are their enemies. The descent (هبوط) of Adam, Eve, and the serpent was from Paradise, for the serpent was one of its most beautiful creatures (دوابّها). And the descent of Iblis was from its vicinity (حواليها), for it was forbidden for him to enter Paradise.

So He expelled them from what they were in And We said, 'Get down, being enemies of one another.'

I say: Perhaps his entry into Paradise was only forbidden openly (بارزاً) such that he would be recognized, because he had entered it hidden in the mouth of the serpent to mislead them with delusion (بغرور), as mentioned in another narration (حديث آخر). By this, the contradiction between this narration and the narration that passed—that if it had been from the Gardens of Eternity (جنان الخلد), Iblis would not have entered it—is resolved. What was meant by it was his entry while he was in the mouth of the serpent. So let this be pondered (فليتدبر).

وَلَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّ (and there shall be for you an abode on the earth) a dwelling place (منزل) and a residence (مقرّ) for livelihood (للمعاش), وَمَتَاعٌ (and enjoyment) benefit (منفعة), إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (for a while). Until the time of death. Allah brings forth your crops and fruits from it, and by means of it, He gives you pleasure (ينزّهكم) and grants you comfort (وينعِّمكم). And in it, He tests you with afflictions (بالبلايا). He grants you the pleasure of the worldly comfort sometimes so that you may remember by it the pure comfort of the Hereafter, which is free from what spoils, nullifies, causes aversion to, and diminishes the comfort of this world. And He tests you sometimes with the afflictions of this world, which contain hardships (الزّحمات) and within their folds are retributions (النّقمات), so that He may warn you thereby of the eternal punishment (عذاب الأبد) which no well-being (عافية) can taint.

and there shall be for you an abode on the earth and enjoyment for a while.

And in the narration of al-Qummi: إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (for a while) means until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة).

I say: There is no contradiction (منافاة) between the two narrations, because death is the minor Resurrection (القيامة الصغرى) for most people and the major Resurrection (الكبرى) for others. Hence, it has been narrated: "Whoever dies, his Resurrection has begun."

(37) ** فَتَلَقَّى آدَمُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ كَلِماتٍ (Then Adam received certain words from his Lord) He was to say them, and so he said them. It was also recited with Adam in the accusative (نصب) and kalimāt (كلمات) in the nominative (رفع). ** فَتابَ عَلَيْهِ (whereat He turned to him mercifully) by means of them. ** إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ** (Indeed, He is the All-forgiving) Who accepts repentance frequently ** الرَّحِيمُ ** (the All-merciful) toward those who repent.

I say: Al-Tawbah (التوبة) means returning (الرجوع) and turning in repentance (الإنابة). When it is attributed to Allah, the Exalted, it is followed by the preposition ‘alā (على), and when it is attributed to the servant, it is followed by the preposition ilā (إلى). Perhaps the former is due to the inclusion of the meaning of compassion (الإشفاق) and kindness (العطف). The meaning of al-tawbah (التوبة) from the servant is his return to Allah through obedience and submission after having disobeyed and transgressed. Its meaning when attributed to Allah is His return with kindness toward His servant by inspiring him with al-tawbah (التوبة) first, and then accepting it from him finally. Thus, Allah has two acts of tawbah (توبة), and the servant has one between them. Allah said: (ثُمَّ تابَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَتُوبُوا) (Then He turned to them mercifully so that they might repent) That is, He inspired them with al-tawbah (التوبة) so that they might return, and then when they returned, He accepted their tawbah (توبة), because He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful. This verse has another meaning which will come in Surat al-Tawbah (سورة التوبة), if Allah wills.

In al-Kafi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both): The words (الكلمات) were: (لا إلهَ إلّا أنْتَ سُبْحانَكَ اللهُمّ وَبِحَمْدِك عملت سوءً وظلمت نفسي فاغفر لي وأنت خير الغافرين لا إله إلّا أنت سبحانك اللهمّ وبحمدك عملت سوءً وظلمت نفسي فاغفر لي وارحمني إنك أنت أرحم الراحمين لا إله إلّا أنت سبحانك اللهمّ وبحمدك عملت سوءً وظلمت نفسي فتب عليّ إنّك التواب الرحيم) (There is no god except You. Glory be to You, O Allah, and with Your praise. I have done wrong and wronged myself, so forgive me, for You are the best of those who forgive. There is no god except You. Glory be to You, O Allah, and with Your praise. I have done wrong and wronged myself, so forgive me and have mercy on me, for indeed You are the Most Merciful of the merciful. There is no god except You. Glory be to You, O Allah, and with Your praise. I have done wrong and wronged myself, so turn to me mercifully, for indeed You are the All-forgiving, the All-merciful). And in one narration: (By the right of Muhammad and Ali and Fatimah and al-Hasan and al-Husayn), and in another: By the right of Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his Household and grant them peace).

In Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him): When the error (الخطيئة) slipped from Adam and he apologized to his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, he said: "O Lord, turn to me mercifully, accept my apology, restore me to my rank, and raise my degree with You, for the deficiency and humiliation of the error have become manifest upon my limbs and the rest of my body." Allah, the Exalted, said: "O Adam, do you not recall My command to you that you should call upon Me by [the right of] Muhammad and his pure Household (آله الطيّبين) during your hardships (شدائدك) and calamities (دواهيك), and in the misfortunes (النّوازل) that overwhelm you?" Adam said: "O Lord, yes." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "Then call upon Me by their right—specifically by Muhammad, Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn—and I shall respond to your request and grant you more than you desire." Adam then said: "O Lord, my God, what rank have they attained with You, that by seeking intercession (التوسل) through them You accept my tawbah (توبة) and forgive my error, while I am the one for whom You commanded Your angels to prostrate, and You permitted him Your Paradise, and You married him to Hawwa (حواء), Your maidservant, and made the noble angels serve him?" Allah, the Exalted, said: "O Adam, I only commanded the angels to honor you by prostrating to you because you were the vessel (وعاء) for these Lights (الأنوار). Had you asked Me by their right before your error to protect you from it, and to make you aware of the temptations of your enemy Iblis (إبليس) so that you might guard against them, I would have certainly done so. However, what is known in My prior knowledge proceeds in accordance with My knowledge. So now, call upon Me by their right, and I shall respond to you." Thereupon, Adam said: "O Allah, by the status (جاه) of Muhammad, Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn, and the pure ones (الطّيبين) from their Household, grant me the favor of accepting my tawbah (توبة), forgiving my slip (زلتي), and restoring me from Your honors (كراماتك) to my rank." Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "I have accepted your tawbah (توبة), I have turned to you with My satisfaction (رضواني), I have directed My bounties (آلائي) and favors (نعمائي) toward you, and I have restored you to your rank among My honors (كراماتي) and increased your share of My mercies (رحماتي)." That is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** فَتَلَقَّى آدَمُ مِنْ رَبِّهِ كَلِماتٍ فَتابَ عَلَيْهِ إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ**. (Then Adam received certain words from his Lord, whereat He turned to him mercifully. Indeed, He is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful.)

(٣٨) قُلْنَا اهْبِطُوا مِنْها جَمِيعاً (38) We said, 'Get down from it, all of you! They were commanded first to descend (بالهبوط), and second, that none of them should precede the others. ** فَإِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ مِنِّي هُدىً فَمَنْ تَبِعَ هُدايَ فَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ Then, should any guidance come to you from Me, those who follow My guidance shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.' It is said that the mā (ما) is extra (مزيدة) for the purpose of emphasizing the condition (الشرط), and for this reason, the nūn (النّون) (of emphasis) is appropriate, even though it does not contain the meaning of a request (الطلب). And the second condition (الشرط الثاني) along with its answer (جوابه) is the answer (جواب) to the first condition (الشرط الأول).

(٣٩) وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا ولآلائنا أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ (39) But those who disbelieve and deny Our signs—they shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it forever.

Al-Ayyashi mentioned a long narrative (حديثاً طويلاً) concerning Adam's disputation (محاجة) with his Lord regarding his sin. He said at the end of it: "Yes, O Lord, the argument (الحجّة) is Yours against us. We have wronged ourselves and disobeyed, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy on us, we shall be among the losers."

And Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Adam descended upon al-Safa and Eve upon al-Marwah. Adam remained prostrating for forty mornings, weeping over his sin and his separation from Paradise. He said: Then Gabriel descended upon Adam and said: "O Adam, did Allah not create you with His two hands, breathe into you of His Spirit, and make His angels prostrate to you?" He said: "Yes." He said: "And He commanded you not to eat from that tree, so why did you disobey Him?" He said: "O Gabriel, verily Iblis swore to me by Allah that he was a sincere advisor to me, and I did not think that anyone created by Allah would swear by Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, falsely." So Gabriel (peace be upon him) said to him: "O Adam, repent to Allah."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: Moses asked his Lord to gather him and Adam together, and He did so. Moses said to him: "O my father, did Allah not create you with His hand, breathe into you of His Spirit, make His angels prostrate to you, and command you not to eat from that tree? So why did you disobey Him?" He said: "O Moses, how long before my creation did you find my sin recorded in the Torah?" He said: "By thirty thousand years." He said: "Then that is it." Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "So Adam overcame Moses in argument (فحجّ آدم موسى)."

And in al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Verily, Allah the Almighty said to them both: ** لا تَقْرَبا هذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ (19) Do not approach this tree. and He pointed out to them the wheat tree (شجرة الحنطة). He did not say to them: "Nor eat from this tree, nor from what was of its kind." So they did not approach that tree, but rather they ate from another [tree] when Satan whispered to them both.

Then he said: And that act of Adam was before prophethood (النبوّة), and it was not a major sin (ذنب كبير) by which he deserved entry into the Fire. Rather, it was among the forgivable minor sins (الصغائر الموهوبة) which are permissible for the Prophets before the revelation (الوحي) descends upon them. When Allah the Almighty chose him and made him a Prophet, he was infallible (معصوماً), committing neither a minor nor a major sin. The Almighty Allah said: (وَعَصى آدَمُ رَبَّهُ فَغَوى ثُمَّ اجْتَباهُ رَبُّهُ فَتابَ عَلَيْهِ وَهَدى) (115) Adam disobeyed his Lord, and went astray. Then his Lord chose him, and turned to him clemently, and guided him. And He said: ** إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى آدَمَ وَنُوحاً** (33) Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah. the Ayah.

And in a narration: Verily, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, created Adam as a proof (حجّة) on His earth and a vicegerent (خليفة) in His lands. He did not create him for Paradise. And the act of disobedience (المعصية) by Adam occurred in Paradise, not on Earth, so that the decrees (مقادير) of the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, might be fulfilled. When He was sent down to Earth and Allah made him a proof and a vicegerent, He made him infallible (عصم) by His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى آدَمَ وَنُوحاً** (33) Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah. the Ayah.

And Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The age of Adam, from the time Allah created him until He took his soul, was nine hundred and thirty years. He was buried in Mecca. The Spirit was breathed into him on Friday after the sun passed the meridian (بعد الزّوال). Then He created his wife from the lowest of his ribs and settled him in His Paradise on that very day. He did not remain settled therein except for six hours of that day until he disobeyed Allah, so He expelled them both from Paradise after sunset, and they did not spend the night therein.

And Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Verily, Allah the Almighty breathed His Spirit into Adam after the sun passed the meridian on Friday. Then He created his wife from the lowest of his ribs—the narrative is as previously mentioned—and he added at the end of it: And they were placed at the courtyard (فناء) of Paradise until morning, and ** بَدَتْ لَهُما سَوْآتُهُما وَناداهُما رَبُّهُما أَلَمْ أَنْهَكُما عَنْ تِلْكُمَا الشَّجَرَةِ (22) their private parts became manifest to them, and their Lord called out to them: 'Did I not forbid you from that tree?' Adam felt ashamed before his Lord, so he humbled himself and said: ** رَبَّنا ظَلَمْنا أَنْفُسَنا (23) Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and we have confessed our sins, so forgive us. Allah said to them both: "Descend from My heavens to the Earth, for no disobedient one shall dwell near Me in My Paradise, nor in My heavens." Then he (peace be upon him) said: When Adam ate from the tree, he remembered what Allah had forbidden him concerning it, and he regretted it. He went to move away from the tree, but the tree seized his head and dragged him toward it and said to him: "Shouldn't your flight have been before you ate from me?"

(٤٠) يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ (O Children of Israel) The children of Ya‘qub. In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: Ya‘qub is Isra’il, and the meaning of Isra’il (إسرائيل) is the servant of Allah (عبد الله), because اسرا is the servant (العبد) and إيل is Allah (الله). And in a narration (رواية): اسرا is the strength (القوّة) and إيل is Allah (الله). ** اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتِيَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ (Remember My blessing which I bestowed upon you) That I sent Muhammad and established him in your city, and I did not burden you with traveling (الحطّ والتّرحال) to Him, and I clarified His signs (علاماته) and the proofs of His truthfulness (دلائل صدقه) so that His condition would not be confusing to you. وَأَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي (and fulfill My covenant) Which He took upon your ancestors—their prophets—and they commanded them to convey it to their successors: that they must surely believe in Muhammad, the Arab (العربي), the Qurayshi (القرشيّ), the Hashimi (الهاشمي), who is made manifest by signs (الآيات) and supported by miracles (المعجزات). Among His signs is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), His brother (شقيقه) and companion (رفيقه), whose intellect is from His intellect, whose knowledge is from His knowledge, and whose forbearance is from His forbearance, supporting His religion with his sword. ** أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ (I will fulfill your covenant) Which I have made obligatory for you: the eternal bliss (نعيم الأبد) in the Abode of Honor (دار الكرامة). وَإِيَّايَ فَارْهَبُونِ ** (and be in awe of Me alone) Regarding opposing Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for I am the One capable of averting the affliction (بلاء) from those who oppose you due to their agreement with Me, while they are unable to avert My retribution (انتقامي) from you if you prefer opposing Me. And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "Fulfill the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali, which is an obligation (فرضاً) from Allah, and I will fulfill for you the Paradise (الجنّة)."

I say: And this applies to every covenant (عهد) of Allah upon everyone.

Al-Qummi narrated: A man said to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: ** ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ (Call Me, I will answer you) yet we call and we are not answered. He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, you do not fulfill your covenant (عهده) with Allah, for He, the Exalted, says: ** أَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ (Fulfill My covenant, I will fulfill your covenant) By Allah, if you fulfilled your covenant with Allah, the Glorified, He would fulfill His covenant with you."

(٤١) وَآمِنُوا بِما أَنْزَلْتُ (And believe in what I have sent down) upon Muhammad, concerning the mention of his prophethood and the Imamate of his brother and his progeny (عترته), مُصَدِّقاً لِما (confirming what) مَعَكُمْ ** (is with you) for the like of this mention is in your Book. وَلا تَكُونُوا أَوَّلَ (١) كافِرٍ بِهِ (And do not be the first to disbelieve in it) It is said that this is an allusion that the obligation was for them to be the first to believe in Him, because they were the people who examined His miracles, possessed knowledge of His affair, sought victory through Him (المستفتحين به), and gave glad tidings of His time.

In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from him (peace be upon him): These are the Jews of Medina who denied the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and betrayed him. They said: "We know that Muhammad is a Prophet and that Ali is his successor (وصيّه), but you are neither that one nor is this one, rather they will come five hundred years after our time."

وَلا تَشْتَرُوا بِآياتِي ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً (And do not sell My signs for a paltry price) In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: Huyayy ibn Akhtab, Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf, and others among the Jews had an annual stipend (مأكلة) from the Jews. They disliked the annulment of this stipend due to the Prophet's command, so they distorted verses from the Torah that contained His description and mention. That is the paltry price (الثمن القليل) intended in the verse. وَإِيَّايَ فَاتَّقُونِ (And be wary of Me [alone]) regarding the concealment of the affair of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the affair of his successor (وصيّه).

footnote (١) *Al-Ayyashi* narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah: **يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ** (O Children of Israel), and he said: "They are us, specifically." And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was heard saying: "I am Your servant, my name is Ahmad, and I am the servant of Allah, my name is Israel. Whatever He commanded him, He has commanded me, and whatever He meant by him, He has meant by me." From him (*منه*), may Allah sanctify his secret (*قدس الله سره*).

(٤٢) وَلا تَلْبِسُوا الْحَقَّ بِالْباطِلِ (42) Do not mix the truth with falsehood Do not mix it with it, by acknowledging it from one aspect and denying it from another aspect. ** وَتَكْتُمُوا It is conjoined (عطف) to the prohibition, or it is in the accusative case (نصب) due to an implied an (أن): ** الْحَقَ such as the prophethood of this one and the Imamate of this one, وَأَنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (and conceal the truth while you know) that you are concealing it, acting arrogantly against your knowledge and your intellects.

(٤٣) وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ (43) And maintain the prayer (The obligatory prayer which Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought, and also maintain the prayer upon Muhammad and his pure progeny (آله الطاهرين)) ** وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ** and give the zakat (from your wealth when it becomes due, and from your bodies when it becomes necessary, and from your assistance when it is sought).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about sadaqat al-fitrah (صدقة الفطرة): Is it among what Allah the Almighty said: وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ (And maintain the prayer and give the zakat)? He said: "Yes." And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it from him (peace be upon him).

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the fitrah (الفطرة) which Allah made obligatory upon the believers.

And in a narration (رواية): Al-Zakat (الزكاة) was revealed, but the people did not yet possess wealth (الأموال); rather, it was the fitrah (الفطرة).

** وَارْكَعُوا and bow down مَعَ الرَّاكِعِينَ with those who bow down (Be humble with those who are humble before the greatness of Allah in submission (الانقياد) to the guardians of Allah (أولياء الله)). And it was said: meaning, in their congregations (جماعاتهم) for prayer.

I say: And this is one instance (فرد) of those instances (ذاك).

footnote (١) *Awwal* (*أول*) is a form of *af'al* (*أفعل*) that has no verb (*فعل*) [from which it is derived]. And it was said that its origin is *aw'al* (*أوءل*), so its *hamzah* (*همزة*) was replaced by a *waw* (*واواً*) for ease, without analogy (*بغير قياس*). Or *awwal* (*أول*) is from *āla, ya'ūlu* (*آل يؤل*), meaning: he returned (*رجع*), so its *hamzah* (*همزة*) was inverted to a *waw* (*واواً*) and then assimilated (*أدغمت*). From him (may his secret be sanctified) (*منه قدّس سرّه*).

** (٤٤) أَتَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبِرِّ (Do you bid people to piety) with charity (الصدقات) and fulfilling trusts (أداء الأمانات) وَتَنْسَوْنَ أَنْفُسَكُمْ (and forget yourselves) you abandon them وَأَنْتُمْ تَتْلُونَ الْكِتابَ (while you recite the Book) the Torah, which commands you to good deeds (الخيرات) and forbids you from evil deeds (المنكرات). أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ (Will you not apply reason?) the punishment (العقاب) that awaits you for commanding that which you yourselves do not practice, and for forbidding that in which you yourselves are immersed. It was revealed concerning the scholars of the Jews and their rebellious, hypocritical leaders, who seized (المحتجين) the wealth of the poor, and who lived off (المستأكلين) the rich, those who used to command good but abandon it, and forbid evil but commit it.

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the orators (الخطباء) and the storytellers (القصَّاص). This is the saying of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "And upon every pulpit (منبر) there is an eloquent orator (خطيب مصقع) among them who lies against Allah, against His Messenger, and against His Book."

I say: And this applies to everyone who describes justice (عدلاً) but deviates to something else.

In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever does not shed his inner doubts (هواجسه), nor free himself from the afflictions (آفات) of his soul and its desires (شهواتها), nor defeat Satan, nor enter into the protection (كنف) of Allah and the security of His infallibility (عصمته), is not fit for commanding the good (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the evil (النهي عن المنكر). Because if he is not of this description, everything he manifests will be a proof (حجة) against him, and people will not benefit from him." The Almighty Allah said: أَتَأْمُرُونَ النَّاسَ بِالْبِرِّ وَتَنْسَوْنَ أَنْفُسَكُمْ (Do you bid people to piety and forget yourselves). And it will be said to him: 'O traitor (يا خائن), do you demand of My creation that which you betrayed your own soul concerning, and let loose the reins upon it?'"

(٤٥) وَاسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ (45) And take recourse to patience regarding the unlawful (الحرام) in order to fulfill trusts (الأمانات), and regarding false leaderships (الرئاسات الباطلة) in order to acknowledge the truth (الاعتراف بالحق) and deserve forgiveness (الغفران), pleasure (الرضوان), and the bliss of Paradise (نعيم الجنان). I say: And regarding all other sins (سائر المعاصي), and for various types of acts of obedience (أصناف الطاعات), and kinds of calamities (أنواع المصيبات), and for the proximity of reaching Paradise (الجنان). In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: that patience (الصبر) is fasting (الصّيام). And in both of them (al-Kafi and al-Faqih), He (peace be upon him) said: If a severe calamity (النازلة الشديدة) befalls a man, let him fast, for Allah the Almighty says: ** اسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلاةِ ** take recourse to patience and prayer. meaning fasting (الصيام). And al-Ayyashi narrates the like of it from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him). ** وَالصَّلاةِ and prayer. the five obligatory prayers (الصلوات الخمس) and the prayer upon the Prophet and his pure Progeny (آله الطاهرين). I say: And every prayer (صلاة), whether obligatory (فريضة) or supererogatory (نافلة), due to what is narrated in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "What prevents one of you, if a worldly grief (غمّ من غموم الدنيا) enters upon him, from performing ablution (يتوضأ), then entering his mosque (مسجده), and performing two rak'ahs (ركعتين), and supplicating to Allah therein? Have you not heard Allah say: (وَاسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلاةِ) And take recourse to patience and prayer." In al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), he said: When something terrified Ali (peace be upon him), he would hasten to prayer (الصلوة), then recite this Ayah: ** وَاسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ وَالصَّلاةِ وَإِنَّها And take recourse to patience and prayer, and it is indeed Al-Qummi says: meaning the prayer (الصلاة). And it was said: meaning the seeking of help through both of them. And in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him): that this act, consisting of the five obligatory prayers (الصلوات الخمس) and the prayer upon Muhammad and his Progeny, along with submission (الانقياد) to their commands, and belief in their secret (سرهم) and public (علانيتهم) matters, and abandoning opposition to them with 'why' (لِمَ) and 'how' (كيف), is indeed great (لَكَبِيرَةٌ): meaning immense (عظيمة). I say: Meaning it is heavy (لثَقيلة) and difficult (شاقّة), like His Almighty saying: (كَبُرَ عَلَى الْمُشْرِكِينَ ما تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَيْهِ) What you call them to is hard on the polytheists. ** إِلَّا عَلَى الْخاشِعِينَ except for the humble. those who fear the punishment of Allah (عقاب الله) for opposing Him regarding His greatest obligations (أعظم فرائضه). I say: And that is because their souls (نفوسهم) are trained (مرتاضة) in such matters, anticipating in return for them that which makes their hardships (مشاقّها) light and causes them to find pleasure (يستلذّ) in their toils (متاعبها). Just as our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "The coolness of my eye (قرة عيني) has been placed in prayer (الصلاة)." And he used to say: "Give us comfort (روّحنا) or give us rest (أرحنا), O Bilal."

(1) Al-Ihtijan (الاحتجان) is the gathering and encompassing of a thing. From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(2) And in al-Majma', narrated from Anas ibn Malik, he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "On the night I was taken on the Night Journey (أسرى بي), I passed by people whose lips were being clipped with shears of fire (بمقاريض من نار). I asked: 'Who are these, O Gabriel?' He replied: 'These are orators (خطباء) from the people of the world who used to command people to righteousness (البر) but forgot themselves.'" From him (may his secret be sanctified).

(٤٦) الَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُمْ مُلاقُوا رَبِّهِمْ (those who are certain that they will encounter their Lord) In al-Tawhid, al-Ihtijaj, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): They are certain that they will be resurrected, and the الظنّ (conjecture) from them is يقين (certainty). And in both of them, He (peace be upon him) said: The encounter (اللقاء) is the resurrection (البعث), and the الظنّ here is اليقين (certainty). And in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him): They estimate and anticipate that they will encounter their Lord—the encounter (اللقاء) which is the greatest of His honor (كرامته) for His servants. ** وَأَنَّهُمْ إِلَيْهِ راجِعُونَ (and that they will return to Him.) To His honors (كراماته) and the bliss of His Gardens (جناته). He said: And He only said يظنون (they are certain/think) because they do not know how their lives will be concluded, for the outcome (العاقبة) is concealed from them. They do not know that with certainty (يقيناً) because they are not safe from changing and altering. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: The believer constantly remains fearful of an evil outcome (سوء العاقبة), and he does not become certain of reaching the pleasure (رضوان) of Allah until the time of the extraction of his soul (نزع روحه) and the appearance of the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) to him—the الحديث (narration). And its completion will come in Surat Ha Mim al-Sajdah, if Allah wills, during the interpretation of إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قالُوا رَبُّنَا اللهُ ثُمَّ اسْتَقامُوا** (Indeed, those who say, 'Our Lord is Allah,' and then remain steadfast), the Ayah.

(٤٧) ** يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتِيَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ (47) O Children of Israel, remember My blessing which I bestowed upon you, when I sent Moses and Aaron to your ancestors (أسلافكم) with prophethood, and they guided them to the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the successorship (وصية) of Ali عليه السلام, and the Imamate (إمامة) of his pure progeny (عترته الطيبين) عليهم السلام. And they took covenants from them regarding that, such that if they fulfilled them, they would be kings in Paradise (الجنان). وَأَنِّي فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ ** there (i.e., I did this to your ancestors). I preferred them in their religion by accepting the guardianship (ولاية) of Muhammad and his progeny عليهم السلام, and in their worldly life by the shading of the clouds (تظليل الغمام), the sending down of manna and quails (المنّ والسلوى), their being given sweet water from the stone, the splitting of the sea for them, their deliverance, and the drowning of their enemies. By this, I preferred them over the people (عالمي) of their time who opposed their way and deviated from their path (سبيلهم).

I say: The only reason Allah addressed the descendants (الأخلاف) concerning what He did to the ancestors (الأسلاف) or what they (the ancestors) did, whether good or evil, is because the Quran was revealed in the language of the Arabs, and they address one another in a similar manner. A man says to the Tamimi whose people raided a certain town and killed those within it: "You raided such-and-such a town, and you did such-and-such, and you killed its inhabitants," even if he was not with them. This is despite the fact that the descendants (الأخلاف) are pleased with what was done to the ancestors (الأسلاف) or what they (the ancestors) did. This is according to Tafsir al-Imam (1) عليه السلام, narrated from al-Sajjad عليه السلام. Its verification has already passed in the Third Introduction (المقدّمة الثالثة).

(48) وَاتَّقُوا يَوْماً And beware of a day the time of the soul's extraction (وقت النزع) لا تَجْزِي نَفْسٌ عَنْ نَفْسٍ شَيْئاً it will not ward off from it any punishment that it has deserved ** وَلا يُقْبَلُ ** (And it was read with the tāʾ (بالتاء)) nor will any intercession be accepted from it, by delaying death from it, ** وَلا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْها عَدْلٌ meaning, a ransom (فداء) in its place, such that [the ransom] is killed and [the soul] is left, nor will they be helped.**

I say: Meaning, in warding off death and punishment.

And in Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from him (peace be upon him): This is the Day of Death, for intercession (الشفاعة) and ransom (الفداء) will not avail against it. But as for the Resurrection (القيامة), verily, We and Our family (أهلنا) shall compensate for Our followers (شيعتنا) with every compensation, so that We shall be upon al-A'raf (الأعراف) between Paradise (الجنة) and the Fire (النار)—Muhammad, and Ali, and Fatimah, and al-Hasan, and al-Husayn, and the pure ones (الطيبون) from their progeny (peace be upon them). We shall see some of Our followers (شيعتنا) in those plains (العرصات). Whoever among them was deficient (مقصراً) and is in some of its hardships, We shall send upon them the best of Our followers, such as Salman, and al-Miqdad, and Abu Dharr, and Ammar, and their likes in the era that follows them, and then in every era until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). They will swoop down upon them like falcons (البزاة) and hawks (الصقور), and they will seize them just as falcons and hawks seize their prey, and they will swiftly convey them to Paradise (الجنة) with speed. And verily, We shall send upon others among Our lovers (محبينا) the best of Our followers, like doves (الحمام), and they will pick them up from the plains (العرصات) just as birds pick up grain, and they will transport them to the Gardens (الجنان) in Our presence. And one of Our followers (شيعتنا) who was deficient (مقصّري) in his deeds will be brought forth, after he has attained al-Wilayah (الولاية), al-Taqiyyah (التقية), and the rights of his brethren (إخوانه). And there will be made to stand opposite him between one hundred and more than that, up to one hundred thousand of the Nussab (النصّاب). It will be said to him: "These are your ransom (فداؤك) from the Fire (النار)." So these believers will enter Paradise (الجنة), and those Nussab will enter the Fire (النار). And that is what Allah, the Mighty and Glorious, said: رُبَما يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (meaning, in al-Wilayah (الولاية)) Perhaps those who disbelieved will wish if they had been Muslims (مسلمين) in the world, submitting to the Imamate (الإمامة), so that their opponents (مخالفوهم) would be made their ransom (فداؤهم) from the Fire (النار).

(٤٩) وَإِذْ نَجَّيْناكُمْ (49) (Remember) when We delivered you And remember when We delivered your ancestors. I say: This is a detailed explanation of what He summarized in His saying: اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتِيَ مِنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ (Remember My blessing upon you, and how I delivered you from Pharaoh's clan). They are those who drew near to him through kinship, his religion (دينه), and his doctrine (مذهبه). ** يَسُومُونَكُم (afflicting you) means they used to torture you. I say: Meaning, they burdened you with the torment (يُكَلِّفُونَكُمُ الْعَذَابَ), derived from (سَامَهُ الْأَمْرَ) meaning he burdened him with the matter (كَلَّفَهُ إِيَّاهُ). It is mostly used in relation to torment (الْعَذَاب) and evil (الشَّر). سُوءَ الْعَذابِ (the worst of the torment) means the severity of the torment. And among their severe torments was that Pharaoh used to burden them with the work of building and mud, and he feared that they would flee from the work, so he ordered them to be shackled. They used to transport that mud on ladders (السَّلَالِيم) to the rooftops, and sometimes one of them would fall and die or become crippled (زَمِنَ), and they would pay no heed to them. Until Allah revealed to Moses (peace be upon him): "Tell them not to begin any work except by invoking blessings (الصَّلَاة) upon Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيّبين), so that it may be lightened for them." So they used to do that, and it was lightened for them. يُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْناءَكُمْ (slaughtering your sons). That was because it was said to Pharaoh that a child would be born among the Children of Israel at whose hands his destruction and the demise of his kingdom would occur, so he ordered the slaughter of their sons. ** وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِساءَكُمْ (and sparing your women) means they kept them alive and took them as slave-girls (إِمَاءً). Then he said, the summary of which is: Perhaps their sons were saved from slaughter and grew up in a hidden place (مَحَلٍّ غَامِضٍ) due to their invocation of blessings upon Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيّبين). Likewise, their women were saved from being taken for bedding (الِافْتِرَاش) due to their invocation of blessings upon him and his Progeny (آله). ** وَفِي ذلِكُمْ (And in that) meaning, in that deliverance from them, ** بَلاءٌ (there was a trial) meaning a blessing (نِعْمَة), ** مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ (from your Lord, a great one) meaning great (كَبِير). The Almighty Allah said: ** يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ اذْكُرُوا (O Children of Israel! Remember) since the trial (الْبَلَاء) was averted from your ancestors and lightened through the invocation of blessings upon Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيّبين), do you not know that if you were to witness them and believe in them, the blessing upon you would be greater and superior, and the favor (فَضْل) of Allah with you would be more abundant (أَجْزَل)?

(٥٠) وَإِذْ فَرَقْنا بِكُمُ الْبَحْرَ (50) And when We parted the sea for you, And remember when We made the water of the sea into parts (فَرْقًا), where one part was cut off from the other, and We saved you there. وَأَغْرَقْنا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ and drowned Pharaoh’s clan, Pharaoh and his people. وَأَنْتُمْ تَنْظُرُونَ while you looked on. at them while they were drowning. That was because when Moses reached the sea, Allah revealed to him: "Say to the Children of Israel: Renew My Oneness (تَوْحِيدِي), and affirm in your hearts the mention of Muhammad, the master of My male and female servants, and renew upon yourselves the guardianship (وِلَايَة) of Ali, the brother of Muhammad, and his pure Progeny (آله الطيبين), and say: 'O Allah, make us pass over the surface of this water, for the water will turn into land for you.'" So Moses said that to them. They replied: "You bring upon us what we dislike! Did we flee from Pharaoh except out of fear of death? And now you plunge us into this deep water with these words! What do we know of what will happen to us because of this?"

Then Kālib ibn Yūḥannā said to Moses—while he was upon his mount, and that gulf was four farasikh (فَرَاسِخ) long—"O Prophet of Allah, did Allah command you with this, that we should say it and enter?" He said: "Yes." He asked: "And do you command me with it?" He said: "Yes." So he stopped and renewed upon himself the Oneness of Allah (تَوْحِيدِ الله), the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the guardianship (وِلَايَة) of Ali and the pure ones (الطّيبين) from their Progeny, as He had commanded him. Then he said: "O Allah, by their status (بِجَاهِهِمْ), make me pass over the surface of this water." Then he urged his horse forward and galloped upon the surface of the water, and behold, the water beneath him was like soft earth, until he reached the end of the gulf. Then he returned galloping.

Then he said to the Children of Israel: "O Children of Israel, obey Moses! This supplication (الدعاء) is nothing but the key to the gates of the Gardens (الجنان), the locks of the gates of the Fires (النيران), the bringer down of provisions (الأرزاق), and the attractor of the pleasure of the All-beneficent, the Guardian, the Creator (الرَّحْمَنِ الْمُهَيْمِنِ الْخَلَّاق) upon Allah's male and female servants." But they refused and said: "We will not walk except upon land." So Allah revealed to Moses: أَنِ اضْرِبْ بِعَصاكَ الْبَحْرَ (that strike the sea with your staff) and say: "O Allah, bless Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيبين) when You cleave it." So he did, and it was cleaved, and the land appeared until the end of the gulf.

Moses said: "Enter it." They said: "The ground is muddy (وَحِلَة); we fear that we will sink in it." Allah said: "O Moses, say: 'O Allah, by the right (بِحَقِّ) of Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيبين), dry it.'" So he said it, and Allah sent upon it the East Wind (رِيحَ الصَّبَا), and it dried. Moses said: "Enter it." They said: "O Prophet of Allah, we are twelve tribes, the sons of twelve fathers. If we enter, every group among us will seek to precede the other, and we do not feel safe from evil occurring between us. If there were a separate path for each group, we would be safe from what we fear."

So Allah commanded Moses to strike the sea according to their number—twelve strikes in twelve places alongside that—and to say: "O Allah, by the status (بِجَاهِ) of Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيبين), make the land clear for us and remove the water from us." So there became exactly twelve paths in it, and the bottom of the land dried by the East Wind (رِيحِ الصَّبَا). He said: "Enter them." They said: "Every group among us that enters one of these paths (سِكَك) will not know what is happening to the others." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "Strike every mountain (طَوْد) of water between these paths (السِّكَك)." So he struck and said: "O Allah, by the status (بِجَاهِ) of Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيبين), make wide windows (طِيقَانًا) in this water so that some of them may see others."

Then they entered them. When they reached the end of them, Pharaoh and his people arrived, and some of them entered. When the last of them had entered, and the first of them intended to exit, Allah, the Exalted, commanded the sea, and it closed upon them, so they drowned, while the companions of Moses looked on at them. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to the Children of Israel during the era of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Since Allah did all of this to your ancestors due to the honor (لِكَرَامَةِ) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and the supplication (دُعَاء) of Moses, which was a supplication seeking nearness through them (دُعَاءَ تَقَرُّبٍ بِهِمْ), what prevents you from understanding that you must believe in Muhammad and his Progeny صلى الله عليهم, seeing that you have witnessed Him now?"

(51)وَإِذْ واعَدْنا مُوسى أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً ثُمَّ اتَّخَذْتُمُ الْعِجْلَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَأَنْتُمْ ظالِمُونَ And when We appointed with Moses forty nights, then you took up the calf [for worship] in his absence, while you were wrongdoers.

(It was also read وعدنا [We promised] without the alif.)

Moses son of Imran (ابن عمران) was supposed to say to the Children of Israel: "When Allah grants you relief and destroys your enemies, I will bring you a Book from your Lord containing His commands, prohibitions, admonitions, lessons (عبره), and parables (أمثاله)." When Allah granted them relief, Allah Almighty commanded Him to come for the appointed time (للميعاد) and fast for thirty days. When it was the last of those days, he used the tooth-stick (استاك) before breaking the fast (قبل الفطر). So Allah Almighty revealed to him: "O Moses, did you not know that the odor (خلوق) of the mouth of the fasting person is sweeter to Me than the scent of musk? Fast ten more [days] and do not use the tooth-stick (تستك) when breaking the fast (عند الإفطار)." Moses did so. Thus, the promise of Allah Almighty was that He would give him the Book after forty nights, and He gave it to him.

Then the Samiri came and caused confusion (شبه) among the weak ones (مستضعفي) of the Children of Israel. He said: "Moses promised you that he would return to you after forty nights, and these are twenty nights and twenty days, making forty. Moses erred concerning his Lord. Your Lord has come to you, desiring to show you that He is capable of calling you to Himself directly, and that He did not send Moses out of any need He had for Him." Then he showed them the calf which he had made. They said to him: "How can the calf be our god (إلهنا)?" He said to them: "This calf will speak to you from your Lord, just as He spoke to Moses from the tree. The God (الإله) is in the calf just as He was in the tree." So they went astray by that and led others astray.

So Moses said: "O calf, was our Lord in you, as these people claim?" The calf spoke and said: "Our Lord is far exalted (عزّ) above the calf containing Him, or anything from the tree and the places encompassing Him. No, by Allah, O Moses! Rather, the Samiri set up a calf with its rear (مؤخّره) against a wall, and he dug a hole in the ground on the other side and seated some of his rebellious followers (مردته) inside. It was he who placed his mouth upon its rear (دبره) and spoke what was spoken when he said: 'This is your god and the god of Moses.' O Moses son of Imran, these people were not abandoned (خذل) by their worship of me and their taking me as a god except because of their negligence (تهاونهم) regarding the prayer upon Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيبين), their denial (جحودهم) of their guardianship (لموالاتهم), the prophethood of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the testament (وصية) of the Successor (الوصي)." Allah Almighty said: "If the worshippers of the calf were abandoned (خذل) due to their negligence regarding the prayer upon Muhammad and Ali, then what do you fear from the greater abandonment (الخذلان الأكبر) in your hostility (معاندتكم) towards them both, when you have witnessed their signs (آياتهما) and recognized their proofs (دلائلهما)?"

And al-Qummi [narrates]: When Moses went to the appointed time (الميقات) to bring them the Tablets of the Torah (ألواح التوراة), and he promised them return after thirty days, when the thirty days ended and Moses had not returned to them, Iblis came to them in the form of an old man (شيخ) and said to them: "Moses has fled and will never return to you. Gather your ornaments (حليّكم) for me so that I may make for you a god (إلهاً) whom you may worship."

On the day Allah drowned Pharaoh and his companions, the Samiri was at the vanguard (مقدّمة) of Moses's people, and he was one of the best of those whom Moses had chosen. The Samiri looked at Gabriel (peace be upon him) while he was upon a mount (مركوب) in the form of a mare (رَمَكة). Whenever she placed her hoof (حافرها) upon a spot of the earth, the spot of her hoof would move. So the Samiri began taking the dust from beneath the hoof of Gabriel's mare (رمكّة جبرئيل), tied it up in a pouch (صرّة), and preserved it. He used to boast about it to the Children of Israel. When Iblis made the calf for them, he said to the Samiri: "Bring the dust you have." So he brought it to him, and he threw it into the hollow (جوف) of the calf. It moved and lowed (خار), and fur (الوبر) and hair grew upon it. So the Children of Israel prostrated to the calf, and the number of those who prostrated to it was seventy thousand.

(٥٢) ثُمَّ عَفَوْنا عَنْكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ ذلِكَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (52) Then We pardoned you after that, so that you might be grateful.

meaning, We pardoned your forefathers for their worship of the calf, so that you, O those who exist among the Children of Israel in the era of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, might be grateful for that favor bestowed upon your ancestors and upon you after them. And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, pardoned them only because they supplicated to Allah through Muhammad and his Progeny صلى الله عليهم (may peace be upon them), and they renewed upon themselves the guardianship (الولاية) through Muhammad, Ali, and their pure Progeny. It was then that He had mercy upon them and pardoned them.

(٥٣) وَإِذْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ (53) And when We gave Moses the Book

And remember إِذْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى (when We gave Moses) the Torah, which you were obligated to believe in and submit to what it necessitates, وَالْفُرْقانَ (and the Criterion). We also gave Him (الفرقان) which distinguishes between the truth (الحق) and the falsehood (الباطل), and distinguishes between the truthful (المحق) and the false (المبطل). That is because when Allah honored them with the Book and belief in it, Allah revealed to Moses: "They have acknowledged this Book, but there remains الْفُرْقانَ (the Criterion), which distinguishes between the believers and the disbelievers. So renew the covenant with them concerning it, for I have sworn a true oath upon Myself that I will not accept faith or deed from anyone except through it."

Moses said: "What is it, O Lord?" Allah said: "O Moses, you shall take a covenant from them that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is the best of the Prophets and the Master of the Messengers, and that his brother and successor (وصيّه) Ali is the best of the successors (الوصيين), and that His chosen ones (أولياءه) whom He establishes are the masters of creation, and that his followers (شيعته) who submit to him and his successors (خلفائه) are the stars of the Highest Paradise (الفردوس الأعلى) and the kings of the Gardens of Eden (جنات عدن)."

He said: So Moses took that covenant from them. Some of them believed it truly, and some gave it with their tongues but not their hearts.

He said: So الْفُرْقانَ (the Criterion) is the manifest light (النور المبين) which shone upon the forehead of whoever believed in Muhammad and Ali and their progeny (عترتهما) and their followers (شيعتهما), and which was absent from the forehead of whoever gave that covenant with his tongue but not his heart. لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (that you may be guided)—meaning, so that you may know that what honors the servant before Allah is the belief in the guardianship (الولاية), just as your ancestors were honored by it.

And it is said that by الْكِتابَ (the Book) is meant the Torah, and by الْفُرْقانَ (the Criterion) is meant the miracles (المعجزات) that distinguish between the truthful (المحق) and the false (المبطل) in their claim, and by guidance (الاهتداء) is meant guidance through pondering the Book and reflecting upon the signs (الآيات).

(54) وَإِذْ قالَ مُوسى لِقَوْمِهِ : And remember, O Children of Israel, when Moses said to his people, the worshippers of the calf: O my people! You have indeed wronged yourselves—meaning, you have harmed them—by taking the calf [for worship]. So turn penitently to your Maker, the One Who (1) created you and fashioned you. It was said: Resolve upon repentance and returning to the One Who created you.

فَاقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ Let some of you kill others; let those who did not worship the calf kill those who did. That—meaning that killing—is better for you in the sight of your Maker, because it is your expiation. Thus, it is better than living in this world and then being eternal in the Fire. فَتابَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ He accepted your repentance before the killing was fully carried out upon your group and before it encompassed all of you. He granted you respite for repentance and preserved you for obedience. This was because when Allah nullified the matter of the calf through Moses, and He made it speak, informing [them] about the deception of al-Samiri (السامري), Moses (peace be upon him) commanded that those who had not worshipped the calf should kill those who had. Most of them disavowed [the act] and said, "We did not worship [it]." And some of them informed against others. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to Moses (peace be upon him): "File this golden calf with iron filings (بَرْداً) (2), then scatter it in the sea. Whoever drinks its water, his lips and nose will turn black if he was fair-skinned, and they will turn white if he was dark-skinned, and his sin will become manifest." So he did this, and the worshippers became manifest. Then Allah commanded the twelve thousand to go out against the rest, brandishing swords, and kill them. And His caller proclaimed: "May Allah curse anyone who protects them with a hand or a foot! And may Allah curse anyone who looks closely at the slain, lest he recognize him as a close friend or relative and bypass him for a stranger!" The slain submitted. The killers said: "We are suffering a greater calamity than them. We are killing our fathers, our sons, our brothers, and our relatives with our own hands, yet we did not worship [the calf]. He has thus equated us with them in calamity." So Allah revealed to Moses: "O Moses, I only tested them with that because they did not isolate themselves from them when they worshipped the calf, nor did they abandon them, nor did they show enmity towards them because of that. Tell them: Whoever supplicates to Allah through Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his pure, goodly Progeny (آله الطاهرين الطيبين), the killing of those deserving of death due to their sins will be made easy for him." So they said [the supplication], and it was made easy for them, and they found no pain in killing them. When the killing intensified among them—and they were six hundred thousand except for twelve thousand—Allah stopped those who had worshipped the calf by means of this same intercession (التوسل). So they interceded through them and sought forgiveness for their sins, and Allah removed the killing from them.

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [narrates]: When Moses returned from the appointed time (الميقات) and his people had worshipped the calf, he said to them, after expressing anger and reproach towards them: So turn penitently to your Maker, so kill your souls. They asked: "How shall we kill ourselves?" He said to them: "Let each one of you return to Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس) with a sword or a knife. When I ascend the pulpit (المنبر), you must be veiled (متلثّمين) so that none of you recognizes his companion, and kill one another." So those who had worshipped the calf gathered, and they were seventy thousand. When Moses (peace be upon him) prayed with them and ascended the pulpit, some of them began killing others until the revelation descended: "Say to them, O Moses, stop the killing, for Allah has turned to you clemently." Ten thousand of them had already been killed.

(1) Meaning: the One Who created you free (بريئاً) from disparity, distinguishing some of you from others with different forms and appearances. Its root is the structure (بري) indicating the purity (خلوص) of a thing from anything else. From him (may Allah sanctify his secret).

(2) Al-Burādah (البرادة) is al-suhālah (السحالة) (with undotted letters), which is the filings (فتات) of gold and silver. [From him].

(٥٥) وَإِذْ قُلْتُمْ (55) And when you said (Your ancestors said): ** يا مُوسى لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ (O Moses, we will not believe you until we see Allah manifestly. Thereupon the thunderbolt seized you) It seized them while you were looking on (and they were looking at the thunderbolt descending upon them). (٥٦) ثُمَّ بَعَثْناكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ مَوْتِكُمْ (56) Then We raised you up after your death) (due to the thunderbolt). I say: He restricted the resurrection (البعث) with death (الموت) because resurrection may occur after fainting or sleep. In this, there is clear evidence for the permissibility of the Return (الرجعة), which our companions affirm, narrating it from their Imams. The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) used this verse as an argument against Ibn al-Kawwa when he denied it, as narrated from him by al-Asbagh ibn Nubatah.

And al-Qummi said: This is proof of the Return (الرجعة) in the Ummah of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, for He said: "There was nothing among the Children of Israel except that there is something similar in my Ummah." This means it is proof of its occurrence. ** لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ** (that you may be grateful) Perhaps your ancestors would be grateful for the life in which they could repent, desist [from sin], and turn back to their Lord, since that death did not persist for them, which would have made their destination the Fire, wherein they would abide forever.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): They were the seventy men whom Moses chose and who went with him to the mountain. They said to him: "You have seen Allah, so show Him to us as you saw Him." He said to them: "I did not see Him." They replied to him: لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً (we will not believe you until we see Allah manifestly). The rest of the story will come, if Allah the Almighty wills, in Surat al-A‘raf.

In Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him): When Moses wished to take the covenant of the Criterion (الفرقان) from them, distinguishing between the truthful and the false regarding Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his prophethood, and regarding Ali and the Imams (peace be upon them) and their Imamate, they said: "We will not believe you that this is the command of your Lord until we see Allah manifestly, informing us of this." Thereupon the thunderbolt seized them openly. Moses (peace be upon him) said to the remaining ones who were not struck: "Will you accept and acknowledge, or else you will join these [who perished]?" They said: "We do not know what befell them. If it only struck them because they rejected you concerning the matter of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali (peace be upon him), then ask Allah, your Lord, by Muhammad and his Progeny (آله) to revive them so that we may ask them why what befell them befell them." So Moses (peace be upon him) supplicated to Allah, and He revived them. They questioned them, and they replied: "What befell us, befell us because our fathers believed in the Imamate of Ali after believing in the prophethood of Muhammad. Indeed, after this death of ours, we saw the kingdoms of our Lord—His heavens, His veils (حجبه), His Throne (عرشه), His Footstool (كرسيه), His Gardens (جنانه), and His Fires (نيرانه). We did not see anyone whose command was more effective in all the kingdoms, or whose authority was greater, than Muhammad, Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them). And when we died by this thunderbolt, we went to the Fires (النيران), and Muhammad and Ali called out to them: 'Hold back your torment from these, for they shall be revived by the request of a supplicant who asked our Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, by us and by our pure Progeny (آلنا الطيبين).' Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to the people of Muhammad's era: 'If, by supplicating through Muhammad and his pure Progeny (آله الطيبين), the darkness of your ancestors—who were struck by the thunderbolt due to their injustice—was dispelled, then it is incumbent upon you not to expose yourselves to the like of that by which they perished, until Allah revived them.'"
