(٨٦) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا بِالْآخِرَةِ (They are the ones who have bought the life of this world for the Hereafter) and were content with the world and its debris instead of the bliss of the Gardens deserved through obedience to Allah. ** فَلا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذابُ وَلا هُمْ يُنْصَرُونَ (so the punishment shall not be lightened for them, nor will they be helped.) Nor will anyone help them to ward off the punishment from them. He (peace be upon him) said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "When the verse was revealed concerning the Jews—that is, those who broke the covenant of Allah, denied the Messengers of Allah, and killed the friends (أولياء) of Allah—shall I not inform you of those among the Jews of this nation (الأمة) who resemble them?" They said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "A people from my nation (أمّتي) who claim to belong to my religion (ملّتي), who kill the most virtuous of my progeny (ذرّيتي) and the purest of my root (أرومتي) (1), and who alter my Law (شريعتي) and my practice (سنّتي), and who kill my two sons, al-Hasan and al-Husayn, just as the ancestors of the Jews killed Zachariah and John. Behold, Allah curses them just as He cursed them, and before the Day of Resurrection, He will send against the remnants of their descendants a guiding, guided one (هادياً مهدياً) from the progeny of the oppressed al-Husayn, who will drive them with the swords of His friends (أوليائه) into the Fire of Hell."

And al-Qummi [narrates]: That it was revealed concerning Abu Dharr (may Allah have mercy on him) and what Uthman ibn Affan did to him. The reason for this was that when Uthman ordered the exile of Abu Dharr (may Allah have mercy on him) to al-Rabadhah, Abu Dharr entered upon him while he was ill, leaning on his staff. Before Uthman were one hundred thousand dirhams that had come to him from some regions, and his companions were around him, looking at it and hoping that he would divide it among them. Abu Dharr said to Uthman: "What is this wealth?" He replied: "One hundred thousand dirhams were brought to us from some of the revenues (الأعمال). I intend to add a like amount to it, and then I will decide what to do with it." Abu Dharr said: "O Uthman, which is greater: one hundred thousand dirhams or four dinars?" Uthman said: "Rather, one hundred thousand dirhams." Abu Dharr said: "Do you not remember when you and I entered upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم one evening and found Him gloomy and sad? We greeted Him, but He did not return our greeting. When morning came, we came to Him and found Him laughing and joyful. I said to Him: 'May my father and mother be sacrificed for You, we entered upon You last night and saw You gloomy and sad, and we returned to You today and saw You laughing and joyful.' He said: 'Yes, four dinars from the spoils (فيء) of the Muslims had remained with me, which I had not divided, and I feared that death might overtake me while they were still with me. I have divided them today, and so I am relieved.'" Uthman looked at Ka'b al-Ahbar and said to him: "O Abu Ishaq, what do you say about a man who has paid the obligatory zakat on his wealth? Is anything further due upon him regarding it?" He replied: "No, even if he were to take a brick of gold and a brick of silver, nothing would be due upon him." Abu Dharr raised his staff and struck Ka'b's head with it, saying: "O son of the polytheist Jewish woman! What concern do you have with looking into the rulings (أحكام) of the Muslims? The saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, is truer than your saying, where He said: ** الَّذِينَ يَكْنِزُونَ الذَّهَبَ وَالْفِضَّةَ وَلا يُنْفِقُونَها فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ فَبَشِّرْهُمْ بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ (Those who hoard gold and silver, and do not spend it in the way of Allah, inform them of a painful punishment) up to His saying: ** فَذُوقُوا ما كُنْتُمْ تَكْنِزُونَ ** (‘Taste what you used to hoard!’)" Uthman said: "O Abu Dharr, you are an old man who has become senile and lost his mind. Were it not for your companionship with the Messenger of Allah, I would have killed you." Abu Dharr said: "You lie, O Uthman! Woe upon you! My beloved, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, informed me, saying: 'They will not tempt you, O Abu Dharr, nor will they kill you.' As for my mind, enough of it remains to remind me of a hadith I heard from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He spoke concerning you and your people." Uthman asked: "And what did you hear from the Messenger of Allah concerning me and my people?" Abu Dharr said: "I heard Him say—and it is His saying صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: 'When the clan of Abu al-As reaches thirty men, they will make the wealth of Allah a circulating possession (دُولاً) (1), the Book of Allah a source of corruption (دَغَلاً) (2), the servants of Allah slaves (خَوَلاً) (3), the righteous ones enemies, and the wicked ones a party.'" Uthman said: "O company of Muhammad's companions, did any of you hear this hadith from the Messenger of Allah?" They said: "No, we did not hear this from the Messenger of Allah." Uthman then said: "Call Ali عليه السلام." So the Commander of the Faithful came to him. Uthman said to him: "O Abu al-Hasan, listen to what this lying old man is saying." The Commander of the Faithful said: "Stop, O Uthman! Do not say 'lying,' for I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله say: 'The green sky has not shaded, nor has the dusty earth carried, anyone with a tongue more truthful than Abu Dharr.'" The companions of the Messenger of Allah said: "Ali spoke the truth. We heard this saying from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله." At that, Abu Dharr wept and said: "Woe upon you! All of you have stretched your necks toward this wealth, thinking that I lie about the Messenger of Allah!"

Then he looked at them and said: "Who among you is the best?" They said: "You tell us, for you are the best of us." He said: "Yes, I left my beloved, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in this cloak (جبّة), and it is still upon me, while you have introduced many innovations (أحداثاً), and Allah will question you about that, but He will not question me." Uthman said: "O Abu Dharr, I adjure you by the right of the Messenger of Allah that you inform me of what I am about to ask you." Abu Dharr said: "By Allah, even if you had not asked me by the right of the Messenger of Allah, I would still inform you." Uthman asked: "Which land do you prefer to be in?" He replied: "Mecca, the sanctuary (حرم) of Allah and the sanctuary of His Messenger. I will worship Allah there until death overtakes me." Uthman said: "No, and no honor (كرامة) for you." Abu Dharr said: "Then Medina, the sanctuary of the Messenger of Allah." Uthman said: "No, and no honor for you." Abu Dharr fell silent. Uthman then asked: "Which land do you hate most to be in?" He replied: "Al-Rabadhah, where I was when I was not upon the religion of Islam." Uthman said: "Go forth to it." Abu Dharr said: "You asked me, and I spoke truthfully to you. Now I ask you, so speak truthfully to me." Uthman said: "Yes." Abu Dharr said: "Inform me, if you were to send me among those companions whom you sent against the polytheists, and they captured me and said: 'We will not ransom him except for a third of what you own,' what would you do?" Uthman said: "I would ransom you." Abu Dharr said: "And if they said: 'We will not ransom him except for half of what you own'?" Uthman said: "I would ransom you." Abu Dharr said: "And if they said: 'We will not ransom him except for all that you own'?" Uthman said: "I would ransom you." Abu Dharr then exclaimed: "Allah is the Greatest (الله أكبر)! My beloved, the Messenger of Allah, said to me one day: 'O Abu Dharr, how will you be when it is said to you: Which land do you prefer to be in? And you say: Mecca, the sanctuary of Allah and the sanctuary of His Messenger. I will worship Allah there until death overtakes me. And it is said: No, and no honor for you. And you say: Then Medina, the sanctuary of the Messenger of Allah. And it is said: No, and no honor for you. Then it is said to you: Which land do you hate most to be in? And you say: Al-Rabadhah, where I was when I was not upon the religion of Islam. And it is said to you: Go forth to it.' I said: 'Will this truly happen, O Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم?' He said: 'Yes, by Him in Whose hand is my soul, it will surely happen.' I said: 'O Messenger of Allah, should I not then place my sword upon my shoulder and strike them, advancing step by step?' He said: 'No. Listen and be silent, even if it is to an Abyssinian slave. And Allah, the Almighty, has revealed a verse concerning you and Uthman, your adversary.' I asked: 'And what is it, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'The saying of Allah, the Almighty,' and He recited this verse."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, in a hadith concerning the aspects (وجوه) of disbelief (الكفر) in the Quran, he said: "The fourth type of disbelief (الكفر) is abandoning what Allah has commanded, and that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic," and he recited this verse. He then said: "So He declared them disbelievers (فكفّرهم) due to their abandonment of what Allah commanded, and He attributed them to faith (الإيمان) but did not accept it from them, nor did it benefit them in His sight."

(1) Al-Arum (الأروم), with fathah on the hamzah, is the root of the tree and the generation (القرن) (ص). Al-Urumah (الأرومة), with dammah, is the root (الأصل) (ق).

(1) Al-Dawlah (الدولة), with dammah, regarding wealth: it is said that the spoils (الفيء) became a circulating possession (دولة) among them, which they pass around, being for one person once and for another person another time. The plural forms are dawalat (دولات) and duwal (دول) (صحاح).

(2) Al-Daghal (الدغل), with harakah (vowel movement), means corruption (الفساد), like al-dakhal (الدخل). It is said: He introduced corruption (أدغل) into the matter when he introduced into it that which opposes and corrupts it (ص).

(3) Al-Khawal (الخول), with harakah (vowel movement), refers to the blessings (النعم), slaves (العبيد), female slaves (الإماء), and others that Allah has given you. And the khawal of a man are his attendants (حشمه) (ص).

(٨٧) ** وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ (87) And We certainly gave Moses the Book (It is) the Torah, which contained Our rulings, and the mention of the virtue of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his Household (أهل بيته), the Imamate of Ali عليه السلام and his successors after him, the nobility of the circumstances of those who submitted (المسلمين) to him, and the wretchedness of the circumstances of the hypocrites (المنافقين) against him.

** وَقَفَّيْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ بِالرُّسُلِ** and followed him up with [other] apostles. We made an apostle follow another apostle.

وَآتَيْنا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ الْبَيِّناتِ and gave Jesus son of Mary manifest proofs We gave him clear signs (الآيات الواضحات), such as reviving the dead, healing the born-blind (الأكمه) and the leper (الأبرص), and informing them of what they ate and what they stored in their houses.

وَأَيَّدْناهُ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ and strengthened him with the Holy Spirit. And it was read with a lightened (مخففاً) letter (القدس). And He is Gabriel (جبرائيل). That was when He raised him from the skylight (روزنة) of his house to the heaven, and cast his likeness (شبهه) upon the one who sought to kill him, so he was killed instead of him. And it was said that He is the Messiah (المسيح).

أقول : وفي رواية أخرى : أنه القى شبهة على رجل من خواصه إثر حياته على حياة نفسه كما يأتي. I say: And in another narration (رواية أخرى): He cast his likeness (شبهة) upon a man from among his close companions (خواصه) immediately after his life, concerning his own life, as will come (later).

والقمّيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام : القى شبهة على رجل من خواصه ليقتل فيكون معه في درجته. كما يأتي في سورة آل عمران إن شاء الله. And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: He cast his likeness (شبهة) upon a man from among his close companions (خواصه) so that he would be killed and thus be with him in his rank (درجته). As will come in Surat Āl ‘Imrān, if Allah wills.

أَفَكُلَّما جاءَكُمْ Is it that whenever there came to you O Jews,

** رَسُولٌ بِما لا تَهْوى أَنْفُسُكُمُ ** an apostle with what your souls did not desire, (He came) taking your covenants (عهودكم) and pledges (مواثيقكم) with what you did not love, such as following the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and rendering obedience (الطاعة) to the friends of Allah (أولياء الله),

** اسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ you acted arrogantly? (You acted arrogantly) against faith (الإيمان) and following (the truth).

** فَفَرِيقاً كَذَّبْتُمْ** A group of them you belied, such as Moses and Jesus,

** وَفَرِيقاً تَقْتُلُونَ and a group you slay. Your predecessors killed Zechariah (زكريا) and John (يحيى), and you sought to kill Muhammad and Ali عليهما السلام, but Allah frustrated your endeavor and turned your plot back upon your own necks (في نحوركم). Thus, the meaning of ** تَقْتُلُونَ ** (you slay) is 'you killed' (قتلتم), just as you say to someone you are reproaching, "Woe to you, why do you lie?" (لم تكذب)—and you do not mean what he will do later, but rather you mean, "Why did you do it, when you are accustomed to it (موطّن)?"

ثمّ قال عليه السلام : ولقد رامت الفجرة الكفرة ليلة العقبة قتل رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم على العقبة ورام من بقي من مردة المنافقين بالمدينة قتل عليّ بن أبي طالب صلوات الله عليه فما قدروا على مغالبة ربهم حملهم على ذلك حسدهم لرسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم في علي لما فخّم أمره وعظم شأنه ثمّ ذكر القصة بطولها وسيأتي ذكر ملخصها من طريق آخر من المجمع في سورة التوبة إن شاء الله. Then he عليه السلام said: Indeed, the wicked disbelievers (الفجرة الكفرة) sought to kill the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the night of al-Aqabah at al-Aqabah. And those who remained of the rebellious hypocrites (مردة المنافقين) in Medina sought to kill Ali ibn Abi Talib صلوات الله عليه, but they were unable to overcome their Lord. What drove them to this was their envy (حسدهم) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم concerning Ali, because He magnified his affair and elevated his status (عظم شأنه). Then he mentioned the story in its entirety. A summary of it will come from another path in al-Majma' in Surat al-Tawbah, if Allah wills.

والعيّاشيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام قال : ضرب الله مثلاً لأمة محمّد صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم فقال لهم : فان ** جاءَكُمْ محمد صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ** بِما لا تَهْوى أَنْفُسُكُمُ بموالاة علي ** اسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ فَفَرِيقاً من آل محمّد صلى الله عليه وآله كَذَّبْتُمْ وَفَرِيقاً تَقْتُلُونَ ، قال : فذلك تفسيرها في الباطن. And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, who said: Allah set forth a parable (مثلاً) for the Ummah of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He said to them: If ** جاءَكُمْ (there came to you) Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ** بِما لا تَهْوى أَنْفُسُكُمُ (with what your souls did not desire)—namely, the loyalty (موالاة) to Ali— اسْتَكْبَرْتُمْ فَفَرِيقاً (you acted arrogantly? A group) from the Household (آل) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم كَذَّبْتُمْ وَفَرِيقاً تَقْتُلُونَ (you belied, and a group you slay). He said: That is its interpretation in the esoteric sense (في الباطن).

(٨٨) وَقالُوا قُلُوبُنا غُلْفٌ (88) And they said, 'Our hearts are uncircumcised.' meaning, containers for goodness (الخير) and knowledge (العلوم) which have encompassed them and contained them. Yet, despite that, we do not recognize for you, O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, any merit (فضلاً) mentioned in any of the Books of Allah, nor upon the tongue of any of the Prophets of Allah. So Allah refuted them with His saying: ** بَلْ لَعَنَهُمُ اللهُ بِكُفْرِهِمْ Nay, Allah has cursed them for their faithlessness. He removed them far from goodness (الخير). فَقَلِيلاً ما يُؤْمِنُونَ So little is that which they believe. meaning, they believe with a little faith (إيماناً قليلاً) in some of what Allah has revealed and disbelieve in some. He (peace be upon him) said: And if it is read غُلُفٌ (1), then they said, 'Our hearts' are in a covering (غطاء), so we do not understand your speech (كلامك) or your discourse (حديثك), just as the Almighty Allah said: وَقالُوا قُلُوبُنا فِي أَكِنَّةٍ مِمَّا تَدْعُونا إِلَيْهِ وَفِي آذانِنا وَقْرٌ وَمِنْ بَيْنِنا وَبَيْنِكَ حِجابٌ And they say, 'Our hearts are in covers, impervious to what you invite us to, and there is a deafness in our ears, and there is a barrier between us and you.' He said: And both readings are true (حقّ), and they have said this and that together.

(٨٩) وَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ كِتابٌ مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ (89) And when there came to them a Book from Allah,

meaning the Jews, a Book from Allah, (which is) the Quran, confirming what is with them of the Torah, in which it was clarified that Muhammad, the unlettered (الأمي), is from the progeny of Isma'il, supported by the best of Allah's creation after him, Ali, the friend (ولي) of Allah. and they had been praying for victory before Muhammad appeared with the message, asking Allah for conquest (الفتح) and triumph against the faithless among their enemies. And Allah used to grant them victory and help them. But when there came to them what they recognized of the description (نعت) and characteristic (صفته) of Muhammad, they disbelieved in it, denying His prophethood out of envy (حسداً) for Him and transgression (بغياً) against Him. So may Allah’s curse be on the faithless.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said regarding this verse: The Jews used to find in their books that the place of migration (مهاجر) of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) was between (عَيْر) and (أُحُد). So they went out seeking the location. They passed by a mountain called (جُبَيْل) and a mountain called (حِدَاد). They said: "(حِدَاد) and (أُحُد) are the same." So they dispersed there. Some of them settled in (تيماء) (1), some in (فدك), and some in (خيبر). Those in (تيماء) longed for some of their brethren, so an Arab (أعرابي) from (قيس) passed by them, and they hired him. He said: "I will pass by you between (عَيْر) (2) and (أُحُد)." They said to him: "When you pass by them, inform us of them." When he reached the middle of the land of Medina, he said to them: "That is (عَيْر) and this is (أُحُد)." So they dismounted from his camels and said: "We have found what we sought, and we have no need for your camels, so go wherever you wish." They wrote to their brethren in (فدك) and (خيبر): "We have found the location, so come to us." They wrote back to them: "We have settled in our homes and acquired wealth, and how close we are to you! When that time comes, how quickly we will come to you!" So they acquired wealth in the land of Medina. When their wealth increased, the news reached Tubba' (3), so he raided them. They fortified themselves against him, and he besieged them. They used to show compassion (يرقون) to the weak among Tubba's companions, throwing dates and barley to them at night. When this reached Tubba', he felt pity for them and granted them safety. They came down to him, and he said to them: "I have found your land pleasant, and I see myself staying among you." They said to him: "That is not for you. It is the place of migration (مهاجر) of a Prophet, and it is not for anyone until that happens." He said to them: "I will leave behind among you members of my family who, when that happens, will assist Him and support Him." So he left behind two tribes: (الأوس) (4) and (الخزرج) (5). When they multiplied there, they began to seize the wealth of the Jews. The Jews used to say to them: "If Allah sends Muhammad among you, we will surely drive you out of our homes and our wealth." But when Allah sent Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), the Ansar believed in Him, and the Jews disbelieved in Him. This is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: and they had been praying for victory against the faithless, but when there came to them what they recognized, they disbelieved in it. So may Allah’s curse be on the faithless.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he was asked about this verse, and he said: There were people in the period between Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) and Jesus. They used to threaten the people of idols with the Prophet, saying: "The Prophet will surely emerge, and He will surely break your idols and do such-and-such to you." But when the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) emerged, they disbelieved in Him.

And al-Qummi (narrated): The Jews used to say to the Arabs before the coming of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "O Arabs, this is the time for a Prophet who will emerge from Mecca, and His place of migration (مهاجرته) will be Medina. He is the last of the Prophets and the best of them. In His eyes is redness, and between His shoulders is the Seal of Prophethood (خاتم النبوّة). He wears the (الشّملة) (1) and contents Himself with a piece of bread (الكِسرة) (2) and a few dates. He rides a bare donkey. He is the smiling fighter (الضحوك القتال). He places His sword on His shoulder, not caring whom He meets. His authority will reach the end of the hoof and the paw (منقطع الخف والحافر). We shall surely kill you by Him, O company of Arabs, like the killing of 'Ad." But when Allah sent His Prophet with this description, they envied Him and disbelieved in Him, just as Allah said: and they had been praying for victory before, the verse.

In Tafsir al-Imam, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: Allah Almighty informed His Messenger (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) about the faith of the Jews in Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) before His appearance, and their praying for victory (استفتاحهم) against their enemies by mentioning Him and sending blessings upon Him and His family. He said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, had commanded the Jews during the days of Moses and after him that if a matter afflicted them or a calamity (داهية) struck them, they should supplicate to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, through Muhammad and His pure family (آله الطيّبين) and seek help (يستنصروا) through them. They used to do this, so much so that the Jews of Medina, many years before the appearance of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), would do this, and they would be spared affliction (البلاء), distress (الدهماء), and calamity (الداهية). Ten years before the appearance of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), the tribes of (أسد) and (غطفان) and a group of polytheists (المشركين) were hostile to the Jews and intended to harm them. The Jews used to repel their evils and afflictions by asking their Lord through Muhammad and His pure family. At one point, (أسد) and (غطفان) targeted them with three thousand horsemen, heading toward one of the Jewish villages around Medina. The Jews met them, being three hundred horsemen, and they supplicated to Allah through Muhammad and His family, so they defeated them and cut them down. (أسد) and (غطفان) said to one another: "Let us seek help against them from the rest of the tribes." So they sought help from the tribes and increased their numbers until they gathered about thirty thousand men. They targeted these three hundred men in their village and forced them into their houses, cutting off the running water that used to enter their villages. They did not grant them safety and said: "No, except that we kill you, take you captive, and plunder you." The Jews said to one another: "What should we do?" Their leaders and men of sound judgment said to them: "Did Moses not command your predecessors and those after them to seek help through Muhammad and His pure family? Did He not command you to supplicate (الابتهال) to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, through them during hardships?" They said: "Yes." They said: "Then do it." So they said: "O Allah, by the status (بجاه) of Muhammad and His pure family, grant us water, for the oppressors have cut off the water from us until our young men have weakened and our children have died or are near death (تماوت) (1), and we are on the verge of destruction." So Allah sent them a heavy, continuous, pouring rain (وابلاً هطلاً صباً متتابعاً) that filled their cisterns (حياضهم), wells, rivers, containers (أوعيتهم), and vessels (ظروفهم). They said: "This is one of the two good outcomes (إحدى الحسنيين)." Then they looked down from their rooftops upon the surrounding armies, and behold, the rain had harmed them greatly, ruining their belongings, weapons, and wealth. Because of this, some of them departed, for the rain came to them out of season, during the intense heat (حمازة) (2) of summer when there is no rain. The remaining armies said: "Suppose you have been given water, from where will you eat? Even if these have departed from you, we will not depart until we conquer you, your families, and your people, and satisfy our rage against you." The Jews said: "The One Who gave us water through our supplication by Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) is capable of feeding us, and the One Who turned away those who departed is capable of turning away the rest." Then they supplicated to Allah through Muhammad and His family to feed them. A great caravan of food arrived, about two thousand camels, mules, and donkeys, laden with wheat and flour. They were unaware of the armies. They reached them while they were asleep and did not perceive them because Allah Almighty made their sleep heavy, until they entered the village and were not prevented. They unloaded their goods there and sold them to the Jews, then departed and went far away, leaving the armies asleep, with no one among them blinking an eye. When they had gone far, they awoke and declared war against the Jews. Some of them began saying to others: "Quickly! (الْوَحَا الْوَحَا) (3)! For these people are severely hungry and will soon submit to us." The Jews said to them: "Far be it! Rather, our Lord has fed us while you were asleep. Such and such food came to us. If we had wanted to kill you while you slept, it would have been easy for us, but we disliked transgression (البغي) against you. So depart from us, or else we will supplicate against you through Muhammad and His family and seek help through them to disgrace you, just as He has fed us and given us water." But they refused, persisting only in transgression. So the Jews supplicated to Allah through Muhammad and His family and sought help through them. Then the three hundred men went out against the thirty thousand, killing some of them, taking captives, crushing them (طحطحوهم) (1), and securing themselves through their captives. No harm would come to them from the armies because of their fear for those they held captive in the hands of the Jews. But when Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) appeared, they envied Him because He was from the Arabs and they denied Him. Then the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) said: "This was Allah Almighty's victory for the Jews over the polytheists by their mention of Muhammad and His family. O community of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), remember Muhammad and His family during your calamities (نوائبكم) and hardships (شدائدكم), and Allah will surely help your angels through Him against the devils who target you. For every one of you has an angel on his right writing his good deeds, and an angel on his left writing his bad deeds, and with him are two devils from Iblis who try to mislead him. If they whisper in his heart, he remembers Allah Almighty and says: لا حول ولا قوة إلّا بالله العلي العظيم (There is no power and no strength except with Allah, the Most High, the Most Great) and sends blessings upon Muhammad and His family, the two devils retreat (خنس) (2) and hide." The narrative.

(1) (تيماء) is the name of a place. p. (2) (عير) is a mountain in Medina. p. (3) Tubba' (تبع), like (سكر), is one of the (التبابعة) (kings) of (حمير). He was named Tubba' due to the large number of his followers (أتباعه). It is also said they were called (تبابعة) because the latter followed the former in kingship. They were seventy Tubba's who ruled all the earth and those in it, both Arabs and non-Arabs. The Middle Tubba' was a believer, etc. Majma'. (4) (الأوس) is the father of a tribe from Yemen, and he is (أوس بن قيلة), the brother of (الخزرج). The Ansar are from them, and (قيلة) is their mother. (5) (الخزرج) is a tribe from the Ansar.

(1) (الشملة) with fathah is a cloak (كساء) smaller than a thick blanket (القطيفة) that one wraps oneself in. Q. (2) (الكسرة) with kasrah is a piece of a broken thing, and its plural is (كسر), like (قطعة) and (قطع). From this is the hadith: He had a piece of bread (كسرة) that he dipped in milk. p.

(1) (تماوت ولداننا) means they died or were on the verge of death. (2) (حمازة القيظ) with the undotted ha (الحاء المهملة) and zay (الزاي) means its intensity. From him (may his secret be sanctified). (3) (الوحا الوحا) with elongation (المد) and shortening (القصر) means speed, the quickness. It is in the accusative case (منصوب) due to an implied verb. (واستوحيته) means I sought help from him.

(٩٠) بِئْسَمَا اشْتَرَوْا بِهِ أَنْفُسَهُمْ (Evil is that for which they sold their souls) Allah condemned the Jews and censured their action in their disbelief in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. It means they sold their souls for the gifts (الهدايا) and superfluous things (الفضول) that reached them. Allah had commanded them to purchase their souls from Him through their obedience to Him, so that He might grant them their souls and the perpetual enjoyment of them in the bliss of the Hereafter. But they did not purchase them; rather, they sold them for what they spent in enmity toward the Messenger of Allah, so that their glory (عزهم) in this world and their leadership (رئاستهم) over the ignorant might remain for them, and so that they might attain forbidden things (المحرمات). They acquired superfluous things (الفضولات) from the lowlifes (السّفلة), turned them away from the path of guidance (سبيل الرشاد), and made them stand upon the way of misguidance (طريق الضلالات). ** أَنْ يَكْفُرُوا بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ (that they should disbelieve in what Allah has sent down) —meaning, what He sent down to Moses concerning the confirmation of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم— ** بَغْياً (out of envy) due to their transgression (بغيهم) and envy (حسدهم) ** أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ اللهُ (that Allah should send down) (and it was read lightly (مخففاً): ** مِنْ فَضْلِهِ عَلى مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ (of His grace upon whomever of His servants He wishes) —meaning, the sending down of the Quran upon Muhammad, in which He made manifest His prophethood, and by which He displayed His sign (آيته), His miracle (معجزته), and the merits (فضائل) of His Household (أهل بيته).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, He said: ** بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ (in what Allah has sent down) refers to [what was sent down] concerning Ali, out of envy (بغياً).

فَباؤُ بِغَضَبٍ عَلى غَضَبٍ (So they earned wrath upon wrath) Meaning, they returned, and upon them was wrath from Allah following [previous] wrath. The first wrath was when they denied Jesus son of Mary, so He made them despicable apes (قِرَدَةً خاسئين) and cursed them upon the tongue of Jesus. The second wrath was when they denied Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, so He empowered the swords of His companions over them until He humbled them thereby. Thus, they either entered Islam willingly (طائعين) or paid the tribute (الجزية) submissively (صاغرين). The Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said: I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله say: "Whoever is asked about knowledge and conceals it when its manifestation is obligatory and the dissimulation (التقيّة) is removed from him, he will come on the Day of Resurrection bridled with a bridle of fire." ** وَلِلْكافِرِينَ عَذابٌ مُهِينٌ (And there is a humiliating punishment for the faithless) Meaning, [the punishment] is manifested for them to inform about the reason (السبب). This is what has been said, and it has many parallels (نظائر) in the Quran.

(٩١) ** وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمْ آمِنُوا بِما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ : upon Muhammad صلى الله على محمّد وآله وسلم, which is the Quran, ** قالُوا نُؤْمِنُ بِما أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْنا (٩١) And when they are told, 'Believe in what Allah has sent down,' they say, 'We believe in what was sent down to us,' which is the Torah (التوراة), ** وَيَكْفُرُونَ بِما وَراءَهُ what is other than it, they do not believe in it, while it is the truth (الْحَقُ) because it is the abrogator (الناسخ) of the abrogated (المنسوخ) which preceded it, ** مُصَدِّقاً لِما مَعَهُمْ and they disbelieve in what is besides it, though it confirms what is with them. which is the Torah (التوراة). ** قُلْ فَلِمَ تَقْتُلُونَ Say: Why were you killing? Why were your ancestors (أسلافكم) killing? ** أَنْبِياءَ اللهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ Say, 'Why then did you kill the prophets of Allah earlier, if you are believers?' in the Torah (التوراة), for indeed in it is the prohibition of killing the prophets, and in it is the command to believe in Muhammad and the Quran. Thus, you have not yet believed in the Torah (التوراة).

And al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This was only revealed concerning a group of Jews who were during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and they did not kill the prophets with their own hands, nor were they in their time. Rather, their predecessors (اوائلهم) who were before them killed them. So, Allah made them among them and attributed the action of their predecessors (اوائلهم) to them because they followed them and befriended them (تولوهم).

I say: The verification (تحقيق) of that has already passed in the Third Introduction (المقدّمة الثالثة).

(٩٢) ** وَلَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ مُوسى بِالْبَيِّناتِ ثُمَّ اتَّخَذْتُمُ الْعِجْلَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ (92) Certainly Moses brought you manifest proofs, then you took up the calf in his absence, after his departure to the mountain, and you opposed his successor whom he appointed and left over you, and he is Harun (هارون). وَأَنْتُمْ ظالِمُونَ ** while you were wrongdoers. because of what you did.

(٩٣) وَإِذْ أَخَذْنَا مِيثَاقَكُمْ (2:93) And when We took a pledge from you

And remember when We took the covenant (مِيثاق) from your predecessors. وَرَفَعْنَا فَوْقَكُمُ الطُّورَ (2:93) and raised the Mount above you

We did that to them when they refused to accept what Moses brought them concerning the religion of Allah, His rulings, and the obligation of venerating Muhammad and his Household (peace be upon them). خُذُوا (2:93) [saying,] ‘Take

We said to them: 'Take what We have given you (مَا آتَيْنَاكُمْ)—what We have granted you of the obligatory duties (الفرائض)—بِقُوَّةٍ (2:93) with might,’

which We have already granted you and by which We have enabled you, and We have removed your excuses regarding its establishment within you. وَاسْمَعُوا (2:93) and listen.’

to what is said to you and what you are commanded. قَالُوا سَمِعْنَا (2:93) They said, ‘We hear,

Your saying, وَعَصَيْنَا (2:93) and we disobey.’

Your command. That is, they disobeyed later, and they also concealed disobedience immediately. They said: "We hear with our ears, and we disobey with our hearts." As for the outward appearance, they all rendered obedience, humbled and abased.

وَأُشْرِبُوا فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْعِجْلَ (2:93) And they were made to drink the calf into their hearts

They were commanded to drink the calf, the filings (سحالته) (1) of which had been scattered (ذرّيت) in the water they were commanded to drink, so that the one who worshipped it could be distinguished from the one who did not, as passed in the interpretation of the Almighty's saying: فَاقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ (2:54) Then kill your souls

He (peace be upon him) said: They were exposed to drinking the calf which they had worshipped, until what they drank of it reached their hearts, بِكُفْرِهِمْ (2:93) because of their disbelief.

They were commanded to do that on account of their disbelief.

I say: There is no contradiction between this interpretation and what is well-known in the interpretation of the verse, which is that its meaning is that the love of it permeated them and its image became firmly established in their hearts due to their excessive passion for it, just as dye permeates a garment and drink permeates the depths of the body. This is permissible because the two matters can be combined, and the outward drinking can be a cause for the inward love. And فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ (into their hearts) is a clarification of the place of the drinking, like His saying: إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا (4:10) Indeed they are only eating fire into their bellies.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that he said: When Moses supplicated to his Lord in secret (ناجى), Allah the Almighty revealed to Him: "O Moses, your people have been tested (فتنت)." He asked: "With what, O Lord?" He said: "With the Samiri." He asked: "And what is the Samiri?" He said: "He fashioned a calf for them from their ornaments." He said: "O Lord, their ornaments cannot possibly be fashioned into a gazelle, or a statue, or a calf, so how were they tested?" He said: "He fashioned a calf for them, and it lowed (فخار)." He asked: "O Lord, and who made it low?" He said: "I did." Thereupon Moses said: إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا فِتْنَتُكَ تُضِلُّ بِهَا مَنْ تَشَاءُ وَتَهْدِي مَنْ تَشَاءُ (7:155) It is nothing but Your test. You lead astray whomever You wish by it, and You guide whomever You wish.

He said: When Moses reached his people and saw them worshipping the calf, he cast the Tablets from his hand, and they were broken.

Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: That should have been when Allah the Almighty informed him. He said: So Moses proceeded and filed the calf from its nose to the tip of its tail, then burned it with fire and scattered it in the sea (اليمّ). He said: One of them would fall into the water, even if he had no need for it, and would expose himself to that ash and drink it. This is the saying of Allah: وَأُشْرِبُوا فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الْعِجْلَ بِكُفْرِهِمْ (2:93) And they were made to drink the calf into their hearts because of their disbelief.

I say: According to this narration, it seems that their love for the calf became the cause of their drinking it, contrary to what preceded.

قُلْ بِئْسَ مَا يَأْمُرُكُمْ بِهِ إِيمَانُكُمْ (2:93) Say, ‘Evil is that which your faith enjoins you,

Your faith in Moses and the Torah—that you should disbelieve in Me, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (2:93) if you are faithful.’

as you claim to be in Moses and the Torah. But refuge is sought in Allah (معاذ الله), your faith in Moses and the Torah does not enjoin you to disbelieve in Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(1) With unpointed letters (بالمهملتين): the fragments (الفتات). ذرّيت means scattered (فرقت). From him, may Allah sanctify his secret (قدس الله سره).

(٩٤) قُلْ: O Muhammad, to these Jews who say that Paradise is exclusively ours, without you and without your Household (أهل بيتك), and that we are afflicted and tested by you, and we are the sincere friends (أولياء) of Allah and the best servants (عباد) of Allah, and our supplication is answered, and nothing of our request is rejected from us— إِنْ كانَتْ لَكُمُ الدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ—meaning Paradise and its bliss— عِنْدَ اللهِ خالِصَةً مِنْ دُونِ النَّاسِ—meaning Muhammad, his Household (أهل بيته), and the believers of his community— فَتَمَنَّوُا الْمَوْتَ—for the liar among you and among your opponents—for Muhammad, Ali, and their progeny say that they are the friends (أولياء) of Allah, to the exclusion of the people who oppose them in their religion, and that their supplication is answered. If you, O assembly of Jews, claim that, then say: "O Allah, cause the liar among us and among our opponents to die, so that the truthful ones may be relieved of us, and so that Your argument may increase in clarity after it has already become clear," ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ**—that you are the rightful ones whose supplication against your opponents is answered. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said after presenting this to them: "No one among you would say it except that his saliva would choke him, and he would die in his place." And the Jews knew that they were the liars and that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and his companions were the truthful ones, so they did not dare to supplicate with it.

قُلْ إِنْ كانَتْ لَكُمُ الدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ عِنْدَ اللهِ خالِصَةً مِنْ دُونِ النَّاسِ فَتَمَنَّوُا الْمَوْتَ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (٩٤) (94) Say: If the abode of the Hereafter is exclusively yours with Allah, to the exclusion of other people, then long for death, if you are truthful.

I say: The well-known interpretation is that the intent behind their longing for death is that they should long for it for themselves, due to their claim that they are the friends (أولياء) and beloved ones of Allah, and their saying: ** لَنْ يَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا مَنْ كانَ هُوداً (None shall enter Paradise except one who is a Jew), for it is written in the Torah that the friends (أولياء) of Allah long for death and do not fear it. The reason for this is that whoever is certain that he is among the people of Paradise longs for it and loves to be delivered to it from the abode full of impurities. Just as the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "By what means did you come to love meeting your Lord?" He said: "When I saw that He had chosen for me the religion of His angels, His messengers, and His prophets, I knew that the One Who honored me with this would not forget me, so I loved meeting Him."

(٩٥) وَلَنْ يَتَمَنَّوْهُ أَبَداً بِما قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ (95) But they will never wish for it because of what their hands have sent ahead. (This refers to) the causes (موجبات) of the Fire, such as disbelief in Muhammad and his Household (آله) and the Quran, and the distortion (تحريف) of the Torah.

** وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالظَّالِمِينَ**
And Allah knows well the wrongdoers.
(This is) a threat to them and an alert that they are wrongdoers (*ظالمون*) in claiming what is not theirs and denying it from those to whom it belongs. Thus it has been said.

(٩٦) وَلَتَجِدَنَّهُمْ أَحْرَصَ النَّاسِ عَلى حَياةٍ (And you will surely find them the greediest of all people for life) [This is] due to their despair concerning the bliss of the Hereafter, because of their immersion in their disbelief, knowing that they have no share with it in any of the goodnesses of Paradise. وَمِنَ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا (and [greedier] than the polytheists.) And [greedier] than those who associate partners (أَشْرَكُوا). This means the Magians (المجوس) who see bliss only in this world and do not hope for any good in the Hereafter. It is said that their specific mention (افرادهم بالذكر) is for emphasis (للمبالغة), for their greed is intense since they have known nothing but the immediate life (الحياة العاجلة). Or, [it is] for increasing the reproach (التوبيخ) and censure (التقريع), for when their greed exceeded the greed of the deniers, while they themselves affirm the recompense (الجزاء), this indicates their knowledge that they are heading toward the Fire. يَوَدُّ أَحَدُهُمْ لَوْ يُعَمَّرُ أَلْفَ سَنَةٍ وَما هُوَ ** (Each of them is eager to live a thousand years, but his being granted life) meaning, the granting of life for a thousand years ** بِمُزَحْزِحِهِ (will not deliver him) [meaning] removing him (مباعده) ** مِنَ الْعَذابِ أَنْ يُعَمَّرَ (from the punishment.) The phrase ** أَنْ يُعَمَّرَ (that he be granted life) is substituted for the pronoun [in ** وَما هُوَ ], and the granting of life (التعمير) was repeated so that it would not be mistakenly thought to refer back to the wish (التمني). وَاللهُ بَصِيرٌ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ** (And Allah is watchful of what they do.) Thus, He will recompense them according to it, and He will deal justly with them, and He will not wrong them.

(٩٧) ** قُلْ مَنْ كانَ عَدُوًّا لِجِبْرِيلَ (Say, 'Whoever is an enemy of Gabriel—) The Almighty's saying: ** قُلْ مَنْ كانَ عَدُوًّا لِجِبْرِيلَ (Say, 'Whoever is an enemy of Gabriel—'). And it was read (وقرئ) with the opening (فتح) of the جيم and the breaking (كسر) of the راء without a hamzah (همز), and with the opening of both (جيم and راء) with a hamzah (مهموزاً) followed by a yā' (ياء) after the hamzah, and without a yā'. then indeed He (فَإِنَّهُ) — meaning Gabriel (جبرائيل) ** نَزَّلَهُ (has brought it down) — meaning He brought down the Quran (نزّل القرآن) ** عَلى قَلْبِكَ (to your heart) — O Muhammad (يا محمّد). And this is like His saying, glory be to Him: ** نَزَلَ بِهِ الرُّوحُ الْأَمِينُ عَلى قَلْبِكَ بِإِذْنِ اللهِ (the Trustworthy Spirit has brought it down upon your heart by Allah’s leave) — by His command (بأمره) ** مُصَدِّقاً لِما بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ (confirming what was before it) — of the Books of Allah (من كتب الله) ** وَهُدىً ** (and as a guidance) — from error (من الضلالة) ** وَبُشْرى لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (and good news for the faithful) — with the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the wilāyah (ولاية) of Ali صلوات الله عليه and the Imams (الأئمة) after him عليهم السلام, that they are truly the friends of Allah (أولياء الله حقاً). He said: They are the followers (شيعة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali عليه السلام and those who followed them from their successors (أخلافهم) and their descendants (ذراريهم).

(٩٨) مَنْ كانَ عَدُوًّا لِلَّهِ (Whoever is an enemy of Allah) By opposing Him stubbornly due to His favor upon His close servants. ** وَمَلائِكَتِهِ ** (and His angels) Who are sent to support them. وَرُسُلِهِ (and His apostles) Who inform about their virtue and call for following them. ** وَجِبْرِيلَ وَمِيكالَ (and Gabriel and Michael) Specifically. And it was recited without a hamzah (همزة) or a yā’ (ياء), and with a hamzah (همزة) but without a yā’ (ياء): ** فَإِنَّ اللهَ عَدُوٌّ لِلْكافِرِينَ (then Allah is the enemy of the faithless.) [The faithless] being those who deny them.

This is the saying of one of the Nussāb (النصاب) [those who harbor enmity towards the Ahl al-Bayt] when the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said concerning Ali عليه السلام: "Gabriel is on his right, Michael is on his left, Israfil is behind him, and the Angel of Death is before him, and Allah, the Exalted, is looking upon him from above His Throne with satisfaction, supporting him." Some of the Nussāb (النصاب) said: "I disavow Allah, Gabriel, Michael, and the angels whose state with Ali is what Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has stated." So Allah said: ** مَنْ كانَ عَدُوًّا** (Whoever is an enemy) to these [angels] out of fanaticism against Ali, then Allah will do to them what an enemy does to an enemy.

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [narrates]: It was revealed concerning the Jews who said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "If the angel who comes to you were Michael, we would have believed in you, for he is the Angel of Mercy and is our friend. But Gabriel is the Angel of Punishment, and he is our enemy."

In Tafsir al-Imam (تفسير الإمام) عليه السلام: Allah condemned the Jews for their hatred of Gabriel, who used to execute Allah's decree concerning them in matters they disliked, such as preventing Daniel عليه السلام from killing Nebuchadnezzar (بخت نصّر) without Nebuchadnezzar having committed any sin, until Allah's decree concerning the Jews reached its term and what was in His prior knowledge befell them. And He also condemned them, and He condemned the Nawāsib (النّواصب) [those hostile to the Ahl al-Bayt] for their hatred of Gabriel and Michael and the angels of Allah who descended to support Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام against the disbelievers, until he humbled them with his sharp sword.

In it [Tafsir al-Imam] and in al-Ihtijaj (الاحتجاج), Abu Muhammad said: Jabir ibn ʿAbd Allāh (جابر بن عبد الله) said: When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم arrived in Medina, they brought to him ʿAbd Allāh ibn Sūriyā (عبد الله بن صوريا), a one-eyed Jewish youth whom the Jews claimed was the most knowledgeable Jew concerning the Book of Allah and the sciences of His prophets. He asked him about certain matters, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم answered him in a way that left him no room to deny anything. Until he said: "One trait remains; if you tell me about it, I will believe in you and follow you: Which angel brings you what you say from Allah?" He said: "Gabriel." Ibn Sūriyā said: "That is our enemy among the angels. He descends with killing, hardship, and war. Our messenger is Michael, who comes with joy and prosperity. If Michael were the one who came to you, we would have believed in you. Michael used to strengthen our kingdom, while Gabriel used to destroy our kingdom, so he is our enemy." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then said to him: "Woe unto you! Are you ignorant of the command of Allah? What fault does Gabriel have if he obeys Allah in what He wills for you? Have you considered fathers and mothers? If they administer unpleasant medicine to their children for their benefit, should their children take them as enemies because of that? No! But you are ignorant of Allah and heedless of His judgment. I bear witness that Gabriel and Michael act by the command of Allah and are obedient to Him. And verily, no one is an enemy to one of them except he who is an enemy to the other, and whoever claims that he loves one of them and hates the other has lied. Likewise, Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and Ali are brothers. So whoever loves them both is among the friends of Allah, and whoever hates them both is among the enemies of Allah. And whoever hates one of them and claims that he loves the other has lied, and they are both innocent of him, and Allah, the Exalted, His angels, and the best of His creation are innocent of him."

The Imam عليه السلام said: Salmān al-Fārisī رضي الله عنه said to him: "What was the beginning of his enmity towards you?" He said: "Yes, O Salmān, he has been hostile to us many times. The most severe of this upon us was that Allah revealed to His prophets: 'Verily, Jerusalem (بيت المقدس) will be destroyed at the hands of a man called Nebuchadnezzar (بخت نصّر).' And in his time, He informed us of the news by which it would be destroyed. And Allah brings about matter after matter, erasing what He wills and confirming what He wills (يَمْحُو مَا يَشَاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ). When that news reached us—the news of the destruction of Jerusalem—our predecessors sent a man from the strongest and most virtuous of the Children of Israel, who was counted among their prophets, called Daniel (دانيال), to seek Nebuchadnezzar in order to kill him. He carried with him a large amount of wealth to spend on this endeavor. When he set out in search of him, he met him in Babylon (بابل) as a weak, poor youth, possessing neither strength nor protection. Our companion seized him to kill him, but Gabriel defended him and said to our companion: 'If your Lord is the One Who commanded your destruction, then He will not give you power over him. And if this is not the case, then why are you killing him?' So our companion believed him and left him, and returned to us and informed us of that. Then Nebuchadnezzar grew strong, became king, invaded us, and destroyed Jerusalem. For this reason, we take him as an enemy, and Michael is an enemy to Gabriel."

Salmān said: "O Ibn Sūriyā, by this intellect, which is guided away from its proper path, you have gone astray! Have you considered how your predecessors sent someone to kill Nebuchadnezzar, even though Allah, the Exalted, had informed in His Books, upon the tongues of His messengers, that he would rule and destroy Jerusalem? Did they intend by this to deny the prophets of Allah in their news and accuse them of lying in their reports? Or did they believe them in the news from Allah, yet still intend to overpower Allah? Were these people and the one they dispatched anything but disbelievers in Allah? And what enmity is permissible to hold against Gabriel when he was preventing him from overpowering Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and forbidding the denial of the news of Allah, the Exalted?" Ibn Sūriyā said: "Indeed, Allah had informed of that upon the tongues of His prophets, but He erases what He wills and confirms (يَمْحُو مَا يَشَاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ)."

Salmān said: "In that case, you should not trust anything in the Torah concerning past or future news, for Allah erases what He wills and confirms. And in that case, perhaps Allah had already dismissed Moses (موسى) and Aaron (هارون) from prophethood, and their claims were invalidated, because Allah erases what He wills and confirms. And perhaps everything they informed you would happen will not happen, and what they informed you would not happen will happen. Likewise, what they informed you about what was, perhaps it never was, and what they informed you never was, perhaps it was. And perhaps He erases what He promised of reward, and perhaps He erases what He threatened of punishment, for He erases what He wills and confirms. And verily, you have made the meaning of 'Allah erases what He wills and confirms' [a justification for disbelief], and because of that, you are disbelievers in Allah, deniers of His news concerning the unseen, and stripped away from the religion of Allah." Then Salmān said: "I bear witness that whoever is an enemy to Gabriel is an enemy to Michael, and they are both enemies to whoever is hostile to them, and Salmān is a friend to whoever is peaceful towards them." Thereupon, Allah, the Exalted, revealed [the verse] in agreement with Salmān's saying: ** قُلْ مَنْ كانَ عَدُوًّا لِجِبْرِيلَ** (Say: ‘Whoever is an enemy of Gabriel’) and the rest of the Ayah.

(٩٩) وَلَقَدْ أَنْزَلْنا إِلَيْكَ آياتٍ بَيِّناتٍ (99) We have certainly sent down to you manifest signs.

[They are] indicators of your truthfulness in your prophethood and the Imamate of Ali (peace be upon him), your brother, clarifying the disbelief (كفر) of whoever doubts you both.

وَما يَكْفُرُ بِها إِلَّا الْفاسِقُونَ and none disbelieve in them except the transgressors.

[Meaning] those who deviate from the religion of Allah and His obedience, from the Jews and the liars among the النّواصب who call themselves Muslims.

The Almighty's saying: (١٠٠) ** أَوَكُلَّما عاهَدُوا واثقوا وعاقدوا عَهْداً ليكونن لمحمّد صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم طائعين ولعلي عليه السلام بعده مؤتمرين وإلى أمره صائرين نَبَذَهُ نبذ العهد ** فَرِيقٌ مِنْهُمْ وخالفه ** بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ** بل أكثر هؤلاء اليهود والنواصب لا يُؤْمِنُونَ في مستقبل أعمارهم لا يرعوون ولا يتوبون مع مشاهدتهم الآيات ومعاينتهم الدلالات. (Is it that whenever they made a covenant, a group of them cast it aside? Nay, most of them do not have faith.)

footnote (1) What is meant by informing about what was and what was not (*أريد بألأخبار عما كان وما لم يكن*) is informing about what is hidden from the senses (*عما غاب عن الحسن*) other than through the path of sensation (*بغير طريق الإحساس*) regarding its existence and non-existence (*بكونه وعدم كونه*). From him (may Allah sanctify his secret) (*منه قدس الله سره*).

** وَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ رَسُولٌ (101) And when there came to them an apostle ** مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ مُصَدِّقٌ لِما مَعَهُمْ (1) from Allah, confirming that which was with them, He said: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: And when it came to them—it came to the Jews and those who follow them from among the النواصب—a Book from Allah, the Quran, containing the description of Muhammad and Ali, and the obligation of their ولاية (guardianship/authority) and the ولاية (guardianship/authority) of their allies, and enmity toward their enemies.

I say: The Apostle (الرسول) was only interpreted as the Book (الكتاب) because the former necessitates the latter, but not the reverse, and so that it may conform to what preceded in its parallel instance, and to conform to the rejected [Book]: نَبَذَ فَرِيقٌ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ كِتابَ اللهِ a faction of those who were given the Book cast away the Book of Allah the Torah and the rest of the books of His prophets ** وَراءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ behind their backs, they abandoned acting upon what was in it, out of envy toward Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم for his prophethood, and toward Ali عليه السلام for his successorship (وصيته), and they denied the virtues of both of them which they had ascertained. ** كَأَنَّهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ as if they did not know. They acted the deed of one who does not know, despite their knowledge that it is the truth.

(١٠٢) وَاتَّبَعُوا ما تَتْلُوا الشَّياطِينُ (102) And they followed what the devils recited concerning Solomon’s kingdom. [This refers to] what the disbelievers among the devils read aloud concerning sorcery (السحر) and conjurations (النّيرنجات). ** عَلى (2) ** مُلْكِ سُلَيْمانَ (concerning Solomon’s kingdom): [This occurred] during his era. They claimed that Solomon was a disbeliever, a sorcerer skilled in it, and that by means of that sorcery (السحر) and those conjurations (النيرنجات), he attained what he attained, ruled what he ruled, and was capable of what he was capable of. They said: "We, too, by means of it, can manifest wonders so that people will submit to us, and we will be independent of submitting to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali عليه السلام."

In al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said: When Solomon perished, Iblis placed the sorcery (السحر), then wrote it in a book, folded it, and wrote on its back: "This is what Asif ibn Barkhiya placed for King Solomon ibn David from the treasures of the knowledge of secrets. Whoever desires such and such and such, let him do such and such." Then he buried it beneath the throne. Then he consulted them about it, and they read it. The disbelievers said: "Solomon only defeated us by means of this!" But the believers said: "Rather, he was a servant of Allah and His Prophet." So Allah said in His Book: ** وَاتَّبَعُوا ما تَتْلُوا الشَّياطِينُ عَلى مُلْكِ سُلَيْمانَ (And they followed what the devils recited concerning Solomon’s kingdom), meaning the sorcery (السحر).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام in a tradition: The questioner asked: "From where did the devils learn sorcery (السحر)?" He replied: "From the same way physicians learned medicine (الطبّ): some of it through experimentation (تجربه) and some of it through treatment (علاج)." ** وَما كَفَرَ سُلَيْمانُ (But Solomon did not disbelieve): Nor did he use sorcery (السحر), as these disbelievers claimed. ** وَلكِنَّ الشَّياطِينَ كَفَرُوا (but the devils disbelieved). It was read with a lightened nūn and the following word in the nominative case (يُعَلِّمُونَ النَّاسَ السِّحْرَ): meaning they disbelieved by teaching people the sorcery (السحر) which they attributed to Solomon ibn David. ** وَما أُنْزِلَ عَلَى الْمَلَكَيْنِ (and what was sent down to the two angels): And by teaching them what was sent down to the two angels بِبابِلَ هارُوتَ وَمارُوتَ** (at Babylon, Hārūt and Mārūt): [These are] the names of the two angels.

Al-Sadiq عليه السلام said: After Noah, sorcerers (السحرة) and deceivers (المموّهون) had become numerous. So Allah the Almighty sent two angels to the Prophet of that time with a mention of what the sorcerers used for sorcery (السحر), and a mention of what would nullify their sorcery and repel their cunning. The Prophet received this from the two angels and conveyed it to the servants of Allah by the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. He commanded them to recognize sorcery (السحر) by means of it and to nullify it, and forbade them from using it to practice sorcery upon people. This is like indicating what poison is, and what repels the danger (غائلة) of the poison. Then it is said to the learner of that: "This is the poison. If you see someone poisoned, repel its danger (غائلته) with such and such, and beware of killing anyone with the poison." He said: That Prophet commanded the two angels to appear to the people in the form of two humans, teach them what Allah had taught them of that, and admonish them. وَما يُعَلِّمانِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ ** (and they would not teach anyone): [Meaning] that sorcery (السحر) and its nullification. ** حَتَّى يَقُولا (without telling him): To the learner. ** إِنَّما نَحْنُ فِتْنَةٌ (We are only a test): A trial (امتحان) for the servants so that they may obey Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in what they learn from this, nullify the cunning of sorcery (السحر) by means of it, and not practice sorcery. ** فَلا تَكْفُرْ (so do not disbelieve): By using this sorcery (السحر), seeking to cause harm by it, and calling people to believe that by means of it you give life and cause death, and perform what none but Allah is capable of, for that is disbelief (كفر). ** فَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ (Yet they learn): Meaning the seekers of sorcery (السحر). ** مِنْهُما (from them): Meaning ** ما تَتْلُوا الشَّياطِينُ عَلى مُلْكِ سُلَيْمانَ (what the devils recited concerning Solomon’s kingdom) of conjurations (النّيرنجات), ** وَما أُنْزِلَ عَلَى الْمَلَكَيْنِ بِبابِلَ هارُوتَ وَمارُوتَ (and what was sent down to the two angels, Hārūt and Mārūt, at Babylon). They learn from these two types [of knowledge] ** ما يُفَرِّقُونَ بِهِ بَيْنَ الْمَرْءِ وَزَوْجِهِ (that by which they cause separation between man and wife). This is [the case of] those who learn to harm people. They learn separation by various kinds of tricks (الحيل), amulets (التمائم), and illusion (الإيهام), [claiming] that something has been buried in such-and-such a place or that such-and-such a thing has been done, in order to corrupt the heart of the wife against the husband and the heart of the husband against the wife, leading to separation between them. وَما هُمْ بِضارِّينَ بِهِ مِنْ أَحَدٍ (But they cannot harm anyone with it): Meaning, those who learn that [sorcery] are not able to harm anyone with it ** إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ اللهِ (except by Allah’s leave): Meaning by Allah's allowing it (تخلية) and His knowledge, for if He willed, He would prevent them by compulsion (الجبر) and subjugation (القهر). ** وَيَتَعَلَّمُونَ ما يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلا يَنْفَعُهُمْ (They learn what harms them, and does not benefit them): Because when they learn that sorcery (السِّحر) to practice sorcery with it and cause harm, they have learned what harms them in their religion and does not benefit them in it; rather, they strip themselves of the religion of Allah by means of it. ** وَلَقَدْ عَلِمُوا (and they certainly know): These learners know ** لَمَنِ اشْتَراهُ (whoever buys it): [Meaning] with his religion, which he strips himself of by learning it, ** ما لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلاقٍ (will have no share in the Hereafter): Meaning no portion (نصيب) in the reward of Paradise (الجنة).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: [This is] because they believe there is no Hereafter (آخرة), so they believe that if it does not exist, they will have no share (خلاق) in the abode of the Hereafter after this world. And even if there is a Hereafter after this world, they, despite their disbelief (كفرهم) in it, will have no share (خلاق) in it.

وَلَبِئْسَ ما شَرَوْا بِهِ أَنْفُسَهُمْ (And surely evil is that for which they sold their souls): They mortgaged them (رهنوها) for punishment (العذاب). ** لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ (had they known): That they had sold the Hereafter (الآخرة) and abandoned their portion (نصيبهم) of Paradise (الجنة), because those who learn this sorcery (السحر) are those who believe that there is no Messenger (رسول), no God (إله), no Resurrection (بعث), and no Gathering (نشور).

footnote (٢) *على* (concerning) is in the meaning of *في* (in), as in the Almighty's saying: ** وَدَخَلَ الْمَدِينَةَ عَلى حِينِ غَفْلَةٍ مِنْ أَهْلِها** (And he entered the city when its inhabitants were in a state of inattention).

(١٠٣) وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَمَثُوبَةٌ (1) مِنْ عِنْدِ اللهِ خَيْرٌ لَوْ كانُوا يَعْلَمُونَ (103) And had they believed and been Godwary, a reward (1) from Allah would have been better, had they known.

The narrator said: I said to Abu Muhammad (peace be upon him): "Indeed, some people among us claim that Harut and Marut were two angels whom the angels chose when the disobedience of the Children of Adam became widespread, and Allah sent them down to the world with a third one, and that they were tempted by al-Zuhrah (Venus) and sought to commit fornication with her, and they drank wine, and killed the forbidden soul (النفس المحرمة), and that Allah the Almighty is punishing them in Babylon, and that the sorcerers learn sorcery from them, and that Allah transformed that woman into this star, which is al-Zuhrah." The Imam (peace be upon him) said: "Allah forbid that! Indeed, the angels of Allah are infallible (معصومون), protected from disbelief (الكفر) and vile deeds by the favors (ألطاف) of Allah the Almighty. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said concerning them:

** لا يَعْصُونَ اللهَ ما أَمَرَهُمْ وَيَفْعَلُونَ ما يُؤْمَرُونَ** (6) They do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they do whatever they are commanded.

And He said: وَلَهُ مَنْ فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَنْ عِنْدَهُ. meaning the angels لا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبادَتِهِ وَلا يَسْتَحْسِرُونَ يُسَبِّحُونَ اللَّيْلَ وَالنَّهارَ لا يَفْتُرُونَ , And He also said concerning the angels: بَلْ عِبادٌ مُكْرَمُونَ لا يَسْبِقُونَهُ بِالْقَوْلِ وَهُمْ بِأَمْرِهِ يَعْمَلُونَ up to His saying مُشْفِقُونَ. (19) To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth, and those who are near Him do not disdain to worship Him, nor do they grow weary. (20) They glorify [Him] night and day, and they do not flag. (26) Rather, they are honored servants. (27) They do not precede Him in speech, and they act by His command. (28) He knows what is ahead of them and what is behind them, and they do not intercede except for him whom He approves, and they are fearful.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [A narration] similar to what is in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him) from His saying: وَاتَّبَعُوا ما تَتْلُوا الشَّياطِينُ (and they followed what the devils recited) up to this point, with an addition that we pointed out in its proper place.

And from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about what people narrate concerning the matter of al-Zuhrah (Venus), that she was a woman by whom Harut and Marut were tempted, and what they narrate concerning the matter of Suhayl (Canopus), that he was a tax collector (عشّاراً) in Yemen. He (peace be upon him) said: "They lied in their saying that they were two stars, and that they were two beasts (دابتين) from the beasts of the sea, and people erred and thought they were the two stars. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, would not transform His enemies into shining lights and then keep them for as long as the heavens and the earth remain. Indeed, the transformed creatures (المسوخ) did not remain for more than three days until they died, and nothing reproduced from them. There is no transformed creature (مسخ) on the face of the earth today. As for those upon whom the name of transformation (المسوخة) has fallen, such as apes, pigs, bears, and their likes, they are only similar to the forms into which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, transformed a people upon whom Allah was wrathful and Whom He cursed due to their denial of the Oneness of Allah (توحيد الله) and their rejection of His Messengers. As for Harut and Marut, they were two angels who taught people sorcery so that they might guard themselves with it against the sorcery of the sorcerers and nullify their cunning (كيدهم). They did not teach anyone anything of that except that they said to him: إِنَّما نَحْنُ فِتْنَةٌ فَلا تَكْفُرْ (We are only a trial, so do not disbelieve). So, a people disbelieved by using what they were commanded to guard against, and they began to separate a man from his wife with what they learned."

I say: As for what they (peace be upon them) denied concerning the matter of Harut and Marut, the transformation of Zuhrah, and their story famous among the people, narrations regarding their authenticity have also been reported from them (peace be upon them). The way to reconcile and harmonize [these narrations] is to interpret the narrations of authenticity as being among the cryptic symbols (مرموزات) and allusions (اشارات) of the ancients. And when they (the Imams) saw that those who narrated them interpreted them according to their literal meaning (ظاهرها), they denied them. There is no harm in presenting them and solving their meaning, for this is their proper place.

Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That ‘Ata’ asked him about Harut and Marut. He (peace be upon him) said: "The angels used to descend from the heaven to the earth every day and night, preserving the deeds of the average people (أوساط أهل الأرض) among the Children of Adam and the Jinn, recording them and ascending with them to the heaven. He said: The inhabitants of the heaven complained loudly about the deeds of the average people of the earth regarding sins, lying against Allah the Almighty, and their audacity towards Him. They declared Allah far removed from what they say and describe. A group of angels said: 'O our Lord, are You not angered by what Your creation does on Your earth, and the lies they attribute to You, and the falsehood they utter, and the sins they commit which You forbade them, while they are in Your grasp and under Your power?' He said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, desired to show the angels His prior knowledge (سابق علمه) concerning all His creation, and to inform them of what He bestowed upon them—namely, the obedience upon which He fashioned them—and what He withheld from them—namely, human desires (الشهوات الإنسانية). So, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to them: 'Select two angels from among yourselves so that I may send them down to the earth and place within them human natures (الطبائع البشرية)—desire (الشهوة), greed (الحرص), and hope (الأمل)—just as they are in the Children of Adam. Then I shall test them in obedience to Me and opposition to [their] caprice (الهوى).' He said: So, Harut and Marut were selected for this, and they were among the angels who spoke most severely in criticizing the Children of Adam and provoking Allah the Almighty's wrath upon them. So, Allah, the Glorified and Almighty, revealed to them: 'Descend to the earth. I have placed within you the natures of desires, greed, hope, and their likes, just as I placed them in the Children of Adam. I command you not to associate anything with Me, nor to kill the soul I have forbidden, nor to commit fornication, nor to drink wine.' Then they descended to the earth in the form and clothing of humans. They descended in the region of Babylon. A lofty building was raised for them, and they approached it. At its gate was a beautiful, lovely woman, adorned, perfumed, radiant, and cheerful towards them. When they contemplated her beauty and charm and spoke with her, she made the deepest impression on their hearts, and the desire that had been placed within them intensified. They inclined towards her with an inclination of temptation (فتنة) and abandonment (خذلان). They spoke with her and solicited her to yield herself to them. She said to them: 'I have a religion (ديناً) that I follow, and it is not part of my religion to respond to what you desire unless you enter into my religion.' They asked: 'What is your religion?' She said to them: 'I have a god (إلهاً). Whoever worships and prostrates to him is among those in my religion, and I will respond to what he asks of me.' They asked: 'What is your god?' She said: 'My god is this idol (الصنم).' Each looked at his companion and said to him: 'These are two characteristics of what we were forbidden: fornication and polytheism (الشرك). For if we prostrate to this idol and worship it, we will have associated partners with Allah. And here we are seeking fornication, and we cannot overcome the desire for it, and it will not be achieved without this.' They said to her: 'We will respond to what you asked.' She said: 'Then take this wine and drink it, for it is an offering (قربان) for you to Him, and by it you will attain your goal.' They consulted with each other and said: 'These are three characteristics of what we were forbidden: polytheism, fornication, and drinking wine. We cannot commit fornication except through these two [acts] until we fulfill our need.' They said: 'How great is the affliction (البليّة) because of you! We have responded to you.' She said: 'Then take and drink this wine and prostrate to the idol.' So, they drank the wine and prostrated. Then they solicited her. When she prepared herself for that, a beggar (سائل) entered upon them and saw them in that state. They were terrified of him. He said: 'Woe to you two! You have secluded yourselves with this beautiful, perfumed woman and sat with her for such an abomination (الفاحشة)! You are certainly two evil men! I will surely expose you!' And he left on that note. She stood up and said: 'No, by my God! You will not reach me now! This man has seen us and knows your location. He will inevitably report your news. Hurry and kill him before he exposes us all. Then, you may fulfill your need, secure and safe.' They rushed towards the man, overtook him, and killed him. Then they returned to her, but they did not see her. Their private parts (سوءاتهما) became visible to them, their plumage (رياشهما) was stripped away, and they were filled with remorse (أسقطا في أيديهما). They heard a caller: 'You descended to the earth among the humans of Allah the Almighty's creation for an hour of the day, and you disobeyed [Him] with four major sins (كبائر المعاصي) which He had forbidden you and warned you about. You neither watched Him nor were you ashamed before Him. You were the most severe in condemning the people of the earth for sins and in fueling His wrath upon them. Now that the nature of His human creation was placed within you, and your infallibility (عصمكم) from sins [was removed], how do you see the place of His abandonment (خذلانه) in you?' He said: Their hearts were so consumed by the love of that woman that they devised methods of sorcery (طرائق من السحر) which the people of that region circulated."

The Imam (peace be upon him) said: "So, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, gave them a choice between the punishment of this world and the punishment of the Hereafter. One of them said to his companion: 'Let us enjoy the desires of this world, since we have come to it, until we reach the punishment of the Hereafter.' The other said: 'The punishment of this world has an end, but the punishment of the Hereafter has no end. It is not fitting for us to choose the severe, everlasting punishment of the Hereafter over the temporary, perishable punishment of this world.' He said: So, they chose the punishment of this world. They used to teach people sorcery in the land of Babylon. Then, when they taught people sorcery, they were raised from the earth into the air, and they are being punished, inverted, and suspended in the air until the Day of Resurrection."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Abu al-Tufayl, who said: I was in the Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة) and I heard Ali (peace be upon him) while He was on the pulpit (المنبر). Ibn al-Kawwa called out to Him from the back of the mosque and said: "O Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), what is al-Huda (الهدى, guidance)?" He (peace be upon him) said: "May Allah curse you! Have you not heard it? It is not guidance (الهدى) you seek, but rather blindness (العمى) you desire." Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Come closer." So, he approached Him and asked Him about some things, and He informed him. Then he said: "Inform me about this red star," meaning al-Zuhrah (Venus). He (peace be upon him) said: "Allah showed His angels His creation while they were committing one of His acts of disobedience. The two angels, Harut and Marut, said: 'These whom You created their fathers with Your Hand and for whom Your angels prostrated, they disobey You.' He said: 'Perhaps if you were tested with the like of what they were tested with, you would disobey Me just as they disobeyed Me.' They said: 'No, by Your Might!' He said: So, He tested them with the like of what the Children of Adam were tested with, namely desire (الشهوة). Then He commanded them not to associate anything with Him, nor to kill the soul that Allah has forbidden, nor to commit fornication, nor to drink wine. Then He sent them down to the earth. They used to judge between people, one in one region and the other in another region. They remained thus until this star (الكوكبة) came to one of them, seeking judgment. She was one of the most beautiful people, and she impressed him. He said to her: 'The right is yours, but I will not judge for you until you yield yourself to me.' She promised him a day. Then she came to the other one. When she sought judgment from him, she made an impression on his soul and impressed him just as she had impressed the other. He said to her the same thing his companion had said. She promised him the same time she had promised his companion. They both met her at that hour. Each was ashamed of his companion when he saw him, and they lowered their heads and bowed. Then the shame was stripped from them. One of them said to his companion: 'O so-and-so, what brought me is what brought you.' He said: Then they informed her and solicited her to yield herself to them. She refused them unless they prostrated to her idol (وثنها) and drank from her drink. They refused her and pleaded with her, but she refused unless they drank from her drink. When they drank, they prayed to her idol. A poor man (مسكين) entered and saw them. She said to them: 'This one will leave and inform about you.' They stood up and killed him. Then they solicited her to yield herself to them, but she refused unless they informed her of what they ascended with to the heaven. They used to judge during the day, and when night came, they ascended to the heaven. They refused her, and she refused to comply. So, they informed her. She said: 'That is so that I may test (1) their statement.' She ascended, and they raised their eyes towards her and saw the inhabitants of the heaven looking down upon her. She reached the heaven and was transformed (مسخت), and she is the star that is seen."

In al-Khisal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his grandfather, who said: "Indeed, the transformed creatures (المسوخ) from the Children of Adam are thirteen..." until they said: "And what is al-Zuhrah?" He said: "She was a woman who tempted Harut and Marut, and Allah transformed her into a star."

And from him, from his father, from his grandfather, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them), who said: I asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about the transformed creatures (المسوخ). He said: "They are thirteen..." until he said: "As for al-Zuhrah, she was a Christian woman (امرأة نصرانية) who belonged to one of the kings of the Children of Israel. She is the one by whom Harut and Marut were tempted, and her name was Nahil, though people call her Nahid."

In al-Ilal, narrated from Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: "And al-Zuhrah was transformed because she was a woman by whom Harut and Marut were tempted."
