And from him, from his father, in a hadith, he said: "As for al-Zuhrah, she was a woman named Nahil, and she is the one whom people say Harut and Marut were tempted by."

I say: The attribution of their temptation to the saying of the people (قول الناس) is evidence for what we stated: that it is among the cryptic symbols (المرموزات). As for solving its meaning (حلّها), perhaps what is meant by the two angels (الملكين) is the spirit (الروح) and the heart (القلب), for they are from the spiritual world (العالم الروحاني) sent down to the physical world (العالم الجسماني) to establish the truth (الحق). They were tempted by the Zuhrah (splendor) of the worldly life (الحياة الدنيا) and fell into the net of desire (الشهوة). They drank the wine of heedlessness (خمر الغفلة), worshipped the idol of caprice (صنم الهواء), and killed their intellect (عقلهما)—which advised them—by preventing its nourishment with knowledge (العلم) and piety (التقوى) and erasing the effect of its advice from themselves. They prepared themselves for fornication with the vile harlot (بغي الدنيا الدنية) of the world, whose cultivation of activity (النشاط) and merriment (الطرب) is overseen by the star called Zuhrah. The world then fled from them and escaped them, as it is its habit to flee from those who seek it, because it is the enjoyment of delusion (متاع الغرور). The radiance of its beauty remained in an elevated place, unattainable by the hands of its seekers, as long as al-Zuhrah remains in the sky. Their love for her in their hearts led them to devise methods of sorcery (طرائق من السحر)—which is subtle in its acquisition (لطف مأخذه) and delicate (ذق)—so they were given a choice for their deliverance from it. After becoming aware and their intellect returning to them, they chose the easier of the two punishments. Then they were raised to the Barzakh (البرزخ) being punished, with their heads downwards, until the Day of Resurrection. This is what occurred to the mind in solving this symbol (الرمز). As for solving the rest of its parts found in the narration of Abu al-Tufayl, that is entrusted to the insight (بصيرة) of those with insight (ذوي البصائر). It was also said: Rather, it is an allusion (إشارة) to the fact that the knowledgeable, perfect person (الشخص العالم الكامل), who is close to the enclosures of holiness (حظائر القدس), may be entrusted to his own deceiving soul (نفسه الغرّارة) and not be reached by Divine care (العناية) and success (التوفيق). So, he casts his knowledge behind his back and turns towards his base sensory desires (مشتهياته الحسّية الخسيسة), turning away from true pleasures (اللّذات الحقيقية) and high ranks (المراتب العليّة), thus descending to the lowest of the low (أسفل السّافلين). And the deficient, ignorant person (الشخص الناقص الجاهل) immersed in sins (الأوزار) may associate with that knowledgeable person, intending thereby corruption (الفساد) and abomination (الفحشاء). But Divine success (توفيق إلهي) reaches him, and he benefits from that knowledgeable person what causes him to turn away from the impurities (ادناس) of the abode of delusion (دار الغرور) and the filth (أرجاس) of the world of falsehood (عالم الزور). He rises, by the blessing of what he learned, from the depths of ignorance (حضيض الجهل) and loss (الخسران) to the summit of glory (أوج العز) and gnosis (العرفان). Thus, the learner ends up in the highest degrees of elevation (العلاء), and the teacher ends up in the lowest abyss of misery (الشقاء).

I say: This solution does not fully correspond to the symbol (الرمز) in all its parts.

(1) Al-Mathūbah (المثوبة), al-Thawāb (الثواب), and al-Ajr (الأجر) are synonyms. The opposite of al-Mathūbah is al-ʿUqūbah (العقوبة, punishment). It is said: thāba yathūbu thawban wa thawāban (ثاب يثوب ثوباً وثواباً) and ithābah ithābatan wa thawāban wa mathūbatan (اثابة إثابة وثواباً ومثوبة). The origin of al-Thawāb is what returns to you from something. M.

(1) Meaning: so that she might say the same thing they said, and thus know whether ascent is possible with that statement or not. From him, may Allah sanctify his secret (قدس الله سره).

(١٠٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (O you who have faith!) Al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): There is nothing in the Qur'an that says ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (O you who have faith!) except that it is in the Torah as "O you poor ones (يا أيها المساكين)." Do not say (1) ** راعِنا (1) Do not say, ‘Rā‘inā,’ (meaning: Observe our circumstances (راع أحوالنا), desire us (راغبنا), and be gentle with us (تأن بنا) in what You teach us until we understand it). And that is because when the Jews heard the Muslims addressing the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم with their saying راعنا (Rā‘inā), and راعنا in their language was an insult (سبّاً) meaning "Listen, may you not hear" (اسمع لا سمعت). Some of them said to others: "If we have been insulting Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم secretly until now, then come, let us insult Him openly now." So they used to say راعنا to Him, intending to insult Him. Sa'd ibn Mu'adh al-Ansari realized this, so he cursed them and threatened them with striking their necks if he heard it from them. Then the Ayah was revealed: ** وَقُولُوا انْظُرْنا but say, ‘Unẓurnā,’ (meaning: Look at us (انظر إلينا)) ** وَاسْمَعُوا and listen. (meaning: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم says a word to you, listen and obey (وأطيعوا)) ** وَلِلْكافِرِينَ And there is a painful punishment (meaning: The slanderers (الشاتمين)) ** عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ for the faithless.

(١٠٥) ما يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ وَلَا الْمُشْرِكِينَ أَنْ يُنَزَّلَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** (105) The faithless among the People of the Book do not wish, nor do the polytheists, that any good should be sent down to you from your Lord. [This is] a clear sign (آية بيّنة) and a miraculous proof (حجّة معجزة) for the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and for his honor and the honor of his Household (أهل بيته). ** وَاللهُ يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِ and Allah singles out whomever He wishes for His mercy, [Meaning] His granting of success (توفيقه) to the religion of Islam and the loyalty (موالاة) to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali عليه السلام. ** مَنْ يَشاءُ Whomever He wishes. And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and al-Baqir (peace be upon them both): [It] means by His prophethood (بنبوّته). ** وَاللهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ and Allah is the Possessor of the great grace. [Meaning] upon the one whom He grants success (وفّقه) to His religion and loyalty (موالاتهما) to them both. أقول : Or [it means] He chooses him for His prophethood (نبوّته), or that which encompasses them both and others.

(١٠٦) ما نَنْسَخْ مِنْ آيَةٍ ** (106) Whatever verse We abrogate by lifting its ruling. Some recited it with a dammah on the nūn and a kasrah on the sīn (نُنْسِخْ). ** أَوْ نُنْسِها ** or cause to be forgotten by lifting its script (رسمها) and obliterating its memorization from the hearts, and from your heart, O Muhammad, as He said: سَنُقْرِئُكَ فَلا تَنْسى إِلَّا ما شاءَ اللهُ (We shall make you recite, and you will not forget, except what Allah may wish) that He causes you to forget, so He removes its remembrance from your heart. And it was recited nansāhā (نَنْسَاهَا) with a fatḥah on the nūn and the affirmation of the alif. ** نَأْتِ بِخَيْرٍ مِنْها ** We bring a better one with that which is greater for your reward and more glorious for your welfare. ** أَوْ مِثْلِها ** or its like. in terms of welfare. Meaning: We do not abrogate (ننسخ) or replace (نبدّل) except that Our purpose in that is your welfare (مصالحكم). I say: That is because welfare (المصالح) differs according to the variation of ages (الأعصار) and individuals (الأشخاص). For indeed, what is beneficial in one age and relative to one person may be harmful in another age and relative to another person. A detailed explanation of this will come, if Allah wills, from the speech of the Infallible (peace be upon him) in the exegesis of the verses concerning the Qiblah. It is said that this [verse] was revealed when they said that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commands something, then prohibits it and commands its opposite. ** أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** Do you not know that Allah has power over all things? He has power over abrogation (النسخ) and replacement (التبديل) for your welfare (مصالحكم) and benefits (منافعكم).

footnote (١) **The Almighty's saying:** ** لا تَقُولُوا راعِنا** (Do not say, 'Rā‘inā') The Muslims used to say, "O Messenger of Allah, *rā‘inā*," meaning, "Listen to us." But the Jews distorted this word and said, "O Muhammad, *rā‘inā*," while inclining toward foolishness (*الرعونة*), intending by it deficiency (*النقيصة*) and slander (*الوقيعة*). When they were reproached, they said, "We say what the Muslims say." So Allah prohibited that by His saying: ** لا تَقُولُوا راعِنا وَقُولُوا انْظُرْنا** (Do not say, 'Rā‘inā,' but say, 'Unẓurnā'). Qatādah said that it was a word the Jews used to say by way of mockery (*الاستهزاء*). And ‘Aṭā’ said it was a word that the Anṣār used to say during the Age of Ignorance (*الجاهلية*), so they were prohibited from it in Islam. And Al-Suddī said that it was the specific speech of a Jew named Rifā‘ah ibn Zayd, who intended foolishness (*الرعونة*) by it, so the Muslims were prohibited from that. (*Majma‘ al-Bayān*).

(١٠٧)أَلَمْ تَعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللهَ لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (107) Do you not know that to Allah belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth? And He is the Knower of their management and their welfare, so He governs you with His knowledge, ** وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍ and you do not have any guardian besides Allah, who manages your welfare, since the Knower of the welfare (العالم بالمصالح) is He, exclusive of others, ** وَلا نَصِيرٍ nor do you have any helper (ناصر) who can protect you from harm (مكروه) if He intends to send it down upon you, or punishment (عقاب) if He intends to inflict it upon you.

(١٠٨) أَمْ تُرِيدُونَ Rather, you desire, O disbelievers of Quraysh and Jews, ** أَنْ تَسْئَلُوا رَسُولَكُمْ concerning the signs (الآيات) that you propose, regarding which you do not know whether they contain your rectification (صلاحكم) or your corruption (فسادكم), كَما سُئِلَ مُوسى مِنْ قَبْلُ and it was proposed to him when it was said to him: ** لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً فَأَخَذَتْكُمُ الصَّاعِقَةُ وَمَنْ يَتَبَدَّلِ الْكُفْرَ بِالْإِيمانِ ** (We will not believe you until we see Allah manifestly. Thereupon the thunderbolt seized you. And whoever exchanges faith for disbelief) after the Apostle's reply to him that what he asked was not suitable for proposing to Allah. So he does not believe when he knows that it is not for him to propose (such things), or after what he proposed becomes manifest to him—if his proposal was correct—he still does not believe upon witnessing what he proposed, or he is not satisfied with the proofs (الدلالات) and clear signs (البينات) that Allah has established, by being obstinate (يعاند) and not adhering to the established argument (الحجّة القائمة). This is because ten Jews approached the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم intending to seek difficulty (يتعنّتوه) from him, asking him about things and being obstinate (يعانتوه) with them. Then, he عليه السلام mentioned things they asked about and signs (آيات) they proposed, and we shall mention them, if Allah wills, in their proper places. ** فَقَدْ ضَلَّ سَواءَ السَّبِيلِ (has certainly strayed from the right way.) He has missed the path of purpose (القصد) leading to the Gardens (الجنان) and has taken the path leading to the Fires (النيران).

(١٠٩) ** وَدَّ كَثِيرٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتابِ لَوْ يَرُدُّونَكُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ إِيمانِكُمْ كُفَّاراً (109) Many of the People of the Book wish they could turn you back as faithless after your faith, due to the doubts (الشبه) they present to you, ** حَسَداً out of envy towards you, because He honored you with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and Ali and their pure progeny (آلهما الطّيبين), ** مِنْ عِنْدِ أَنْفُسِهِمْ from their own souls, It was said: meaning they wished that مِنْ عِنْدِ أَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (from their own souls) and their desires, not stemming from their religious adherence (تدينهم) or their inclination towards the truth (الحق); or [it means] an intense envy arising from the core of their souls, ** مِنْ بَعْدِ ما تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الْحَقُ ** after the truth has become manifest to them. through the miracles (المعجزات) indicating the truthfulness of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the virtue (فضل) of Ali and their progeny (peace be upon them). It was said: and through the descriptions (النعوت) mentioned in the Torah (التوراة). ** فَاعْفُوا وَاصْفَحُوا Yet excuse and forbear, regarding their ignorance (جهلهم), and confront them with the proofs (حجج) of Allah, and repel their falsehoods (أباطيلهم) with them. It was said: al-Afw (العفو) is abandoning the punishment for the sin, and al-Safh (الصفح) is abandoning the reproach (تثريبه) for it, ** حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ اللهُ بِأَمْرِهِ until Allah brings His command. concerning them, by killing [them] on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca (فتح مكّة). إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ. (Indeed Allah has power over all things.)

(110) ** وَأَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتُوا الزَّكاةَ (and maintain the prayer and give the zakat) It was said that this is conjoined (عطف) to ** فَاعْفُوا ** (so pardon), as if He commanded them to be patient, to oppose [evil], and to seek refuge in Allah through worship (العبادة) and righteousness (البر). وَما تُقَدِّمُوا لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ ** (and whatever good you send ahead for your souls) Such as prayer, or wealth that you spend in obedience to Allah, or influence (جاه) that you exert for your believing brethren, by which you draw benefits to them and ward off harms from them. ** تَجِدُوهُ عِنْدَ اللهِ (you will find it with Allah) You will find its reward, by which your evil deeds (سيئاتكم) are erased, your good deeds (حسناتكم) are multiplied, and your ranks (درجاتكم) are elevated. إِنَّ اللهَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (Indeed Allah is watchful of what you do) He is All-Knowing (عالم); neither an apparent deed nor a hidden thought (باطن ضمير) is concealed from Him, according to your beliefs (اعتقاداتكم) and intentions (نيّاتكم).

(١١١) ** وَقالُوا means the Jews and the Christians. The Jews said: ** الْيَهُودُ لَنْ يَدْخُلَ الْجَنَّةَ إِلَّا مَنْ كانَ هُوداً (١) (The Jews say, 'None shall enter paradise except one who is a Jew) meaning a Jew. ** أَوْ نَصارى (or a Christian) meaning, and the Christians said: "None shall enter Paradise except one who was a Christian." ** تِلْكَ أَمانِيُّهُمْ (That is their wishful thinking) which they wish for without proof (حجّة). ** قُلْ هاتُوا بُرْهانَكُمْ (Say, 'Produce your proof') your argument (حجتكم) for your assertion (مقالتكم). ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (if you are truthful.') in your claim (دعواكم).

** بَلى مَنْ أَسْلَمَ وَجْهَهُ لِلَّهِ (112) Yes, whoever submits his face to Allah when he heard the truth and its proof (برهانه) وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ ** (while he is a doer of good) in his deed for Allah ** فَلَهُ أَجْرُهُ ** (he shall have his reward) his reward (ثوابه) ** عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ (with his Lord) on the Day of Separation (الفصل) and Judgment (القضاء) ** وَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ (and no fear shall befall them) when the disbelievers fear what they witness of the punishment (العقاب) ** وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (nor shall they grieve) at the time of death, because the glad tidings of the Gardens (الجنان) come to them.

(١١٣) وَقالَتِ الْيَهُودُ لَيْسَتِ النَّصارى عَلى شَيْءٍ (113) The Jews say, 'The Christians are not based on anything,' of the religion; rather, their religion is false and disbelief (كفر). وَقالَتِ النَّصارى لَيْسَتِ الْيَهُودُ عَلى شَيْءٍ and the Christians say, 'The Jews are not based on anything,' of the religion; rather, their religion is false and disbelief (كفر), because each of the two groups is a blind follower (مقلّد) without proof (حجّة). وَهُمْ يَتْلُونَ الْكِتابَ while they recite the Book. yet they do not reflect upon it so that they may act upon what it necessitates and thus be saved from error (الضلالة). كَذلِكَ قالَ الَّذِينَ لا يَعْلَمُونَ Thus said those who do not know the truth (الحق) and did not look into it from the perspective commanded by Allah, a saying similar to theirs, where some of them declare others to be disbelievers (يكفر بعضهم بعضاً). فَاللهُ يَحْكُمُ بَيْنَهُمْ Allah will judge between them between the two groups يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ فِيما كانُوا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ. in the world, making clear their error (ضلالتهم) and their wickedness (فسقهم), and He will recompense every one of them according to what he deserves.

He (peace be upon him) said: Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon them) said: This was revealed only because a group of Jews and a group of Christians came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, judge between us." So he (peace be upon him) said: "Relate your story to me." The Jews said: "We are the believers in Allah, the One, the Wise, and His allies (أولياؤه), and the Christians are not based on anything of the religion and the truth (الحق)." And the Christians said: "Rather, we are the believers in Allah, the One, the Wise, and His allies (أولياؤه), and these Jews are not based on anything of the truth (الحق) and the religion." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "All of you are mistaken (مخطئون), false (مبطلون), and wicked (فاسقون) regarding the religion and command of Allah." The Jews said: "And how can we be disbelievers (كافرين) when the Book of Allah, the Gospel (الإنجيل), is among us, and we recite it?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Jews and Christians, you have opposed the Book of Allah and have not acted upon it. If you had been acting upon the two Books, you would not have declared one another disbelievers (كفر بعضكم بعضاً) without proof (حجّة). Because Allah sent down His Books as a cure for blindness and a clarification from error (الضلالة), guiding the worlds through them to a straight path (صراط مستقيم). And the Book of Allah, if you do not act upon it, will be a calamity (وبالاً) upon you, and the proof (حجّة) of Allah, if you do not submit to it, you will be disobedient (عاصين) to Allah and exposed to His wrath (سَخَطه)." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم turned to the Jews and said: "Beware lest you be afflicted, due to opposing the command of Allah and opposing His Book, by what afflicted your predecessors, concerning whom Allah said:" فَبَدَّلَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا قَوْلاً غَيْرَ الَّذِي قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَنْزَلْنا عَلَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا رِجْزاً مِنَ السَّماءِ. But the wrongdoers changed the saying with one other than that which they had been told. So We sent down on the wrongdoers a plague from the sky

footnote (١) Regarding (*هود*), there are three sayings. The first is that it is the plural of (*هائد*), like (*عائد*) and (*عود*), and (*عائط*) and (*عوط*), and it is a plural for both feminine and masculine forms using a single word form. And (*الهائد*) is the penitent (*التائب*), the one who returns to the truth (*الحق*). The second is that it is a verbal noun (*مصدر*) that is suitable for both singular and plural, just as one says (*رجل فطر*) (a man who breaks fast) and (*قوم فطر*) (a people who break fast), or (*رجل صوم*) (a fasting man) and (*قوم صوم*) (a fasting people). The third is that its meaning is: except those who were Jews (*يهوداً*), so the extra (*ياء*) was omitted. (*مجمع*) [Majma' al-Bayan].

(١١٤) وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ مَنَعَ مَساجِدَ اللهِ أَنْ يُذْكَرَ فِيهَا اسْمُهُ (114) And who is a greater wrongdoer than someone who bars Allah’s mosques from His Name being mentioned in them These are the mosques of the best of the believers in Mecca, from which they were barred from worshiping by forcing the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to leave Mecca. And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام and al-Qummi, that they are: Quraysh, when they prevented the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم from entering Mecca and the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام). And narrated from Zayd ibn Ali, from his forefathers, from Ali عليهم السلام, that He (the verse) intended the entire earth, due to the saying of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "The earth has been made a mosque for me, and its dust a purifier." أقول : وهو عام لكل مسجد وكل مانع وان نزل خاصاً. I say: And this is general for every mosque and every preventer, even if it was revealed concerning a specific instance. وَسَعى فِي خَرابِها and strives for their ruin? The ruin of those mosques so that they are not maintained through obedience to Allah. ** أُولئِكَ ما كانَ لَهُمْ أَنْ يَدْخُلُوها إِلَّا خائِفِينَ ** Such people are not entitled to enter them except fearfully. Due to His justice and His decree enforced upon them, that they should enter them as disbelievers, [facing] His swords and His scourges. أقول : يعني إمام العدل فهو وعد للمؤمنين بالنصرة واستخلاص المساجد منهم وقد أنجز وعده بفتح مكّة لمؤمني ذلك العصر وسينجزه لعامة المؤمنين حين ظهور العدل. I say: He means the Imam of Justice (إمام العدل). Thus, it is a promise to the believers of victory and the recovery of the mosques from them. He has already fulfilled His promise with the conquest of Mecca for the believers of that era, and He will fulfill it for the general body of believers upon the manifestation of Justice. And al-Ayyashi narrates from Muhammad ibn Yahya: Meaning, they will not accept faith except with the sword over their heads. لَهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيا خِزْيٌ For them is disgrace in the world Which is His expulsion of them from the Sanctuary (الحرم) and His prevention of them from returning to it. وَلَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ عَذابٌ عَظِيمٌ and for them is a great punishment in the Hereafter Due to their disbelief and their wrongdoing. He said, Ali ibn al-Husayn عليهم السلام said: "And indeed, among the hypocrites (المنافقين) and the weak ones who resembled the hypocrites (أشباه المنافقين), there was an intention to ruin the mosques in Medina and to ruin all the mosques of the world by what they intended: the killing of Ali عليه السلام in Medina and the killing of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on their way to al-Aqabah, meaning during the Battle of Tabuk (غزوة تبوك)." هذا آخر ما وجد من تفسير أبي محمّد الزكي مرتباً مجتمعاً وما وجد منه متفرقاً نذكره في مواضعه إن شاء الله. This is the last of what was found of the Tafsir of Abu Muhammad al-Zaki, arranged and collected. Whatever was found of it scattered, we shall mention it in its proper places, if Allah wills (إن شاء الله).

(١١٥) وَلِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ (115) To Allah belong the east and the west. فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah. إِنَّ اللهَ واسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ Indeed Allah is all-encompassing, all-knowing.

The Almighty's saying: وَلِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ (To Allah belong the east and the west.) means the two sides (ناحيتي) of the earth, meaning all of it belongs to Him. فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ (So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah.) It was said: meaning His Essence (ذاته), since no place is devoid of Him. إِنَّ اللهَ واسِعٌ (Indeed Allah is all-encompassing,) in Essence (ذاتاً), knowledge (علماً), power (قدرة), mercy (رحمة), and in granting ease (توسعة) to His servants. عَلِيمٌ (all-knowing.) of the interests (مصالح) of all and what issues forth (يصدر) from all in every place and direction (جهة).

In al-Qummi: It (the Ayah) was revealed concerning the supererogatory prayer (صلاة النافلة), which you may pray wherever you turn when you are traveling. As for the obligatory prayers (الفرائض), then His Almighty saying: وَحَيْثُ ما كُنْتُمْ فَوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُ (and wherever you may be, turn your faces toward it.) means that the obligatory prayers (الفرائض) are not prayed except toward the Qiblah.

In al-Majma': The like of this (is mentioned). He said: This is what is narrated from our Imams (أئمتنا)...

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah revealed this Ayah specifically concerning supererogatory prayers (التطوع): فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ واسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah. Indeed Allah is all-encompassing, all-knowing.) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed the evening prayer (المساء) upon his riding beast, wherever it turned with him, when he went out to Khaybar and when he returned from Makkah, placing the Ka'bah behind his back. He said: Zurarah said: I asked Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him): "Is prayer while traveling, on a ship, and on a litter (المحمل) the same?" He said: "All supererogatory prayers (النافلة) are the same. You gesture (تومئ إيماء) wherever your mount or your ship turns. As for the obligatory prayer (الفريضة), you must descend from the litter (المحمل) to the ground, except in case of fear. If you fear, then you gesture. As for the ship, pray standing in it, and strive to face the Qiblah with your utmost effort, for Noah had prayed the obligatory prayer in it standing, facing the Qiblah, while it was sealed upon them (مطبقة عليهم)." I said: "And how did he know the Qiblah so as to face it while it was sealed upon them?" He said: "Jibrāʾīl used to align him toward it." I said: "Do I face it (the Qiblah) in every Takbir?" He said: "As for the supererogatory prayer (النافلة), no. Rather, in the supererogatory prayer, you perform the Takbir facing other than the Qiblah most of the time." Then he said: "All of that is a Qiblah for the one performing the supererogatory prayer, for He said: فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ واسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah. Indeed Allah is all-encompassing, all-knowing.)"

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who recites the Sajdah (السجدة) while he is upon the back of his riding beast. He said: "He prostrates wherever it turns, for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to pray the supererogatory prayer (النافلة) upon his she-camel while facing al-Madinah, saying: فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ إِنَّ اللهَ واسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah. Indeed Allah is all-encompassing, all-knowing.)"

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who stands for prayer, then looks after he has finished and sees that he has deviated from the Qiblah to the right or the left. He said: "His prayers have passed (are valid), and what is between the East and the West is a Qiblah. This Ayah was revealed concerning the Qiblah of the perplexed (المتحيّر): وَلِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ. (To Allah belong the east and the west. So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah.)"

In al-Tawhid, narrated from Salman al-Farsi رضي الله عنه, in the narration concerning the Jathaliq (الجاثليق) who asked the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) questions, and He answered them: Among what he asked was: "Inform me about the Face (وجه) of the Lord, the Blessed and Exalted." Ali (peace be upon him) called for fire and wood and kindled it. When it blazed, Ali (peace be upon him) said: "Where is the face (وجه) of this fire?" The Christian said: "It is a face from all its boundaries (حدودها)." Ali (peace be upon him) said: "This fire is managed (مدبّرة) and created (مصنوعة), and its face is not known, and its Creator does not resemble it: وَلِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ (To Allah belong the east and the west. So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah.) Nothing is hidden from our Lord." What is narrated in al-Khisal from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in the answers to the questions of the Jew is similar to this.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The Face of Allah (وجه الله) are the Proofs (الحُجج) Whom Allah has joined with Himself and with His Messenger, and Whose obedience He has made obligatory upon the servants, similar to that which He made obligatory upon them for Himself.

(١١٦) وَقالُوا اتَّخَذَ اللهُ وَلَداً (116) And they say, 'Allah has taken a son.' قالت اليهود ** عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللهِ وقالت النصارى (١) ** الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ اللهِ وقالت مشركوا العرب الملائكة بنات الله ** سُبْحانَهُ The Jews said: عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللهِ (Uzayr is the son of Allah). And the Christians said: الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ اللهِ (The Messiah is the son of Allah). And the polytheists of the Arabs said: The angels are the daughters of Allah. Glory be to Him! تنزيه له عن ذلك فانه يقتضي التشبيه والحاجة والفناء ** بَلْ لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ [This is] a declaration of His transcendence (تنزيه) from that, for it necessitates resemblance (التشبيه), need (الحاجة), and annihilation (الفناء). Rather, to Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. بل كله ملك له عزير والمسيح والملائكة وغيرهم كُلٌّ لَهُ قانِتُونَ Rather, all of it is His possession (ملك): Uzayr, the Messiah, the angels, and others. All are obedient to Him. منقادون مقرّون له بالعبودية طبعاً وحيلة لا يمتنعون عن مشيئته وتكوينه فكيف يكونون مجانسين له ومن حقّ الولد أن يجانس والده. [They are] submissive (منقادون), acknowledging servitude (العبودية) to Him, naturally (طبعاً) and by means (حيلة). They do not refuse His will (مشيئته) and His creation (تكوينه). So how can they be of the same genus (مجانسين) as Him, when it is the right of the child to be of the same genus (يجانس) as his parent?

(117) ** بَدِيعُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (Originator of the heavens and the earth)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding its exegesis: He originated all things through His knowledge without a prior example, so He originated the heavens and the earth, and before them there were neither heavens nor earths. Do you not hear His saying, the Almighty: ** وَكانَ عَرْشُهُ عَلَى الْماءِ** (and His Throne was upon the water)

وَإِذا قَضى أَمْراً (When He decrees a matter)

He intended its action and its creation, just as He said: ** إِنَّما أَمْرُهُ إِذا أَرادَ شَيْئاً أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ** (All His command, when He wills something, is to just say to it, ‘Be,’ and it is.)

Not with a sound that strikes, nor with a call that is heard. Rather, His speech (glory be to Him) is an act of origination (إنشاء) from Him, and its like was not existent before that. If it were eternal (قديماً), it would be a second god. Thus it is stated in Nahj al-Balaghah: He speaks but does not utter words (يَلْفِظ), and He wills but does not conceal [in His heart] (يُضْمِر).

In al-Kafi and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Kazim: The will (الإرادة) of the creature is the inner thought (الضمير) and what appears to him afterwards of grace (الفضل). As for Allah, the Almighty, His will for the action is His bringing it into existence (احداثه), and nothing else. This is because He does not deliberate (يُروّي), nor does He harbor an intention (يَهُمّ) (2), nor does He reflect (يَتَفَكّر). These attributes are negated from Him, and they are attributes of creation. Thus, the will of Allah is the action itself, and nothing else. ** يَقُولُ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ (He says to it, ‘Be,’ and it is) without utterance (لفظ), or speaking with a tongue, or intention (همة), or reflection (تفكر), and there is no modality (كيف) for that, just as there is no modality (كيف) for Him.

And in a narration: And 'Be' (كن) is an act of making (صنع) from Him, and what comes into existence from Him is the thing made (المصنوع).

(1) It is said that the reason for this error (الضلالة) is that the adherents of previous laws (الشرائع المتقدمة) used to apply the term 'Father' (الأب) to Allah based on the consideration that He is the First Cause (السبب الأول), until they said that the father (الأب) is the lesser father (الأب الأصغر) and Allah (glory be to Him) is the greater Father (الأب الأكبر). Then the ignorant among them assumed that the intended meaning was that of procreation (الولادة), and they believed this through blind imitation (تقليداً). "From him (منه ره)." (2) الهم (intention/concern) is the soul's discourse (حديث النّفس) regarding an action. It is said: هم بالأمر يهم هما (he intended the matter, he intends it, intention), and its plural is هموم. واهمة الأمر (the one intending the matter) is when he is concerned with it and speaks to himself about it. The difference between الهم (intention) regarding a thing before one wills it (يريده) and intends it (يقصده) is that he speaks to himself about it while simultaneously proceeding toward its action. [Majma' (مجمع)].

(١١٨) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ لا يَعْلَمُونَ لَوْ لا يُكَلِّمُنَا اللهُ أَوْ تَأْتِينا آيَةٌ (And those who do not know say, 'Why does not Allah speak to us, or [why does not] a sign come to us?')

The ignorant ones among the polytheists (المشركين) and those among the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) who do not act upon their knowledge [said]: Why does not Allah speak to us, or [why does not] a sign come to us?

I say: This is like His saying, glory be to Him, in al-Muddaththir: ** يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً كَذلِكَ قالَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ (Rather, every one of them desires to be given open scrolls. The like of their saying was said by those who were before them.) —from the past nations (الأمم الماضية)— ** مِثْلَ قَوْلِهِمْ (the like of their saying.) So they said: ** أَرِنَا اللهَ جَهْرَةً (Show us Allah manifestly.) And: ** هَلْ يَسْتَطِيعُ رَبُّكَ أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنا مائِدَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ تَشابَهَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ (Can your Lord send down to us a table laden with food from the sky? Their hearts are alike.) The hearts of these people and those before them are alike in blindness (العمى) and obstinacy (العناد). ** قَدْ بَيَّنَّا الْآياتِ لِقَوْمٍ يُوقِنُونَ (We have certainly made the signs clear for a people who are certain.) —of the realities (الحقائق).

(١١٩) إِنَّا أَرْسَلْناكَ بِالْحَقِّ بَشِيراً وَنَذِيراً (119) We have surely sent you with the truth as a bearer of good news and as a warner. So, it is not incumbent upon you if they persist or show arrogance. ** وَلا تُسْئَلُ عَنْ أَصْحابِ الْجَحِيمِ. and you will not be questioned concerning the inhabitants of the Blaze. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that it is in the prohibitive form (على النهي), as it was recited. (١٢٠) وَلَنْ تَرْضى عَنْكَ الْيَهُودُ وَلَا النَّصارى حَتَّى تَتَّبِعَ مِلَّتَهُمْ (120) The Jews will never be pleased with you, nor will the Christians, unless you follow their creed. This is an exaggeration (مبالغة) in causing the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to despair of their acceptance of Islam, for if they are not pleased with him until he follows their creed, then how will they follow his creed? Thus it was said. ** قُلْ إِنَّ هُدَى اللهِ Say: 'Indeed the guidance of Allah meaning Islam ** هُوَ الْهُدى is the [true] guidance.' to the truth, not what they call you to. ** وَلَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتَ أَهْواءَهُمْ And if you follow their desires their deviated opinions (آرائهم الزائغة) (1) ** بَعْدَ الَّذِي جاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ ما لَكَ مِنَ اللهِ مِنْ وَلِيٍّ وَلا نَصِيرٍ ** after the knowledge that has come to you, you will have no guardian or helper against Allah. who could repel His punishment from you. And this is of the category: "I mean you, but listen, O neighbor."

(١٢١) ** الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ يَتْلُونَهُ حَقَّ تِلاوَتِهِ (121) Those whom We have given the Book recite it as it should be recited. (It means) pausing when the Garden (الجنة) and the Fire (النار) are mentioned, asking (Allah) for the former and seeking refuge (in Him) from the latter. This is according to al-Majma' (narrated) from al-Ayyashi, from al-Sadiq عليه السلام. And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (al-Sadiq عليه السلام): They are the Imams (الأئمة). Al-Ayyashi also narrated it. أُولئِكَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِهِ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْخاسِرُونَ.** Those are the ones who believe in it, and whoever disbelieves in it—it is they who are the losers.

footnote (١) *الزيغ* (deviation) is doubt (*الشك*) and swerving (*الجور*) from the truth (*الحق*) «ص».

(١٢٢) يا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ اذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتِيَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَأَنِّي فَضَّلْتُكُمْ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ. (122) O Children of Israel! Remember My favour which I bestowed upon you, and that I preferred you to all the nations. (١٢٣)** وَاتَّقُوا يَوْماً لا تَجْزِي نَفْسٌ عَنْ نَفْسٍ شَيْئاً وَلا يُقْبَلُ مِنْها عَدْلٌ وَلا تَنْفَعُها شَفاعَةٌ وَلا هُمْ يُنْصَرُونَ ** (123) And beware of a day when no soul will avail another soul in any way, nor will any ransom be accepted from it, nor will any intercession benefit it, nor will they be helped.

The interpretation of the two verses has already preceded. It was said that since He began their story with the command to remember the favors, fulfill their rights, beware of wasting them, and fear the Hour and its terrors, He repeated that and concluded the discourse with them, as an emphasis in counsel and as an indication that it is the summary (فذلكة) of the story and the objective (المقصود) thereof.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that al-ʿadl (العدل) is the obligatory duty (الفريضة). And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that al-ʿadl (العدل) is the ransom (الفداء).

** وَإِذِ ابْتَلى إِبْراهِيمَ رَبُّهُ بِكَلِماتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ قالَ إِنِّي جاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِماماً قالَ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي قالَ لا يَنالُ عَهْدِي الظَّالِمِينَ** (124) And when his Lord tested Abraham with certain words, and he fulfilled them, He said, 'Indeed, I am going to make you an Imam for mankind.' Said he, 'And from among my descendants?' He said, 'My pledge does not extend to the wrongdoers.'

Al-Qummi said: It is that with which He tested him, from what he saw in his sleep concerning the slaughter of his son. So Abraham (peace be upon him) fulfilled them, resolved upon them, and submitted. When he resolved, the Blessed and Exalted said, as a reward for his truthfulness, submission, and acting upon what Allah commanded Him: إِنِّي جاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِماماً قالَ (Indeed, I am going to make you an Imam for mankind. Said he) — Abraham — وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي قالَ (And from among my descendants?) — Glorious is His Majesty — ** لا يَنالُ عَهْدِي الظَّالِمِينَ (My pledge does not extend to the wrongdoers.) Meaning, there shall not be a tyrannical (ظالم) Imam by My covenant. Then He sent down upon him the al-Hanifiyyah (الحنيفيّة), which is purity (الطهارة), and it consists of ten things: five concerning the head and five concerning the body. As for those concerning the head, they are: trimming the mustache (أخذ الشارب), letting the beard grow (اعفاء اللحى), trimming the hair (طمّ الشعر), the siwak (السّواك), and using the toothpick (الخلال). And as for those concerning the body, they are: shaving the hair from the body (حلق الشعر), circumcision (الختان), clipping the nails (قلم الأظافر), bathing from ritual impurity (الغسل من الجنابة), and purification with water (الطهور بالماء). So this is the pure al-Hanifiyyah (الحنيفية الطاهرة) which Abraham (peace be upon him) brought; it was not abrogated, nor will it be abrogated until the Day of Resurrection.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: They are the words (الكلمات) which Adam received from his Lord, and He turned to him in forgiveness. This is when he said: "O Lord, I ask You by the right of Muhammad, Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) that You turn to me in forgiveness." فَتابَ عَلَيْهِ إِنَّهُ هُوَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ (Then He turned to him clemently. Indeed, He is the All-clement, the All-merciful.) He was asked: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, what does His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, mean: ** فَأَتَمَّهُنَ (and he fulfilled them)?" He said: "It means he fulfilled them up to the al-Qa'im (القائم): twelve Imams, nine of whom are from the progeny of al-Husayn (peace be upon them)."

And al-Ayyashi, narrated implicitly (مضمراً), said: He fulfilled them with Muhammad and Ali and the Imams from the progeny of Ali (peace be upon them). He said: And Abraham said: "O Lord, hasten for Muhammad and Ali what You promised me concerning them both, and hasten Your victory for them both."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, took Abraham as a servant (عبداً) before He took him as a Prophet (نبياً). And indeed, Allah took him as a Prophet before He took him as a Messenger (رسولاً). And indeed, Allah took him as a Messenger before He took him as a Friend (خليلاً). And indeed, Allah took him as a Friend before He made him an Imam (إماماً). When He gathered all these things for him, He said: ** إِنِّي جاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِماماً ** He said: Because of the greatness of this position in the eyes of Abraham, he said: وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي (And from among my descendants?). He said: لا يَنالُ عَهْدِي الظَّالِمِينَ (My pledge does not extend to the wrongdoers). He said: The foolish (السفيه) shall not be the Imam of the pious (التقيّ). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever worshipped an idol (صنماً) or a statue (وثناً) shall not be an Imam.

I say: And in this is an allusion (تعريض) to the Three (الثلاثة) since they worshipped idols (الأصنام) before Islam.

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a long hadith: Indeed, the Imamate (الإمامة) is a rank that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, specifically granted to Abraham the Friend (إبراهيم الخليل) (peace be upon him) after Prophethood (النبوّة) and Friendship (الخلّة), as a third rank and a virtue by which He honored him and raised (1) his mention. So He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: إِنِّي جاعِلُكَ لِلنَّاسِ إِماماً (Indeed, I am going to make you an Imam for mankind). The Friend (peace be upon him) said, rejoicing in it: ** وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِي قالَ (And from among my descendants?) — Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: ** لا يَنالُ عَهْدِي الظَّالِمِينَ (My pledge does not extend to the wrongdoers.) So this Ayah invalidated the Imamate of every wrongdoer (ظالم) until the Day of Resurrection, and it became restricted to the chosen ones (الصفوة).

(١٢٥) وَإِذْ جَعَلْنَا الْبَيْتَ the Ka'bah ** مَثابَةً a place of return and a site of recurrence ** لِلنَّاسِ وَأَمْناً. (125) And when We made the House a resort for mankind and a sanctuary.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever enters the Sanctuary (الحرم) from among mankind seeking refuge therein, he is safe from the wrath of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ). And whoever enters it from among the wild beasts and birds is safe from being disturbed or harmed until it leaves the Sanctuary (الحرم).

وَاتَّخِذُوا And it was recited with a fathah on the kha (وَاتَّخَذُوا) ** مِنْ مَقامِ إِبْراهِيمَ مُصَلًّى It is the stone upon which is the trace of his foot. And take the station of Abraham as a place of prayer.**

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means by that the two rak'ahs of the obligatory tawaf. And similar to it is found in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): How great is the slander of the people of Syria (أهل الشام) against Allah, the Most High! They claim that when Allah, the Blessed and Exalted (تبارك وتعالى), ascended to the heaven, He placed His foot upon the rock of Jerusalem (بيت المقدس). Yet a servant from among the servants of Allah placed his foot upon a rock, and Allah commanded us to take it as a place of prayer (مُصَلًّى)—the rest of the hadith.

And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: Three stones descended from Paradise (الجنة): the Station of Abraham (مقام إبراهيم), the Stone of the Children of Israel (حجر بني إسرائيل), and the Black Stone (الحجر الأسود).

وَعَهِدْنا إِلى إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْماعِيلَ أَنْ طَهِّرا بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْعاكِفِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ and We charged Abraham and Ishmael [with the command]: 'Purify My House for those who circle around it, and those who stay in it, and those who bow and prostrate.'

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): (He meant) to keep the polytheists (المشركين) away from it. And he said: When Abraham (peace be upon him) built the House (البيت) and people performed the pilgrimage (حج), the Ka'bah complained to Allah, the Most High, about what it suffered from the breaths of the polytheists (المشركين). So Allah, the Most High, revealed to it: "Be comforted, O My Ka'bah, for I shall send forth in the Last Age a people who cleanse themselves with twigs (قضبان) of trees and use toothpicks (يتخللون)."

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked whether women should perform ghusl (ritual bath) when they come to the House (البيت). He said: "Yes, indeed Allah, the Most High, says: طَهِّرا بَيْتِيَ لِلطَّائِفِينَ وَالْعاكِفِينَ وَالرُّكَّعِ السُّجُودِ 'Purify My House for those who circle around it, and those who stay in it, and those who bow and prostrate.' Thus, it is incumbent upon the servant (العبد) not to enter except while he is pure, having washed off sweat and filth (الأذى), and having purified himself." And similar to this is found in al-Kafi.

(١) Al-Ishādah (الإشادة) is raising the voice with something, and ashāda bi-dhikrihi (أشاد بذكره) is when he raised his status (ص).

(١٢٦) وَإِذْ قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ رَبِّ اجْعَلْ هذا بَلَداً آمِناً وَارْزُقْ أَهْلَهُ مِنَ الثَّمَراتِ (And when Abraham said, 'My Lord, make this a secure town, and provide its people with fruits) In al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): When Abraham supplicated to his Lord to provide his family with fruits (الثمرات), He commanded a piece of al-Urdun (الأردن) (2) to travel with its fruits until it circumambulated (طافت) the House (البيت). Then He commanded it to turn back to this place which was named al-Ta'if (الطائف), and for that reason, it was named Ta'if (طائفاً). And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [It] means from the fruits of the hearts (ثمرات القلوب), that is, make them beloved to the people so that they may frequent them (لينتابوا إليهم) and return. I say: This is an esoteric interpretation (تأويل) and that is an exegesis (تفسير). The evidence for the esoteric interpretation (التأويل) is His saying in Surat Ibrahim: ** فَاجْعَلْ أَفْئِدَةً مِنَ النَّاسِ تَهْوِي إِلَيْهِمْ (So make the hearts of a part of mankind incline toward them). In al-Awali, there is another narrative (حديث آخر) which will come there, if Allah wills. مَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ (those of them who believe in Allah and the Last Day.) Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) who said: He meant us by that, and His friends (أوليائه), and the followers (شيعة) of His successor (وصيه). قالَ (He said)—Allah said— وَمَنْ كَفَرَ (And him who is faithless)—I shall also provide for him— فَأُمَتِّعُهُ (I shall let him enjoy [the good things of the world]). It was also recited with takhfif (بالتخفيف) [as فَأُمْتِعُهُ]. ** قَلِيلاً ثُمَّ أَضْطَرُّهُ إِلى عَذابِ النَّارِ وَبِئْسَ الْمَصِيرُ (for a while, then I shall compel him to the punishment of the Fire, and what a wretched destiny!) [Regarding] the punishment of the Fire (عذاب النار), he said: He meant by that whoever denied His successor (وصيه) and did not follow him from among his nation (أمته). By Allah, this nation (هذه الأمة) is likewise.

(١٢٧) وَإِذْ يَرْفَعُ إِبْراهِيمُ الْقَواعِدَ مِنَ الْبَيْتِ وَإِسْماعِيلُ رَبَّنا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا (127) And when Abraham raised the foundations of the House with Ishmael, [they prayed]: 'Our Lord, accept from us!

our seeking nearness to You by building the House ** إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ السَّمِيعُ** (127) Indeed You are the All-hearing,

of our supplication ** الْعَلِيمُ ** (127) the All-knowing,

of our intentions.

In al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: When Ishmael reached the age of manhood (مبلغ الرجال), Allah commanded Abraham to build the House. He (Abraham) said: "O Lord, in which spot (بقعة)?" He (Allah) said: "In the spot where I sent down the Dome (القبّة) upon Adam, and the Sanctuary (الحرم) was illuminated by it." Abraham did not know in which place to build it, for the Dome which Allah sent down upon Adam had remained standing until the days of the Flood, the days of Noah. When the world drowned, Allah raised that Dome, and its location remained undrowned. For this reason, it was named the Ancient House (البيت العتيق), because it was freed (أعتق) from drowning. So Allah sent Gabriel, who marked out for him the location of the House. Then Allah sent down upon him the foundations (القواعد) from Paradise. The Stone (الحجر), when Allah sent it down upon Adam, was whiter than snow, but when the hands of the disbelievers touched it, it turned black. So Abraham (peace be upon him) built the House, and Ishmael transported the stones from Dhū Ṭuwā (ذي طوى). He raised it nine cubits into the sky, then He (Allah) guided him to the location of the Stone (الحجر). Abraham extracted it and placed it in the location where it is now. When he built it, he made two doors for it: a door facing the East and a door facing the West. The door facing the West is called al-Mustajār (المستجار). Then he cast upon it the Idhkhir (الإذخر) plant, and Hagar hung a cloak on its door that she had with her, and they would shelter (يَكِنُّون) beneath it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a tradition: When Allah permitted him to build, Abraham came and said: "O my son, Allah has commanded the building of the Ka'bah." They uncovered it, and behold, it was a single red stone. Allah Almighty revealed to him: "Place its structure upon it." And Allah Almighty sent down four angels who gathered the stones for him, and the angels handed them (the stones) to the two of them until it was completed to twelve cubits. He prepared two doors for it: a door through which one enters and a door through which one exits, and they placed thresholds (عتباً) and iron bolts (شرجاً) upon its doors.

And narrated from one of the two (Imams) (peace be upon them), he said: Indeed, Allah Almighty commanded Abraham to build the Ka'bah, to raise its foundations (قواعدها), and to show the people their rituals (مناسكهم). So Abraham and Ishmael built the House, a course (ساقاً) (1) every day, until they reached the location of the Black Stone (الحجر الأسود).

Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: Then Abu Qubays (أبو قبيس) called out to Abraham: "You have a trust (وديعة) with me." So he gave him the Stone (الحجر), and he placed it in its location.

In al-Ilal (العلل) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down the Stone (الحجر) for Adam (peace be upon him) from Paradise. The House (البيت) was a white pearl, and Allah raised it to the heaven, but its base (اسُّه) remained. It is directly above this House, and seventy thousand angels enter it every day, never returning to it again. Then Allah commanded Abraham and Ishmael to build the House upon the foundations (القواعد).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Ishmael was the first person whose tongue spoke Arabic (العربية). While they were building, his father would say: "hāy" (هاي), meaning: "Give me a stone." Ishmael would reply to him in Arabic (العربية): "O my father, here is a stone (هاك حجراً)." Thus, Abraham was building, and Ishmael was handing (the stones) to him.

** رَبَّنا وَاجْعَلْنا مُسْلِمَيْنِ (Our Lord! Make us submissive) submissive (منقادين), sincere (مخلصين) ** لَكَ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِنا to You, and from our progeny أُمَّةً ** and make some of our progeny a group (جماعة) who are sought out (يؤمون), meaning they are intended (يقصدون) and they are followed/taken as examples (يقتدى بهم) مُسْلِمَةً لَكَ submissive to You. And they are the People of the House (أهل البيت) whom Allah removed all impurity (الرجس) from and purified them with a thorough purification (تطهيراً), thus narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). In a narration by al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) from him (peace be upon him): He meant by the community (الامة) Banu Hashim (بني هاشم) specifically. وَأَرِنا And show us (عرفنا). And it was read with the sukun on the rāʾ (بإسكان الراء) wherever it occurs. مَناسِكَنا our places/rites of worship (متعبداتنا). And al-nusk (النسك) originally means worship (العبادة), and it became common for the Hajj (الحجّ) due to the hardship (الكلفة) and distance from habit (البعد عن العادة) involved in it. ** وَتُبْ عَلَيْنا and turn to us clemently إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ from what is not fitting (عما لا ينبغي). Indeed You are the All-clement, the All-merciful إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ for whoever repents (لمن تاب).

** رَبَّنا وَاجْعَلْنا مُسْلِمَيْنِ لَكَ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِنا أُمَّةً مُسْلِمَةً لَكَ وَأَرِنا مَناسِكَنا وَتُبْ عَلَيْنا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ (١٢٨) ** (Our Lord! Make us submissive to You, and [raise] from our progeny an exclusive community submissive to You. And show us our rites [of worship], and turn to us clemently. Indeed You are the All-clement, the All-merciful.)

(١٢٩) ** رَبَّنا وَابْعَثْ فِيهِمْ (129) Our Lord, and raise up among them among the Muslim community (الأمة المسلمة) ** رَسُولاً مِنْهُمْ a messenger from among them meaning, from that community (الأمة). This is likewise narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and al-Ayyashi narrated it. No one was sent from their progeny except our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And al-Qummi said: meaning the children of Isma'il. He said: That is why the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "I am the prayer (دعوة) of my father Ibrahim (peace be upon him)." يَتْلُوا عَلَيْهِمْ آياتِكَ who shall recite to them Your signs, He shall recite to them and convey to them what is revealed to him of the proofs (دلائل) of Monotheism (التوحيد) and Prophethood (النبوّة). ** وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ and teach them the Book and wisdom, that by which their souls are perfected, consisting of knowledge (المعارف) and rulings (الأحكام). وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ and purify them. from polytheism (الشرك) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي). إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْعَزِيزُ Indeed You are the All-mighty, the One Who is not subdued and is not overcome regarding what He wills. الْحَكِيمُ the All-wise. the One Who perfects the matter (الأمر) and the Maker (الصانع) according to wisdom (الحكمة).

footnote (١) The sāq (*الساق*) is every vein (*عرق*) of the wall (*الحائط*).

(١٣٠) وَمَنْ يَرْغَبُ عَنْ مِلَّةِ إِبْراهِيمَ (And who would turn away from the creed of Abraham) This is a rhetorical rejection and denial, meaning, no one turns away from his creed ** إِلَّا مَنْ سَفِهَ نَفْسَهُ (except him who fools himself) except him who holds it (his soul) in contempt, humiliates it, and treats it lightly. It is said that its origin is سفه نفسه (with نفسه in the nominative case (الرفع)), and it was put in the accusative case (نصب) for specification (التميز), like غبن رأيه. And it is said that سفه (with الكسر) is transitive (متعدّ), and (with الضم) is intransitive (لازم). What came in the Hadith testifies to this: "Arrogance (الكبر) is that you treat the truth foolishly (تسفه الحق) and hold people in contempt (تغمض الناس)."

In al-Mahasin, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "No one is upon the creed of Abraham except us and our followers (شيعتنا), and all other people are innocent (براء) of it."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq and al-Kazim (peace be upon them both): what is similar in meaning.

وَلَقَدِ اصْطَفَيْناهُ فِي الدُّنْيا وَإِنَّهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ لَمِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ (And We certainly chose him in the world, and in the Hereafter He is indeed among the righteous.) This is a proof and clarification for that, for whoever possesses this attribute is worthy of being followed. No one turns away from following him except a fool (سفيه) or one who pretends to be foolish (متسفّه).

(١٣١) إِذْ قالَ لَهُ رَبُّهُ أَسْلِمْ قالَ ** (131) When his Lord said to him, 'Submit,' he said, hastening to submission (الإذعان) and sincerity of the inner self (إخلاص السر): أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. (I submit to the Lord of all the worlds.) (١٣٢) وَوَصَّى بِها (132) And Abraham enjoined it upon his children, and Jacob [too]: meaning, by the religion (بالملّة) or by this word (بهذه الكلمة), that is, by the word ** أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ. And it was read (وقرئ) أوصى ( إِبْراهِيمُ بَنِيهِ وَيَعْقُوبُ (Abraham enjoined it upon his children, and Jacob [too]): And Jacob also enjoined it upon his children: ** يا بَنِيَّ إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفى لَكُمُ الدِّينَ (O my sons! Allah has chosen for you the religion), meaning the religion of Islam (دين الإسلام), ** فَلا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ (so do not die unless you are Muslims). He commanded them to be firm (بالثبات) upon Islam (على الإسلام) such that decay (الزوال) would not approach it under any circumstance (بحال).

(١٣٣) أَمْ كُنْتُمْ شُهَداءَ إِذْ حَضَرَ يَعْقُوبَ الْمَوْتُ (Were you present as witnesses when death approached Jacob?) This is a denial (على الإنكار), meaning: you were not present. It is said that the Jews said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Do you not know that Jacob enjoined his sons to follow Judaism the day He died?" So, [this verse] was revealed: إِذْ قالَ لِبَنِيهِ ما تَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ بَعْدِي (when he said to his sons, 'What will you worship after me?'). He intended by this to affirm them upon monotheism (التوحيد) and Islam, and to take their covenant upon steadfastness regarding both of them. ** قالُوا نَعْبُدُ إِلهَكَ وَإِلهَ آبائِكَ إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْماعِيلَ وَإِسْحاقَ (They said, 'We shall worship your God, and the God of your fathers, Abraham, Ishmael, and Isaac—) Ishmael was counted among his forefathers (آبائه) because the Arabs call the paternal uncle (العم) a father (أباً), just as they call the grandfather (الجدّ) a father (أباً). This is due to the necessity of honoring both of them like honoring him [the father]. And in the Hadith: "A man's paternal uncle is the twin (صنو) of his father." ** إِلهاً واحِداً (the One God,) This is an explicit declaration of monotheism (التوحيد). ** وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُسْلِمُونَ**. (and to Him do we submit.')

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): That it occurred concerning al-Qa'im (peace be upon him).

I say: Perhaps His intent (peace be upon him) is that it applies to the Qa'im of the Household of Muhammad (peace be upon them), such that every Qa'im among them says that to his sons at the time of death, and they respond to him with what [Jacob's sons] responded with.

(١٣٤) تِلْكَ أُمَّةٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ (That is a nation that has passed away) meaning Abraham (إبراهيم), Jacob (يعقوب), and their sons. لَها ما كَسَبَتْ وَلَكُمْ ما كَسَبْتُمْ (theirs is what they earned, and yours is what you have earned) For each is the reward of his deed. I say: Meaning, your affiliation (انتسابكم) with them will not benefit you; rather, the benefit is through deeds (بالأعمال). وَلا تُسْئَلُونَ عَمَّا كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (and you will not be questioned about what they used to do.) You will not be taken to task for their evil deeds (بسيئاتهم), just as you will not be rewarded for their good deeds (بحسناتهم).

(١٣٥) وَقالُوا كُونُوا هُوداً أَوْ نَصارى تَهْتَدُوا ** (135) And they say, 'Be Jews or Christians, that you may be guided.' The Jews said: "Be Jews, you will be guided." And the Christians said: "Be Christians, you will be guided." ** قُلْ بَلْ مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ ** (Say, 'No, rather the creed of Abraham,) Rather, we are the followers of the creed of Abraham, following him, حَنِيفاً (a monotheist), inclining away from every religion toward the religion of truth. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "The الحنيفيّة (monotheism) is Islam." And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "The الحنيفية has left nothing out, such that among its practices are trimming the mustache, clipping the nails, and circumcision (الختان)." وَما كانَ إبراهيم مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ (and Abraham was not one of the polytheists.') This is an allusion (تعريض) to the People of the Two Books (أهل الكتابين), for they claimed to follow the creed of Abraham, yet despite that, they were upon polytheism (الشرك).

(١٣٦) The Almighty's saying: ** قُولُوا آمَنَّا بِاللهِ**. (Say, 'We believe in Allah)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He (Allah) only meant by this ['We believe in Allah'] Ali, Fatimah, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (peace be upon them), and it applies to the Imams (peace be upon them). Then the saying returns from Allah concerning the people, so He said: ** فَإِنْ آمَنُوا (So if they believe), meaning the people, بِمِثْلِ ما آمَنْتُمْ بِهِ (with the like of what you believe in) [the rest of] the Ayah. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated implicitly (مُضْمَراً): As for His saying ** قُولُوا (Say), they are the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them).

وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْنا (and what has been sent down to us) means the Quran, وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلى إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْماعِيلَ وَإِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْباطِ (and what was sent down to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes) means the Scrolls (الصحف). And the Tribes (الْأَسْبَاطِ) are the grandchildren (حَفَدَة) of Jacob.

Al-Ayyashi: Narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked whether the sons of Jacob were prophets. He said: "No, but they were Tribes (أَسْبَاطاً), the children of prophets. They did not depart the world except as fortunate ones (سُعَدَاء); they repented and remembered what they had done."

وَما أُوتِيَ مُوسى وَعِيسى (and what was given to Moses and Jesus) [means] the Torah and the Gospel, ** وَما أُوتِيَ النَّبِيُّونَ (and what was given to the prophets) collectively, those mentioned among them and those not mentioned, مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ (from their Lord) [meaning] sent down to them from their Lord. ** لا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْهُمْ ** (We do not differentiate between any of them) like the Jews who believe in some and disbelieve in others. And أَحَدٍ (any one), due to its occurrence in the context of negation (سِيَاقِ النَّفْي), is general (عَمَّ), so it is permissible (سَاغَ) for ** بَيْنَ (between) to be annexed to it. وَنَحْنُ لَهُ (and we are Muslims to Him) [meaning] to Allah مُسْلِمُونَ (submitting) [meaning] obedient (مُذْعِنُونَ) and sincere (مُخْلِصُونَ).

In al-Khisal, concerning what the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) taught his companions: When you recite ** قُولُوا آمَنَّا (Say, 'We believe'), then say 'We believe' up to His saying مُسْلِمُونَ** (submitting).

And in al-Faqih, in his recommendations to his son Muhammad ibn al-Hanafiyyah: And affirmation (الإِقْرَار) and expression (التَّعْبِير) by the tongue concerning what the heart has bound itself to is obligatory (فُرِضَ). So the Mighty and Majestic said: قُولُوا آمَنَّا بِاللهِ وَما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْنا (Say, 'We believe in Allah and what has been sent down to us') [the rest of] the Ayah.

(١٣٧) فَإِنْ آمَنُوا meaning, the rest of the people ** بِمِثْلِ ما آمَنْتُمْ بِهِ (meaning) in what you believe in. And the word al-mithl (المثل) is inserted redundantly in its place, just as in His Almighty saying: وَشَهِدَ شاهِدٌ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ عَلى مِثْلِهِ (and a witness from the Children of Israel bears witness to its like), meaning, to it (عليه). And it was recited by omitting it. they are certainly guided, but if they turn their backs, meaning, they turn away: فَإِنَّما هُمْ فِي شِقاقٍ (meaning) in disbelief (كفر). Thus it is stated in al-Majma' narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). And its origin is opposition (المخالفة) and hostility (المناوأة), for every one of the opposing parties is on a side (شق) other than the side of the other. ** فَسَيَكْفِيكَهُمُ اللهُ (This is) a consolation and reassurance for the believers, and a promise to them of protection and victory over those who oppose them. ** وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ of your sayings ** الْعَلِيمُ of your sincerity.

(١٣٨) ** صِبْغَةَ اللهِ (The dye of Allah) Allah has dyed us with a dye, and it is ** فِطْرَتَ اللهِ الَّتِي فَطَرَ النَّاسَ عَلَيْها (the nature of Allah upon which He originated mankind). Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) interpreted it as al-Islam (الإسلام), as is found in al-Kafi (الكافي) and narrated by al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is the dyeing of the believers with al-Wilayah (الولاية) in the Covenant (الميثاق). And it was said that it was named a dye (صبغة) because its effect appeared upon them, just as the effect of the dye appears upon the dyed object, and it permeated their hearts just as the dye permeates the garment. Or (it is used) for comparison (للمشاركة), for the Christians used to immerse their children in yellow water which they called baptism (المعمودية), and they would say it is a purification for them, and through it their Christianity is realized. وَمَنْ أَحْسَنُ مِنَ اللهِ صِبْغَةً (And who is better than Allah in dyeing?) No dye is better than His dye. ** وَنَحْنُ لَهُ عابِدُونَ (and we are His worshippers.) This is an allusion (تعريض) against them, meaning: We do not associate partners with Him like your association of partners.

(١٣٩) قُلْ أَتُحَاجُّونَنا Are you disputing with us ** فِي اللهِ concerning His affair and His selection of a prophet from the Arabs? It is said that the People of the Book said: "All the prophets are from us, and our religion is older, and our Book precedes yours. If you were a prophet, you would be from us." So, [this Ayah] was revealed. ** وَهُوَ رَبُّنا وَرَبُّكُمْ He has no exclusivity (اختصاص) that belongs to one people over another; He bestows His mercy upon whom He wills. ** وَلَنا أَعْمالُنا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمالُكُمْ So, it is not far-fetched that He honors us due to our deeds. ** وَنَحْنُ لَهُ مُخْلِصُونَ We are monotheists (موحدون); we dedicate ourselves purely to Him through faith (الايمان) and obedience (الطاعة), unlike you.

(139) Say, 'Do you argue with us concerning Allah, while He is our Lord and your Lord? For us are our deeds, and for you are your deeds, and we are devoted to Him.'

** أَمْ تَقُولُونَ ** (Or do you say) And it was read with the ي (yā’). ** إِنَّ إِبْراهِيمَ وَإِسْماعِيلَ وَإِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَالْأَسْباطَ كانُوا هُوداً أَوْ نَصارى قُلْ أَأَنْتُمْ أَعْلَمُ أَمِ اللهُ ** (Or do you say that Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob, and the Tribes were Jews or Christians? Say: Are you better informed, or Allah?) Allah, the Mighty and the Majestic, has already negated Judaism and Christianity from Abraham by His saying, glory be to Him: ** ما كانَ إِبْراهِيمُ يَهُودِيًّا وَلا نَصْرانِيًّا وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ كَتَمَ شَهادَةً عِنْدَهُ مِنَ اللهِ ** (Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian. And who is a greater wrongdoer than him who conceals a testimony that he has from Allah?) It was said: It means that no one is a greater wrongdoer than the People of the Book, since they concealed Allah's testimony for Abraham regarding al-Hanifiyyah (الحنيفية) and his innocence (البراءة) of Judaism and Christianity, or than us, if we were to conceal this testimony. And in this is an allusion (تعريض) to their concealment of Allah's testimony for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding prophethood (النبوة) and for Ali عليه السلام regarding successorship (الوصاية) in their books and elsewhere. ** وَمَا اللهُ بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ ** (And Allah is not oblivious of what you do.) And it was read with the ي (yā’), and it is a threat (وعيد) to them.

** تِلْكَ أُمَّةٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ لَها ما كَسَبَتْ وَلَكُمْ ما كَسَبْتُمْ وَلا تُسْئَلُونَ عَمَّا كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (141) That is a nation that has passed. What they earned is for them, and what you have earned is for you, and you will not be questioned about what they used to do.

It was said that the repetition is for emphasis in warning and restraining from what has become firmly established in dispositions concerning boasting of ancestors and relying upon them. Or, the address in what preceded was for them, and in this verse, it is for us, as a warning against following their example. Or, the intended meaning of the nation (الْأُمَّة) in the first instance is the Prophets (الْأَنْبِيَاء), and in the second, the ancestors (أَسْلَاف) of the Jews and the Christians (الْيَهُودِ وَالنَّصَارَى).

(١٤٢) The Almighty's saying: ** سَيَقُولُ السُّفَهاءُ مِنَ النَّاسِ (The foolish among the people will say) These are those whose intellects are light (خفّ أحلامهم) or who deem them insignificant (استمهنوها) through blind imitation (التقليد) and turning away from contemplation (النظر). He means those who deny the change of the Qiblah from among the hypocrites (المنافقين), the Jews (اليهود), and the polytheists (المشركين). The benefit of presenting this news beforehand is to prepare the soul and ready the answer. ما وَلَّاهُمْ (What has turned them away) meaning: what has diverted them ** عَنْ قِبْلَتِهِمُ الَّتِي كانُوا عَلَيْها (from the qiblah they followed?) meaning Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem). ** قُلْ لِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ (Say: ‘To Allah belong the east and the west’) No place is exclusive to Him over another place. ** يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ (He guides whomever He wishes to a straight path) which is what wisdom (الحكمة) and benefit (المصلحة) necessitate, whether turning towards Bayt al-Maqdis sometimes or towards the Ka'bah at other times.

In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** ما نَنْسَخْ مِنْ آيَةٍ أَوْ نُنْسِها. (We do not abrogate any sign or cause it to be forgotten) And also in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was in Mecca, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded Him to turn towards Bayt al-Maqdis in his prayers, and to place the Ka'bah between himself and it if possible. If it was not possible, he was to face Bayt al-Maqdis however he could. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did that throughout his stay there for thirteen years. When he was in Medina, and was obligated to face Bayt al-Maqdis, he faced it, turning away from the Ka'bah for seventeen months. A group of rebellious Jews (مردة اليهود) began to say: "By Allah, Muhammad does not know how to pray until he started turning towards our Qiblah and adopting our guidance (هدانا) and rituals (نسكنا) in his prayers." This greatly distressed the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم when the news reached him from them. He disliked their Qiblah and loved the Ka'bah. Jibra'il (Gabriel) came to him, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "O Jibra'il, I wish Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, would turn me away from Bayt al-Maqdis to the Ka'bah, for I have been harmed by what reaches me from the Jews concerning their Qiblah." Jibra'il (peace be upon him) said: "Then ask your Lord to turn you towards it, for He will not reject your request nor disappoint your desire." When his supplication was completed, Jibra'il ascended, then returned immediately and said: "Recite, O Muhammad: ** قَدْ نَرى تَقَلُّبَ وَجْهِكَ فِي السَّماءِ (We have certainly seen you turning your face about in the sky) and the following verses. The Jews then said: ما وَلَّاهُمْ عَنْ قِبْلَتِهِمُ الَّتِي كانُوا عَلَيْها (What has turned them away from the qiblah they followed?) Allah answered them with the best response, saying: ** قُلْ لِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ (Say: ‘To Allah belong the east and the west’) He owns both of them, and His command to turn to one direction is like His command to you to turn to another direction. يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ (He guides whomever He wishes to a straight path) which is what sets them right (مصلحهم) and leads them through obedience to the Gardens of Bliss (جنات النعيم).

A group of Jews came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Muhammad, this Qiblah, Bayt al-Maqdis, you prayed towards it for fourteen years, then you abandoned it now. If what you were upon was truth (حقاً), then you have abandoned it for falsehood (باطل), for whatever contradicts the truth is falsehood. Or, if it was falsehood, you were upon it for this entire period, so what assures us that you are not upon falsehood now?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Rather, that was truth, and this is truth." Allah the Almighty says: (The Almighty's saying: قُلْ لِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ) (Say: ‘To Allah belong the east and the west. He guides whomever He wishes to a straight path). If He knew that your benefit (صلاحكم), O servants, lay in facing the East, He commanded you with it. And if He knew that your benefit lay in facing the West, He commanded you with it. So, do not deny the management (تدبير) of Allah the Almighty concerning His servants and His intention towards your benefits (مصالحكم)." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "You abandoned work on the Sabbath (يوم السبت), then you worked on the rest of the days, then you abandoned it on the Sabbath, then you worked afterwards. Did you abandon truth for falsehood, or falsehood for truth, or falsehood for falsehood, or truth for truth? Say whatever you wish, for it is the saying of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and His answer to you." They said: "Rather, abandoning work on the Sabbath is truth, and working afterwards is truth." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Likewise, the Qiblah of Bayt al-Maqdis was truth in its time, and then the Qiblah of the Ka'bah is truth in its time."

They said: "O Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, did it occur to your Lord (بدا لربك) regarding what He had commanded you—as you claim—concerning praying towards Bayt al-Maqdis, when He transferred you to the Ka'bah?" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Nothing occurred to Him concerning that, for He is the Knower of consequences (العواقب) and the Possessor of benefits (المصالح). He does not correct an error in Himself, nor does He originate a new opinion contrary to a previous one—He is exalted far above that. Nor does any obstacle befall Him that prevents Him from His will. Nothing occurs (يبدو) except to one whose description is this, and He, the Mighty and Majestic, is greatly exalted above these attributes." Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "O Jews, inform me about Allah: Does He not cause sickness, then health, and health, then sickness? Did anything occur to Him (أبدا له) in that? Does He not give life and cause death? Did anything occur to Him in each of those?" They said: "No." He said: "Likewise, Allah obligates His Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to worship by praying towards the Ka'bah after He had obligated him to worship by praying towards Bayt al-Maqdis, and nothing occurred to Him in the first instance." He said: "Does Allah not bring winter following summer, and summer following winter? Did anything occur to Him in each of those?" They said: "No." He said: "Likewise, nothing occurred to Him regarding the Qiblah." Then he said: "Did He not obligate you in winter to guard against the cold with thick clothes, and obligate you in summer to guard against the heat? Did anything occur to Him in summer such that He commanded you contrary to what He had commanded you in winter?" They said: "No." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Likewise is Allah in His obligation upon you to worship something at one time for a benefit He knows, and then His obligation upon you to worship something else at another time for another benefit He knows. If you obey Allah in both situations, you deserve His reward."

And Allah revealed: (The Almighty's saying: وَلِلَّهِ الْمَشْرِقُ وَالْمَغْرِبُ فَأَيْنَما تُوَلُّوا فَثَمَّ وَجْهُ اللهِ) (To Allah belong the east and the west. So whichever way you turn, there is the face of Allah) If you turn by His command, then there is the direction (الوجه) through which you seek Allah and hope for His reward. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O servants of Allah, you are like the sick, and Allah, the Lord of the worlds, is like the physician. The well-being (صلاح) of the sick person lies in what the physician knows and manages (يدبّره), not in what the sick person desires and proposes. Therefore, submit the matter to Allah, and you will be among the successful (الفائزين)."

(١٤٣) ** وَكَذلِكَ جَعَلْناكُمْ أُمَّةً ** (143) And thus We made you an nation Al-Qummi: Meaning, الأئمة (Imams). ** وَسَطاً a middle He said: Meaning, just (عدلاً), an intermediary (واسطة) between the Messenger and the people. I say: Thus, the address is specifically for the infallible ones (peace be upon them). ** لِتَكُونُوا شُهَداءَ عَلَى النَّاسِ that you may be witnesses to the people Meaning, on the Day of Resurrection. وَيَكُونَ الرَّسُولُ عَلَيْكُمْ شَهِيداً and that the Messenger may be a witness to you. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "We are the middle nation (الأمة الوسط), and we are Allah's witnesses over His creation and His proofs (حججه) on His earth and in His heaven." And in the Hadith of the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر), narrated from him (peace be upon him): "By Allah, the matter has been decreed that there should be no disagreement among the believers. And for that reason, He made them witnesses over the people, so that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم may bear witness against us, and we may bear witness against our Shi'a (شيعتنا), and our Shi'a may bear witness against the people." I say: He meant by the Shi'a (الشيعة) the elite among the Shi'a (خواص الشيعة) who are with them and in their rank, just as they said: "Our Shi'a are with us and in our rank," so that it does not contradict the previous narration and the narrations that follow. In Shawāhid al-Tanzīl, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "He meant us by His saying: that you may be witnesses to the people. For the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is a witness against us, and we are Allah's witnesses over His creation and His proof (حجته) on His earth. And we are those concerning whom Allah said: And thus We made you an nation a middle." And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "We are the namat (نمط) of the Hijaz (الحجاز)." He was asked: "And what is the namat of the Hijaz?" He said: "The middle of the anmāt (الأنماط). Indeed, Allah says: And thus We made you an nation a middle." He said: "To us returns the extremist (الغالي), and by us is joined the deficient one (المقصّر)." And in al-Manāqib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Allah only revealed: And thus We made you a middle nation that you may be witnesses to the people and that the Messenger may be a witness to you. He said: And none can be witnesses over the people except the الأئمة (Imams) and the Messengers. As for the nation (الأمّة), it is not permissible for Allah to call them as witnesses when among them are those whose testimony is not admissible in this world concerning a bundle of herbs (حزمة بقل)."
