I say: Perhaps the meaning intended is that Allah revealed this. It has already passed in the supplication (دعاء) of Abraham: and from our progeny an nation submissive to You. And you have known there that the word الأمة (nation) is in the sense of the intended (المقصود); the group (الجماعة) was named by it because the factions (الفرق) aim for it (تؤمها). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) who said: "Do you think that Allah meant by this Ayah all the people of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة) among the monotheists (الموحدين)? Do you see that someone whose testimony is not admissible in this world concerning a sā' (صاع) of dates, Allah would seek his testimony on the Day of Resurrection and accept it from him in the presence of all past nations? No, Allah did not mean such a one among His creation. He meant the nation (الأمة) for whom the supplication of Abraham was obligatory: You are the best nation that has been brought forth for mankind. And they are the middle الأئمة (Imams), and they are the best nation that has been brought forth for mankind." I say: Since the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Successors (الأوصياء) are infallible (معصومين) from lying, and trust in their testimony for Allah, the Glorified, against the nations is permissible, unlike the rest of mankind, Allah, the Almighty, appointed a witness from among them in every nation to testify against them that Allah sent His Messenger to them and completed His proof (حجّته) against them, and that among them were those who obeyed Him and those who disobeyed Him, so that they may not deny it tomorrow. Thus, the Prophet testifies for Allah against the الأئمة (Imams) that Allah sent him to them and that they obeyed Him. And the الأئمة testify for Allah against the nations that Allah sent the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to them, and for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He conveyed the message to them, and that among them were those who obeyed Him and those who disobeyed Him. Likewise, our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم testifies for the rest of the Prophets against their nations that the Prophets conveyed the messages of their Lord to their nations. The full discussion on this will come in Sūrat al-Nisā' (سورة النساء), if Allah wills (إنشاء الله). وَما جَعَلْنَا الْقِبْلَةَ الَّتِي كُنْتَ عَلَيْها (143) We did not appoint the qiblah you were following Meaning Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس). إِلَّا لِنَعْلَمَ مَنْ يَتَّبِعُ الرَّسُولَ مِمَّنْ يَنْقَلِبُ عَلى عَقِبَيْهِ except that We might ascertain those who follow the Messenger from those who turn back on their heels Meaning, they apostatize (يرتد) from their religion, being accustomed (ألفاً) to the Qiblah (قبلة) of their forefathers. In Tafsir al-Imam and al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Meaning, except that We might know that from him in existence (وجوداً) after We already knew that it would exist (سيوجد)." He said: "That is because the inclination (هوى) of the people of Mecca was towards the Ka'bah (الكعبة), so Allah desired to distinguish the follower of Muhammad from the one who opposed Him by following the Qiblah (القبلة) which they disliked, but which Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded. And since the inclination of the people of Medina was towards Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس), He commanded them to oppose it and turn towards the Ka'bah (الكعبة), so that it might become clear who agrees with Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in what he dislikes, for he is his confirmer and supporter," though the prayer towards Bayt al-Maqdis was at that time. ** لَكَبِيرَةً إِلَّا عَلَى الَّذِينَ هَدَى اللهُ though it was indeed hard except for those whom Allah has guided And who knew that Allah commands worship contrary to what a person desires, in order to test his obedience in opposing his inclination (هواه). وَما كانَ اللهُ لِيُضِيعَ إِيمانَكُمْ Allah would not let your faith go to waste Meaning your prayer (صلاتكم). ** إِنَّ اللهَ بِالنَّاسِ لَرَؤُفٌ رَحِيمٌ. Indeed Allah is most kind and merciful to the people. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about al-Iman (الايمان - faith): Is it a saying (قول) and an action (عمل), or a saying without an action? He said: "Al-Iman is entirely action, and the saying is part of that action, obligated by Allah, made clear in His Book, its light is manifest, and its proof (حجّته) is firm; the Book testifies to it and calls to it. And when His Prophet turned towards the Ka'bah (الكعبة) away from Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس), the Muslims said to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: 'What about our prayer (صلاتنا) which we used to pray towards Bayt al-Maqdis? What is our state regarding it, and the state of those of our dead who passed away while praying towards Bayt al-Maqdis?' So Allah revealed: Allah would not let your faith go to waste. Thus, He named the prayer (الصلاة) Iman (إيمانا). So, whoever meets Allah preserving his limbs (جوارحه), fulfilling for every limb what Allah has obligated upon it, he meets Allah with his Iman complete, and he is among the people of Paradise. And whoever betrays anything of it or transgresses what Allah commanded regarding it, he meets Allah with deficient Iman (ناقص الايمان)."

(١٤٤) قَدْ نَرى تَقَلُّبَ وَجْهِكَ فِي السَّماءِ (144) We have certainly seen you turning your face about in the sky. That is, the turning of your face in the direction of the sky, looking expectantly for the revelation (الوحي). And it is said that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم felt in his heart (روعه) and expected from his Lord that He would turn him toward the Ka'bah, because it was the Qiblah of his father Ibrahim, and the older of the two Qiblahs, and more conducive for the Arabs to accept faith (الايمان), and for the purpose of opposing the Jews. فَلَنُوَلِّيَنَّكَ قِبْلَةً تَرْضاها (We will surely turn you to a qiblah that will satisfy you.) which you love and long for, due to religious objectives that accorded with the will (مشيّة) of Allah, the Almighty, and His wisdom (حكمته). ** فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ (So turn your face) Direct your face ** شَطْرَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ (towards the Sacred Mosque.) towards it. The Mosque (المسجد) was mentioned merely as sufficient indication, by observing the direction (الجهة).

And al-Qummi said: This verse precedes the verse ** سَيَقُولُ السُّفَهاءُ** (The foolish will say).

In al-Faqih: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed toward Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) after the prophethood for thirteen years in Mecca and nineteen months in Medina. Then the Jews reproached him, saying to him: "You are a follower of our Qiblah." He was greatly distressed by this. When it was part of the night, he عليه السلام went out, turning his face toward the horizons of the sky. When morning came, he prayed the dawn prayer (الغداة). When he had prayed two rak'ahs of the noon prayer (الظهر), Jibrāʾīl came and said to him: ** قَدْ نَرى تَقَلُّبَ وَجْهِكَ فِي السَّماءِ فَلَنُوَلِّيَنَّكَ قِبْلَةً تَرْضاها فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ—the verse. Then he took the hand of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and turned his face toward the Ka'bah, and those behind him turned their faces, such that the men stood in the place of the women and the women in the place of the men. Thus, the beginning of his prayer was toward Bayt al-Maqdis and the end of it was toward the Ka'bah. The news reached a mosque in Medina whose people had already prayed two rak'ahs of the Asr (afternoon) prayer, so they turned toward the Qiblah. Thus, the beginning of their prayer was toward Bayt al-Maqdis and the end of it was toward the Ka'bah. That mosque was therefore named Masjid al-Qiblatayn (The Mosque of the Two Qiblahs).

And al-Qummi narrated something similar, saying: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was in the Mosque of Banī Sālim.

وَحَيْثُ ما كُنْتُمْ فَوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُ (And wherever you may be, turn your faces towards it.) He singled out the Messenger for the address, in veneration of him and in fulfillment of his desire (رغبته). Then He generalized it, explicitly stating the universality of the ruling (الحكم) for the entire Ummah and all other places, and as an emphasis on the matter of the Qiblah, and as a specification for the Ummah regarding adherence (المتابعة). وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ لَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ (Indeed, those who were given the Book know that it is the truth from their Lord.) Due to their knowledge that it is His custom, the Almighty, to specify a Qiblah for every law (شريعة), and because their books contain the information that he (the Prophet) would pray toward the two Qiblahs. ** وَمَا اللهُ بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ (And Allah is not oblivious of what they do.) A promise (وعد) and a threat (وعيد) for both parties.

(١٤٥) وَلَئِنْ أَتَيْتَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ بِكُلِّ آيَةٍ (145) And even if you brought those who were given the Book every sign, proof (برهان) and argument (حجة). ** ما تَبِعُوا قِبْلَتَكَ ** they would not follow your direction of prayer, because the obstinate one (المعاند) is not benefited by the indication (الدلالة). وَما أَنْتَ بِتابِعٍ قِبْلَتَهُمْ nor are you going to follow their direction of prayer. a cutting off of their ambitions (أطماعهم). ** وَما بَعْضُهُمْ بِتابِعٍ قِبْلَةَ بَعْضٍ Nor will some of them follow the direction of prayer of others. due to the rigidity (تصلب) of every faction (حزب) in what it holds. وَلَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتَ أَهْواءَهُمْ مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَكَ مِنَ الْعِلْمِ ** And if you follow their desires after the knowledge that has come to you, on the path of an impossible hypothesis (الفرض المحال), or what is meant by it is someone else from his nation (أمته), of the type: 'I mean you, but listen, O neighbor (إياك أعني واسمعي يا جارة)'. إِنَّكَ إِذاً لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ you will indeed be one of the wrongdoers. He emphasized His threat (تهديده) and exaggerated in it, in glorification of the truth (الحق), and encouragement to follow it (اقتفائه), and warning against following desire (الهواء), and deeming the commission of sin by the Prophets (الأنبياء) as enormous (استعظاماً).

(١٤٦) الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ (146) The ones whom We have given the Book meaning their scholars (علماءهم) ** يَعْرِفُونَهُ they know Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by his description (نعت), his attribute (صفة), his mission (مبعث), his place of migration (مهاجر), and the description (صفة) of his companions (أصحابه) in the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإنجيل) كَما يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْناءَهُمْ just as they know their own sons in their homes (منازلهم) وَإِنَّ فَرِيقاً and indeed a group, and they are the obstinate ones (المعاندون) excluding the believers (المؤمنين), ** مِنْهُمْ لَيَكْتُمُونَ الْحَقَّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ. (146) The ones whom We have given the Book know him just as they know their own sons, and indeed a group of them conceal the truth while they know.

(١٤٧) الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ (147) The truth is from your Lord. إنك لرسول إليهم ** فَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ الشاكين. You are indeed a messenger to them. So do not be among the doubters. (The doubters (الشاكين)). (١٤٨) وَلِكُلٍّ وِجْهَةٌ (148) And for everyone there is a direction ولكل قوم قبلة وملّة وشرعة ومنهاج يتوجهون إليها هُوَ مُوَلِّيها الله موليها إياهم وقرئ مولاها بالألف أي قد وليّها فَاسْتَبِقُوا الْخَيْراتِ الطاعات (And for every people there is a qiblah, a religion (مِلَّة), a law (شِرْعَة), and a path (مِنْهَاج) toward which they turn) He is its former. (Allah is the One Who turns them toward it (مُوَلِّيهَا إِيَّاهُمْ). And it was recited مولاها with an alif, meaning He has already taken charge of it (قَدْ وَلِيَّهَا).) So race toward all good works (The acts of obedience (الطاعات)). وفي الكافي عن الباقر عليه السلام : الْخَيْراتِ ** الولاية. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The good works (الْخَيْراتِ) are the Wilayah (الولاية). أَيْنَ ما تَكُونُوا يَأْتِ بِكُمُ اللهُ جَمِيعاً Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you all together. قيل أينما متم في بلاد الله يأت بكم الله جميعاً إلى المحشر يوم القيامة. It was said: Wherever you die in the lands of Allah, Allah will bring you all together to the place of gathering (المحشر) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). وفي اخبار أهل البيت أن المراد به أصحاب المهديّ في آخر الزمان And in the narrations (أخبار) of the People of the House (أهل البيت), the intended meaning is the companions of the Mahdi (المهديّ) at the end of time (آخر الزمان). وفي المجمع والعيّاشيّ عن الرضا عليه السلام : أن لو قام قائمنا لجمع الله جميع شيعتنا من جميع البلدان. In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): If our Qa'im (قائمنا) were to rise, Allah would gather all our followers (شيعتنا) from all the lands. وفي الإكمال والعيّاشيّ عن الصادق عليه السلام : لقد نزلت هذه الآية في أصحاب القائم وإنهم المفتقدون من فرشهم ليلاً فيصبحون بمكّة وبعضهم يسير في السحاب نهاراً نعرف اسمه واسم أبيه وحليته ونسبه In al-Ikmal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This verse was certainly revealed concerning the companions of the Qa'im (القائم). They are those who will be missed from their beds at night, and they will wake up in Mecca. Some of them travel in the clouds during the day; we know his name, the name of his father, his description (حليته), and his lineage (نسبه). إِنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ على الإماتة والأحياء والجمع. Indeed Allah has power over all things (Over causing death (الإماتة), giving life (الأحياء), and gathering (الجمع)).

(١٤٩) وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ (149) And from wherever you depart للسفر في البلاد فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ for travel in the lands, then turn your face toward the Sacred Mosque إذا صلّيت وَإِنَّهُ لَلْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ when you pray, and indeed, it is the truth from your Lord وإن التوجه إلى الكعبة للحق الثابت المأمور به من ربك وَمَا اللهُ بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ And indeed, turning toward the Ka'bah is the established truth commanded by your Lord, and Allah is not unaware of what you do وقرئ بالياء. And it was read with the yāʾ (ي).

(١٥٠) وَمِنْ حَيْثُ خَرَجْتَ فَوَلِّ وَجْهَكَ شَطْرَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ وَحَيْثُ ما كُنْتُمْ فَوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ شَطْرَهُ (150) And from wherever you may emerge, turn your face toward the Holy Mosque, and wherever you may be, turn your faces toward it.

It is said that the ruling was repeated due to the multiplicity of its reasons (علل). For He, the Almighty, mentioned three reasons for the change [of Qiblah]: honoring the Messenger, seeking His pleasure, and the running of the Divine custom (العادة الإلهيّة) that the people of every religion (ملّة) and the claimant of every call (صاحب دعوة) should face a direction (جهة) which they turn toward and by which they are distinguished, and repelling the arguments (حجج) of the opponents, as will follow. And He coupled the effect (معلولها) with every reason (علّة), just as the signified (المدلول) is coupled with every one of its signs (دلائله), for the sake of clarification (تقريباً) and confirmation (تقريراً). Furthermore, the Qiblah holds great significance, and abrogation (النّسخ) is among the places of temptation (الفتنة) and doubt (الشبهة), so it is fitting (فبالحري) that its command be emphasized and its mention be repeated time after time. لِئَلَّا يَكُونَ لِلنَّاسِ عَلَيْكُمْ حُجَّةٌ (Lest people should have any argument against you) is the reason (علّة) for His saying, the Almighty: ** فَوَلُّوا ** (turn [your faces]). The meaning is that turning away from Bayt al-Maqdis (بيت المقدس) toward the Ka'bah repels the argument (احتجاج) of the Jews that the one described in the Torah has the Ka'bah as his Qiblah, and that Muhammad denies our religion and follows us in our Qiblah. [It also repels] the argument of the polytheists (المشركين) that he claims the religion (ملّة) of Abraham (إبراهيم) yet opposes his Qiblah. إِلَّا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْهُمْ.

Al-Qummi: ** إِلَّا here means "and not" (ولا) and is not an exception (استثناء). Meaning: and not ** الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْهُمْ (those of them who are wrongdoers).

And it is said that its meaning is: except for the weak argument (الحجة الداحضة) from the obstinate ones (المعاندين), who said: "He only turned toward the Ka'bah out of inclination toward the religion of his people and love for his country, or a change of mind occurred to him (بدا له) so he returned to the Qiblah of his forefathers, and he is likely to return to their religion."

فَلا تَخْشَوْهُمْ (So do not fear them), for their slanders (مطاعنهم) will not harm you. ** وَاخْشَوْنِي (and fear Me), so do not disobey what I have commanded you. وَلِأُتِمَّ نِعْمَتِي عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (and that I may complete My blessing upon you, and so that you may be guided). Narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: The completion of the blessing (تمام النعمة) is entering Paradise (الجنة). And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The completion of the blessing is dying upon Islam (الإسلام).

I say: There is no contradiction (لا تنافي) between the two narrations due to the necessary connection (تلازم) between the two matters.

(151) كَما أَرْسَلْنا فِيكُمْ رَسُولاً مِنْكُمْ (151) Just as We sent to you an apostle from among yourselves, meaning ** وَلِأُتِمَّ نِعْمَتِي عَلَيْكُمْ (and that I may complete My blessing upon you) just as I completed it by sending a messenger from among you يَتْلُوا عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِنا وَيُزَكِّيكُمْ (who recites to you Our signs, and purifies you,) He carries you toward that by which you become pure (أزكياء). He placed it (purification) before teaching (التعليم) based on the intention (القصد), but He delayed it in the supplication (دعوة) of Abraham (إبراهيم) based on the action (الفعل). ** وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ الْكِتابَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمْ ما لَمْ تَكُونُوا تَعْلَمُونَ (and teaches you the Book and wisdom, and teaches you what you did not know.) through thought (الفكر) and contemplation (النظر), since there is no way to know it except through revelation (الوحي). And He repeated the verb (teaches you) to indicate that it is another category (جنس آخر).

(152) فَاذْكُرُونِي (152) So remember Me, (through obedience) أَذْكُرْكُمْ I will remember you, (with reward) وَاشْكُرُوا لِي and thank Me, (for what I have bestowed upon you) وَلا تَكْفُرُونِ and do not be ungrateful to Me. (by denying the blessings and disobeying the command). He intended by al-kufr (الكفر) the ingratitude for blessings (كفر النعم), as stated in al-Kafi (الكافي).

And in al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Qummi (القمّيّ), narrated from Imam al-Baqir (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both): The remembrance of Allah for the people of obedience is greater than their remembrance of Him. Do you not see that He says: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ. (So remember Me, I will remember you.)

And in al-Khisal (الخصال), narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Remember Allah in every place, for He is with you.

And in al-Kafi (الكافي), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: "O son of Adam, remember Me in an assembly (ملأ), and I will remember you in an assembly (ملأ) better than your assembly." And narrated from him (peace be upon him) in the tradition concerning Jesus (عيسى): "O Jesus, remember Me within yourself, and I will remember you within Myself. And remember Me in your assembly (ملئك), and I will remember you in an assembly (ملأ) better than the assembly of the children of Adam."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Verily, no one among His believing servants remembers Allah except that He remembers him with good. So, give Allah from yourselves diligence (الاجتهاد) in His obedience.

And in al-Majma' (المجمع) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The Prophet (may Allah's peace and blessings be upon him and his Household) said: "Verily, the angel descends the scroll (الصحيفة) at the beginning of the day and the beginning of the night, writing therein the deeds of the son of Adam. So, fill the beginning of it with good and the end of it with good, for Allah will forgive you what is between that, if He wills. For He says: فَاذْكُرُونِي أَذْكُرْكُمْ. (So remember Me, I will remember you.)

And in al-Khisal (الخصال), narrated from him (peace be upon him): Regarding affliction (البلاء) from Allah, patience (الصبر) is an obligation (فريضة); regarding decree (القضاء) from Allah, submission (التسليم) is an obligation (فريضة); and regarding blessing (النعمة) from Allah, gratitude (الشكر) is an obligation (فريضة).

And narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (السجّاد): Whoever says al-hamdulillah (الحمد لله) has fulfilled the gratitude for all of Allah's blessings.

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The gratitude for every blessing is piety (الورع) concerning what Allah has forbidden.

And in al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether gratitude (الشكر) has a limit (حدّ) such that if a man performs it, he is considered grateful (شاكراً). He said: "Yes." It was asked: "And what is it?" He said: "It is al-hamdulillah (الحمد لله) for every blessing He has bestowed upon me, and if He has a right concerning what He has bestowed upon him, he fulfills it." And from this is the saying of Allah: سُبْحانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنا هذا (Glory be to Him who has harnessed this for us) until he enumerated the verses (آيات).

(153) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اسْتَعِينُوا بِالصَّبْرِ (153) O you who have faith! Take recourse to patience against acts of disobedience (المعاصي) and the desires of the soul (حظوظ النفس) وَالصَّلاةِ and prayer. which is the mother of acts of worship (أم العبادات), the ascension (معراج) of the believers, and the intimate communion (مناجاة) with the Lord of the worlds إِنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ Indeed Allah is with the patient. with victory (بالنصر) and the answering of the supplication (إجابة الدعوة).

In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a discourse of His, He said: So, whoever endures patiently (صبر) out of compulsion (كرهاً), and does not complain to the creation (الخلق), and does not despair due to the tearing of his veil (هتك ستره), then he is from the general populace (العام), and his share is what Allah said: وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ (and give good news to the patient), meaning: with Paradise (بالجنة). And whoever receives the afflictions (البلايا) with acceptance (بالرّحب), and endures patiently (صبر) with tranquility (سكينة) and dignity (وقار), then he is from the elite (الخاص), and his share is what He said: إِنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ (Indeed Allah is with the patient).

(١٥٤) وَلا تَقُولُوا لِمَنْ يُقْتَلُ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَمْواتٌ (And do not call those who are slain in the way of Allah 'dead') meaning: they are dead بَلْ أَحْياءٌ (Rather, they are living) rather, they are living وَلكِنْ لا تَشْعُرُونَ (but you are not aware) of their state.

In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from Yunus ibn Zabyan, from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that he (the Imam) said to him: "What do the people say concerning the souls of the believers?" He said: "They say they are in the crops (حواصل) of green birds, in lamps beneath the Throne (العرش)." He (the Imam) said: "Glory be to Allah (سبحان الله)! The believer is more honored by Allah than that He should place his soul in the crop (حوصلة) of a bird. O Yunus, when that happens (i.e., when the believer dies), Muhammad, Ali, Fatima, al-Hasan, and al-Husayn (may the blessings of Allah be upon them all) and the Proximate Angels (الملائكة المقرّبون) come to him. When Allah the Almighty takes his soul, He transforms that soul into a mold (قالب) like his mold (قالب) in the world (الدنيا). So they eat and drink. When a newcomer arrives among them, they recognize him by that form (الصورة) which he had in the world (الدنيا)."

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the souls of the believers. He said: "They are in Paradise (الجنة) in the forms (صور) of their bodies (أبدانهم). If you were to see him, you would say: 'That is so-and-so.'"

(١٥٥) وَلَنَبْلُوَنَّكُمْ And We will surely afflict you with the affliction of the tester, (to see) whether you will be patient upon the tribulation and submit to the decree, ** بِشَيْءٍ مِنَ الْخَوْفِ وَالْجُوعِ وَنَقْصٍ مِنَ الْأَمْوالِ وَالْأَنْفُسِ وَالثَّمَراتِ وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ** (155) And We will surely test you with a measure of fear and hunger and a shortage of wealth, lives, and fruits. And give good news to the patient. meaning, with Paradise, as has passed (previously).

And in Nahj al-Balagha: Indeed, Allah tests His servants, when they commit evil deeds, with a shortage of fruits (نقص الثمرات), the withholding of blessings (حبس البركات), and the locking of the treasuries of goodness (خزائن الخيرات), so that a repentant person may repent, one who desists may desist, one who remembers may remember, and one who is deterred may be deterred.

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): That these are signs of the rising (قيام) of the Qā’im (القائم), which will be from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, for the believers. He said: ** بِشَيْءٍ مِنَ الْخَوْفِ (with a measure of fear) is from the kings of Banu Umayyah at the end of their dominion. And ** وَالْجُوعِ (and hunger) is due to the high prices they set. ** وَنَقْصٍ مِنَ الْأَمْوالِ (and a shortage of wealth) is the corruption of trade and the scarcity of surplus (الفضل). And ** وَنَقْصٍ مِنَ الْأَنْفُسِ (and a shortage of lives) is widespread death (الموت الذريع). And ** الثَّمَراتِ (and a shortage of fruits) is due to the scarcity of the yield (ريع) of what is sown. ** وَبَشِّرِ الصَّابِرِينَ (And give good news to the patient) at that time, with the hastening of the emergence (خروج) of the Qā’im (peace be upon him). Then he said: This is its interpretation (تأويله). Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: ** وَما يَعْلَمُ تَأْوِيلَهُ إِلَّا اللهُ وَالرَّاسِخُونَ فِي الْعِلْمِ**.

(١٥٦) الَّذِينَ إِذا أَصابَتْهُمْ مُصِيبَةٌ (156) those who, when an affliction visits them, In the Hadith: Everything that harms the believer is an affliction (مصيبة) for him. قالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجِعُونَ. say, 'Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed, to Him do we return.' In Nahj al-Balagha: Indeed, our saying إِنَّا لِلَّهِ (Indeed, we belong to Allah) is an acknowledgment (إقرار) of His ownership (الملك) over ourselves, and our saying ** إِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجِعُونَ (and indeed, to Him do we return) is an acknowledgment (إقرار) of our demise (الهلك) upon ourselves. And in al-Majma': Narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Whoever says istirja' (i.e., إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجِعُونَ) upon an affliction, Allah will mend his affliction, improve his outcome, and grant him a righteous successor (خلفاً صالحاً) that satisfies him. And He said: Whoever is afflicted by a calamity and performs istirja' (even if its time has passed), Allah writes for him the same reward (الأجر) as He wrote for him on the day he was afflicted. And in al-Kafi: Narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: There is no servant who is afflicted by a calamity, and performs istirja' when remembering the calamity, and is patient when it strikes him, except that Allah forgives him what has passed of his sin. And every time he remembers a calamity and performs istirja' upon remembering the calamity, Allah forgives him every sin between the two instances. And narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Whoever remembers an affliction, even after a time, and says: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجِعُونَ (Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed, to Him do we return) and الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds), [and then says]: "O Allah, reward me for my affliction and replace it for me with something better than it," he will have the same reward (الأجر) as he had at the first shock of the affliction. And in al-Khisal and al-Ayyashi: Narrated from him عليه السلام, from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Four characteristics (خصال)—whoever possesses them will be in the Greatest Light of Allah (نور الله الأعظم): (1) He whose safeguard (عصمة) in his affair is the testimony that there is no god except Allah and that I am the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم; (2) and he who, when an affliction visits him, says: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ (Indeed, we belong to Allah, and indeed, to Him do we return); (3) and he who, when he attains good, says: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ (All praise belongs to Allah); (4) and he who, when a sin (خطيئة) visits him, says: أستغفر الله وأتوب إليه (I seek forgiveness from Allah and I repent to Him).

(١٥٧) أُولئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ صَلَواتٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُهْتَدُونَ (157) Upon them are the blessings of their Lord and His mercy, and it is they who are the guided. It is said that al-salāh (الصلاة) from Allah means purification (التزكية), forgiveness (المغفرة), mercy (الرحمة), kindness (اللطف), and beneficence (الإحسان).

In al-Khisal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): Allah said: "I made the world (الدنيا) a bounty (فَيْضاً) among My servants. So, whoever lends Me a loan (قَرْضاً) from it, I will give him for every single one of them ten up to seven hundred fold, and whatever I wish beyond that. And whoever does not lend Me a loan from it, but I take it from him forcibly (قَسْراً), I grant him three qualities (خِصَال)—had I granted one of them to My angels, they would have been pleased: al-Salāh (الصلاة), al-Hidāyah (الهداية), and al-Rahmah (الرحمة)." Indeed, Allah Almighty says: ** الَّذِينَ إِذا أَصابَتْهُمْ مُصِيبَةٌ** (the verse).

(158) إِنَّ الصَّفا وَالْمَرْوَةَ ** (Indeed, [the hills of] Safa and Marwah) They are the signs (عَلَما) of two mountains in Mecca. ** مِنْ شَعائِرِ اللهِ (are among Allah’s sacraments.) [They are] among the signs (أعلام) of His rituals (مناسكه), being the plural of شعيرة (شَعِيرَة) which is the sign (العلامة). ** فَمَنْ حَجَّ الْبَيْتَ أَوِ اعْتَمَرَ (So whoever makes the Pilgrimage to the House, or performs the ʿUmrah,) Al-Hajj (الحجّ) linguistically means the intention (القصد), and al-I'timar (الاعتمار) means the visitation (الزيارة). They were then specialized legally (شرعاً) to mean the intention toward the House and its visitation in the two specific manners (الوجهين المخصوصين). فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِما (there is no sin upon him in circumambulating them.) Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Meaning, there is no hardship (حرج) ** عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِما (upon him in circumambulating them).

In al-Kafi (الكافي) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked whether the running (السعي) between al-Safa and al-Marwah was an obligation (فريضة) or a tradition (سنة). He said: "An obligation (فريضة)." It was said: "Did not Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, say: ** فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِما (there is no sin upon him in circumambulating them)?" He said: "That was concerning ʿUmrat al-Qada' (عمرة القضاء). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم stipulated to them that they should remove the idols (الأصنام) from al-Safa and al-Marwah. Then a man became preoccupied and missed the running (السعي) until the days passed and the idols were returned. They came to Him and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, so-and-so did not run between al-Safa and al-Marwah, and the idols have been returned.' So Allah, the Exalted, revealed: ** إِنَّ الصَّفا وَالْمَرْوَةَ ** (Indeed, [the hills of] Safa and Marwah) until His saying: ** فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَطَّوَّفَ بِهِما (there is no sin upon him in circumambulating them), meaning, while the idols were upon them."

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [narrated]: Quraysh had placed their idols between al-Safa and al-Marwah, and they would wipe themselves with them when they performed the running (سعوا). When the affair of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم during the Battle of al-Hudaybiyyah (غزوة الحديبيّة) occurred, and they prevented Him from the House, they stipulated to Him that they would vacate the House for Him in the following year so that He could complete His ʿUmrah for three days, and then depart from it. When ʿUmrat al-Qada' (عمرة القضاء) took place in the seventh year of the Hijra (الهجرة), He entered Mecca and said to Quraysh: "Remove your idols so that I may run (أسعى)." So they removed them. The narration is similar to that in al-Kafi (الكافي) with a slight difference (بأدنى تفاوت).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Muslims used to think that the running (السعي) between al-Safa and al-Marwah was something done by the polytheists (المشركون). So Allah revealed this verse. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The running (السعي) between al-Safa and al-Marwah was made a humiliation (مذلّة) for the arrogant tyrants (الجبّارين). وَمَنْ تَطَوَّعَ خَيْراً (And whoever volunteers good,) meaning, he increases the circumambulation (الطواف) or performs another act of obedience (طاعة اخرى). It was also recited with the ya' (الياء), the doubling (تشديد) of the ta' (الطاء), and the jazm (جزم) of the ayn (العين) [i.e., يَطَّوَّعْ]. فَإِنَّ اللهَ شاكِرٌ عَلِيمٌ (Allah is indeed thankful, all-knowing.) [Meaning, He is] rewarding for it, nothing is hidden from Him.

I say : The following verse and those after it, up to His saying, the Glorified: ** كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ (It is prescribed for you, when death approaches any of you), are among what was found of the commentary (تفسير) of Abu Muhammad al-Zaki (أبي محمّد الزكيّ). Our commentary (تفسيرنا) concerning them will be based upon it, just as it was concerning what preceded, wherever His commentary is found.

(١٥٩) The Almighty's saying: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ ما أَنْزَلْنا مِنَ الْبَيِّناتِ (Indeed those who conceal what We have sent down of manifest proofs) such as the Jewish scholars (أحبار اليهود) who conceal the verses (الآيات) testifying to the matter of Muhammad and Ali (peace be upon them both), their descriptions (نعتهما), and their characteristics (حليتهما); and like the Nawasib (النواصب) who conceal what was revealed concerning the virtue of Ali (peace be upon him). وَالْهُدى (and guidance) and everything that guides to the necessity of following them both and believing in them both. مِنْ بَعْدِ ما بَيَّنَّاهُ لِلنَّاسِ فِي الْكِتابِ (after We have clarified it for mankind in the Book) in the Tawrah (التوراة) and other [books]. أُولئِكَ يَلْعَنُهُمُ اللهُ وَيَلْعَنُهُمُ اللَّاعِنُونَ (they shall be cursed by Allah and cursed by the cursers) meaning those from whom the curse upon them proceeds, such as the angels (الملائكة) and the two weighty ones (الثّقلين) [mankind and jinn], even their own selves, for the disbelievers say: "May Allah curse the disbelievers."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: اللَّاعِنُونَ (the cursers), He said: "We are them." And they have also said [it refers to] the vermin of the earth (هوام الأرض).

And in al-Ihtijaj and Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him), in a place other than this: Abu Muhammad (peace be upon him) said: It was asked of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Who are the best of Allah's creation after the Imams of Guidance (أئمة الهدى) and the Lamps of Darkness (مصابيح الدّجى)?" He said: "The scholars (العلماء) when they are righteous." It was asked: "Then who are the worst of Allah's creation after Iblis, Pharaoh, and Thamud, and after those who assume your names (المتسمّين بأسمائكم), take your titles (المتلقّبين بألقابكم), seize your positions (الآخذين لأمكنتكم), and conspire in your kingdoms (المتآمرين في ممالككم)?" He said: "The scholars (العلماء) when they are corrupt. They are the ones who manifest falsehoods (الأباطيل) and conceal the truths (الحقائق). And concerning them, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: أُولئِكَ يَلْعَنُهُمُ اللهُ وَيَلْعَنُهُمُ اللَّاعِنُونَ."

And narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) that He said: "Whoever is asked about knowledge that he knows and conceals it, he will be bridled on the Day of Resurrection with a bridle of fire."

And in al-Qummi, narrated marfu'an (مرفوعاً) from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): "If innovations (البدع) appear in my Ummah (أمتي), the scholar (العالم) must manifest his knowledge. And whoever does not do so, upon him is the curse of Allah."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): A man came to Salman al-Farsi (may Allah be pleased with him) and said: "Narrate to me." So he remained silent concerning him. Then he returned, and he remained silent. Then he returned, and he remained silent. So the man turned away while reciting this verse: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ (Indeed those who conceal). So [Salman] said to him: "Come forward. If we found a trustworthy person (أميناً), we would narrate the tradition (الحديث) to him."

(١٦٠) ** إِلَّا الَّذِينَ تابُوا (160) Except those who repent, from concealment (الكتمان), وَأَصْلَحُوا and reform, what they corrupted by rectification (بالتدارك), ** وَبَيَّنُوا and clarify, what Allah mentioned concerning the description (نعت) and attribute (صفته) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and what the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم mentioned concerning the merit (فضل) and guardianship (ولايته) of Ali, so that their repentance may be complete. ** فَأُولئِكَ أَتُوبُ عَلَيْهِمْ ** then I will relent toward them, with acceptance (بالقبول) and forgiveness (والمغفرة). وَأَنَا التَّوَّابُ الرَّحِيمُ and I am the All-relenting, the All-merciful. the One Who is excessive (المبالغ) in accepting repentance (قبول التوبة) and pouring forth mercy (وإفاضة الرحمة).

(١٦١) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا in their rejection of the prophethood of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the ولاية of Ali عليه السلام ** وَماتُوا وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ أُولئِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةُ اللهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ** (Indeed, those who disbelieve and die while they are disbelievers—upon them shall be the curse of Allah and the angels and all mankind.) The distance from mercy has settled upon them.

(١٦٢) خَالِدِينَ فِيهَا (remaining in it forever) In the curse, in the Fire of Hell (جهنم). لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابُ (the punishment shall not be lightened for them) For a day nor an hour. وَلَا هُمْ يُنْظَرُونَ (nor will they be granted any respite) They will be granted respite (يمهلون).

(١٦٣) وَإِلَهُكُمْ إِلَهٌ وَاحِدٌ (Your God is the One God) It was said: meaning, the One among you Who is deserving of worship (العبادة) is one, having no partner who is rightful to be worshipped or to be called a god (إلهاً). لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ (there is no god except Him) This is an affirmation (تقرير) of the Oneness (الوحدانية) and a removal of the possibility that it might be imagined that there is a god (إلهاً) in existence, but that He is not deserving of worship (العبادة) from them. الرَّحْمَنُ الرَّحِيمُ (the All-beneficent, the All-merciful) Like the proof (الحجة) for it (i.e., for the Oneness).

(١٦٤) إِنَّ فِي خَلْقِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (Indeed, in the creation of the heavens and the earth) without pillars from beneath them to prevent them from falling, nor any tether from above them to restrain them from collapsing upon you. And you, O servants (العباد) and maidservants (الإماء), are captives (أسرى) in My grasp. The earth is beneath you; there is no refuge for you from it, wherever you flee, and the heaven is above you; there is no escape (محيص) for you from it, wherever you go. For if I will, I can destroy you by this [the earth], and if I will, I can destroy you by that [the heaven]. Then, what is in the heavens: the sun, illuminating your day so that you may disperse for your livelihoods (معايشكم), and the moon, shining in your night so that you may see in its darkness. And He compelled you, by resting in the darkness, to cease the continuous toil (الكدّ) which exhausts your bodies. ** وَاخْتِلافِ اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ (and the alternation of the night and the day) which follow one another, bringing upon you the wonders that your Lord brings forth in His world, such as granting happiness (إسعاد) and misery (اشقاء), granting might (إعزاز) and humiliation (إذلال), granting wealth (إغناء) and poverty (إفقار), summer and winter, autumn and spring, fertility (خصب) and drought (قحط), and fear and security. ** وَالْفُلْكِ الَّتِي تَجْرِي فِي الْبَحْرِ بِما يَنْفَعُ النَّاسَ (and the ships that sail in the sea with what benefits the people) which Allah made your mounts (مطاياكم), resting neither night nor day, and they do not demand fodder (علفاً) or water from you. And He sufficed you with the winds for the burden of moving them with your own strength, which would not be sufficient if the winds ceased to blow for them, for the completion of your interests and benefits, and for you to attain the needs for yourselves. وَما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ مِنَ السَّماءِ مِنْ ماءٍ (and the water that Allah sends down from the sky) as heavy rain (وابلاً), continuous downpour (هطلاً), and drizzle (رذاذاً). It does not descend upon you all at once, drowning you and destroying your livelihoods (معايشكم), but rather it descends gradually from above, until it covers the lowlands (الأوهاد), the hills (التلال), and the ravines (التلاع). ** فَأَحْيا بِهِ الْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها (and revives the earth with it after its death) bringing forth its vegetation, its grains, and its fruits. ** وَبَثَّ فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ دَابَّةٍ ** (and scatters in it every kind of animal) some of which are for your families and your livelihoods (معايشكم), and some of which are fierce beasts (سباع ضارية) that guard your livestock (أنعامكم) for you, lest they stray from you, out of fear that the beasts will prey upon them. ** وَتَصْرِيفِ الرِّياحِ (and the changing of the winds) which nurture your grains, bring your fruits to maturity, and dispel the stagnation of the air and the scarcity (الإقتار) from you. And it was recited with the singular form: الريح (al-rīḥ). وَالسَّحابِ الْمُسَخَّرِ (and the clouds disposed) the subjugated (المذلّل), standing ** بَيْنَ السَّماءِ وَالْأَرْضِ (between the sky and the earth) carrying its rains, moving by the permission of Allah, and pouring them down wherever He is commanded. لَآياتٍ (there are indeed signs) clear proofs ** لِقَوْمٍ يَعْقِلُونَ (for a people who apply reason) who reflect upon them with their intellects.

(165) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَتَّخِذُ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ أَنْداداً (165) Yet among the people are those who choose peers besides Allah (They are) idols, and leaders whom they obey. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, they are the followers (أولياء) of so-and-so and so-and-so, whom they took as Imams instead of the Imam Whom Allah appointed as an Imam for the people. That is why He said: وَلَوْ يَرَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا (the Ayah)." Then he (peace be upon him) said: "By Allah, they are the Imams of oppression (أئمة الظلم) and their adherents (أشياعهم)." يُحِبُّونَهُمْ كَحُبِّ اللهِ (They love them as they love Allah). It is said: meaning they glorify them and obey them like His glorification and the inclination towards His obedience, that is, they equate them with Him in love and obedience. ** وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَشَدُّ حُبًّا لِلَّهِ but the faithful have a more intense love for Allah. (They have a more intense love for Allah) than these people who take peers alongside Allah for their peers, because the believers see Lordship (الربوبية) and Power (القدرة) belonging to Allah, and they do not associate anything with Him, so their love is sincere for Him. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): They are the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them). I say: Meaning الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (those who have faith). The verification of the meaning of the love of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, will come in Surat Āl ‘Imrān during the exegesis of the Almighty's saying: قُلْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ اللهَ (Say, 'If you love Allah'), if Allah wills. وَلَوْ يَرَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا If only the wrongdoers could see (If only the wrongdoers could see) by taking idols as peers to Allah, the Glorified, and the disbelievers (الكفّار) and the wicked (الفجار) as examples for Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) and Ali (peace be upon him). And it was read with the tā’ (ت): إِذْ يَرَوْنَ الْعَذابَ ** (when they see the punishment), meaning when they see the punishment that befalls them due to their disbelief and obstinacy. And it was read with the ḍamm of the yā’ (بضمّ الياء). أَنَّ الْقُوَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعاً وَأَنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعَذابِ that all might belongs to Allah, and that Allah is severe in punishment. أَنَّ الْقُوَّةَ لِلَّهِ (that all might belongs to Allah). They know أَنَّ الْقُوَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعاً (that all might belongs to Allah), He punishes whom He wills and honors whom He wills, and the disbelievers have no might by which they can prevent His punishment. وَأَنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعَذابِ (and that Allah is severe in punishment). And they know أَنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعَذابِ. And it was said that the apodosis (جواب) of law (لو) is omitted, meaning: they would regret with the deepest regret.

(١٦٦) إِذْ تَبَرَّأَ الَّذِينَ اتُّبِعُوا (When those who were followed disown [their followers]) Meaning, if these people who took rivals (besides Allah) could see when the leaders disown those who followed them—the subjects (الرعايا) and the followers (الاتباع). ** وَرَأَوُا الْعَذابَ وَتَقَطَّعَتْ بِهِمُ الْأَسْبابُ (and they see the punishment, and all their ties are cut asunder) [Meaning] the connections (الوصلات) that were between them, through which they maintained contact, so their stratagem (حيلتهم) is exhausted, and they are unable to escape the punishment of Allah by any means.

(١٦٧) وَقالَ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوا ** The followers ** لَوْ أَنَّ لَنا كَرَّةً They wish that they had a return to the world ** فَنَتَبَرَّأَ مِنْهُمْ ** there كَما تَبَرَّؤُا مِنَّا here. (167) And those who followed will say, 'If only we had a return, we would disown them just as they disowned us.'

** كَذلِكَ Just as some of them disowned others, يُرِيهِمُ اللهُ أَعْمالَهُمْ حَسَراتٍ عَلَيْهِمْ** Thus does Allah show them their deeds as regrets for them. And that is because they worked in the world for other than Allah, or not according to the manner which Allah commanded, so they will see that their deeds have no reward, and they will see the deeds of others which were for Allah, whose reward Allah has magnified for their doers.

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: ** يُرِيهِمُ اللهُ أَعْمالَهُمْ حَسَراتٍ عَلَيْهِمْ (Thus does Allah show them their deeds as regrets for them). It is the man who leaves his wealth, not spending it in obedience to Allah out of stinginess (بُخْلاً), then he dies and leaves it to someone who uses it in obedience to Allah or disobedience to Allah. If he uses it in obedience to Allah, [the deceased] sees it in the scale of another, and thus sees it as a regret, even though the wealth belonged to him. And if he uses it in disobedience to Allah, [the deceased] strengthened him with that wealth until he used it in disobedience to Him, the Mighty and Majestic.

وَما هُمْ بِخارِجِينَ مِنَ النَّارِ (and they will not leave the Fire.) And their punishment will be eternal (سَرْمَداً دَائِماً) because their sins were disbelief (كُفْراً), and the intercession of no prophet, nor successor (وَصِيّ), nor the best of their good followers (خِيَارِ شِيعَتِهِمْ) will reach them.

** يا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ كُلُوا مِمَّا فِي الْأَرْضِ (O mankind, eat of what is in the earth) from the types of its fruits and its foods, ** حَلالاً طَيِّباً (lawful and good) for you, provided you obey your Lord concerning the veneration of those whom He has venerated, and the disdain for those whom He has disgraced and belittled. And it was said that it was revealed concerning a people who forbade themselves fine foods and clothing. ** وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ (and do not follow in the footsteps of Satan.) that which he steps toward with you and tempts you with, concerning disobedience to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Every oath sworn by other than Allah is among the footsteps of Satan (خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ).

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them both), regarding its meaning: إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ. (He is indeed your manifest enemy.)

(169) إِنَّما يَأْمُرُكُمْ بِالسُّوءِ وَالْفَحْشاءِ وَأَنْ تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللهِ ما لا تَعْلَمُونَ (He only commands you to [commit] evil and indecency, and to say about Allah what you do not know.) It was said: such as taking partners (الأنداد), making forbidden things lawful (تحليل المحرمات), and making good things forbidden (تحريم الطيبات). I say: In this is an indication of the absolute prohibition against following conjecture (الظنّ) in religious matters (المسائل الدينية). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Beware of two characteristics, for in them perished those who perished: beware of giving people religious rulings (تفتي) based on your opinion (رأيك) and practicing a religion (تدين) based on what you do not know." And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the right of Allah, the Almighty, upon the servants. He said: "That they say what they know and stop at what they do not know."

(١٧٠) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اتَّبِعُوا ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ (170) And when they are told, 'Follow what Allah has sent down,'

(in His Book). It is said that the pronoun refers to the people (الناس), and the address shifted from addressing them directly to calling out concerning their misguidance, as if He turned to the intelligent ones (العقلاء) and said to them: "Look at these fools (الحمقى), what do they answer?" قالُوا بَلْ نَتَّبِعُ ما أَلْفَيْنا عَلَيْهِ آباءَنا they say, 'No, but we follow what we found our fathers following.' "What we found our fathers following is sufficient for us" (referring to the religion (الدين) and the school of thought (المذهب)). أَوَلَوْ كانَ آباؤُهُمْ لا يَعْقِلُونَ شَيْئاً وَلا يَهْتَدُونَ ** What, even if their fathers did not comprehend anything and were not guided? (and were not guided) to the truth (الحق) and the correctness (الصواب).

I say: In this is proof of the obligation to employ insight (البصيرة), even in knowing whom one imitates (يقلّده).

(١٧١) وَمَثَلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي يَنْعِقُ بِما لا يَسْمَعُ إِلَّا دُعاءً وَنِداءً (171) The parable of the faithless is that of one who yells at something that hears nothing except a call and a cry.

وَمَثَلُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (The parable of the faithless) in their worship of idols (الأصنام) and their taking of rivals (الأنداد) besides Muhammad and Ali ** كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي يَنْعِقُ (is that of one who yells) meaning, who calls out ** بِما لا يَسْمَعُ (at something that hears nothing) from him, ** إِلَّا دُعاءً وَنِداءً (except a call and a cry), [meaning, the one being called] does not understand what is intended from him, so that he might aid the one seeking aid (المستغيث) or assist the one who seeks his help (استعانه).

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Meaning, their parable in your calling them to faith is like the parable of the caller (الناعق) in his calling of the livestock (البهائم) being called, which does not understand, but only hears the sound.

أقول : I say: What is meant by this is that the parable of the one who calls them is like the parable of the one who calls livestock (البهائم). For, due to their immersion in blind imitation (التقليد), they do not pay attention to what is recited to them, nor do they reflect upon what is established with them. Thus, in this regard, they are like the livestock (البهائم) that is called out to, so it hears the sound but does not know its purpose (مغزاه), and perceives the cry (النداء) but does not understand its meaning. This meaning, despite its need for implied context (الإضمار), is clearer than the first [interpretation], because idols (الأصنام) neither hear a call (دعاء) nor a cry (نداء), just as they do not understand what is intended from them. This is unless that [first interpretation] is considered to be from the category of the compound similitude (التمثيل المركب), or unless their taking of rivals (الأنداد) mentioned in the hadith is made an interpretation of their worship of idols (الأصنام), and by both the rivals (الأنداد) and the idols (الأصنام), the leaders of misguidance (أئمة الضلال) are intended.

صُمٌّ بُكْمٌ عُمْيٌ فَهُمْ لا يَعْقِلُونَ (They are) deaf, dumb, and blind; so they do not apply reason.

صُمٌّ بُكْمٌ عُمْيٌ (They are deaf, dumb, and blind) regarding guidance (الهدى) ** فَهُمْ لا يَعْقِلُونَ** (so they do not apply reason) to the command of Allah, the Glorious.

The Almighty's saying: ** يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُلُوا مِنْ طَيِّباتِ ما رَزَقْناكُمْ وَاشْكُرُوا لِلَّهِ (O you who have faith! Eat of the good things that We have provided you, and thank Allah) for what He has provided you and made lawful for you, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ إِيَّاهُ تَعْبُدُونَ**. (if it is Him that you worship.)

I say: Meaning: thank Him for His blessings if it is true that you dedicate worship exclusively to Him and acknowledge that He is the Master (مولى) of blessings. For His worship is not complete except through gratitude (الشكر), by believing that the blessing is from Allah, by utilizing the blessings for the purpose for which they were created, and by praising Allah with your tongues. And it is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Allah the Almighty says: "I and the jinn and mankind are engaged in a great matter (نبأ عظيم): I create, yet others are worshipped; and I provide sustenance, yet others are thanked."

(١٧٣) إِنَّما حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةَ (He has only forbidden you the carrion) which died a natural death (حتف أنفها) without ritual slaughter (ذباحة) from the aspect that Allah permitted it (i.e., permitted the prohibition) ** وَالدَّمَ وَلَحْمَ الْخِنْزِيرِ وَما أُهِلَّ بِهِ لِغَيْرِ اللهِ (and blood, and the flesh of swine, and that over which other than Allah has been invoked.) That over which the name of other than Allah was mentioned from among the slaughtered animals (الذبائح), and these are the ones with which the disbelievers (الكفّار) seek closeness (تتقرب) using the names of their rivals (أندادهم) whom they took besides Allah ** فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ (But should someone be compelled) to something from these forbidden things ** غَيْرَ باغٍ وَلا عادٍ (without being rebellious or aggressive,) And he is not rebellious (باغٍ) during necessity against an Imam of guidance (إمام هدى), nor aggressive (معتد) speaking falsehood (قوّال بالباطل) regarding the prophethood of one who is not a prophet, or the Imamate of one who is not an Imam.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The rebellious one (الباغي) is he who rebels against the Imam (الامام), and the aggressive one (العادي) is he who cuts off the road (يقطع الطريق). Carrion (الميتة) is not lawful for them both.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that which is similar in meaning. And in one narration: The rebellious one (الباغي) is the oppressor (الظالم), and the aggressive one (العادي) is the usurper (الغاصب).

In al-Tahdhib and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The rebellious one (الباغي) is the seeker of game (باغي الصيد), and the aggressive one (العادي) is the thief (السارق). It is not permissible for them both to eat carrion (الميتة) if they are compelled. It is forbidden for them both; it is not permissible for them as it is for the Muslims.

And in it (al-Tahdhib) and in al-Faqih, narrated from al-Jawad, from his father, from his forefathers (peace be upon them): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked, and it was said to Him: "We are in the land of Iraq, and famine (المخمصة) afflicts us. When does carrion (الميتة) become lawful for us?" He said: "As long as you have not had breakfast (تصطبحوا), or dinner (تغتبقوا), or carried vegetables (تحتقبوا بقلاً), then this is your concern (فشأنكم بهذا)." ‘Abd al-Azim said: "So I said to him, O son of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, what is the meaning of the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ غَيْرَ باغٍ وَلا عادٍ (But should someone be compelled without being rebellious or aggressive)?" He replied: "The aggressive one (العادي) is the thief (السارق), and the rebellious one (الباغي) is he who seeks game (يبغي الصيد) out of insolence (بطراً) and amusement (لهواً), not to benefit his family (عياله) with it. It is not permissible for them both to eat carrion (الميتة) if they are compelled. It is forbidden for them both in the state of necessity (الاضطرار), just as it is forbidden for them both in the state of choice (الاختيار). Nor is it permissible for them both to shorten the fast (صوم) or the prayer (صلاة) during travel." (The rest of) the narration (الحديث).

فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (there will be no sin upon him.) in consuming these things. ** إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ ** (Indeed Allah is all-forgiving,) a Coverer (ستار) of your faults ** رَحِيمٌ (all-merciful.) towards you when He made lawful for you in necessity what He forbade for you in ease (الرخاء).

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever is compelled (اضطر) to eat carrion (الميتة), blood (الدم), and the flesh of swine (لحم الخنزير), but does not eat anything of that until he dies, then he is a disbeliever (كافر).

(١٧٤) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْتُمُونَ ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ مِنَ الْكِتابِ وَيَشْتَرُونَ بِهِ ثَمَناً قَلِيلاً (174) Indeed those who conceal what Allah has sent down of the Book, and sell it for a paltry gain, a meager worldly commodity, and they attain through it leadership among the ignorant in this world. أُولئِكَ ما يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ those who do not eat into their bellies It is said: meaning, the fullness of their bellies. It is said: he ate in his belly, and he ate in part of his belly. And in the Hadith: "Eat in part of your belly, you will be healthy (تَعِفُّوا)." إِلَّا النَّارَ but fire, instead of their attainment of the meager worldly gain, due to their concealing the truth. وَلا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ and Allah will not speak to them on the Day of Resurrection, with a word of goodness, rather He curses them and disgraces them. And it is said that this is an allusion (كناية) to His Almighty wrath upon them, and an indication of their deprivation from closeness (الزلفى) to Allah. وَلا يُزَكِّيهِمْ nor will He purify them, from their sins. It is said: nor will He praise them. ** وَلَهُمْ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ and there is a painful punishment for them. painful (موجع) in the Fire.

(175) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ اشْتَرَوُا الضَّلالَةَ بِالْهُدى (They are the ones who have bought error for guidance) in this world (الدنيا) وَالْعَذابَ بِالْمَغْفِرَةِ (and torment for forgiveness) in the Hereafter (الآخرة), by concealing the truth for worldly purposes (الأغراض الدنيوية). فَما أَصْبَرَهُمْ عَلَى النَّارِ (How enduring they are of the Fire!) How daring (أجرأهم) they are regarding an action that necessitates the torment of the Fire upon them.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi: How enduring they are regarding an action which they know will lead them to the Fire.

In al-Majma': How much they perform the deeds (أعمال) of the people of the Fire.

And al-Qummi: How daring (أجرأهم) they are regarding the Fire, all of it, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

(١٧٦) ذلِكَ العذاب بِأَنَّ اللهَ نَزَّلَ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِ (176) That [is] because Allah has sent down the Book with the truth. meaning: what they are promised (of punishment) will afflict them and will not miss them. And it was said: He sent it down with the truth, but they rejected it through denial and concealment. وَإِنَّ الَّذِينَ اخْتَلَفُوا فِي الْكِتابِ And indeed those who differ concerning the Book by some of them saying it is sorcery (سحر), and another saying that it is poetry (شعر), and another saying that it is divination (كهانة), and so on. لَفِي شِقاقٍ خلاف بَعِيدٍ are certainly in a long schism. (disagreement (خلاف)) far from the truth. It is as if the truth is on one side (شق) and they are on another side (شق) besides it, which opposes it.

(١٧٧) لَيْسَ الْبِرَّ The pleasing action (الفعل المرضي). It was recited with the accusative case (بالنصب): أَنْ تُوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ قِبَلَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ (Righteousness is) not that you turn your faces to the east or the west.

(The turning of faces) towards the Ka'bah. It is said that this was a rebuttal to those among the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) who engaged excessively in discussing the matter of the Qiblah when it was changed to the Ka'bah, with every faction claiming that righteousness (البر) was turning towards their Qiblah. The East (المشرق) is the Qiblah of the Christians (النصارى), and the West (المغرب) is the Qiblah of the Jews (اليهود).

In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): The Jews said: "We have prayed many prayers towards this Qiblah of ours, and among us are those who revive the night in prayer towards it, and it is the Qiblah of Moses (موسى) with which we were commanded." And the Christians said: "We have prayed many prayers towards this Qiblah of ours, and among us are those who revive the night in prayer towards it, and it is the Qiblah of Jesus (عيسى) with which we were commanded." And each of the two factions said: "Do you think our Lord will invalidate these many deeds of ours and our prayers towards our Qiblah because we do not follow Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم according to his desire concerning himself and his brother?" So Allah revealed: "O Muhammad, say: لَيْسَ الْبِرَّ"

The obedience (الطاعة) by which you attain the Gardens (الجنان) and deserve forgiveness (الغفران) and pleasure (الرضوان): أَنْ تُوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ (that you turn your faces) in your prayers قِبَلَ الْمَشْرِقِ ** (to the east) O Christians (النصارى), and towards الْمَغْرِبِ (or the west) O Jews (اليهود), while you are disobedient to the command of Allah and enraged against the friend (ولي) of Allah.

وَلكِنَّ الْبِرَّ (but righteousness is) It was recited with the lightening (تخفيف) of لكن and the nominative case (رفع) of البر. مَنْ آمَنَ [that of] those who have faith

It is said that this means the righteousness (البر) that ought to be prioritized is the righteousness of those who have faith بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ وَالْكِتابِ وَالنَّبِيِّينَ (in Allah and the Last Day and the angels and the Book and the prophets), وَآتَى الْمالَ عَلى حُبِّهِ (and give away wealth for the love of Him). He gave, for the sake of Allah the Almighty, to the deserving among the believers, despite his love for the wealth and his severe need for it, hoping for life and fearing poverty, because he is healthy (صحيح) yet stingy (شحيح). ذَوِي الْقُرْبى (to relatives). He gave to the poor relatives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم as a gift (هدية) and an act of righteousness (برّاً), not as Sadaqah (صدقة), because Allah has exalted them above Sadaqah. And he gave to his own relatives as Sadaqah and an act of righteousness (برّاً). وَالْيَتامى ** (and the orphans). To the poor orphans of Banu Hashim (بني هاشم) as an act of righteousness (برّاً), not Sadaqah. And to orphans other than them as Sadaqah and connection (صلة). وَالْمَساكِينَ (and the needy)—the needy among the people— وَابْنَ السَّبِيلِ (and the traveller) who is passing through and cut off, having no provision (نفقة) with him. وَالسَّائِلِينَ ** (and the beggars)—those who beg (يتكفّفون)— وَفِي الرِّقابِ (and for [the freeing of] the slaves), meaning in freeing them, i.e., the Mukatabin (المكاتبين), helping them so they may fulfill their obligations and be freed. وَأَقامَ الصَّلاةَ (and establish the prayer) with its limits (بحدودها) وَآتَى الزَّكاةَ (and pay the zakat)—the obligatory one (الواجبة) upon him for his believing brothers. وَالْمُوفُونَ بِعَهْدِهِمْ إِذا عاهَدُوا (and those who fulfill their promise when they make a promise). It is said that this is an ‘Atf (عطف) (conjunction) upon مَنْ آمَنَ (those who have faith), encompassing the covenant of Allah and the covenant of people. وَالصَّابِرِينَ ** (and the patient). This is in the accusative case (نصبه) due to praise (على المدح), and it was not conjoined (عطف) due to the superiority of patience (الصبر) over all other deeds. فِي الْبَأْساءِ (in distress), meaning in fighting the enemies. And there is no enemy more hostile to fight than Iblis (إبليس) and his rebellious ones (مردته). He calls upon Him and repels him and them by sending blessings (الصلاة) upon Muhammad and his pure progeny (آله الطيبين). وَالضَّرَّاءِ (and hardship)—poverty (الفقر) and severity (الشدة). And there is no poverty more severe than the poverty of a believer who is forced to beg (التكفف) from the enemies of the progeny of Muhammad (peace be upon them). He remains patient in this and considers what he takes from their wealth as spoils (مغنماً), cursing them with it, and using what he takes to renew the remembrance of the guardianship (ولاية) of the pure, purified ones (الطّيبين الطاهرين). وَحِينَ الْبَأْسِ (and in the time of battle). At the severity of fighting, he remembers Allah and sends blessings (يصلي) upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and upon Ali (علي), the friend (ولي) of Allah. He befriends the friends of Allah with his heart and tongue, and likewise, he shows enmity towards the enemies of Allah. أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ صَدَقُوا (These are the ones who are truthful) in their faith and who confirm their sayings with their actions. وَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الْمُتَّقُونَ (and it is they who are the Godwary) regarding what they were commanded to guard against (اتقائه).

It is said that the Ayah, as you see, is comprehensive of all human perfections (الكمالات الانسانية), indicating them explicitly or implicitly. For these perfections, despite their multiplicity and dispersion, are confined to three things: soundness of belief (صحة الاعتقاد), excellence of social conduct (حسن المعاشرة), and refinement of the soul (تهذيب النفس). The first is alluded to by His saying: مَنْ آمَنَ up to... وَالنَّبِيِّينَ. The second is alluded to by His saying: وَآتَى الْمالَ up to وَفِي الرِّقابِ. And the third is alluded to by His saying: وَأَقامَ الصَّلاةَ until the end of the Ayah. For this reason, the one who combines these qualities is described as truthful (بالصدق) with respect to his faith and belief, and as God-wary (بالتقوى) with respect to his social conduct with creation and his dealings with the Truth (الحق). To this, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم alluded when He said: "Whoever acts upon this Ayah has perfected faith (الايمان)."

(١٧٨) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ (O you who have faith! Retaliation is prescribed for you in the matter of the slain:) It was said: meaning, it was decreed (فُرِضَ) and made obligatory (أُوجِبَ) upon you. الْقِصاصُ فِي الْقَتْلى ** (Retaliation in the matter of the slain) means equality (المساواة), and that the killer should be treated in the same manner as the slain person was treated by him when he killed him. ** الْحُرُّ بِالْحُرِّ وَالْعَبْدُ بِالْعَبْدِ وَالْأُنْثى بِالْأُنْثى. (the free man for the free man, and the slave for the slave, and the female for the female.)

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This (ruling) is for the community of Muslims; it is not exclusively for the believers (المؤمنين).

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): A free man is not killed for a slave, but he is severely beaten and must pay the blood money (دية) of the slave. Nor is a man killed for a woman, unless her family pays half of his blood money (ديته) to his family.

And in al-Ayyashi, what is similar in meaning: It was said that there were blood feuds between two tribes of the Arabs, and one of them had superiority (طول) over the other. So they swore that they would kill a free man for a slave, a male for a female, and two men for one man. When Islam came, they sought judgment from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and this (verse) was revealed. So He commanded them to be equal (يتكافئوا). ** فَمَنْ عُفِيَ لَهُ ** (But if anyone is pardoned by his brother) meaning, the offender (الجاني) who is pardoned by his brother (أَخِيهِ), who is the guardian of the blood (ولي الدم). It was said that the term of brotherhood (الأخوة) was mentioned so that one of them might show compassion (يعطف) toward his companion by mentioning the brotherhood of Islam (أخوة الإسلام) which is established between them. ** شَيْءٌ ** (anything) of the pardon, which is the pardon from retaliation (القصاص) without the blood money (الديّة). ** فَاتِّباعٌ (then there should be a fair pursuit) then let there be a pursuit (اتباع) from the pardoner (العافي), meaning, a demand for the blood money (الدية) ** بِالْمَعْرُوفِ ** (with fairness), and this is an injunction to the guardian (الولي) that he should demand the blood money with fairness (بالمعروف), by not wronging the offender (الجاني) with an increase (in demand) nor treating him harshly (يعنّفه). ** وَأَداءٌ إِلَيْهِ (and a discharge to him) from the offender (الجاني) to the pardoner (العافي) ** بِإِحْسانٍ (with goodness), an injunction to the offender (الجاني) that he should not procrastinate (يماطله) nor diminish his right, but rather thank him for his pardon (عفوه).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is proper for the one who has the right (الحق) not to make things difficult (يعسر) for his brother if he has agreed to a settlement (صالحه) for blood money (دية). And it is proper for the one upon whom the right rests not to procrastinate (يمطل) with his brother if he is able to give him what is due, and he should discharge it to him with goodness (بإحسان). ذلِكَ (That) meaning, this choice (التخيير) ** تَخْفِيفٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَرَحْمَةٌ (is a remission from your Lord and a mercy), because of the facilitation (التسهيل) and benefit (النفع) contained within it. For if there were only killing and pardon, the soul of the guardian of the slain would rarely be content with pardoning without receiving compensation (عوض). Thus, the killer would rarely escape being killed. In al-Awali, it is narrated: Retaliation (القصاص) was mandatory (حتماً) in the law (شرع) of Moses, and blood money (الدية) was mandatory (حتماً) in the law (شرع) of Jesus. Then the tolerant Hanifiyyah (الحنفية السّمحة) came, permitting both matters together. It was said that the Jews were prescribed retaliation alone, and the Christians were prescribed absolute pardon, while this Ummah (الأمة) was given the choice between the two and the blood money (الدية), as a facilitation (تيسيراً) for them. فَمَنِ اعْتَدى بَعْدَ ذلِكَ (But whoever transgresses after that) by accepting the blood money (الدية), or pardoning, or settling, and then later comes and mutilates (يمثل) or kills (the offender)—as narrated in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him)—فَلَهُ عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ** (there shall be a painful punishment for him).

(179) وَلَكُمْ O community (أمة) of Muhammad ** فِي الْقِصاصِ حَياةٌ (179) And there is life for you in retribution, O men of understanding! Because whoever intends (همّ) to commit murder, and knows that retribution (يقتصّ) will be taken from him, and therefore refrains (فكفّ) from murder, it is life for the one he intended to kill, and life for this offender (الجاني) who wanted to kill, and life for others besides them among the people, when they know that retribution (القصاص) is obligatory (واجب), they will not dare (لا يجترءون) to commit murder out of fear (مخافة) of retribution (القصاص). It was said: This is among the most concise (أوجز) and eloquent (أفصحه) speech.

And in al-Amali, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: There are four things I said, and Allah sent down confirmation (تصديقي) of them in His Book. He counted among them: I said: Killing reduces killing (القتل يقلّ القتل). Then the Almighty Allah sent down: ** وَلَكُمْ فِي الْقِصاصِ حَياةٌ يا أُولِي الْأَلْبابِ (179) And there is life for you in retribution, O men of understanding! (O possessors of intellects (العقول)). It was said: He called upon them to reflect upon the wisdom (حكمة) of retribution (القصاص), which involves preserving lives (استبقاء الأرواح) and safeguarding souls (حفظ النفوس). لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ.** (179) so that you may be Godwary.

(180) كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذَا حَضَرَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ (180) It is prescribed for you that when death approaches one of you, when its causes are present and its signs (أمارات) appear, إِنْ تَرَكَ خَيْراً if he leaves behind any property, abundant wealth (مالاً كثيراً). In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He entered upon a mawla (مولى) of his during his illness, and the mawla had seven hundred dirhams (درهم) or six hundred dirhams. The mawla said: "Should I not make a bequest (أوصي)?" He (peace be upon him) said: "No. Allah only said: إِنْ تَرَكَ خَيْراً (if he leaves behind any property), and you do not possess abundant wealth (كثير مال)." الْوَصِيَّةُ لِلْوَالِدَيْنِ وَالْأَقْرَبِينَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ the testament is for the parents and the relatives, in an honourable manner— with the thing (بالشيء) which the intellect knows that there is no injustice (جور) in it, nor deviation (جنف)— حَقًّا عَلَى الْمُتَّقِينَ a duty on the Godwary. Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشي): Narrated from one of the two Imams (أحدهما): It (this verse) is abrogated (منسوخة) by the verse of inheritance (آية المواريث), and it was interpreted as being said out of dissimulation (التقية) because it conforms to the doctrine of the general populace (مذهب العامّة) and contradicts the Quran. And due to what is in al-Kafi (الكافي) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about making a bequest (الوصية) to an heir (للوارث), and He said: "It is permissible (تجوز)," then He recited this verse. There are many other narrations (أخبار أُخر كثيرة) with this meaning. I say: The abrogation of obligation (الوجوب) does not negate the persistence of permissibility (الجواز). In al-Majma' (المجمع) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them) who said: "Whoever does not make a bequest (يوص) at the time of his death to his relatives (ذوي قرابته) who do not inherit, has concluded his deed with an act of disobedience (معصية)." In al-Faqih (الفقيه) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is something which Allah the Almighty has appointed for the Master of this Affair (صاحب هذا الأمر). He was asked: "Is there a limit (حدّ) for that?" He said: "The minimum that it can be is one-third of the third (ثلث الثلث)." And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is a right (حقّ) which Allah has placed in the wealth (أموال) of the people for the Master of this Affair (صاحب هذا الأمر). He was asked: "Is there a defined limit (حدّ محدود) for that?" He said: "Yes." He was asked: "How much?" He said: "Its minimum is one-sixth (السدس) and its maximum is one-third (الثلث)."

(١٨١) فَمَنْ بَدَّلَهُ بَعْدَ ما سَمِعَهُ فَإِنَّما إِثْمُهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ يُبَدِّلُونَهُ إِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (But whoever changes it after hearing it, its sin shall only be on those who change it. Indeed, Allah is All-hearing, All-knowing.)

A warning for the one who alters (the will) unjustly.

In al-Kafi, narrated from one of the two (Imams) (peace be upon them both), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), regarding a man who bequeathed his wealth for the way of Allah (في سبيل الله). He (the Imam) said: "Give it to the one for whom he bequeathed it, even if he was a Jew or a Christian. Indeed, Allah says," and He recited this verse. There are many narrations (أخبار) with this meaning, and in several of them (it is mentioned) that he must pay compensation (يغرمها) if he violates (the will).

(١٨٢) فَمَنْ خافَ مِنْ مُوصٍ (So whoever fears from a testator) And it was recited with the fathah on the waw and the shaddah on the sad (مُوَصٍّ), meaning he expected and knew: جَنَفاً أَوْ إِثْماً (a bias or a sin) meaning a deviation from the truth (الحق), whether by mistake (بالخطإ) or intentionally (بالتعمد). This is according to al-Majma' narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him).

And in al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means when he transgresses in the bequest (الوصية).

And al-Ayyashi added: beyond the third (على الثلث). And another meaning will come for it.

And in al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, bias (الجنف) in a bequest is among the major sins (الكبائر). فَأَصْلَحَ بَيْنَهُمْ (and sets things right between them) between the heirs (الورثة) and the beneficiaries (الموصي لهم) فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (there will be no sin upon him) regarding the alteration, because it is an alteration of falsehood (باطل) to truth (الحق). إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.) This is a promise for the one who sets things right, and the mention of forgiveness (المغفرة) is to correspond with the mention of sin (الإثم).

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of Allah, the Almighty: فَمَنْ بَدَّلَهُ (So whoever alters it). He said: The verse that follows it abrogated it: فَمَنْ خافَ مِنْ مُوصٍ جَنَفاً أَوْ إِثْماً فَأَصْلَحَ بَيْنَهُمْ فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (So whoever fears from a testator a bias or a sin, and sets things right between them, there will be no sin upon him). He said: It means the executor (الموصي إليه). If he fears a bias (جنفاً) from the testator (الموصي) in what he bequeathed to him, concerning that which Allah is not pleased with, being contrary to the truth (خلاف الحق), then there is no sin upon the executor (الموصى إليه) if he restores it to the truth (الحق) and to what Allah is pleased with, from the path of goodness (سبيل الخير).

And in a narration in al-Kafi: Indeed, Allah has permitted the executor (الموصى إليه) to change the bequest if it was not according to what is right (بالمعروف) and contained bias (جنف), and to restore it to what is right (المعروف), due to His saying, the Almighty: فَمَنْ خافَ مِنْ مُوصٍ جَنَفاً أَوْ إِثْماً فَأَصْلَحَ بَيْنَهُمْ فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (So whoever fears from a testator a bias or a sin, and sets things right between them, there will be no sin upon him.) And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): If a man makes a bequest (وصية), it is not lawful for the executor (الوصي) to change his bequest; rather, he must execute it as he bequeathed, unless he bequeathed something other than what Allah commanded, thereby disobeying in the bequest and committing injustice (يظلم). In that case, it is permissible for the executor (الموصى إليه) to restore it to the truth (الحق).
