For example, a man who has heirs (ورثة) and makes all the wealth for some of his heirs and deprives others of it. It is permissible for the executor (الوصي) to restore it to the truth. And that is His saying, the Almighty: جَنَفاً أَوْ إِثْماً (a bias or a sin.) So, bias (الجنف) is the inclination toward some of your heirs to the exclusion of others. And sin (الإثم) is that you command the construction of fire temples (بيوت النيران) or the acquisition of intoxicants (المسكر). In this case, it is lawful for the executor (الوصي) not to act upon any of that.

(183) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الصِّيامُ (183) O you who have faith! Fasting is prescribed for you

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse and about His saying, the Glorified: كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْقِتالُ (Fighting is prescribed for you). He said: "All of this encompasses the misguided (الضلال), the hypocrites (المنافقين), and everyone who affirmed the apparent call (الدعوة الظاهرة)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "The pleasure of the call (النداء) removed the fatigue of worship (العبادة) and the hardship (العناء)." كَما كُتِبَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ (just as it was prescribed for those who were before you) from the Prophets (الأنبياء) and the nations (الأمم). And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "The first of them was Adam." Meaning: it is an ancient worship (عبادة قديمة); Allah has not left any nation devoid of its obligation upon them. He did not reveal it upon you alone.

And in this, there is encouragement toward the action and comfort for the soul. لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (so that you may be Godwary) of sins (المعاصي), for fasting breaks the desire (الشهوة) which is the greatest of its causes. And in the Hadith: "Whoever is unable to afford marriage (الباه) should fast, for fasting is a shield (وجاء) for him."

(١٨٤) أَيَّاماً مَعْدُوداتٍ (A number of days) It was said: meaning few (قلائل), for the few are counted by counting (يعد عداً) while the many are poured out (يهال هيلاً), or meaning fixed by a known number.

فَمَنْ كانَ مِنْكُمْ مَرِيضاً ** (But should any of you be sick) [Meaning] a sickness where fasting harms him and causes hardship (يعسر), as is indicated by the Almighty's saying: وَلا يُرِيدُ بِكُمُ الْعُسْرَ (and He does not desire hardship for you) أَوْ عَلى سَفَرٍ (or on a journey) [Meaning] one undertaking a journey (راكب سفر).

فَعِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ ** (then [he shall fast] the same number of other days) So upon him is the same number of other days. This is an explicit text regarding the obligation of breaking the fast (وجوب الإفطار) for the sick person and the traveler, as has been narrated from our Imams (peace be upon them) in many reports, until they said: "The one who fasts Ramadan while traveling is like the one who breaks the fast while resident." This was narrated in al-Kafi, al-Tahdhib, and al-Faqih.

And in the three [books], in the narration of al-Zuhri from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): "Whoever fasts while traveling or sick, upon him is the making up [of the fast] (القضاء), because Allah the Almighty says: فَمَنْ كانَ مِنْكُمْ مَرِيضاً أَوْ عَلى سَفَرٍ فَعِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ (But should any of you be sick or on a journey, then [he shall fast] the same number of other days)." And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله called a people who fasted when they should have broken the fast and shortened [the prayer] (قصر) 'disobedient ones' (عصاة). He said: 'And they are the disobedient ones until the Day of Resurrection, and we recognize their sons and the sons of their sons until this very day of ours.'"

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about someone who fasted while traveling. He said: "If it reached him that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم forbade that, then upon him is the making up [of the fast] (القضاء). But if it did not reach him, then there is nothing upon him." In another narration: "And if he fasted it out of ignorance (بجهالة), he does not have to make it up (لم يقض)."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked: "What is the limit (حد) of the sickness during which a man breaks the fast and leaves praying while standing?" He said: بَلِ الْإِنْسانُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ ٠ بَصِيرَةٌ (Rather man is a witness to himself) And He is more knowledgeable of what he can bear (بما يطيقه).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "He is entrusted with it (مؤتمن عليه), delegated to it (مفوّض إليه). If he finds weakness (ضعفاً), let him break the fast, and if he finds strength (قوة), let him fast, regardless of the nature of the sickness (كان المريض على ما كان)."

And in it (al-Kafi): He (peace be upon him) was asked about the limit (حدّ) of the sickness due to which fasting is abandoned. He said: "If he is unable to eat the pre-dawn meal (يتسحّر)."

And in al-Faqih, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "The fasting person, if he fears for his eyes due to ophthalmia (الرمد), should break the fast." And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "Whenever fasting harms him, breaking the fast is obligatory (واجب) for him."

As for the limit (حدّ) of the journey during which one breaks the fast, it is the intention of traveling eight farasikh (فراسخ) or more, either going or including the return, provided his journey is not interrupted before that distance by the intention of residing for ten days, or by the passing of thirty days while he is hesitant (متردداً) in a town, or by arriving at a town where he has a residence (منزل) in which he resides for six months. If it is interrupted by any of these, then it has become two journeys with a period of residence (حضور) between them. And [the condition is] that traveling is not his occupation (عمله), unless the journey becomes strenuous for him and causes him severe hardship (مشقّة شديدة). And that the journey must be permissible (جائزاً) for him, and that he must be hidden from the walls of the town or its call to prayer (أذانه) must be concealed from him. This is what we have derived from the reports of our Imams (peace be upon them) regarding the conditions of the journey that necessitates breaking the fast in fasting and shortening the prayer (التقصير في الصلاة). We have clarified this in our book called al-Wafi (الوافي); whoever wishes to examine it should refer to it.

وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ يُطِيقُونَهُ (And those who can afford it) If they break the fast, فِدْيَةٌ طَعامُ مِسْكِينٍ (must pay a ransom, feeding a needy person) In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Food for needy persons (طعام مساكين)," and He recited it thus.

It was said that the one capable of fasting who had no excuse was given a choice between fasting and paying the ransom (الفدية), which was half a sa' (صاع) for every day, or, it was said, a mudd (مد). This was in the beginning of Islam when fasting was prescribed for them and they were not accustomed to it, so they were permitted to break the fast and pay the ransom. Then that was abrogated (نسخ) by His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: فَمَنْ شَهِدَ مِنْكُمُ الشَّهْرَ فَلْيَصُمْهُ (So let whoever of you witnesses the month fast it)

And it was said that it is not abrogated (غير منسوخ). Rather, what is meant by this is the pregnant woman near term (الحامل المقرب), the nursing mother with little milk (المرضعة القليلة اللبن), the old man (الشيخ), and the old woman (الشيخة). For when He mentioned the sickness that removes the obligation (المسقط للفرض), and there were other reasons that are not conventionally considered sickness but with which fasting is difficult (يشق معها الصوم), He mentioned their ruling. Thus, the implied meaning (تقديره) is: "And upon those who can afford it (وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ يُطِيقُونَهُ)"—then something occurred to them that prevents the ability (يمنع الطاقة)—فِدْيَةٌ (must pay a ransom).

And this is what is narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). This is supported by what was reported in the anomalous readings (شواذ القراءة) from Ibn Abbas: وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ يُطًيٍقُونَهُ (And upon those who bear it with difficulty), meaning: they undertake it with effort (يتكلّفونه).

Based on this, His saying وَأَنْ تَصُومُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ (and that you should fast is better for you) is a new, independent statement (كلاماً مستأنفاً) that has no connection to what preceded it. Its implied meaning (تقديره) is: "And indeed, your fasting is a great good for you."

This is what they said regarding the meaning of the Ayah. However, it occurs to the mind that we have no need for such far-fetched interpretations (التكلفات البعيدة), such as sometimes asserting abrogation (النسخ) despite the infallible reports (الأخبار المعصومية) indicating the contrary, or sometimes committing to omission (الحذف) and implied meaning (التقدير) and separating what appears to be connected, especially since those mentioned narrations are not established (عدم ثبوت تلك الروايات).

This is because Allah, the Glorified, لا يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْساً إِلَّا وُسْعَها (Allah does not task any soul beyond its capacity) as He stated in the decisive part (محكم) of His Book. And al-wus' (الوسع) is less than al-taqah (الطاقة) (ability), as was narrated in its interpretation from the People of the House (peace be upon them). Therefore, no soul is tasked with what is equivalent to its taqah (ability)—that is, what is habitually difficult (يشق عليها) for it to bear and causes hardship (يعسر).

So, those who can afford the fast (الذين يطيقون الصوم)—meaning the fast is within their ability (بقدر طاقتهم) but they experience hardship (مشقة) and difficulty (عسر) with it—Allah did not obligate them absolutely (على سبيل الحتم), like the old man (الشيخ), the pregnant woman (الحامل), and their likes. Rather, He gave them a choice between it and the ransom (الفدية), as an expansion (توسيعاً) and mercy from Him. Then He made fasting better for them than the ransom in reward (الأجر) and recompense (الثواب) if they chose hardship over ease (السعة).

This is supported by the anomalous reading (القراءة الشاذة), just as it is supported by what they mentioned. This is also indicated by what was narrated in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: الَّذِينَ يُطِيقُونَهُ ** (those who can afford it). He said: "The very old man (الشيخ الكبير) and the one afflicted by intense thirst (العطاش)." In a narration: "The woman who fears for her child and the very old man (الشيخ الكبير)."

And the Almighty's saying: وَأَنْ تَصُومُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ (and that you should fast is better for you) indicates that the one who can afford it (المطيق) is he who is able to fast, reaching a limit in ability (حداً في القدرة) that is below the limit where the obligation (التكليف) is imposed upon him.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) concerning an old man who became weak (ضعف) regarding the fast of the month of Ramadan. He said: "He should give charity (يتصدّق) for every day with what suffices as food for a needy person (طعام مسكين)." In a narration: "A mudd (مد) for every day."

فَمَنْ تَطَوَّعَ خَيْراً (And whoever volunteers good) Meaning, he increases the amount of the ransom (الفدية). And it was read يَطّوَّع (yaṭṭawwaʿ) as in the Ayah of Hajj. فَهُوَ (it). Meaning, the volunteering (التطوع). خَيْرٌ لَهُ وَأَنْ تَصُومُوا (is better for him. And that you should fast) O you who can afford it (أيها المطيقون). خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ (is better for you) Than the ransom and the voluntary good (تطوّع الخير). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (if you know) [Meaning] what is in fasting of virtue (الفضيلة) if you fast, or if you are among the people of knowledge (أهل العلم), you would know that.

(١٨٥) شَهْرُ رَمَضانَ (The month of Ramadān) meaning: The numbered days (الأيّام المعدودات) are the month of Ramadān. In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah only made the fasting of the month of Ramadān obligatory upon the Prophets (الأنبياء) not the nations (الأمم). Then Allah favored this nation (الأمة) with it and made its fasting obligatory upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and upon his nation (أمته). الَّذِي أُنْزِلَ فِيهِ الْقُرْآنُ (in which the Qur’ān was sent down) meaning: its clarification (بيانه) and its interpretation (تأويله), as its verification passed in the Ninth Introduction (المقدّمة التاسعة) of this book. And it was read without the hamzah (بغير الهمزة) wherever it occurred. هُدىً لِلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّناتٍ مِنَ الْهُدى وَالْفُرْقانِ (as guidance for mankind, and manifest proofs of the guidance and the Criterion) Its exegesis (تفسيره) has already passed in that Introduction (المقدّمة). فَمَنْ شَهِدَ مِنْكُمُ الشَّهْرَ (So whoever of you sights the month) meaning: whoever is present during the month and is not traveling (مسافراً). فَلْيَصُمْهُ (let him fast it) In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "How clear it is: whoever sights it, let him fast it, and whoever travels, let him not fast it." In al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "When the month of Ramadān begins, Allah has a condition therein. Allah the Almighty said: (فَمَنْ شَهِدَ مِنْكُمُ الشَّهْرَ فَلْيَصُمْهُ) (So whoever of you sights the month, let him fast it). Thus, when the month of Ramadān begins, it is not permissible for a man to leave except for Hajj (حج) or Umrah (عمرة), or for wealth (مال) he fears will perish, or for a brother (أخ) he fears will be destroyed. And it is not permissible for him to leave in order to destroy his brother's wealth. When the night of the twenty-third has passed, then let him leave wherever he wishes." وَمَنْ كانَ مَرِيضاً أَوْ عَلى سَفَرٍ فَعِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَيَّامٍ أُخَرَ (But should anyone of you be sick or on a journey, let it be a number of other days) This was repeated as an emphasis on the command to break the fast (الإفطار), and that it is a firm obligation (عزيمة) whose abandonment is not permissible. يُرِيدُ اللهُ بِكُمُ الْيُسْرَ وَلا يُرِيدُ بِكُمُ الْعُسْرَ (Allah desires ease for you, and He does not desire hardship for you) He desires to make things easy for you and not difficult. For this reason, He commanded you to break the fast (الإفطار) during illness and travel. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, Allah has bestowed charity (تصدق) upon the sick and the travelers (مسافريها) of My nation (امتي) by allowing the shortening [of prayer] (التقصير) and the breaking of the fast (الإفطار). Would any of you be pleased if, when he gives a charity (صدقة), it is returned to him?" In al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, presented a gift (هدية) to me and to my nation (أمتي) which He did not present to any of the [previous] nations (الأمم), as an honor (كرامة) from Allah to us." They asked: "What is that, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Breaking the fast (الإفطار) during travel and shortening the prayer (التقصير) in prayer. So, whoever does not do that has rejected Allah's gift." وَلِتُكْمِلُوا الْعِدَّةَ (and so that you may complete the number) And He legislated the entirety of what was mentioned so that you may complete the number (عدة) of the days of the month. And it was read litukammilū (لتكمّلوا) with a muthqal (مثقّلاً) (doubled letter). وَلِتُكَبِّرُوا اللهَ عَلى ما هَداكُمْ (and magnify Allah for guiding you) And so that you may glorify Allah (ولتعظّموا الله) and exalt Him (وتمجّدوه) for His guidance (هدايته) to you. وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (and that you may give thanks) [meaning] for His making the matter easy (تسهيله الأمر) for you. In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "The takbir (التكبير) was made more numerous in the Eid prayer (صلاة العيد) than in other prayers because the takbir is only glorification (تعظيم) of Allah and exaltation (تمجيد) for what He has guided and granted well-being (عافى), just as He, the Mighty and Exalted, said: وَلِتُكَبِّرُوا اللهَ عَلى ما هَداكُمْ وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (and magnify Allah for guiding you, and that you may give thanks)." In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, there is takbir (تكبيراً) on al-Fitr (الفطر), but it is recommended (مسنون)." I said: "And where is it?" He said: "On the night of al-Fitr, during the Maghrib (المغرب) and the final Isha (العشاء الآخرة) prayers, and during the Fajr (الفجر) prayer, and during the Eid prayer (صلاة العيد). Then it ceases." I said: "How should I say it?" He said: "You say: الله أكبر الله أكبر لا إله إلّا الله والله أكبر ولله الحمد الله أكبر على ما هدانا (Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest, and all praise belongs to Allah, Allah is the Greatest for what He has guided us to). And this is the saying of Allah the Almighty: (وَلِتُكْمِلُوا الْعِدَّةَ) (and so that you may complete the number)—meaning the fasting (الصيام)—وَلِتُكَبِّرُوا اللهَ عَلى ما هَداكُمْ** (and magnify Allah for guiding you)."

(١٨٦) وَإِذا سَأَلَكَ عِبادِي عَنِّي فَإِنِّي قَرِيبٌ (186) And when My servants ask you about Me, [say that] I am indeed near.

Say to them that I am near. It is narrated that a Bedouin said to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Is our Lord near, so we may whisper to Him (فَنُنَاجِيهِ), or far, so we may call out to Him (فَنُنَادِيهِ)?" So, this [Ayah] was revealed.

I say: His proximity (قُرْبُهُ) Almighty is an expression of His companionship (مَعِيَّتِهِ)—Mighty and Majestic is He—just as He, glory be to Him, said: وَهُوَ مَعَكُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ (And He is with you wherever you may be.)

Just as His companionship (مَعِيَّتِهِ) with things is not through mingling (مُمَازَجَة) or interpenetration (مُدَاخَلَة), and His separation (مُفَارَقَتُهُ) from them is not through distinctness (مُبَايَنَة) or removal (مُزَايَلَة), so too, His proximity (قُرْبُهُ) is not through gathering (اجْتِمَاع) or location (أَيْن), and His distance (بُعْدُهُ) is not through separation (افْتِرَاق) or interval (بَيْن). Rather, it is in another manner, closer than this proximity and further than this distance. For this reason, the Almighty said: **وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ ** (and We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.)

And He said: وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْكُمْ وَلكِنْ لا تُبْصِرُونَ (and We are nearer to him than you, but you do not perceive.)

And in the intimate supplication (مُنَاجَاة) of the Master of the Martyrs (peace and blessings be upon him): "My God, how near You are to me, and how far I am from You! And how compassionate You are toward me! So what is it that veils me from You?"

And indeed, one finds His proximity to His servant as if he sees Him, just as our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Worship Allah as if you see Him, for if you do not see Him, He surely sees you."

If it is asked: "How can one thing be near to another, while that other is far from it?" We say: This is like when you have a beloved who is present with you, yet you are blind to him (فِي عَمَى), neither seeing him nor sensing his presence. Thus, he is near to you, but you are far from him.

أُجِيبُ دَعْوَةَ الدَّاعِ إِذا دَعانِ (I answer the call of the caller when he calls Me.)

This is an affirmation of proximity and a promise of response to the caller. So let them respond to Me when I call them to faith (الإِيمَان) and obedience (الطَّاعَة), just as I respond to them when they call Me for their important needs (لِمَهَامِّهِمْ).

**وَلْيُؤْمِنُوا بِي ** (and let them believe in Me,)

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Meaning, "And let them be certain that I am capable of granting them what they ask for."

And al-Ayyashi narrated what is similar in meaning.

لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْشُدُونَ (so that they may be rightly guided.)

He said: Meaning, "So that they may attain the truth (الْحَقّ) and be guided to it."

It is narrated that Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) recited: أَمَّنْ يُجِيبُ الْمُضْطَرَّ إِذا دَعاهُ (Is He who answers the distressed when he invokes Him) He was asked: "Why is it that we supplicate, yet we are not answered?" He replied: "Because you supplicate to One Whom you do not know, and you ask for what you do not understand."

Thus, distress (الِاضْطِرَار) is the essence (عَيْن) of the religion (الدِّين). Frequent supplication (كَثْرَةُ الدُّعَاءِ) coupled with blindness (الْعَمَى) concerning Allah is a sign of abandonment (الْخِذْلَان). Whoever does not witness the humility (ذِلَّة) of his soul (نَفْسِهِ), his heart (قَلْبِهِ), and his innermost being (سِرِّهِ) beneath the power (قُدْرَة) of Allah, judges Allah by his request (بِالسُّؤَال) and assumes that his request is a supplication (دُعَاء). And judging Allah is an act of audacity (الْجُرْأَة) toward Allah.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was said to him regarding His saying, glory be to Him: ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ (Invoke Me and I will respond to you.) "We invoke Him, but we do not see a response." He said: "Do you think that Allah, Mighty and Majestic is He, has broken His promise?" He replied: "No." He said: "Then what is the reason for that?" He replied: "I do not know." He said: "But I will inform you: Whoever obeys Allah, Mighty and Majestic is He, in what He commanded, and then invokes Him through the proper channel of supplication (جِهَةِ الدُّعَاءِ), He responds to him." It was asked: "And what is the proper channel of supplication (جِهَةِ الدُّعَاءِ)?" He said: "You begin by praising Allah, and mentioning His blessings (نِعَمِهِ) upon you, then you thank Him, then you send blessings upon the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, then you mention your sins (ذُنُوبِكَ) and acknowledge them (فَتُقِرُّ بِهَا), then you seek refuge from them. This is the proper channel of supplication (جِهَةِ الدُّعَاءِ)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, a servant supplicates, and Allah says to the two angels: "I have responded to him, but withhold his need (احْبِسُوهُ بِحَاجَتِهِ), for I love to hear his voice." And indeed, a servant supplicates, and Allah, Blessed and Exalted is He, says: "Hasten his need (عَجِّلُوا لَهُ حَاجَتَهُ), for I detest his voice."

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "Indeed, Allah Almighty says: ادْعُونِي أَسْتَجِبْ لَكُمْ (Invoke Me and I will respond to you.) Yet we invoke Him, and He does not respond to us." He said: "That is because you do not fulfill the covenant of Allah. And Allah says: أَوْفُوا بِعَهْدِي أُوفِ بِعَهْدِكُمْ (Fulfill My covenant, and I will fulfill your covenant.) By Allah, if you were to fulfill your covenant to Allah, He would fulfill His covenant to you."

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever is pleased that his supplication be answered, let him purify his earnings (مَكْسَبَهُ).

And it is narrated from him (peace be upon him): If one of you desires that he should not ask his Lord for anything except that He grants it to him, then let him despair of all people (فَلْيَيْأَسْ مِنَ النَّاسِ كُلِّهِمْ), and let him have no hope (رَجَاء) except with Allah, Mighty and Majestic is He. When Allah knows this from his heart, he will not ask Him for anything except that He grants it to him. Another narration concerning this topic will come in Surat al-Mu'min, if Allah wills.

(187) أُحِلَّ لَكُمْ لَيْلَةَ الصِّيامِ الرَّفَثُ إِلى نِسائِكُمْ (It is lawful for you, on the night of the fast, to lie with your wives.) Meaning, the night from which you enter the state of fasting, the al-rafath (الرَّفَثُ) with your wives. This is a euphemism (كناية) for intercourse (الجماع), because it rarely lacks rafath, which is the explicit mention of what ought to be alluded to euphemistically (يكنّى عنه). It is governed by the preposition إلى (ila) because it implies the meaning of reaching/leading to (الإفضاء).

** هُنَّ لِباسٌ لَكُمْ وَأَنْتُمْ لِباسٌ لَهُنَ (They are a garment for you, and you are a garment for them.) This is a new sentence (استيناف) explaining the reason for the permissibility (الإحلال), which is the lack of patience to abstain from them and the difficulty of avoiding them due to frequent mixing (كثرة المخالطة) and intense closeness (شدة الملابسة).

** عَلِمَ اللهُ أَنَّكُمْ كُنْتُمْ تَخْتانُونَ أَنْفُسَكُمْ (Allah knows that you have been betraying yourselves.) It is derived from al-khiyanah (الخيانة), like al-iktisab (الاكتساب) is derived from al-kasb (الكسب), and it is more emphatic than it (الخيانة). Meaning, you wrong your souls by exposing them to punishment (العقاب) and diminishing their share of reward (الثواب).

** فَتابَ عَلَيْكُمْ (So He turned to you clemently) When you repented, He granted you a concession (رخّص لكم) and removed the strictness (التشديد) from you.

** وَعَفا عَنْكُمْ (and excused you.) He erased its effect (أثره) from you.

** فَالْآنَ بَاشِرُوهُنَ (So now consort with them,) Intercourse (الجماع) is alluded to euphemistically (كنى) by al-mubasharah (المباشرة), which is the joining of skin to skin (الصاق البشرة بالبشرة).

** وَابْتَغُوا ما كَتَبَ اللهُ لَكُمْ (and seek what Allah has ordained for you.) It is said: It means seek what He has decreed for you and established in the Preserved Tablet (اللوح) concerning offspring (الولد) through consorting; meaning, do not consort merely to satisfy lust (قضاء الشهوة) alone, but rather to seek what Allah instituted marriage (النكاح) for, which is procreation (التناسل). And it is also said: And seek what Allah has ordained for you of permissibility (الاباحة) after prohibition (الحظر), for Allah loves that His concessions (رخصه) be accepted, just as He loves that His strict commands (عزائمه) be accepted.

** وَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا حَتَّى يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمُ الْخَيْطُ الْأَبْيَضُ مِنَ الْخَيْطِ الْأَسْوَدِ مِنَ الْفَجْرِ (And eat and drink until the white streak of dawn becomes manifest to you from the black streak [of the night].) He likened the first appearance of the dawn (الفجر) stretching across the horizon (الأفق), and the darkness of the night (ظلمة الليل) extending alongside it, to two threads (خيطين), one white and one black. He sufficed with clarifying the white thread by His saying مِنَ الْفَجْرِ** (of the dawn) instead of clarifying the black thread, due to the former indicating the latter.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the whiteness of the day from the blackness of the night. In a narration: It is the dawn (الفجر) about which there is no doubt. In another narration: It is not the white [light] that ascends (صعداء). Indeed, Allah did not place His creation in doubt concerning this. Then He recited this verse and said: The one that stretches across (المعترض) ....

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked: "Do I eat during the night in the month of Ramadan until I doubt [the dawn]?" He said: "Eat until you do not doubt."

In it (al-Tahdhib), and in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked about two men who stood up in Ramadan. One of them said, "This is the dawn (الفجر)," and the other said, "I do not see anything." He said: "Let the one who is not certain of the dawn eat, but eating is forbidden for the one who claimed that he saw the dawn, because Allah says: ** وَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا حَتَّى يَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمُ** (And eat and drink until it becomes manifest to you) the Ayah."

In al-Kafi, al-Faqih, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This verse was revealed concerning Khawwat ibn Jubayr al-Ansari (خوّات بن جبير الأنصاري). He was with the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم during the Battle of the Trench (الخندق) while fasting. He reached evening (أمسى) while in that state. Before this verse was revealed, if one of them slept, food and drink were forbidden to him. Khawwat came to his family when evening arrived and asked, "Do you have any food?" They said, "Do not sleep until we prepare food for you." He leaned back and slept. They said to him, "You have done it [slept]." He said, "Yes." So he spent the night in that state. He woke up and went early to the Trench, and he began to faint (يغشى عليه). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed by him, and when He saw what was afflicting him, Khawwat informed Him of his situation. So Allah revealed the verse concerning him. And al-Qummi added to what was added: And marriage (النكاح) was forbidden both night and day during the month of Ramadan. He said: And a group of young men (قوم من الشبّان) used to engage in marriage (ينكحون) at night during the month of Ramadan. So Allah revealed [the verse]. In al-Jawami', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: Eating was forbidden in the month of Ramadan at night after sleeping, and marriage (النكاح) was forbidden both night and day. And a man among the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم called Mut'im ibn Jubayr (مطعم بن جبير) slept before breaking his fast (يفطر), and he was present at the digging of the Trench (حفر الخندق), so he fainted (أغمي عليه). And a group of young men used to engage in marriage secretly (سراً) at night during the month of Ramadan. So the verse was revealed, and marriage was made lawful at night, and eating was made lawful after sleeping. That is His saying: ** وَعَفا عَنْكُمْ** (and excused you).

In al-Majma', the general populace (العامّة) differed regarding the name of this man. Then he mentioned his story narrated from them in another manner. He said: So Umar said: "O Messenger of Allah, I apologize to You for something similar to this. I returned to my family after I prayed the evening prayer (العشاء) and I consorted with my wife." And men stood up and confessed to the like of what they heard. So the verse was revealed.

ثُمَّ أَتِمُّوا الصِّيامَ إِلَى اللَّيْلِ (Then complete the fast until the night.) This is a clarification of the end time of the fast (الصيام).

** وَلا تُبَاشِرُوهُنَّ وَأَنْتُمْ عاكِفُونَ فِي الْمَساجِدِ (But do not consort with them while you are staying in the mosques for devotion.) Meaning, performing i'tikaf (معتكفون) in them. And al-i'tikaf (الاعتكاف) is for a person to confine himself in the congregational mosque (المسجد الجامع) for worship (للعبادة).

** تِلْكَ (These) Meaning, the rulings (الأحكام) that were mentioned

** حُدُودُ اللهِ (are Allah’s bounds,) Meaning Allah's sacred prohibitions (حرمات الله) and His forbidden acts (مناهيه).

** فَلا تَقْرَبُوها (so do not approach them.) In the Noble Prophetic Hadith: "Indeed, every king has a preserve (حمىً), and the preserve of Allah is His forbidden acts (محارمه). So whoever falls around the preserve is likely to fall into it."

كَذلِكَ (Thus) Like that clarification (التّبيين),

** يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ آياتِهِ (does Allah clarify His signs) Meaning His proofs (حججه) and His evidences (دلائله),

** لِلنَّاسِ** (for mankind,) Regarding what He commanded them and what He forbade them,

** لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ** (so that they may be Godwary.) Of opposing His commands and prohibitions.

(188) وَلا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوالَكُمْ بَيْنَكُمْ (And do not eat up your wealth among yourselves) Let not some of you consume the wealth of others بِالْباطِلِ** (falsely) by means of that which is not lawful and which Allah has not legislated.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He means by (الباطل) the false oath by which wealth is seized.

In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man among us who possesses something by which he sustains himself, but he owes a debt. Should he feed his dependents with it until Allah the Almighty grants Him ease so that he may pay his debt, or should he borrow against his own responsibility (على ظهره) during this difficult time and severe struggle for livelihood, or should he accept charity? He (peace be upon him) said: "He should pay his debt with what He possesses, and he should not consume the wealth of people unless he has the means to repay them. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: وَلا تَأْكُلُوا أَمْوالَكُمْ بَيْنَكُمْ بِالْباطِلِ (And do not eat up your wealth among yourselves falsely)."

وَتُدْلُوا بِها إِلَى الْحُكَّامِ (nor offer it to the judges) This is grammatically connected (عطف) to the prohibited action (المنهي) or is in the accusative case (نصب) by an implied (أن). Al-Idla' (الإدلاء) means casting/throwing (الإلقاء). That is: And do not cast its matter and the judgment concerning it to the judges (الحكام) لِتَأْكُلُوا (that you may eat up) through litigation فَرِيقاً (a part) a portion مِنْ أَمْوالِ النَّاسِ بِالْإِثْمِ (of the people’s wealth sinfully) by means of that which necessitates sin (إثماً), such as false testimony (شهادة الزور) and the false oath (اليمين الكاذبة), or through reconciliation (الصلح) while knowing that the one for whom the judgment is made is an oppressor (ظالم) وَأَنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (while you know) that you are acting falsely (مبطلون).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, already knew that there would be oppressive judges (حكاماً يجورون) in the Ummah. However, He did not mean the judges of the people of justice (حكام أهل العدل), but rather He meant the judges of the people of oppression (حكام أهل الجور)."

And al-Qummi said that the Scholar (العالم) (peace be upon him) said: "Allah already knew that there would be judges who rule unjustly (بغير الحق), so He forbade seeking judgment from them, because they do not rule with justice (بالحق), and thus wealth is invalidated."

In al-Tahdhib and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He wrote in its interpretation that the judges (الحكام) are the magistrates (القضاة). Then he wrote beneath it: "This is when a man knows that he is an oppressor (ظالم), yet the magistrate rules in his favor. He is not excused for taking that which was ruled for him, if he already knew that he was an oppressor."

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Quraysh used to gamble with a man regarding his family and his wealth, so Allah forbade them from doing so.

I say: The verse is general (تعمّ) and encompasses all, and there is no contradiction (تنافي) among the narratives (الأخبار).

(189) يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْأَهِلَّةِ (They question you concerning the new moons) (They question) concerning their states of increase and decrease, and the aspect of wisdom in that. قُلْ هِيَ مَواقِيتُ لِلنَّاسِ وَالْحَجِ (Say, 'They are appointed times for the people and for the Hajj.') Meaning: signs (مَعَالِم) by which people appoint the times for their acts of worship (عِبَادَاتِهِم), their cultivation (مَزَارِعِهِم), their trade (مَتَاجِرِهِم), the due dates of their debts (مَحَالّ دُيُونِهِم), and the waiting periods (عِدَد) of their women. And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): (They are appointed times) for their fasting (صَوْمِهِم), their breaking of the fast (فِطْرِهِم), and their Hajj. وَلَيْسَ الْبِرُّ بِأَنْ تَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ (Goodness is not that you enter houses) And it was read (وقرئ) with the kasrah on the bā’ (بِكَسْرِ الْبَاءِ) wherever it occurs. مِنْ ظُهُورِها (from their backs.) In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When they entered the state of ihram (أَحْرَمُوا), they would not enter their houses through their doors, but they would dig a hole (نَقْبًا) in the backs of their houses—meaning, in their rear part (مُؤَخَّرِهَا)—and enter and exit through it. Thus, they were forbidden from making this a religious practice (التَّدَيُّن بِهَا). وَلكِنَّ الْبِرَّ مَنِ اتَّقى (But goodness is [the goodness of] him who is Godwary.) (Goodness is [the goodness of] him who) feared (اتَّقَى) what Allah has forbidden. This is narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوابِها (And enter houses by their doors.) And in al-Mahasin, al-Majma', and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means that one should approach the matter from its proper direction (وَجْهِهِ), whatever the matter may be. أقول : And from this is derived the taking of the rulings of the religion (أَحْكَام الدِّين) from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and his pure progeny (عِتْرَتِهِ الطَّيِّبِين), because they are the doors (أَبْوَاب) to the city of the knowledge of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, all of them, just as He said: "I am the city of knowledge and Ali is its door, and the city is not approached except through its door." And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Allah has appointed people (أَهْلًا) for knowledge and has made their obedience obligatory upon the servants by His saying: وَأْتُوا الْبُيُوتَ مِنْ أَبْوابِها (And enter houses by their doors). And the houses (الْبُيُوت) are the houses of knowledge (بُيُوت الْعِلْم) which the Prophets entrusted, and their doors (أَبْوَابُهَا) are their successors (أَوْصِيَاؤُهُم). And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): We are the houses (الْبُيُوت) whose doors Allah commanded to be approached. We are the Door of Allah and His houses through which He is approached. So, whoever follows us and affirms our guardianship (وِلَايَتِنَا) has approached the houses through their doors, and whoever opposes us and prefers others over us has approached the houses from their backs. If Allah had willed, He would have introduced Himself to the people so that they would know Him and approach Him through His Door, but He made us His doors, His path (صِرَاطَهُ), His way (سَبِيلَهُ), and His Door through which He is approached. He (peace be upon him) said: So, whoever deviates from our guardianship (وِلَايَتِنَا) and prefers others over us has approached the houses from their backs, and indeed, they are deviating from the path. And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them) are the doors of Allah (أَبْوَابُ الله), His ways (سُبُلُهُ), the callers to Paradise (الْجَنَّة), the leaders to it (الْقَادَة إِلَيْهَا), and the guides to it (الْأَدِلَّاء عَلَيْهَا) until the Day of Resurrection. وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (and be Godwary of Allah) regarding the alteration of His rulings (أَحْكَامِهِ), لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (so that you may be felicitous.) so that you may attain guidance (الْهُدَى) and goodness (الْبِرّ).

(١٩٠) وَقاتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (And fight in the way of Allah) Strive (جاهدوا) to elevate His word and strengthen His religion الَّذِينَ يُقاتِلُونَكُمْ ** (those who fight you) This abrogates (ناسخة) His saying, the Almighty: ** كُفُّوا أَيْدِيَكُمْ (Restrain your hands) This is according to al-Majma' narrated from them (peace be upon them). وَلا تَعْتَدُوا (and do not transgress) By initiating the fighting, or surprising them with it without a prior call (دعوة), or by mutilation (المثلة), or by killing those whom you have been forbidden to kill, such as women, children, the elderly (المشايخ), and those under covenant (المعاهدين). إِنَّ اللهَ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ. (Indeed Allah does not like the transgressors.)

(١٩١) وَاقْتُلُوهُمْ حَيْثُ ثَقِفْتُمُوهُمْ (and kill them wherever you find them) wherever you found them (وجدتموهم). This abrogates (ناسخة) the saying of Him, the Mighty and Majestic: وَلا تُطِعِ الْكافِرِينَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ وَدَعْ أَذاهُمْ (and do not obey the faithless and the hypocrites, and disregard their annoyance). This is according to al-Majma' narrated from them (peace be upon them). وَأَخْرِجُوهُمْ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَخْرَجُوكُمْ (and expel them from where they expelled you) meaning: expel them from Mecca (مكّة) just as they expelled you from it. And this was indeed done on the Day of the Conquest (يوم الفتح) to those among them who did not embrace Islam. وَالْفِتْنَةُ أَشَدُّ مِنَ الْقَتْلِ (and persecution is more severe than killing) It is said that its meaning is that their polytheism (شركهم) in the Sacred Precinct (الحرم) and their hindering you from it is more severe than your killing them therein. وَلا تُقاتِلُوهُمْ عِنْدَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ حَتَّى يُقاتِلُوكُمْ فِيهِ (Do not fight them near the Holy Mosque unless they fight you in it) Do not initiate fighting against them, thereby violating the sanctity of the Sacred Precinct (الحرم). فَإِنْ قاتَلُوكُمْ فَاقْتُلُوهُمْ (But if they fight you, then kill them) So, do not be concerned about fighting them there, for they are the ones who violated its sanctity. And it was recited (وقرئ): وَلا تَقْتُلُوهُمْ حَتَّى يَقْتُلُوكُمْ فَإِنْ قَتَلُوكُمْ (and do not kill them until they kill you, but if they kill you) without the alif (بدون الألف). كَذلِكَ جَزاءُ الْكافِرِينَ (Such is the retribution of the faithless) كَذلِكَ (Such) means: like that (مثل ذلك). جَزاءُ الْكافِرِينَ (is the retribution of the faithless) meaning: their retribution is that what they did is done to them.

(١٩٢) فَإِنِ انْتَهَوْا (If they desist) from fighting and polytheism (الشرك). ** فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful). He forgives them what has passed. (١٩٣) وَقاتِلُوهُمْ حَتَّى لا تَكُونَ فِتْنَةٌ (And fight them until there is no more persecution) meaning polytheism (شرك). This is according to al-Majma' narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). وَيَكُونَ الدِّينُ (and all religion belongs) meaning obedience (الطاعة) and worship (العبادة) belong to Allah alone, purely, with no share for Satan in it. فَإِنِ انْتَهَوْا (But if they desist) from polytheism (الشرك), ** فَلا عُدْوانَ إِلَّا عَلَى الظَّالِمِينَ (then there shall be no aggression except against the wrongdoers). So, do not commit aggression against those who desist. The recompense (الجزاء) was named by the name of aggression (الاعتداء) for the sake of congruity (المشاكلة) and pairing of speech (ازدواج الكلام), just as in His saying, glory be to Him: ** وَجَزاءُ سَيِّئَةٍ سَيِّئَةٌ مِثْلُها ** (The recompense of an evil deed is an evil deed like it), and similar to it is فَاعْتَدُوا عَلَيْهِ (then commit aggression against him) as will come later. And al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both): Meaning, ** فَلا عُدْوانَ إِلَّا (then there shall be no aggression except) against the descendants (ذرّية) of the murderers of al-Husayn (peace be upon him). And in a narration: Allah does not commit aggression against anyone except against the progeny (نسل ولد) of the murderers of al-Husayn (peace be upon him). And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, what do you say concerning the narration reported from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: 'When the Riser (القائم) appears, He will kill the descendants (ذراري) of the murderers of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) due to the deeds of their fathers'?" He (peace be upon him) said: "It is so." It was said: "Then what is the meaning of the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَلا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى (and no bearer shall bear the burden of another)?" He (peace be upon him) said: "Allah speaks the truth in all His sayings. However, the descendants (ذراري) of the murderers of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) are pleased with the deeds of their fathers and boast about them. And whoever is pleased with something is like the one who committed it. If a man were killed in the East, and a man in the West were pleased with his killing, the one who is pleased would be a partner to the killer in the sight of Allah. The Riser (القائم) (peace be upon him) will only kill them when He appears because of their pleasure with the deed of their fathers." I say: That is because they are truly of the same kind (سنخ) and reality (حقيقة) as them, such that if they were able to do what those (ancestors) were able to do, they would commit the same deeds, as was established in the Third Introduction (المقدّمة الثالثة).

(١٩٤) الشَّهْرُ الْحَرامُ بِالشَّهْرِ الْحَرامِ (The sacred month for the sacred month) It was said that the polytheists fought them in the year of al-Hudaybiyyah in Dhū al-Qaʿdah, and it coincided that their departure for the Umrat al-Qadāʾ (Compensatory Umrah) was in it. So, they disliked fighting them due to its sanctity. Thus, it was said to them: This month is for that [month], and its violation is for its violation, so do not be concerned about it. And in al-Majmaʿ, a similar narration is reported from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). وَالْحُرُماتُ قِصاصٌ (and all sanctities are subject to retaliation) Meaning: Every sanctity (حُرْمَة)—which is that which must be preserved—is subject to retaliation (قِصَاص). So, since they violated the sanctity of your month, do the same to them. In al-Tahdhīb and al-ʿAyyāshī, narrated anonymously (مُضْمَراً): He was asked whether the Muslims should initiate fighting against the polytheists in the Sacred Month. He said: If the polytheists initiated it by deeming it permissible (اِسْتِحْلَالِهِمْ), and then the Muslims saw that they could prevail over them therein, that is the meaning of His saying, the Glorified: الشَّهْرُ الْحَرامُ بِالشَّهْرِ الْحَرامِ وَالْحُرُماتُ قِصاصٌ (The sacred month for the sacred month, and all sanctities are subject to retaliation). فَمَنِ اعْتَدى عَلَيْكُمْ فَاعْتَدُوا عَلَيْهِ بِمِثْلِ مَا اعْتَدى عَلَيْكُمْ (So whoever aggresses against you, aggress against him in kind, just as he aggressed against you) This is a summary (فَذْلَكَة) and confirmation of what preceded. In al-Tahdhīb, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding a man who killed a man in the Sacred Precinct (الْحَرَم) and stole in the Sacred Precinct (الْحَرَم). He said: The prescribed punishment (الْحَدّ) shall be carried out against him, and it is a humiliation (صَغَار) for him because he did not respect the sanctity of the Sacred Precinct (الْحَرَم). And Allah the Almighty has said: فَمَنِ اعْتَدى عَلَيْكُمْ فَاعْتَدُوا عَلَيْهِ بِمِثْلِ مَا اعْتَدى عَلَيْكُمْ (So whoever aggresses against you, aggress against him in kind, just as he aggressed against you)—meaning, in the Sacred Precinct (الْحَرَم). And He said: فَلا عُدْوانَ إِلَّا عَلَى الظَّالِمِينَ (But aggression is not lawful except against the wrongdoers). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (and be wary of Allah) In seeking victory (الِانْتِصَار), so do not transgress beyond what has not been permitted for you. وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ مَعَ الْمُتَّقِينَ (and know that Allah is with the Godwary) So He guards them and sets their affairs aright.

(195) وَأَنْفِقُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (and spend in the way of Allah) in al-Jihad (striving) and all other avenues of righteousness (البر). وَلا تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى التَّهْلُكَةِ (and do not cast yourselves into perdition with your own hands) through extravagance (الإسراف), neglecting the means of livelihood (وجه المعاش), and everything that leads to destruction (الهلاك).

In al-Majalis, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, He said: "Obedience to the ruler (السلطان) is obligatory, and whoever abandons obedience to the ruler has abandoned obedience to Allah and has entered into His prohibition, for Allah says: وَلا تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى التَّهْلُكَةِ وَأَحْسِنُوا إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ (and do not cast yourselves into perdition with your own hands, and be virtuous; indeed Allah loves the virtuous)."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام, He said: "If a man were to spend everything he possesses in one of the ways of Allah, he would not have acted virtuously (أحسن) nor would he have been guided to goodness (الخير). Does not Allah say: وَلا تُلْقُوا بِأَيْدِيكُمْ إِلَى التَّهْلُكَةِ وَأَحْسِنُوا إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ (and do not cast yourselves into perdition with your own hands, and be virtuous; indeed Allah loves the virtuous)?" meaning: those who are moderate (المقتصدين).

And in al-Mahasin, narrated from Him عليه السلام, He said: "When the believer perfects his deed (أحسن عمله), Allah multiplies his deed seven hundredfold for every good deed (حسنة). That is the saying of Allah, the Glorified: يُضاعِفُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ (He multiplies for whomever He wishes). So, perfect your deeds (أحسنوا أعمالكم) which you perform for the reward of Allah."

He was asked: "And what is al-Ihsan (virtue/perfection)?" He replied: "When you pray, perfect your bowing (ركوعك) and your prostration (سجودك). When you fast, beware of everything that corrupts your fast. When you perform Hajj, beware of what is forbidden to you during your Hajj and your Umrah." He added: "And every deed you perform for Allah, let it be pure from impurity (الدنس)."

(196) وَأَتِمُّوا الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ (And complete the Ḥajj and the ‘Umrah) Perform them completely and perfectly, fulfilling their conditions (شَرَائِط), pillars (أَرْكَان), and rituals (مَنَاسِك). ** لِلَّهِ (for Allah) Purely for the sake (لِوَجْهِ) of Allah. This is an explicit text regarding the obligation (وُجُوب) of the Umrah just like the obligation of the Hajj.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, it is narrated: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about this verse, and He said: "They are both obligatory (مَفْرُوضَان)."

And in it (al-Kafi), and in al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "The Umrah is obligatory (وَاجِبَة) upon creation, holding the same status as the Hajj for whoever is able (اسْتَطَاع), because Allah says: ** وَأَتِمُّوا الْحَجَّ وَالْعُمْرَةَ لِلَّهِ (And complete the Ḥajj and the ‘Umrah for Allah)." It was asked: "If someone performs Tamattu' (تَمَتَّع) with the Umrah until the Hajj, does that suffice for him?" He said: "Yes."

And in a narration, He said: "The completion (تَمَام) of both means performing them and avoiding what the muhrim (الْمُحْرِم) must avoid during them."

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (أَمِير الْمُؤْمِنِين) and al-Sajjad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both): "It means establish them until the end of what is required in them."

In al-Khisal and al-Uyun, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "The completion (تَمَام) of both is the avoidance (اجْتِنَاب) of obscenity (الرَّفَث), transgression (الْفُسُوق), and disputation (الْجِدَال) during the Hajj."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from them both (peace be upon them): That which is similar in meaning.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "When you enter the state of ihram (أَحْرَمْتَ), you must adhere to the fear of Allah (تَقْوَى الله), frequent remembrance of Allah (ذِكْر الله), and little speech except for good, for indeed, part of the completion of the Hajj and the Umrah is that a person guards his tongue except from good, just as Allah the Almighty said: ** فَمَنْ فَرَضَ فِيهِنَّ الْحَجَّ فَلا رَفَثَ وَلا فُسُوقَ وَلا جِدالَ فِي الْحَجِّ** (Whoever undertakes the Ḥajj [vows] in them, there shall be no sexual contact, nor transgression, nor disputation during the Ḥajj)."

And in it (al-Kafi), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: "The completion of the Hajj is meeting the Imam (لِقَاء الْإِمَام)."

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "When one of you performs Hajj, let him conclude his Hajj with visiting us (بِزِيَارَتِنَا), because that is part of the completion of the Hajj."

I say: And in this time (الزَّمَان), visiting their graves (قُبُورِهِم) substitutes for visiting them and meeting them, as is understood from other narrations (أَخْبَار أُخَر). There is no contradiction (مُنَافَاة) between these narrations, because all of that is part of the completion of the Hajj. ** فَإِنْ (But if) أُحْصِرْتُمْ (you are prevented) If fear, an enemy (عَدُوّ), or illness prevents you from proceeding to it while you are in the state of ihram (مُحْرِمُون) for Hajj or Umrah, and you are thus prevented. This is narrated from them (peace be upon them) in al-Majma'.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The prevented (الْمَحْصُور) is different from the barred (الْمَصْدُود). The prevented (الْمَحْصُور) is the sick person, and the barred (الْمَصْدُود) is the one whom the polytheists (الْمُشْرِكُون) turn back, just as they turned back the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Companions (الصَّحَابَة), which is not due to illness. The barred (الْمَصْدُود) is permitted to approach women (تَحِلّ لَهُ النِّسَاء), but the prevented (الْمَحْصُور) is not permitted to approach women." ** فَمَا اسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ (then [send] whatever offering is feasible) Then, if you wish to exit the state of ihram (التَّحَلُّل مِنَ الْإِحْرَام), you must offer whatever sacrificial animal (الْهَدْي) is feasible, whether it be a camel (بَعِير), a cow (بَقَرَة), or a sheep (شَاة).

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "It means a sheep (شَاة). It was set based on the least strength among the people, so that it accommodates both the strong and the weak."

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "A sheep suffices him, but a camel (الْبَدَنَة) and a cow are better (أَفْضَل)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The barred (الْمَصْدُود) slaughters (يَذْبَح) when he is barred, and his companion returns and approaches women. The prevented (الْمَحْصُور) sends his sacrificial animal (هَدْي) and appoints a day for them, and when the sacrificial animal reaches [its destination], this person exits ihram (أَحَلّ) in his place." And from Him (peace be upon him): "If a man is prevented (أُحْصِر), he sends his sacrificial animal. If he recovers (أَفَاق) and finds himself feeling light, he should proceed if he thinks he can catch up with the people. If he arrives in Mecca before the sacrificial animal is slaughtered (يُنْحَر), he must remain in his state of ihram until he completes all the rituals (الْمَنَاسِك), and he should slaughter his sacrificial animal, and nothing is incumbent upon him. But if he arrives in Mecca after his sacrificial animal has been slaughtered, then he must perform Hajj the following year (مِنْ قَابِل) or Umrah." It was asked: "What if he dies while in ihram before reaching Mecca?" He said: "The Hajj must be performed on his behalf if it was the Hajj of Islam (حَجَّة الْإِسْلَام), and the Umrah [must be performed on his behalf], for it is only a duty incumbent upon him." وَلا تَحْلِقُوا رُؤُسَكُمْ (Do not shave your heads) Do not exit ihram (تَحِلُّوا). ** حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ الْهَدْيُ مَحِلَّهُ (until the offering reaches its destination) Its place (مَكَانَهُ) where it must be slaughtered (يُنْحَر). فَمَنْ كانَ مِنْكُمْ مَرِيضاً ** (But should any of you be sick) An illness that necessitates shaving (الْحَلْق). ** أَوْ بِهِ أَذىً مِنْ رَأْسِهِ (or have a hurt in his head) Such as a wound (جِرَاحَة) or lice (قَمْل). ** فَفِدْيَةٌ (let him make a ransom) Then a ransom (فِدْيَة) is incumbent upon him if he shaves. مِنْ صِيامٍ أَوْ صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ نُسُكٍ (by fasting, or charity, or sacrifice)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "If a man is prevented (أُحْصِر), he sends his sacrificial animal. If his head causes him harm before his sacrificial animal is slaughtered, then he slaughters a sheep (شَاة) in the place where he was prevented, or he fasts, or he gives charity (يَتَصَدَّق). The fasting is three days, and the charity is for six needy people (مَسَاكِين), half a sa' (نِصْف صَاع) for each needy person."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed by Ka‘b ibn ‘Ujrah while lice (الْقَمْل) were falling from his head, and he was in the state of ihram. He asked him: 'Does it harm you, O Ummak (أُمَّك)?' He replied: 'Yes.' So this verse was revealed. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded him to shave, and He stipulated the fasting to be three days, the charity to be for six needy people, two mudd (مُدَّيْن) for each needy person, and the sacrifice (النُّسُك) to be a sheep." Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: "For everything in the Quran that uses 'or' (أَوْ), the person has a choice (بِالْخِيَار) to select whatever he wishes. And for everything in the Quran that says, 'Whoever does not find such-and-such, then such-and-such is incumbent upon him,' the first option is the choice (الْخِيَار)."

I say: The first option (الْخِيَار) means the good (الْخَيْر) and the one worthy of choice (الْحَرِيّ بِالِاخْتِيَار). فَإِذا أَمِنْتُمْ (But when you are secure) Meaning, when you are not prevented (مُحْصَرِين) and are in a state of security (أَمْن) and ease (سَعَة). ** فَمَنْ تَمَتَّعَ بِالْعُمْرَةِ (whoever enjoys the ‘Umrah) Whoever benefits (اسْتَمْتَع) and profits (انْتَفَع) after exiting the state of ihram from his Umrah by making permissible what was forbidden (مُحَرَّم) to him. ** إِلَى الْحَجِ (until the Ḥajj) Until he enters the state of ihram for the Hajj. ** فَمَا اسْتَيْسَرَ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ (then [let him offer] whatever offering is feasible) Then a sacrificial animal (دَم) that is feasible is incumbent upon him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "A sheep." فَمَنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ (But whoever does not find) The sacrificial animal (الْهَدْي). ** فَصِيامُ ثَلاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ فِي الْحَجِ (then [he shall fast] three days during the Ḥajj) During the time of the Hajj and the days of being occupied with it. The best is that he fasts the seventh of Dhul-Hijjah (ذِي الْحِجَّة), the eighth, and the ninth.

And in al-Kafi also, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), regarding the one performing Tamattu' (الْمُتَمَتِّع) who does not find the sacrificial animal, He said: "He fasts one day before Yawm al-Tarwiyah (يَوْم التَّرْوِيَة), Yawm al-Tarwiyah, and Yawm Arafah (يَوْم عَرَفَة)." It was asked: "What if he arrived on Yawm al-Tarwiyah?" He said: "He fasts three days after al-Tashriq (التَّشْرِيق)." It was asked: "His camels (جِمَالُهُ) did not stay for him." He said: "He fasts Yawm al-Hasbah (يَوْم الْحَصْبَة) and two days after it." It was asked: "What is al-Hasbah?" He said: "The day of his departure (يَوْم نَفْرِهِ)." It was asked: "Does he fast while he is traveling (مُسَافِر)?" He said: "Yes. Is he not traveling on Yawm Arafah? We, the People of the House (أَهْل بَيْت), say that based on the saying of Allah the Almighty: فَصِيامُ ثَلاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ فِي الْحَجِ ** (then [he shall fast] three days during the Ḥajj) He says: During Dhul-Hijjah (ذِي الْحِجَّة). وَسَبْعَةٍ إِذا رَجَعْتُمْ (and seven when you return) To your families (أَهَالِيكُم). If he decides to reside (الْإِقَامَة) in Mecca, he should observe the arrival of the people of his land, and when he thinks they have entered [their homes], he should fast the seven days. This is narrated in al-Kafi from them (peace be upon them).

تِلْكَ عَشَرَةٌ كامِلَةٌ (That is ten complete [days]) They do not fall short of the complete sacrifice (الْأُضْحِيَّة الْكَامِلَة).

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about Sufyan al-Thawri, what he meant by "complete" (كَامِلَة). He said: "Seven and three." He (peace be upon him) said: "Would this be confusing to a person of intellect (ذِي حِجَى) that seven and three is ten?" He said: "Then what is it, may Allah set you right?" He said: "Look." He said: "I do not know. Then what is it, may Allah set you right?" He said: "The complete (الْكَامِلَة)—its completeness is the completeness of the sacrifice (الْأُضْحِيَّة), whether you perform it or do not perform it."

ذلِكَ (That) Meaning, the Tamattu' (التَّمَتُّع). لِمَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ أَهْلُهُ حاضِرِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ (is for him whose family are not present at the Holy Mosque) In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: "Whoever's dwelling (مَنْزِلُهُ) is eighteen mil (مِيلًا) in front of it, eighteen mil behind it, eighteen mil to its right, and eighteen mil to its left, there is no Mut'ah (مُتْعَة) for him, such as Marr (مَرّ) (1) and its likes." And in it (al-Kafi), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "That is the people of Mecca (أَهْل مَكَّة). There is no Mut'ah for them, nor is Umrah incumbent upon them." It was asked: "What is the limit (حَدّ) of that?" He said: "Forty-eight mil from all directions (نَوَاحِي) of Mecca, excluding Usfan (عُسْفَان) and Dhat Irq (ذَات عِرْق)." وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (And be wary of Allah) By safeguarding His commands (أَوَامِرِهِ) and prohibitions (نَوَاهِيهِ), especially during the Hajj. وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ (and know that Allah is severe in retribution) For whoever does not fear Him, violates His command, and transgresses His limits (حُدُودَهُ).

(١٩٧) الْحَجُّ أَشْهُرٌ مَعْلُومَاتٌ (The Hajj is in months well-known) الْحَجُ (The Hajj) means the time (وقت) of its ihram (إحرامه) and its rituals (مناسكه). أَشْهُرٌ مَعْلُوماتٌ (is in months well-known), which are Shawwal (شوال), Dhu al-Qa'dah (ذو القعدة), and Dhu al-Hijjah (ذو الحجّة). This is narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both) in several reports. They (peace be upon them both) said: "It is not permissible for anyone to perform Hajj during months other than these, and whoever enters into the ihram (أحرم) for Hajj in months other than the months of Hajj, there is no Hajj for him."

فَمَنْ فَرَضَ فِيهِنَّ الْحَجَ (Whoever undertakes Hajj [rituals] in them) فَمَنْ فَرَضَ فِيهِنَّ الْحَجَ (Whoever undertakes Hajj [rituals] in them). In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "The undertaking (الفرض) is the talbiyah (التلبية), the marking (الاشعار), and the collaring (التقليد). Whichever of these he performs, he has undertaken the Hajj."

فَلَا رَفَثَ وَلَا فُسُوقَ وَلَا جِدَالَ فِي الْحَجِّ (there shall be no sexual contact, nor transgression, nor disputing during the Hajj) فَلا رَفَثَ وَلا فُسُوقَ (there shall be no sexual contact, nor transgression). It was recited with the raf' (الرفع) and tanwin (التنوين) in both words. وَلا جِدالَ فِي الْحَجِ (nor disputing during the Hajj) during its days. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): al-Rafath (الرفث) is sexual intercourse (الجماع), al-Fusuq (الفسوق) is lying (الكذب) and reviling (السباب), and al-Jidal (الجدال) is a man saying: "No, by Allah (لا والله)" and "Yes, by Allah (بلى والله)." And it is added in al-Kafi: He said concerning al-Jidal (الجدال), a sheep (شاة) [is due], and concerning al-Fusuq (الفسوق), a cow (بقرة) [is due], and al-Rafath (الرفث) invalidates the Hajj (فساد الحجّ).

وَمَا تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ يَعْلَمْهُ اللَّهُ وَتَزَوَّدُوا فَإِنَّ خَيْرَ الزَّادِ التَّقْوَى (And whatever good you do, Allah knows it. And take provision, for the best provision is God-wariness) وَما تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ يَعْلَمْهُ اللهُ (And whatever good you do, Allah knows it)—this is an encouragement toward righteousness (البر). ** وَتَزَوَّدُوا (And take provision)—[meaning] for your return (معادكم), [take] God-wariness (التّقوى). ** فَإِنَّ خَيْرَ الزَّادِ التَّقْوى (for the best provision is God-wariness). It is said that they used to perform Hajj without provision (زاد), thus becoming a burden (كلا) upon the people, so they were commanded to take provision and to beware of imposing (الإبرام) and burdening (التثقيل) the people.

وَاتَّقُونِ يَا أُولِي الْأَلْبَابِ (and be wary of Me, O people of understanding!) ** وَاتَّقُونِ يا أُولِي الْأَلْبابِ (and be wary of Me, O people of understanding!)—for the requirement of understanding (اللبّ) is the fear of Allah (خشية الله). He followed the encouragement toward God-wariness (التقوى) by [stating] that the intended purpose of it should be Allah, the Glorified, and dissociation (التبري) from all besides Him.

(١٩٨) لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَبْتَغُوا (198) There is no sin upon you in seeking in seeking فَضْلاً مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (bounty from your Lord). They used to feel sinful (يَتَأَثَّمُونَ) regarding trade during the Hajj, so the sin (الجناح) was lifted from them concerning that, as mentioned in al-Majma' narrated from them (peace be upon them). And in a narration: فَضْلاً (bounty) means forgiveness (مغفرة). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): فَضْلاً مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (bounty from your Lord) means provision (الرزق). When a man has exited his state of ihram and completed his rituals (نسكه), let him buy and sell during the season. فَإِذا أَفَضْتُمْ (And when you stream down) means you push yourselves forth in abundance, derived from (أَفَاضَ الْمَاءَ) when one pours water abundantly, مِنْ عَرَفاتٍ (from ‘Arafāt). فَإِذا أَفَضْتُمْ مِنْ عَرَفاتٍ فَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ عِنْدَ الْمَشْعَرِ الْحَرامِ (And when you stream down from ‘Arafāt, then remember Allah near the Holy Landmark.) In Tafsir al-Imam: "And you proceed to al-Muzdalifah, فَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ عِنْدَ الْمَشْعَرِ الْحَرامِ (then remember Allah near the Holy Landmark)." He said: [Remember Him] through His blessings (نعمائه) and His bounties (آلائه), and by sending blessings (الصلاة) upon the master of His prophets (سيد أنبيائه) and upon Ali, the master of His chosen ones (سيد أصفيائه). وَاذْكُرُوهُ كَما هَداكُمْ (and remember Him as He has guided you) to His religion (دينه) and belief in His Messenger. And it was said: meaning, remember Him with a good remembrance (ذكراً حسناً) just as He guided you with a good guidance (هداية حسنة). And it was said: meaning, a remembrance that corresponds to your guidance by Him. I say: The intent of the kāf (الكاف) in speech such as this is not similitude (التشبيه); rather, the intent is to justify the command (تعليل الطلب) by the existence of that which necessitates it, and that the thing commanded is not strange (غريب); rather, if it occurs, it is in its proper place (موضعه). The meaning is: Remember Him in return for His guidance to you, for He guided you, so it is fitting (فبالحرى) that you remember Him. This has many parallels (نظائر كثيرة) in speech, but it has been confused by many scholars (الأعلام). وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ (though before that you were indeed among the astray.) And indeed you were مِنْ قَبْلِهِ (before it)—that is, before the guidance (الهدى)—لَمِنَ الضَّالِّينَ (among the astray), the ignorant (الجاهلين), not knowing how to remember Him or worship Him. And in Tafsir al-Imam: الضَّالِّينَ (the astray) means those astray from His religion (دينه) before He guided you to His religion.

(199) ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا Then let your departure (إفاضتكم) be ** مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفاضَ النَّاسُ (from where the people depart). It was said: meaning from Arafat (عرفات).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Quraysh and their allies, who were among the الحمس (the zealous ones), would not stand (يقفون) with the people at Arafat (بعرفات) nor depart (يفيضون) from it. They would say: "We are the people of Allah the Almighty's Sanctuary (حرم الله تعالى), so we do not leave the Sanctuary (الحرم)." Thus, they would stand at al-Mash'ar (بالمشعر) and depart from it. So, Allah commanded them to stand at Arafat (بعرفات) and depart from it.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): the like of this is found in several narrations (أخبار).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He meant by ** النَّاسُ (the people) Abraham (إبراهيم), Ishmael (إسماعيل), and Isaac (إسحاق), and those after them who departed from Arafat (عرفات).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Husayn (peace be upon him): "We are ** النَّاسُ ** (the people)." And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a narration concerning the Hajj of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace), he said: Then he went early in the morning, and the people were with him. Quraysh used to depart from al-Muzdalifah (المزدلفة), which is Jam' (جمع), and they would prevent the people from departing from it. Then the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) approached, and Quraysh hoped that His departure would be from where they used to depart. So, Allah the Almighty revealed: ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفاضَ النَّاسُ وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا اللهَ (Then depart from where the people depart, and ask Allah for forgiveness), meaning Abraham (إبراهيم), Ishmael (إسماعيل), and Isaac (إسحاق), for their departure was from it, and the departure of those who came after them.

I say: Based on these narrations (الأخبار), the meaning of ** ثُمَ (then) is sequential order in rank (الترتيب في الرتبة) due to the difference between the two departures (الافاضتين), just as in your saying: "Be good to the people, then do not be good to anyone other than the noble one (الكريم)."

And in al-Majma', he raised a question, which is that ثُمَّ (then) denotes sequence, so what is the meaning of sequence here? He answered that our companions (أصحابنا) narrated that there is a case of advancement and delay (تقديماً وتأخيراً) here, the estimation (تقديره) of which is: ** لَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَنْ تَبْتَغُوا فَضْلاً مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفاضَ النَّاسُ فَإِذا أَفَضْتُمْ مِنْ عَرَفاتٍ فَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ عِنْدَ الْمَشْعَرِ الْحَرامِ وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا اللهَ (There is no sin upon you in seeking bounty from your Lord. Then depart from where the people depart. So when you depart from Arafat, remember Allah near the Holy Site, and ask Allah for forgiveness). And he mentioned another interpretation, which is that the intended meaning is the departure (الإفاضة) from al-Muzdalifah (المزدلفة) to Mina (منى) on the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النحر) before sunrise. He said: And the Ayah indicates this, because His saying ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا (Then depart) indicates that it is a second departure (إفاضة ثانية).

I say: This contradicts the narrations (الأخبار) reported concerning the reason for the revelation of the Ayah from both the paths of the الخاصّة (Shi'a) and the العامّة (Sunni), as previously mentioned, except for what is found in Tafsir al-Imam, for in it is: ** ثُمَّ أَفِيضُوا مِنْ حَيْثُ أَفاضَ النَّاسُ (Then depart from where the people depart), meaning: return from al-Mash'ar al-Haram (المشعر الحرام) from where the people returned from Jam' (جمع). He said: And the people (الناس) in this context are the pilgrims (الحاجّ) other than the الحمس, for the الحمس used not to depart from Jam' (جمع). This is as you see, and the knowledge is with Allah. ** وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا اللهَ (and ask Allah for forgiveness), meaning: seek forgiveness from Allah for your ignorance (جاهليّتكم) in changing the rituals (المناسك). ** إِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful). He forgives the sin of the one who seeks forgiveness (المستغفر) and shows mercy to him.

(٢٠٠) فَإِذا قَضَيْتُمْ مَناسِكَكُمْ (When you have finished your rituals) meaning, you have finished the acts of Hajj, فَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ كَذِكْرِكُمْ آباءَكُمْ أَوْ أَشَدَّ ذِكْراً (then remember Allah as you remember your fathers, or with a more intense remembrance.) So remember the remembrance of Allah through His bounties (آلائه) with you and His kindness (إحسانه) towards you, and be excessive in it, just as you do in the remembrance of your fathers through their deeds and their glorious achievements (مآثرهم), or even more intensely than that.

In Tafsir al-Imam: He gave them a choice between that and did not obligate them to have a more intense remembrance of Him than their remembrance of their fathers, even though the favors (نعم) of Allah upon them are more numerous and greater than the favors of their fathers upon them.

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When they finished the Hajj, they would gather there, enumerating the glories (مفاخر) and achievements (مآثرهم) of their fathers, and recalling their ancient days and their great favors (أياديهم الجسيمة). So, Allah, the Glorified, commanded them to remember Him instead of remembering their fathers in this place, or with a more intense remembrance (أشدّ ذكراً), or to exceed that by remembering the favors (نعم) of Allah, the Glorified, enumerating His bounties (آلاءه), and thanking His blessings (نعمائه). This is because even though their fathers had favors (أياد) and blessings (نعم) upon them, the favors of Allah, the Glorified, upon them are greater, and His favors (أياديه) with them are more magnificent (أفخم). And because He, the Glorified, is the Bestower of those achievements (المآثر) and glories (المفاخر) upon their fathers and upon them. فَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَقُولُ (Then among the people are those who say) — for the people are divided between one who is meager (مقلّ), seeking nothing by his remembrance except the world, and one who is abundant (مكثر), seeking by it the good of both abodes. So, they should be among those who are abundant (المكثرين). رَبَّنا آتِنا (‘Our Lord, give us) — meaning, make our giving and our grant فِي الدُّنْيا (in the world,’) specifically, وَما لَهُ فِي الْآخِرَةِ مِنْ خَلاقٍ (and he has no share in the Hereafter.) a portion (نصيب) and a share (حظ), because his concern (همّه) is restricted to the world; he does not perform any deed for the Hereafter, nor does he seek any good from it.

**وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَقُولُ رَبَّنا آتِنا فِي الدُّنْيا حَسَنَةً ** (201) And among them are those who say, 'Our Lord, grant us good in this world and good in the Hereafter, and save us from the punishment of the Fire.'

such as health (الصحة), security (الأمن), sufficiency (الكفاف), and success in goodness (توفيق الخير). وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً (and good in the Hereafter) such as mercy (الرحمة) and proximity (الزّلفة). وَقِنا عَذابَ النَّارِ (and save us from the punishment of the Fire) through forgiveness (المغفرة) and pardon (العفو).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: [The good (الحسنة) is] the pleasure (رضوان الله) of Allah and Paradise (الجنة) in the Hereafter, and abundance in livelihood (السعة في المعاش) and good character (حسن الخلق) in this world (الدنيا).

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): [The good (الحسنة)] in this world (الدنيا) is the righteous wife (المرأة الصالحة), and in the Hereafter (الآخرة) is the Hurā' (الحوراء). And the punishment of the Fire (عَذابَ النَّارِ) is the evil wife (المرأة السوء).

And it was said: The good (الحسنة) in this world (الدنيا) is knowledge (العلم) and worship (العبادة), and in the Hereafter (الآخرة) is Paradise (الجنّة). And the punishment of the Fire (عذاب النّار) is the desires (الشهوات) and the sins (الذّنوب) leading to it.

I say: All of that consists of examples (أمثلة) for what is intended by it, so there is no contradiction (تنافي) among them.

(202) أُولئِكَ (Those) In Tafsir al-Imam: Those who supplicate with this supplication, according to this description, ** لَهُمْ نَصِيبٌ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا (for them is a share of what they have earned). He said: [It is] from the reward (ثواب) of what they have earned in this world and the Hereafter.

I say: The reason it was said "what they have earned" is that the deeds themselves are embodied in beautiful forms (صور حسنة) by which their doer is blessed, or in ugly forms (صور قبيحة) by which their doer is tormented, as has been narrated in many reports (أخبار كثيرة) from the People of Infallibility (أهل العصمة). And in the Prophetic Hadith: "They are only your deeds returning to you." وَاللهُ سَرِيعُ الْحِسابِ (and Allah is swift at reckoning). He reckons all creatures, despite their multitude and the abundance of their deeds, in the measure of a blink of an eye, as has been narrated in the report (الخبر).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), that He said: Its meaning is that He reckons the creatures all at once (دفعة) just as He provides for them all at once (دفعة).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked how Allah, the Glorified, reckons the creation while they do not see Him. He replied: Just as Allah provides for them while they do not see Him.

In Tafsir al-Imam: Because no affair distracts Him from another affair, nor does one reckoning distract Him from another reckoning. So, when He reckons one person, He is, in that very state, reckoning the entirety. The reckoning of the entirety is completed with the completion of the reckoning of the one. This is like His saying: ما خَلْقُكُمْ وَلا بَعْثُكُمْ إِلَّا كَنَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ (15) (Your creation and your resurrection are but as a single soul). And what is close to this will come in Surat al-An'am.

I say: And the swiftness of reckoning (سرعة الحساب) has another meaning that converges with this meaning and supports it, which is that Allah reckons the servant in this world in every instant (آن) and moment (لحظة). He rewards him for his deed in every movement (حركة) and stillness (سكون). He recompenses his acts of obedience (طاعاته) with divine success (التوفيقات) and his acts of disobedience (معاصيه) with abandonment (الخذلانات). Thus, good attracts good, and evil calls toward evil. Whoever reckons his soul (نفسه) in this world knows this meaning. For this reason, it has been narrated: "Reckon your souls before you are reckoned." And this is among the secrets (الأسرار) that none touch except the purified ones (المطهرون).

(203) وَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ فِي أَيَّامٍ مَعْدُوداتٍ (203) (And remember Allah during the numbered days.) meaning the days of al-Tashriq (أيّام التشريق). And the remembrance of Allah therein is the takbir (التكبير) following the prayers, from the noon (ظهر) of the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النحر) until the dawn prayer (صلاة الفجر) of the third day for those who are in Mina (منى), and in the cities (الأمصار) until ten prayers. And the takbir (التكبير) is: الله أكبر الله أكبر لا إله إلّا الله والله أكبر الله أكبر ولله الحمد الله أكبر على ما هدأنا الله أكبر على ما رزقنا من بهيمة الأنعام (Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, there is no god but Allah, and Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, and to Allah belongs all praise. Allah is the Greatest for what He has guided us to. Allah is the Greatest for what He has provided us of the grazing livestock (بهيمة الأنعام)). This is narrated from them (peace be upon them) in al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and others. فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ hastened the departure (النفر) from Mina (منى) فِي يَوْمَيْنِ (in two days) after the Day of Sacrifice (يوم النحر), when he has finished stoning the Jamarat (رمي الجمار), فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَنْ تَأَخَّرَ (there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays) until he stones on the third day, فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (there is no sin upon him). It was said that the meaning of negating sin (نفي الإثم) through hastening (التعجل) and delaying (التأخر) is to offer a choice between the two, and a refutation of the People of Ignorance (اهل الجاهلية), for some of them considered the one who hastened sinful, and some of them considered the one who delayed sinful.

In al-Faqih: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about this verse, and He said: "It is not that this is wide-ranging, that he may do this if he wishes, or do that if he wishes. Rather, he returns forgiven, with no sin (إثم) upon him and no fault (ذنب) for him."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Him (peace be upon him) that He said: "He returns forgiven, with no fault (ذنب) for him."

لِمَنِ اتَّقى (for him who is Godwary.)

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "For him who is Godwary (لمن اتقى) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ)." He said: "And it is narrated: that he emerges from sins like the day his mother gave birth to him."

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "For him who is Godwary (لمن اتقى) of hunting (الصيد), meaning during his state of ihram (إحرامه). For if he commits it, it is not permissible for him to depart (ينفر) in the first departure (النفر الأول)." And al-Ayyashi narrated what is similar in meaning.
