And in al-Faqih, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "For him who is Godwary (لمن اتقى) of hunting (الصيد) until the people of Mina (منى) depart in the final departure (النفر الأخير)."

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "For him who is Godwary (لمن اتّقى) among them of hunting (الصيد), and Godwary of lewd talk (الرّفث), transgression (الفسوق), disputation (الجدال), and whatever Allah has forbidden him during his state of ihram (إحرامه)."

And in Tafsir al-Imam: فَمَنْ تَعَجَّلَ فِي يَوْمَيْنِ (But whoever hastens in two days) of the days of al-Tashriq (أيّام التشريق) and returns from his pilgrimage (حجّه) to the lands from which he departed, فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ وَمَنْ تَأَخَّرَ (there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays) until the completion of the third day, فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (there is no sin upon him)—meaning, there is no sin upon him from his past faults (ذنوبه السالفة), because all of them have been forgiven for him by this pilgrimage (حجّته) of his, which is coupled with his regret over them and his caution against them. لِمَنِ اتَّقى (for him who is Godwary) of committing the destructive sins (الموبقات) thereafter. For if he commits them, the sin (إثمها) of those acts will be upon him, and those past faults (الذنوب السالفة) will not be forgiven for him by a repentance (توبة) that He has invalidated by his destructive sins (موبقاته) committed afterwards. Rather, forgiveness is granted only by a repentance (توبة) that he renews.

I say: That is because the past faults (الذنوب السالفة) are what led their perpetrator to repeat them, since the motive for them after repentance (التوبة) is indeed the repetition.

And in al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It means whoever dies before returning to his family, فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (there is no sin upon him); and whoever delays, فَلا إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ (there is no sin upon him), for him who is Godwary (لِمَنِ اتَّقى) of the major sins (الكبائر)."

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): اتَّقى (is Godwary) of arrogance (الكبر), which is to be ignorant of the truth (الحق) and to slander its people (أهله).

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It is only for you, while the people are the common folk (سواد), and you are the pilgrims (الحاجّ)."

I say: He intended that the negation of sin (نفي الإثم) in both cases is exclusive to the people of Godwariness (التقوى), and they are the Shi'a (الشيعة), and no one else.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "By Allah, you are them! Indeed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'None remains firm upon the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) except the Godwary (المتّقون).'"

And in Tafsir al-Imam: وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (and be Godwary of Allah) O pilgrims (الحاجّ) whose past faults (سالف ذنوبهم) have been forgiven by their pilgrimage (حجّهم) coupled with their repentance (توبتهم). So, do not repeat the destructive sins (الموبقات), lest their burdens return to you and their bearing becomes heavy upon you, and they will not be forgiven for you except by a repentance (توبة) thereafter. وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (and know that you will be mustered toward Him.) so He may recompense you for what you do. And al-hashr (الحشر) is the gathering (الجمع) and the joining of what is scattered (ضمّ المتفرِّق).

(٢٠٤) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يُعْجِبُكَ قَوْلُهُ (204) And among the people is he whose speech about the life of the world impresses you, His saying pleases you and is magnified in your heart فِي الْحَياةِ الدُّنْيا in the life of the world by his manifestation of the religion (الدين) and Islam (الإسلام) to you, and his adornment in your presence with piety (الورع) and benevolence (الإحسان) ** وَيُشْهِدُ اللهَ عَلى ما فِي قَلْبِهِ and he calls Allah to witness what is in his heart, by swearing to you that he is a sincere believer (مؤمن مخلص), confirming his saying with his action وَهُوَ أَلَدُّ الْخِصامِ ** while he is the most contentious of adversaries. He is severe in enmity and argumentation against the Muslims.

Al-Qummi (reports): It was revealed concerning the Second (الثاني), and it is also said concerning Mu'awiyah.

And al-Ayyashi (reports) from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): So-and-so (فلان) and so-and-so (فلان).

I say: It includes the generality of the hypocrites (المنافقين), even if it was revealed specifically.

(٢٠٥) وَإِذا تَوَلَّى (And when he turns away) He turned his back and departed from you. It is said: He gained control of the affair and became a governor (والياً). ** سَعى فِي الْأَرْضِ لِيُفْسِدَ فِيها (he strives in the land to cause corruption in it) Meaning, through disbelief (بالكفر) which contradicts what he manifested, and injustice (والظلم) which is contrary to what he promised. ** وَيُهْلِكَ الْحَرْثَ (and to destroy the crops) The cultivation (الزّرع), by burning it or corrupting it. ** وَالنَّسْلَ (and the livestock) The offspring (الذرّية), by killing the animals, thereby cutting off their progeny (نسله).

And in al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** الْحَرْثَ (the crops) in this context means the religion (الدين), and ** وَالنَّسْلَ (the livestock) means the people (الناس).

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): ** يُهْلِكَ الْحَرْثَ وَالنَّسْلَ (he destroys the crops and the livestock) through his injustice (بظلمه) and the wickedness of his conduct (سوء سيرته).

I say: And among these [consequences] is that Allah withholds the rain due to the ill-omen (بشؤم) of his injustice, thereby destroying the crops (الحرث) and the livestock (النسل), in addition to other results of injustice (نتائج الظلم). وَاللهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْفَسادَ (and Allah does not like corruption). He does not approve of it, nor does He abandon the punishment for it.

(٢٠٦) وَإِذا قِيلَ لَهُ اتَّقِ اللهَ (206) When he is told, 'Be wary of Allah,' And abandon the evil of your deed (صنيعتك), أَخَذَتْهُ الْعِزَّةُ بِالْإِثْمِ arrogance carries him to sin. The pride (الأنفة) and the zeal (حميّة) of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية) carried him to the sin (الإثم) which he is commanded to guard against (باتّقائه), and compelled him to commit it (ارتكابه) out of obstinacy (لجاجاً), derived from your saying: "I seized him (أخذته) with such-and-such," meaning, "I carried him to it (حملته عليه) and compelled him to it (وألزمته إياه)."

So he increases evil upon his evil, and adds injustice (ظلماً) to his injustice (ظلمه). ** فَحَسْبُهُ جَهَنَّمُ ** Hell will suffice him; It suffices him as recompense (جزاء) and punishment (عذاباً) for the evil of his action. وَلَبِئْسَ الْمِهادُ and it is surely an evil resting place. Meaning, the bed (الفراش) which He prepares for him (يمهدها) and in which he will remain perpetually (دائماً فيها). Thus were the three verses interpreted.

(This interpretation is found) in Tafsir al-Imam, except for what was attributed to others.

(٢٠٧) وَمِنَ النَّاسِ مَنْ يَشْرِي يبيع نَفْسَهُ ببذلها لله ابْتِغاءَ مَرْضاتِ اللهِ طلباً لرضاه فيعمل بطاعته ويأمر الناس بها (207) And among the people is he who sells his soul, seeking the pleasure of Allah. The general populace (العامّة) narrated from a group of the Companions (الصحابة) and the Successors (التابعين). And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) and a number of our companions (أصحابنا) narrated from our Imams (أئمتنا) in several reports: that it was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) when he slept on the Prophet's bed and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم fled to the cave (الغار).

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): that what is meant by the Ayah is the man who is killed for enjoining the right (الأمر بالمعروف) and forbidding the wrong (النهي عن المنكر).

I say: meaning that it is general (عامة) even though it was revealed for a specific instance (خاصّة).

And in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him): These are the best of the companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم; the people of Mecca tormented them to turn them away from their religion, and among them are Bilal (بلال), Suhayb (صهيب), Khabbab (خباب), and Ammar ibn Yasir (عمّار بن ياسر) and his father.

وَاللهُ رَؤُفٌ بِالْعِبادِ (And Allah is most kind to the servants.) It is narrated: When he (Ali) slept on his bed, Gabriel (جبرائيل) stood by his head and Michael (ميكائيل) stood by his feet, and Gabriel was calling out, "Bravo! Bravo! (بَخّ بَخّ) Who is like you, O Ali ibn Abi Talib (عليّ بن أبي طالب)? Allah boasts of you to the angels."

And in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him): As for those who seek the pleasure of their Lord, He will grant them the utmost of their hopes and increase them beyond that which their aspirations have not reached. And as for the wicked (الفاجرون), He deals gently with them in calling them to His obedience, and He does not withhold His great generosity (كرامته) from anyone whom He knows will repent of his sin.

(٢٠٨) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا ادْخُلُوا فِي السِّلْمِ (O you who have faith! Enter into peace) (It means) in submission (الاستسلام) and obedience (الطاعة). And it was read with fathah (السَّلْمِ), and it carries the same meaning.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "It is our Wilayah (ولايتنا)." And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It is concerning the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him)." And narrated from both of them: "They were commanded to obey us and know us." كَافَّةً all together (جميعاً). وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ by separation (التفرق) and division (التفريق).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): ** السِّلْمِ is the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali and the Imams (peace be upon them) and the successors (الأوصياء) after him. And خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ is the Wilayah (ولاية) of so-and-so and so-and-so (فلان وفلان). And in one narration: "It is the Wilayah (ولاية) of the second (الثاني) and the first (الأول)."

And in Tafsir al-Imam: ** السِّلْمِ (means) in peaceful submission (المسالمة) to the religion of Islam. ** كَافَّةً (means) as a group (جماعة). Enter into it and bring in all of Islam (جميع الإسلام), so accept it and act upon it. And do not be among those who accept part of it and act upon it, but reject part of it and abandon it. He said: And part of this is entering into the acceptance of the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali, for it is like entering into the acceptance of the prophethood of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. For he is not a Muslim who says that Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is the Messenger of Allah, and acknowledges him, but does not acknowledge that Ali is his successor (وصيه), his Caliph (خليفته), and the best of his Ummah (أمته). And He said: ** خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ (are) that which he leads you toward from the paths of error (الغيّ) and misguidance (الضلالة), and commands you to commit destructive sins (الآثام الموبقات). إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ manifest in enmity (ظاهر العداوة).

(٢٠٩) فَإِنْ زَلَلْتُمْ (If you slip up) from entering into peace (السلم). ** مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَتْكُمُ الْبَيِّناتُ (The arguments (الحجج) and the evidences (الشواهد) that what you were called to is true). فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ (Dominant (غالب), Whom vengeance upon you does not render incapable). ** حَكِيمٌ (He does not take vengeance except with justice (بالحق)). But if you slip up after the manifest proofs have come to you, then know that Allah is All-mighty, All-wise. (209)

(210) هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ اللهُ (Do they await anything but that Allah should come to them) meaning, that the command of Allah or His might (بأس) should come to them فِي ظُلَلٍ (in the shadows), which is the plural of ẓullah (ظُلَّة), and it is that which shades you مِنَ الْغَمامِ (of the clouds), meaning from the white clouds which are the expected source (مَظِنَّة) of mercy, so if the punishment comes from them, it is more severe ** وَالْمَلائِكَةُ ** (and the angels). And the angels will come if it is read with the rafʿ (الرفع) (nominative case), and [He will come] with them if it is read with the jarr (الجرّ) (genitive case).

In al-Uyun and al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) regarding: ** إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ اللهُ (but that Allah should come to them), [He said: it means] with the angels ** فِي ظُلَلٍ مِنَ الْغَمامِ (in the shadows of the clouds). He said: And thus it was revealed: وَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ (and the matter is decided), meaning the command for their destruction is completed and finished. ** وَإِلَى اللهِ (and to Allah) تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ (all matters are returned). And it was read with the opening of the tāʾ (التاء) and the breaking of the jīm (الجيم) [i.e., تَرْجِعُ] wherever it occurs.

In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Meaning, do these deniers await, after Our clarification of the signs (الآيات) to them and Our cutting off their excuses (مَعَاذِير) with miracles (المعجزات), ** إِلَّا أَنْ يَأْتِيَهُمُ اللهُ فِي ظُلَلٍ مِنَ الْغَمامِ (but that Allah should come to them in the shadows of the clouds), and that ** الْمَلائِكَةُ ** (the angels) should come to them, just as they proposed to you their impossible proposal in this world regarding the coming of Allah, upon Whom coming is not permissible, and their false proposal regarding the coming of the angels, who do not come except with the cessation of this worship (التعبد), because that is the time for the coming of the angels (الأملاك) with destruction (الإهلاك). Thus, in their proposal for the coming of the angels, they are ignorant. وَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ** (and the matter is decided). Meaning, do they await the coming of the angels? For when they come, that is when the matter of their destruction is decided.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) who said: When Allah decides (بَدَا لَهُ) to make His creation manifest and gather them for that which is inevitable, He commands a caller (مُنَادِيًا) to call out, and mankind (الإنس) and jinn (الجن) gather faster than the blink of an eye. Then He permits the lowest heaven (السماء الدنيا) to descend, and it is behind the people. And He permits the second heaven to descend, and it is double the size of the one preceding it. When the inhabitants of the lowest heaven see it, they say: "Has our Lord come?" They say: "No, but He is coming," meaning His command, until every heaven descends, with each one being behind the other, and it is double the size of the one preceding it. Then the command of Allah descends ** فِي ظُلَلٍ مِنَ الْغَمامِ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ وَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ (in the shadows of the clouds, and the angels, and the matter is decided). And to your Lord تُرْجَعُ الْأُمُورُ (all matters are returned). Then He commands a caller to call out: ** يا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ إِنِ اسْتَطَعْتُمْ أَنْ تَنْفُذُوا مِنْ أَقْطارِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ فَانْفُذُوا لا تَنْفُذُونَ إِلَّا بِسُلْطانٍ (O company of jinn and humans! If you can pass through the confines of the heavens and the earth, then pass through. You will not pass through except with a power).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: He descends in seven domes (قِبَاب) of light, and it is not known in which one He is when He descends in the plain (ظَهْر) of Kufa (الكوفة). This is when He descends. And in another narration from him (peace be upon him), he said: It is as if I see the Qāʾim (القائم) of my Household (أهل بيتي) having ascended your Najaf (نجفكم), having unfurled the banner of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. When he unfurls it, the angels of Badr descend upon him. And he said: He is descending in domes (قِبَاب) of light when He descends in the plain of Kufa (الكوفة) upon al-Faruq (الفاروق). This is when He descends. As for قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ (and the matter is decided), it is the marking (الوَسْم) upon the snout (الخُرْطُوم) on the Day the disbeliever is marked.

I say: Perhaps the intended meaning is that He descends upon a command by which He distinguishes between the believer (المؤمن) and the disbeliever (الكافر), and that what is meant by the matter being decided (قضاء الأمر) is the distinction of one from the other by marking the snout (خُرْطُوم) of the disbeliever, and that is during the Return (الرجعة).

(211) سَلْ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ كَمْ آتَيْناهُمْ مِنْ آيَةٍ بَيِّنَةٍ (211) Ask the Children of Israel how many a manifest sign We gave them.

[This refers to] a manifest miracle (مُعْجِزَة ظَاهِرَة) at the hands of their prophets, or a sign (آيَة) in the Torah bearing witness to the truth of Muhammad's prophethood.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He used to recite كَمْ آتَيْناهُمْ مِنْ آيَةٍ بَيِّنَةٍ (how many a manifest sign We gave them), and among them were those who believed, and among them were those who denied (جَحَدَ), and among them were those who affirmed (أَقَرَّ), and among them were those who altered (بَدَّلَ).

And al-Ayyashi (الْعَيَّاشِيّ) did not mention the recitation (الْقِرَاءَة), but rather narrated the addition (الزِّيَادَة) as if it were an interpretation (تَفْسِير), and he mentioned أَنْكَرَ (denied) instead of بَدَّلَ (altered). [Regarding] وَمَنْ يُبَدِّلْ نِعْمَةَ اللهِ (Whoever changes Allah’s blessing)—His signs (آيَات) which are the cause of guidance (الْهُدَى) and salvation (النَّجَاة), which are among the greatest (أَجَلّ) favors (النِّعَم)—he makes them the cause of misguidance (الضَّلَالَة) and the increase of defilement (الرِّجْس) مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَتْهُ (after it has come to him), meaning, after he knew it or was able to know it, فَإِنَّ اللهَ شَدِيدُ الْعِقابِ (Allah is indeed severe in retribution). So He punishes him with the severest punishment because he committed the severest crime (جَرِيمَة).

(212) زُيِّنَ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا (212) The life of the world has been glamorized for the faithless, It was made beautiful in their eyes, and its love was absorbed into their hearts, until they rushed headlong after it. ** وَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا and they ridicule the faithful. [They ridicule] the poor among the believers who have no share of it, and those among the believers who are pious. ** فَوْقَهُمْ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ But those who are Godwary shall be above them on the Day of Resurrection. Because they [the believers] will be in علّيين (the highest ranks) and in honor (الكرامة), while they [the disbelievers] will be in سجين (the lowest depths) and in regret (الندامة). ** وَاللهُ يَرْزُقُ مَنْ يَشاءُ And Allah provides for whomever He wishes [He provides] in both abodes (الدارين) بِغَيْرِ حِسابٍ without any reckoning. without measure (بغير تقدير). For He expands [provision] in the world, sometimes as a gradual enticement (استدراجاً) and other times as a trial (ابتلاء), and He grants the people of Paradise what cannot be counted.

(٢١٣) كانَ النَّاسُ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (213) The people were a single nation. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: "This was before the sending of Noah (peace be upon him). They were a single nation, then it became manifest to Allah (فَبَدَا لِلَّهِ), so He sent the Messengers before Noah (peace be upon him)." He was asked: "Were they upon guidance (هُدَى) or upon error (ضَلَالَة)?" He said: "Rather, they were astray (ضُلَالًا), neither believers, nor disbelievers, nor polytheists." And in another narration from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "That was because when Adam and his righteous progeny passed away, Seth (شِيث), his successor (وَصِيّه), remained, unable to manifest the religion of Allah that Adam and his righteous progeny were upon. This was because Cain (قَابِيل) threatened him with murder, just as he had killed his brother Abel (هَابِيل). So, he proceeded among them with dissimulation (التَّقِيَّة) and concealment (الْكُتْمَان). They increased in error (ضَلَالًا) every day until the successor (الْوَصِيّ) retreated to an island in the sea, worshipping Allah. Then it became manifest to Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, that He should send the Messengers. If these ignorant people were asked, they would say that the matter has been concluded (قَدْ فُرِغَ مِنَ الْأَمْر), and they would lie. It is only a matter that Allah decrees every year." Then He recited regarding it: يفرق كل امر حكيم (Every wise command is distinguished), so Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, decrees what will occur in that year, whether hardship (شِدَّة), ease (رَخَاء), rain (مَطَر), or otherwise. He was asked: "Were they astray (ضَلَالًا) before the Prophets, or upon guidance (هُدَى)?" He said: "They were not upon guidance (هُدَى). They were upon the primordial nature (فِطْرَة) of Allah upon which He created mankind: لا تبديل لخلق الله (There is no altering Allah’s creation). They would not have been guided until Allah guided them. Do you not hear the saying of Abraham: لَئِنْ لَمْ يَهْدِنِي رَبِّي لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ (If my Lord does not guide me, I will surely be among the astray people)? meaning, forgetting the covenant (الْمِيثَاق)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him), He said: "Before Noah, there was a nation of error (ضَلَال). Then it became manifest to Allah (فَبَدَا لِلَّهِ), so He sent the Messengers. It is not as they say, 'He has not ceased' (لَمْ يَزَل), and they lie. What occurs of hardship (شِدَّة) or ease (رَخَاء) or rain (مَطَر) is distinguished on the Night of Decree (لَيْلَة الْقَدْر), according to what He wills to decree until the like of it."

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Before Noah, they were a single nation upon the primordial nature (فِطْرَة) of Allah, neither guided (مُهْتَدُون) nor astray (ضَلَالًا). Then Allah sent the Prophets."

I say: The error (الضَّلَال) negated in this narration is intended to mean adherence to polytheism (الشِّرْك) or disbelief (الْكُفْر), while the error (الضَّلَال) affirmed in the previous narration means being devoid of religion (الْخُلُو عَنِ الدِّين). Thus, there is no contradiction between the two.

And al-Qummi [said regarding]: ** كانَ النَّاسُ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً (The people were a single nation): Before Noah, they were upon a single doctrine (مَذْهَب), then they differed, so Allah sent the Prophets. It is said that [the phrase 'then they differed'] was omitted because His saying فِيمَا اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ** (concerning what they differed in) indicates it.

I say: There is no indication in it that the difference (الِاخْتِلَاف) occurred before the sending [of the Prophets]. Rather, the apparent meaning is that the difference (الِاخْتِلَاف) in the Ayah refers to their difference in religion after the sending [of the Prophets], especially since the apparent meaning of the previous narrations indicates that there was no difference (اخْتِلَاف) before the sending. And it is said: Rather, they differed after the sending, concerning the Messengers.

فَبَعَثَ اللهُ النَّبِيِّينَ مُبَشِّرِينَ وَمُنْذِرِينَ Then Allah sent the prophets as bearers of good news and as warners, so that He might establish the proof (الْحُجَّة) against them. This is according to al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

وَأَنْزَلَ مَعَهُمُ الْكِتابَ بِالْحَقِّ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَ النَّاسِ فِيمَا اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ وَمَا اخْتَلَفَ فِيهِ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ أُوتُوهُ and He sent down with them the Book with the truth, that it may judge between the people concerning what they differed in. And none differed in it except those who were given it, They made the revelation of the Book, which was sent down to remove disagreement (الْخِلَاف), a cause for the severity of the difference (الِاخْتِلَاف).

مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّناتُ بَغْياً after the manifest proofs had come to them, out of envy among themselves. out of envy (حَسَدًا) and injustice (ظُلْمًا) among themselves, due to their greed for the world (الدُّنْيَا).

فَهَدَى اللهُ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لِمَا اخْتَلَفُوا فِيهِ مِنَ الْحَقِ Then Allah guided those who had faith to the truth about which they differed, [The phrase] مِنَ الْحَقِ (of the truth) is an explanation (بَيَان) for لِمَا (concerning what).

بِإِذْنِهِ وَاللهُ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ. by His will, and Allah guides whomever He wishes to a straight path.

(٢١٤) أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَنْ تَدْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِكُمْ مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلِكُم مَسَّتْهُمُ الْبَأْساءُ وَالضَّرَّاءُ وَزُلْزِلُوا حَتَّى يَقُولَ الرَّسُولُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ مَتى نَصْرُ اللهِ أَلا إِنَّ نَصْرَ اللهِ قَرِيبٌ (214) Did you suppose that you would enter paradise while there has not yet come to you the like of [what happened to] those who passed away before you? Hardship and distress afflicted them, and they were convulsed, until the Apostle and those who had faith with him said, ‘When will Allah’s help come?’ Look! Allah’s help is indeed near.

It is said that the phrase أَحَسبتم (Did you suppose) is a way of deeming the supposition unlikely (استبعاد للحسبان) and an encouragement for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the believers toward patience (الصبر) and steadfastness (الثبات) against those who differed with him and showed enmity toward him. وَلَمَّا يَأْتِكُمْ [It refers to something] whose coming is expected (متوقّع إتيانه) and awaited (منتظر). ** مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ خَلَوْا مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ [Meaning] their condition (حالهم) which is an example (مثل) in severity (الشدة). مَسَّتْهُمُ الْبَأْساءُ وَالضَّرَّاءُ This is an explanation of the example (بيان للمثل): [afflictions] such as killing (القتل) and being driven out from family (الأهل) and wealth (المال). وَزُلْزِلُوا And they were severely shaken (ازعاجاً شديداً) by the hardships (الشدائد) that afflicted them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He used to recite: وَزُلْزِلُوا ثُمَّ زُلْزِلُوا (and they were convulsed, then they were convulsed) until ** حَتَّى يَقُولَ ** (until the saying). And it was recited with the nominative case (بالرفع): ** الرَّسُولُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ ** (the Apostle and those who had faith with him). [This occurred] due to the extremity of the severity (تناهي الشدة) and the prolongation of the duration (استطالة المدة), such that the ropes of patience (حبال الصبر) were severed: ** مَتى نَصْرُ اللهِ ** (When will Allah’s help come?). [This was said] seeking to hasten it (استبطاءً له) due to its delay. ** أَلا إِنَّ نَصْرَ اللهِ قَرِيبٌ ** (Look! Allah’s help is indeed near). This was said to them as a fulfillment (إسعافاً لهم) of their request for immediate victory (عاجل النصر). It is said that in this [verse] there is an indication that reaching Allah, the Exalted, and achieving honor (الكرامة) in His presence is attained by rejecting caprice (الهوى) and pleasures (اللذّات), and by enduring hardships (الشدائد) and spiritual disciplines (الرياضات), just as he (peace be upon him) said: "Paradise is surrounded by detestable things (المكاره), and the Fire is surrounded by desires (الشهوات)."

In al-Khara'ij, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), he said: "Why do you stretch forth your eyes? Are you not safe? Indeed, those before you who were upon the same [path] as you are now, were seized, and their hands and feet were cut off, and they were crucified." Then he recited this verse.

(٢١٥) يَسْئَلُونَكَ ما ذا يُنْفِقُونَ (215) They ask you what they should spend. meaning: what thing should they spend. قُلْ ما أَنْفَقْتُمْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ Say, ‘Whatever good you spend meaning: of wealth فَلِلْوالِدَيْنِ وَالْأَقْرَبِينَ وَالْيَتامى وَالْمَساكِينِ وَابْنِ السَّبِيلِ is for the parents and the relatives, the orphans, the needy and the traveler.’ They were asked about what is spent (المنفق), but the answer was given by clarifying the recipients (المصرف) because that is more important, since the expenditure is not considered valid unless it falls upon its proper place. It is said that the question also implicitly included the recipients (المصرف), even if it was not mentioned in the Ayah, based on what is narrated: that ‘Amr ibn al-Jumūḥ al-Anṣārī was an old man (هِماً) of great wealth. So he said: "O Messenger of Allah, what should we spend from our wealth, and where should we place it?" So, the Ayah was revealed. وَما تَفْعَلُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللهَ بِهِ عَلِيمٌ And whatever good you do, Allah indeed knows it. If you do good, then Allah knows its essence (كنهه) and fully pays its reward.

(٢١٦) كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْقِتالُ وَهُوَ كُرْهٌ لَكُمْ (216) Fighting has been prescribed for you, though it is repulsive to you. difficult for you, naturally repulsive. وَعَسى أَنْ تَكْرَهُوا شَيْئاً It may be that you dislike something at present وَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ while it is good for you. in the outcome. And thus is most of what they have been obligated with, for the nature dislikes it, yet it is the pivot of their righteousness and the cause of their success. وَعَسى أَنْ تُحِبُّوا شَيْئاً at present وَهُوَ شَرٌّ لَكُمْ while it is bad for you. in the outcome. And thus is most of what they have been forbidden from, for the soul loves it and desires it, yet it leads them to ruin. And He only mentioned 'perhaps' (عسى) because when the soul is disciplined, the matter is reversed for it. But Allah knows what is good for you وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ and you do not know. that.

(٢١٧) يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الشَّهْرِ الْحَرامِ قِتالٍ فِيهِ (217) They ask you about the sacred month, if fighting is allowed in it. It is said that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Jahsh, his maternal cousin, with a detachment (سرية) in Jumada al-Akhira, two months before the Battle of Badr, to lie in wait for a caravan belonging to Quraysh. In the caravan were 'Amr ibn 'Abd Allah al-Hadrami and three others with him. They killed him, captured two, and drove off the caravan, which contained the trade goods of al-Ta'if. This occurred on the first of Rajab, though they thought it was still Jumada al-Akhira. Quraysh said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has violated the Sacred Month, a month in which the fearful feel safe and people disperse to their livelihoods." This was difficult for the members of the detachment, and they said: "We will not leave until our repentance is revealed." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم returned the wealth of the caravan and the captives, and then [the verse] was revealed. And al-Qummi narrated something close to this, with additions at the end: Quraysh wrote to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم [saying]: "You have violated the Sacred Month, shed blood in it, and taken wealth." Much discussion arose concerning this. The Companions said: "O Messenger of Allah, is killing permissible in the Sacred Month?" So, [the verse] was revealed. قُلْ قِتالٌ فِيهِ كَبِيرٌ Say: Fighting in it is a great sin. [It is] enormous (عظيم). The discourse ends here, then He began and said: وَصَدٌّ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ but barring [people] from the way of Allah meaning: but what they did by barring [people] from the way of Allah, meaning Islam, وَكُفْرٌ بِهِ and disbelief in Him and their disbelief in Allah, وَالْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرامِ and the Sacred Mosque and the Sacred Mosque (وَبِالْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ), based on the estimation of the preposition باء (bi-), or [meaning] their barring [people] from the Sacred Mosque, provided that disbelief in Allah (الْكُفْرُ بِاللهِ) is the very act of barring [people] from the way of Allah (الصَّدُّ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ), so that it is not extraneous between the things conjoined (المعطوفين), or that its precedence, though its proper place is later, is due to the extreme importance given to it, as in His Almighty saying: وَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ كُفُواً أَحَدٌ (and there is none comparable to Him). وَإِخْراجُ أَهْلِهِ and expelling its people and the expulsion of the people of the Sacred Mosque, who are the Messenger of Allah and the believers, مِنْهُ أَكْبَرُ from it is a greater sin [it is] a greater burden (أعظم وزراً) with Allah than the killing that occurred in the Sacred Month. وَالْفِتْنَةُ أَكْبَرُ مِنَ الْقَتْلِ and persecution is a greater sin than killing. And what they committed, such as expulsion and polytheism (الشرك), is more heinous than the killing that occurred. وَلا يَزالُونَ يُقاتِلُونَكُمْ حَتَّى يَرُدُّوكُمْ عَنْ دِينِكُمْ They will not cease fighting you until they turn you away from your religion so that they may turn you away from it. This is a report concerning the permanence of the enmity of the disbelievers towards them, and that they will not desist from it until they turn them away from this religion of theirs, إِنِ اسْتَطاعُوا if they can. [This phrase implies] deeming their ability unlikely (استبعاد لاستطاعتهم) and giving notice that they will not turn them away. وَمَنْ يَرْتَدِدْ مِنْكُمْ عَنْ دِينِهِ And whoever of you turns away from his religion [meaning] returns from it, فَيَمُتْ وَهُوَ كافِرٌ and dies while he is faithless meaning, upon apostasy (الردة), فَأُولئِكَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمالُهُمْ فِي الدُّنْيا their works shall fail in the world due to the loss of the fruits of Islam, وَالْآخِرَةِ and the Hereafter. due to the loss of the reward. وَأُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيهَا خالِدُونَ They shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it [forever], like all other disbelievers (الكفّار).

(٢١٨) **إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَالَّذِينَ هاجَرُوا وَجاهَدُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أُولئِكَ يَرْجُونَ رَحْمَتَ اللهِ ** (218) Indeed, those who have believed, and those who have emigrated and struggled in the way of Allah—it is they who expect Allah’s mercy.

It was said that it was revealed concerning the story of Ibn Jahsh and his companions, and their killing of al-Hadrami in Rajab, when some people thought that even if they were safe from sin (الإِثم), they would not have any reward (أجر). ** وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ for what they did by mistake (خطأ) and lack of caution (قلّة احتياط), ** رَحِيمٌ by granting abundant reward (الأجر) and recompense (الثواب).

(٢١٩) يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ (219) They question you concerning wine and gambling. meaning, concerning the practice (تعاطيهما) of both. قُلْ فِيهِما (Say, 'In them) in the practice of both, إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ (is a great sin,') because they are the key to everything. And it was recited (وقرئ) with the letter thā’ (الثاء المثلثة) [i.e., كثير (much) instead of كبير (great)]. وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ (and benefits for the people) such as merriment (الطرب), earning money, and other things. وَإِثْمُهُما أَكْبَرُ مِنْ نَفْعِهِما (but their sin is greater than their benefit.') meaning, the corruptions (المفاسد) that arise from them are greater than the benefits expected from them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), that He said: "Indeed, wine (الخمر) is the head of every sin and the key to every evil." And He said: "Indeed, Allah made locks for evil, and He made the keys to them intoxicating drink (الشراب)." And He said: "Allah has not been disobeyed by anything more severe than drinking intoxicants (المسكر). Indeed, one of them abandons the obligatory prayer (الصلاة الفريضة) and assaults his mother, his sister, and his daughter while he is senseless (لا يعقل)." And He said: "It is worse than abandoning prayer (ترك الصلاة) because he reaches a state where he does not recognize his Lord." And He said: "Allah forgives everyone in the month of Ramadan except for three: the drinker of intoxicants (صاحب مسكر), the owner of two sheep (صاحب شاهَيْنِ), or the hostile person (مشاحن)." And He said: "Everything upon which gambling (قومر) is played is maysir (ميسر)." And He interpreted the hostile person (المشاحِن) as the person of innovation (صاحب البدعة) who separates from the community (الجماعة).

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: "Allah never sent a prophet except that it was in the knowledge of Allah the Almighty that when He perfected His religion for him, the prohibition of wine (تحريم الخمر) would be included in it. Wine has never ceased to be forbidden. Rather, they are moved from one characteristic (خصلة) to another, and if that were imposed upon them all at once (جملة), they would be cut off short of the religion." He said: "There is no one kinder (أرفق) than Allah the Almighty. And part of His kindness, may He be blessed and exalted, is that He moves them from one characteristic to another. If it were imposed upon them all at once, they would perish."

And narrated from them (peace be upon them): The first thing revealed concerning the prohibition of wine was the Almighty's saying: يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ قُلْ فِيهِما إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَإِثْمُهُما أَكْبَرُ مِنْ نَفْعِهِما (They question you concerning wine and gambling. Say, 'In them is a great sin, and benefits for the people, but their sin is greater than their benefit.') When this verse was revealed, the people sensed its prohibition and knew that sin (الإثم) was something that ought to be avoided. But Allah the Almighty did not impose it upon them completely (من كل طريق) because He said: وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ (and benefits for the people). Then Allah revealed another verse: إِنَّمَا الْخَمْرُ وَالْمَيْسِرُ وَالْأَنْصابُ وَالْأَزْلامُ رِجْسٌ مِنْ عَمَلِ الشَّيْطانِ فَاجْتَنِبُوهُ (Indeed, wine, gambling, idols, and divining arrows are abominations of Satan's handiwork, so shun them) لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (so that you may be felicitous). This verse was stronger than the first and harsher in prohibition. Then He made it a third time with another verse, which was harsher than the first and the second, and more severe. So, the Almighty said: إِنَّما يُرِيدُ الشَّيْطانُ أَنْ يُوقِعَ بَيْنَكُمُ الْعَداوَةَ وَالْبَغْضاءَ فِي الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ وَيَصُدَّكُمْ عَنْ ذِكْرِ اللهِ وَعَنِ الصَّلاةِ فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُنْتَهُونَ (All that Satan wants is to incite enmity and hatred between you with wine and gambling, and to avert you from the remembrance of Allah and from prayer. Will you then desist?).

So, the Almighty commanded shunning them and explained the reasons (عللها) for them, and because of which He forbade them. Then Allah the Almighty clarified and revealed His prohibition in the fourth verse, along with what was indicated in this aforementioned preceding verse, by His saying, the Almighty: قُلْ إِنَّما حَرَّمَ رَبِّيَ الْفَواحِشَ ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها وَما بَطَنَ وَالْإِثْمَ وَالْبَغْيَ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ (Say, 'My Lord has only forbidden indecencies, both outward and inward, and sin and unjust aggression'). And He, may He be mighty and majestic, said in the first verse: يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ قُلْ فِيهِما إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ (They question you concerning wine and gambling. Say, 'In them is a great sin, and benefits for the people'). Then He said in the fourth verse: قُلْ إِنَّما حَرَّمَ رَبِّيَ الْفَواحِشَ ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها وَما بَطَنَ وَالْإِثْمَ (Say, 'My Lord has only forbidden indecencies, both outward and inward, and sin'). Thus, He, may He be mighty and majestic, informed that sin (الإثم) is in wine and other things, and that it is forbidden. This is because when Allah the Almighty intends to make an ordinance obligatory (يفترض فريضة), He reveals it gradually (شيئاً بعد شيء) until people accustom themselves to it and become settled regarding Allah's command and prohibition concerning it. This method, stemming from Allah the Almighty's management (التدبير) of them, is more correct (أصوب), closer for them to adopt it, and less likely to cause them aversion to it.

And from Ali ibn Yaqtin, he said: Al-Mahdi (1) asked Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) whether wine (الخمر) was forbidden (محرّمة) in the Book of Allah the Almighty, for people only knew the prohibition (النهي) of it and did not know its absolute forbidding (التحريم). So, Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) said to him: "Rather, it is forbidden in the Book of Allah, O Commander of the Faithful." He asked Him: "In which place is it forbidden in the Book of Allah, may He be mighty and majestic, O Abu al-Hasan?" He replied: "The saying of Allah the Almighty: قُلْ إِنَّما حَرَّمَ رَبِّيَ الْفَواحِشَ ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها وَما بَطَنَ وَالْإِثْمَ وَالْبَغْيَ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِ (Say, 'My Lord has only forbidden indecencies, both outward and inward, and sin and unjust aggression')" until He said: "As for al-ithm (الإثم), it is wine itself. And Allah has said in another place: يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْخَمْرِ وَالْمَيْسِرِ قُلْ فِيهِما إِثْمٌ كَبِيرٌ وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ (They question you concerning wine and gambling. Say, 'In them is a great sin, and benefits for the people'). So, al-ithm (الإثم) in the Book of Allah is wine and gambling (الميسر), and their sin is greater, just as Allah the Almighty said." Al-Mahdi said: "O Ali ibn Yaqtin, this is a Hashimite legal ruling (فتوى هاشمية)." He said: I replied to him: "You have spoken the truth, by Allah, O Commander of the Faithful. All praise belongs to Allah Who did not let this knowledge depart from you, the People of the House (أهل البيت)." He said: By Allah, al-Mahdi could not restrain himself from saying to me: "You have spoken the truth, O Rāfiḍī (يا رافضي)." And what we have omitted from this narrative will come in Surat al-A'raf, if Allah the Almighty wills.

وَيَسْئَلُونَكَ ما ذا يُنْفِقُونَ And they question you concerning what they should spend. It is said that Ibn al-Jumūḥ also asked Him. He first asked about the spender and the recipient, then He asked about the manner (كيفية) and amount (قدره) of the spending (الأنفال). قُلِ الْعَفْوَ (Say, 'The surplus.') And it was recited with the raf' [nominative case, العفو]. And al-'Afw (الْعَفْوَ) is the opposite of hardship (الجهد). It means that one spends what is easy for him to give, without reaching the point of hardship (الجهد) or exhausting his capacity (استفراغ الوسع). It is said: "Take the surplus (العفو) from me, and you will maintain my affection." And it is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "One of you comes with all his wealth, gives it in charity (يتصدق به), and then sits begging people. Indeed, charity (الصدقة) is only from a position of sufficiency (ظهر غنى)."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): al-'Afw (الْعَفْوَ) is moderation (الوسط).

In al-Majma' from Him (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi, He said: "Neither stinginess (إقتار) nor extravagance (إسراف)."

In al-Tibyan and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): al-'Afw (الْعَفْوَ) is what remains after the provision for the year (قوت السنة).

In al-Majma' from Him: That was abrogated (نسخ) by the verse of Zakat (الزكاة).

كَذلِكَ (Thus), just as He made clear that the surplus (العفو) is better than hardship (الجهد), يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمُ الْآياتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَفَكَّرُونَ (does Allah clarify the signs for you, so that you may reflect).

(1) He is Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Mansur al-Dawaniqi, the father of al-Rashid.

(220) فِي الدُّنْيا وَالْآخِرَةِ (in the world and the Hereafter) in the affairs of the two abodes (الدارين), so you take what is most beneficial (الأصلح) and most advantageous (الأنفع). وَيَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ الْيَتامى (And they ask you concerning the orphans)

In al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the verse ** إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوالَ الْيَتامى ظُلْماً** (Indeed those who consume the property of orphans wrongfully) was revealed, everyone who had an orphan with him expelled him, and they asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم about expelling them, so [this verse] was revealed.

In al-Majma', narrated from him (Imam al-Sadiq) and from his father (peace be upon them both): When the verse ** وَآتُوا الْيَتامى أَمْوالَهُمْ (Give the orphans their property) was revealed, they disliked mixing (مخالطة) with the orphans, and this became difficult for them, so they complained to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. Then the verse قُلْ إِصْلاحٌ لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ (Say: 'To set right their affairs is better') was revealed. Their mixing with them for their betterment is better than their avoidance of them. وَإِنْ تُخالِطُوهُمْ (And if you mix with them)—meaning, you associate with them (تعاشروهم) and share with them (تشاركوهم)—فَإِخْوانُكُمْ** (they are your brothers), for they are your brothers in religion, and it is the right of a brother to mix with his brother.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "You take out from their property an amount sufficient for them, and you take out from your property an amount sufficient for you, and then you spend it." I said: "What if they are orphans, both young and old, and some of them have better clothing than others, and some eat more than others, yet their property is shared?" He replied: "As for the clothing, the price of his clothing is upon each person among them. As for the food, make it shared (جميعاً), for the young one will soon eat like the old one."

In a narration: And they should not diminish (يرزأن) (1) anything from their property; indeed, it is the Fire.

وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ الْمُفْسِدَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِ (And Allah knows the corruptor from the reformer) It is not hidden from Him who mixes with them for the purpose of reform or corruption, and He will recompense them according to their mixing.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "We visit a brother of ours in a house of orphans, and they have a servant. We sit on their carpet, drink from their water, and their servant serves us. Sometimes we eat food there brought by our companion, and sometimes it includes some of their food. What is your view on that?" He replied: "If there is benefit for them in your visiting him, then there is no harm. But if there is harm, then no." And he said: ** بَلِ الْإِنْسانُ عَلى نَفْسِهِ بَصِيرَةٌ (Rather, man is a witness against his own soul) So, it is not hidden from you, and Allah the Almighty has said: ** وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ الْمُفْسِدَ مِنَ الْمُصْلِحِ (And Allah knows the corruptor from the reformer).

وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَأَعْنَتَكُمْ (Had Allah wished, He would have put you in hardship) He would have burdened you with hardship (العنت), which is difficulty (المشقة), and would not have permitted you to mix with them. ** إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ (Indeed Allah is all-mighty) Dominant (غالب), capable of whatever He wills, ** حَكِيمٌ ** (all-wise) He does what wisdom necessitates and what capacity allows Him.

(٢٢١) وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكاتِ (Do not marry idolatresses) Do not marry the disbelieving women (الكافرات) حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَّ وَلَأَمَةٌ (until they come to believe. Certainly, a believing slave woman) (who is) owned (مملوكة) مُؤْمِنَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ مُشْرِكَةٍ (is better than an idolatress) (even if she is) free (حرة) وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَتْكُمْ (though she should impress you.) the idolatress (المشركة) by her beauty or her wealth, and you love her. وَلا تُنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ (Do not marry [your daughters] to idolaters) Do not marry the believing women (المؤمنات) to them حَتَّى يُؤْمِنُوا وَلَعَبْدٌ (until they come to believe. Certainly, a believing slave) (who is) owned (مملوك) مُؤْمِنٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ مُشْرِكٍ (is better than an idolater) (even if he is) free (حرّ) وَلَوْ أَعْجَبَكُمْ (though he should impress you.) his beauty, or his wealth, or his condition (حاله). أُولئِكَ (They) This refers (إشارة) to the idolaters (المشركين) and the idolatresses (المشركات) يَدْعُونَ إِلَى النَّارِ (invite to the Fire) (They invite) to disbelief (الكفر) which leads to the Fire. Thus, it is their right that they should not be befriended (يوالوا) nor intermarried with (يصاهروا). وَاللهُ يَدْعُوا إِلَى الْجَنَّةِ وَالْمَغْفِرَةِ (but Allah invites to paradise and forgiveness) (He invites) to the performance of that which necessitates Paradise (الجنّة) and forgiveness (المغفرة), namely, faith (الايمان) and obedience (الطاعة) بِإِذْنِهِ (by His leave,) by His command (أمره) and His enabling (توفيقه). وَيُبَيِّنُ آياتِهِ (and He clarifies His signs) His commands (أوامره) and His prohibitions (نواهيه) لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (for the people so that they may take admonition.) and be exalted (ويتعظمون).

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: This (verse) is abrogated (منسوخة) by the Almighty's saying in Surat al-Ma'idah: الْيَوْمَ أُحِلَّ لَكُمُ الطَّيِّباتُ (Today the good things have been made lawful for you) up to His saying الْمُحْصَناتُ مِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُوا الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ إِذا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَ (and chaste women from among those who were given the Book before you, when you have given them their dowries). He said: So this verse وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكاتِ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنَ (Do not marry idolatresses until they come to believe) was abrogated for him (the Muslim man). And His saying وَلا تُنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَتَّى يُؤْمِنُوا (Do not marry [your daughters] to idolaters until they come to believe) remained as it was (على حاله), not abrogated (لم ينسخ), because it is not lawful (لا يحل) for a Muslim man to marry (his daughter to) an idolater (المشرك), but it is lawful for him to marry an idolatress (المشركة) from among the Jews (اليهود) and the Christians (النصارى). Al-Nu'mani (النعمانيّ) likewise stated this in his book (كتابه). Both of them counted the Almighty's saying: وَلا تَنْكِحُوا الْمُشْرِكاتِ (Do not marry idolatresses) among the abrogated verses (منسوخ النصف من الآيات). The complete discussion regarding this will come in Surat al-Ma'idah, if Allah the Almighty wills.

footnote (١) *لا يرزأن* (with the *rā'* (*الراء*) being unpointed (*المهملة*)) means they do not diminish (*لا ينقصن*) nor do they take anything from it (*ولا يصيبن منها شيئاً*). "From him (*منه*)."

(٢٢٢) وَيَسْئَلُونَكَ (١) عَنِ الْمَحِيضِ It is the verbal noun (مصدر) of hāḍat (حاضت). قُلْ هُوَ أَذىً something repulsive (مستقذر) that harms the one who approaches it, causing aversion (نفرة) from it. فَاعْتَزِلُوا النِّساءَ فِي الْمَحِيضِ meaning, avoid having intercourse with them during the time of menstruation, and do not approach them through intercourse حَتَّى يَطْهُرْنَ meaning, the blood ceases from them. And whoever reads yaṭṭahharūn (يَطَّهَّرْنَ)—it is derived from yataṭahharūn (يَتَطَهَّرْنَ), meaning they bathe (يغتسلن) (2).

In al-Kafi, Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked: "What is permissible for the husband of a menstruating woman from her?" He replied: "Everything except the vulva (القبل) itself."

In a narration: "Let him approach her wherever he wishes, as long as he avoids the place of the blood." The narrations (الأخبار) concerning this meaning from them (peace be upon them) are numerous.

فَإِذا تَطَهَّرْنَ meaning, they have bathed (اغتسلن), فَأْتُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَكُمُ اللهُ Meaning: "Seek the child (الولد) from where Allah has commanded you." This is narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), as will follow. And what is meant by "from where Allah has commanded you" is the place of approach (المأتي) which He commanded you with and made lawful for you. The seeking of the child (طلب الولد) is derived only from the word min (من).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) concerning a woman whose menstrual blood ceases in the last of her days, he said: "If her husband is afflicted by lust (شبق), let him command her to wash her private part (فرجها), then he may touch her if he wishes before she performs the full bath (تغتسل)."

In another narration: "And the full bath (الغسل) is more beloved to me." And he (peace be upon him) was asked: "If she performs tayammum (تيممت) from menstruation, is she lawful for her husband?" He said: "Yes," meaning after she has become clean (طهرت).

إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ التَّوَّابِينَ from sins وَيُحِبُّ الْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ meaning, those who purify themselves with water (بالماء) and those who keep away (المتنزهين) from filth (الأقذار).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, Allah loves the servant who is tried (المفتن) and penitent (التواب). And the one from whom that [sin] does not occur is more virtuous (أفضل)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "People used to cleanse themselves (يستنجون) using cotton (الكرسف) [or] stones (الأحجار). Then al-wuḍūʾ (الوضوء) was innovated, and it is a noble characteristic (خلق كريم). So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded it and performed it. Then Allah revealed in His Book: إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ التَّوَّابِينَ وَيُحِبُّ الْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ."

I say: What is meant by al-wuḍūʾ (الوضوء) is cleansing oneself (الاستنجاء) with water (بالماء).

In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "People used to cleanse themselves (يستنجون) with three stones (أحجار) because they used to eat unripe dates (البُسر), and thus their excrement (بعر) was hard. Then a man from the Ansar ate gourds (الدّبا), and his stomach softened (لان بطنه), so he cleansed himself with water (بالماء). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم sent for him. The man came, fearing that something displeasing concerning his cleansing with water might have been revealed about him. He [the Prophet] asked him: 'Have you done anything today?' He replied: 'O Messenger of Allah, by Allah, nothing compelled me to cleanse myself with water except that I ate food that softened my stomach, and the stones were of no use to me, so I cleansed myself with water.' The Messenger of Allah said: 'Congratulations to you! For Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has revealed a verse concerning you, so rejoice: إِنَّ اللهَ يُحِبُّ التَّوَّابِينَ وَيُحِبُّ الْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ.' You were the first one to do this, the first of the penitent (التوابين) and the first of those who keep clean (المتطهرين)."

In a narration: The man was al-Bara’ ibn Ma’rur al-Ansari (البراء بن معرور الأنصاري). And he [the author of al-Faqih] mentioned both [narrations] in al-Faqih as mursal (مرسلاً)...

footnote (١) He mentioned *yas'alūnaka* (*يسألونك*) three times without the *wāw* (*الواو*), then three times with it, because the first questions were at separate times, and the last three were at one time. Therefore, He mentioned it with the particle of conjunction (*حرف الجمع*). Thus it was said. (*منه ره*).

footnote (٢) Narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: You were only commanded to keep away from having intercourse with them when they menstruate, and you were not commanded to expel them from the houses, as the non-Arabs (*الأعاجم*) do. This is the moderation between the extremism of the Jews, who used to expel them from the houses, and the negligence of the Christians, who used to have intercourse with them and did not care about menstruation. (*منه*).

(٢٢٣) نِساؤُكُمْ حَرْثٌ لَكُمْ ** (223) Your women are a tillage for you. [Your women are a tillage for you] means they are places of tillage for you. He likened them to it, comparing what is cast into their wombs—the drops (النّطف)—to seeds (بالبذور). فَأْتُوا حَرْثَكُمْ أَنَّى شِئْتُمْ ** (So come to your tillage whenever you like). It was said: meaning, from whichever direction (جهة) you like.

And al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): meaning, whenever you like, in the vagina (الفرج). In another narration: at any hour you like. And in another: from her front and from her back, in the front passage (في القبل).

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): The Jews used to say that if a man approached his wife from behind, his child would be born cross-eyed (أحول). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: نِساؤُكُمْ حَرْثٌ لَكُمْ فَأْتُوا حَرْثَكُمْ أَنَّى شِئْتُمْ (Your women are a tillage for you. So come to your tillage whenever you like), [meaning] from behind or from the front, contrary to the saying of the Jews, and He did not mean in their anuses (في أدبارهن).

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding a man approaching his wife in her anus (في دبرها). He said: "There is no harm (لا بأس) if she consents." It was asked: "Then where is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَأْتُوهُنَّ مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَكُمُ اللهُ (So come to them from where Allah has commanded you)?" He said: "This is concerning the seeking of a child. So seek the child from where Allah has commanded you. Indeed, Allah the Almighty says: نِساؤُكُمْ حَرْثٌ لَكُمْ فَأْتُوا حَرْثَكُمْ أَنَّى شِئْتُمْ (Your women are a tillage for you. So come to your tillage whenever you like)."

I say: There is no contradiction between the two narrations, because the intent of the first [narration] is to negate the indication of this verse regarding the permissibility of the anuses (الأدبار). And the intent of the second [narration] is to negate the indication of His saying, the Almighty: ** مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَكُمُ اللهُ (from where Allah has commanded you) regarding its prohibition. As for his recitation of this verse immediately following that, it is an appeal by him to it, [indicating] that Allah, the Glorified, only intended the seeking of a child, since He named them the tillage (الحرث). And it is possible that His saying, the Almighty: مِنْ حَيْثُ أَمَرَكُمُ اللهُ is a reference to the command for intercourse (بالمباشرة) and the seeking of a child in His saying, the Glorified: ** فَالْآنَ بَاشِرُوهُنَّ وَابْتَغُوا ما كَتَبَ اللهُ لَكُمْ. (So now consort with them, and seek what Allah has ordained for you.)

And in the second narration, there is an indication that what the permissibility of which is dependent upon purification (التطهر) is specifically the place of tillage (موضع الحرث), and not the other places (سائر المواضع).

In al-Kafi, Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about approaching women in their rear parts (في أعجازهن). He said: "She is your plaything (لعبتك), do not harm her."

In a narration: "And the woman is a plaything (لعبة), she is not to be harmed, and she is a tillage (حرث) as Allah said." And in another: "There is no harm (لا بأس به) in it, but I do not like that you do it."

وَقَدِّمُوا لِأَنْفُسِكُمْ ** and send ahead [some good] for your souls, [and send ahead [some good] for your souls]. It was said: meaning, that which is stored up for you of righteous deeds (الأعمال الصالحة). And it was said: it is the seeking of a child. And it was said: it is saying the Basmalah (التسمية) upon intercourse (على الوطئ). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (and be wary of Allah) and do not dare (تجتَرُوا) to commit forbidden acts (المناهي). ** وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ مُلاقُوهُ (and know that you will meet Him). So take provision with which you will not be disgraced. وَبَشِّرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (And give good news to the faithful). Perhaps the intent is: And give good news to whoever confirms you and complies with your command, with the meeting [of Him], the honor (الكرامة), and the everlasting bliss (النعيم الدائم) at that time.

(٢٢٤) وَلا تَجْعَلُوا اللهَ عُرْضَةً لِأَيْمانِكُمْ (Do not make Allah an object of your oaths) Al-ʿurḍah (العرضة) is applied to that which obstructs something, thereby preventing it, and to that which is exposed to a matter. And the meaning according to the first interpretation is: لا تَجْعَلُوا اللهَ (Do not make Allah) a barrier to the types of good you swore concerning, such that what is meant by al-ayman (الايمان) are the matters sworn upon. And based on this, the saying of al-Sadiq (عليه السلام) was narrated in its exegesis: "If you are called to reconcile between two people, do not say, 'I have an oath (علي يمين) that I will not do it.'" And according to the second interpretation: ** لا تَجْعَلُوا اللهَ (Do not make Allah) an object exposed to your oaths, thereby cheapening Him through frequent swearing. And based on this, His saying (peace be upon him) was narrated: "Do not swear by Allah, neither truthfully nor falsely, for Allah says: ** وَلا تَجْعَلُوا اللهَ عُرْضَةً لِأَيْمانِكُمْ (Do not make Allah an object of your oaths)." And in a narration: "Whoever swears by Allah falsely commits disbelief (كفر), and whoever swears by Allah truthfully commits a sin (اثم), for Allah says," and he recited the Ayah. And the three narrations are reported in al-Kafi, and al-Ayyashi mentioned the first two in a single narration. And from him (peace be upon him): "It means the man who swears not to speak to his brother or not to speak to his mother, and what resembles that." أَنْ تَبَرُّوا وَتَتَّقُوا وَتُصْلِحُوا بَيْنَ النَّاسِ (that you may be righteous and Godwary and set things right between people.) This is an explanation for al-ayman (الايمان), meaning the matters sworn upon, according to the first interpretation. And it is the reason (علّة) for the prohibition according to the second interpretation, meaning: I forbid you from it desiring your righteousness, your Godwariness, and your setting things right between people. For the frequent swearer (الحلّاف) is audacious toward Allah, and the one audacious toward Allah the Almighty cannot be righteous (برّاً), Godwary (متّقياً), nor trustworthy in reconciling disputes (إصلاح ذات البين). And for that reason, Allah the Almighty condemned the frequent swearer, saying: وَلا تُطِعْ كُلَّ حَلَّافٍ مَهِينٍ (And do not obey any contemptible habitual swearer). وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ (and Allah is all-hearing) of your oaths, ** عَلِيمٌ (all-knowing) of your intentions (بنيّاتكم).

(٢٢٥) لا يُؤاخِذُكُمُ اللهُ (Allah will not take you to task) with punishment (بالعقوبة) and expiation (والكفّارة) بِاللَّغْوِ فِي أَيْمانِكُمْ (for the frivolous [oaths] in your oaths)—the trivial (الساقط) which has no intention (عقد) accompanying it, but rather runs according to the habit of the tongue, like the saying of the Arabs: "No, by Allah (لا والله)" and "Yes, by Allah (وبلى والله)" merely for emphasis (لمجرّد التأكيد). And similarly, in al-Majma', it is narrated from both of them (peace be upon them) (عنهما عليهما السلام). وَلكِنْ يُؤاخِذُكُمْ بِما كَسَبَتْ قُلُوبُكُمْ ** (but He will take you to task for what your hearts have earned) —that is, what your hearts agreed upon (واطأت) with your tongues therein and what you resolved (عزمتموه), like His saying, glory be to Him: ** بِما عَقَّدْتُمُ الْأَيْمانَ (for the oaths that you have sworn in earnest). For the earning (كسب) of the heart is the intention (العقد), the resolve (النيّة), and the purpose (القصد). وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ (and Allah is all-forgiving) in that He does not take you to task for frivolous oaths (بلغو الايمان), حَلِيمٌ (all-clement.) in that He does not impose a waiting period (تربصاً للتوبة) for repentance when taking you to task for a serious oath (يمين الجد).

(٢٢٦) لِلَّذِينَ يُؤْلُونَ مِنْ نِسائِهِمْ ** (226) For those who swear off their wives, [They] swear not to have sexual intercourse with them, intending harm (مضارّة) to them. And al-īlāʾ (الإيلاء) is the oath (الحلف). Its transitivity is with (على), but since this oath implies the meaning of distance (البعد), it was made transitive with (من). ** تَرَبُّصُ أَرْبَعَةِ أَشْهُرٍ a waiting period of four months. By waiting for it and pausing within it, so they are not demanded anything [during this time]. But if they return (فَإِنْ فاؤُ), meaning they return to them by urging [themselves] and [offering] the expiation of the oath (كفّارة اليمين), and they have sexual intercourse, provided they are able (القدرة), or they promise it [intercourse], provided they are unable (العجز), فَإِنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ then Allah is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. He does not follow them with punishment (عقوبة).

(٢٢٧) وَإِنْ عَزَمُوا الطَّلاقَ فَإِنَّ اللهَ سَمِيعٌ (But if they resolve on divorce, Allah is indeed all-hearing, all-knowing.) (227) سَمِيعٌ regarding their divorce عَلِيمٌ regarding their intentions (بضمائرهم).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Al-Īlāʾ (الإيلاء) is when a man swears concerning his wife that he will not have intercourse with her. If she is patient with him, then she has the right to be patient. But if she refers the matter to the Imam (الامام), He gives him a respite of four months. Then after that, He says to him: "Either you return to marital relations (المناكحة) or you divorce (her)." If he refuses (أبى), he imprisons him forever (أبداً).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (al-Sadiq) and from his father (peace be upon them both), they both said: If a man swears an oath (آلى) not to approach his wife, she has no say (قول) and no right during the four months, and there is no sin upon him for refraining from her during the four months. If the four months pass before he touches her, and she remains silent and is content, then he is free (في حل) and at ease (وسعة). But if she refers her matter (to the authority), it is said to him: "Either you return (يفيئ) and touch her, or you divorce (her)." And the resolve for divorce (عزم الطلاق) is that he leaves her alone (يخلى عنها). Then when she menstruates and becomes pure, he divorces her. And he has a greater right to take her back (رجعتها) as long as three menstrual cycles (قروء) have not passed. This is the Īlāʾ (الإيلاء) which Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed in His Book and the Sunnah of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(٢٢٨) وَالْمُطَلَّقاتُ يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنْفُسِهِنَّ ثَلاثَةَ قُرُوءٍ (228) And the divorced women shall wait by themselves for three courses. meaning those with whom intercourse has occurred (المدخول بهن) who are subject to the menstrual periods (ذوات الاقراء), because the verses (الآيات) and narrations (الأخبار) indicate that the ruling for others besides them is different from this. they shall wait (ينتظرن). This is a declarative statement (خبر) carrying the meaning of a command (الأمر) for emphasis and to indicate that it is something they must comply with (يمتثلن), so it is as if they have already complied, and He is informing about it. This is an incitement (تهييج) and an imposition (تعب) upon them to observe the waiting period (التربص), for the souls (نفوس) of women are inclined (طوامح) toward men, so they are commanded to suppress (يقمعنها) them and compel them to observe the waiting period. They shall not marry during this time.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: The courses (الاقراء) are the periods of purity (الاطهار).

And from Zurarah, he said: I said to Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him): "I heard Rabi‘ah al-Ra’y say that if she sees blood from the third menstruation (الحيضة الثالثة), she is irrevocably separated from him (بانت منه), and the course (القرء) is only the period between the two menstruations (ما بين الحيضتين), and he claimed that he derived this through his own opinion (برأيه)." Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: "He lied. By my life, he did not say that based on his opinion (برأيه), but rather he took it from ‘Ali (peace be upon him)." I said to Him: "And what did ‘Ali (peace be upon him) say regarding it?" He said: "He used to say: 'If she sees blood from the third menstruation (الحيضة الثالثة), her waiting period (عدّتها) has ended, and he has no claim (سبيل) over her. The course (القرء) is only the period between the two menstruations (ما بين الحيضتين), and she is not permitted to marry until she performs the ritual bath (تغتسل) from the third menstruation.'"

And in another narration: He said: I heard Rabi‘ah al-Ra’y say: "Whoever believes that the courses (الاقراء) which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, named in the Quran are only the period of purity (الطهر) between the two menstruations (ما بين الحيضتين)." He (peace be upon him) said: "He lied. He did not say it based on his opinion (برأيه), but rather it reached him from ‘Ali (peace be upon him)." I said to Him: "May Allah set your affairs right (أصلحك الله), did ‘Ali (peace be upon him) say that?" He said: "Yes. The course (القرء) is only the period of purity (الطهر) during which the blood is gathered (يقرى فيه الدم) and collected (تجمعه), and when the menstruation (المحيض) comes, it is expelled (دفعه)."

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The waiting period (عدة) for the woman who has not menstruated, and the woman suffering from irregular bleeding (المستحاضة) who has not menstruated, and the woman suffering from irregular bleeding (المستحاضة) who has not become pure (لم تطهر), is three months. And the waiting period (عدة) for the woman who menstruates and whose menstruation is regular (تستقيم حيضها) is three courses (ثلاثة قروء). And the course (القرء) is the collection of blood (جمع الدم) between the two menstruations (بين الحيضتين).

وَلا يَحِلُّ لَهُنَّ أَنْ يَكْتُمْنَ ما خَلَقَ اللهُ فِي أَرْحامِهِنَّ And it is not lawful for them to conceal what Allah has created in their wombs, such as a child (الولد) or menstrual blood (دم الحيض), in order to hasten the waiting period (العدة) or to nullify the right of return (حق الرجعة).

In al-Majma‘, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Pregnancy (الحبل) and menstruation (الحيض).

And al-Qummi (said): It is not lawful (لا يحل) for the woman to conceal her pregnancy (حملها), or her menstruation (حيضها), or her purity (طهرها). And Allah has entrusted three things to women: purity (الطهر), menstruation (الحيض), and pregnancy (الحبل).

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means it is not lawful (لا يحل) for her to conceal the pregnancy (الحمل) if she is divorced while she is pregnant (حبلى) and the husband does not know about the pregnancy, and he has a greater right (أحق بها) to her during that pregnancy, as long as she has not given birth (ما لم تضع).

إِنْ كُنَّ يُؤْمِنَّ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ if they believe in Allah and the Last Day. meaning that doing so contradicts faith (الايمان), thereby magnifying the gravity of their action.

وَبُعُولَتُهُنَّ أَحَقُّ بِرَدِّهِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ إِنْ أَرادُوا إِصْلاحاً and their husbands are worthier of taking them back during that time, if they desire reconciliation. their husbands (أزواجهن) are worthier of taking them back to the marriage (النكاح) and returning to them (الرجعة إليهن) during the time of the waiting period (زمان التربص), if they desire by the return (الرجعة) reconciliation of what is between them, and they do not intend to harm them (مضارتهن).

وَلَهُنَّ مِثْلُ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِنَّ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ They have a status similar to that which is due from them, in a rightful manner, They have rights (حقوق) over them to the men, in terms of obligation (الوجوب) and entitlement (الاستحقاق), but not in kind (في الجنس), in a manner that is not rejected (لا ينكر) by the Sacred Law (الشرع) nor by the customs of women (عادات النساء). Thus, they should not burden the men with what is not their right, nor should the men burden them with what is not the men's right.

وَلِلرِّجالِ عَلَيْهِنَّ دَرَجَةٌ and men have a degree over them. meaning an increase in right (زيادة في الحق) and a merit (فضيلة) due to their maintenance (قيامهم) of them.

In al-Faqih: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about the right (حقّ) of the woman over her husband. He said: "He should fill her stomach (يشبع بطنها) and clothe her body (يكسو جثتها), and if she acts ignorantly (جهلت), she should be forgiven."

And in it (al-Faqih) and in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: A woman came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "O Messenger of Allah, what is the right (حقّ) of the husband over the woman?" He said to her: "That she obeys him and does not disobey him, and that she does not give charity (تتصدق) from his house of anything except with his permission (باذنه), and that she does not fast voluntarily (تصوم تطوعاً) except with his permission, and that she does not deny him herself even if she is upon the back of a saddle (على ظهر قتب), and that she does not leave her house except with his permission. For if she leaves without his permission, the angels of the heaven (ملائكة السماء), the angels of the earth (ملائكة الأرض), the angels of wrath (ملائكة الغضب), and the angels of mercy (ملائكة الرّحمة) curse her until she returns to her house." She said: "O Messenger of Allah, who among people has the greatest right (أعظم الناس حقاً) over a man?" He said: "His father (والده)." She said: "Then who among people has the greatest right over a woman?" He said: "Her husband (زوجها)." She said: "Do I have a right over him equal to the right he has over me?" He said: "No, not even one out of every hundred." So she said: "By Him Who sent you with the truth as a Prophet, no man shall ever possess my neck (رقبتي) again."

وَاللهُ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ and Allah is All-mighty, All-wise. He is capable of taking vengeance (الانتقام) upon whoever violates the rulings (الأحكام). He legislates them (يشرعها) based on wisdom (حكم) and benefits (مصالح).

(٢٢٩) الطَّلاقُ مَرَّتانِ (229) The divorce is twice. Meaning, the revocable divorce (التطليق الرجعي) is two, for the third one makes the separation final (باين). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He was asked where the third [divorce] was, and He replied: "or a release with kindness (أَوْ تَسْرِيحٌ بِإِحْسانٍ)." فَإِمْساكٌ بِمَعْرُوفٍ then [there is] retention with kindness. Meaning, by reconciliation (بالمراجعة) and good companionship (حسن المعاشرة). أَوْ تَسْرِيحٌ بِإِحْسانٍ or release with kindness. By divorcing her the third time after reconciliation (الرجعة), as mentioned in the aforementioned Prophetic narration, or by not reconciling with her until she becomes finally separated (تبين) from him and completes the waiting period (العدة). Thus, retention (الإمساك) is taking [her back], and release (التسريح) is letting [her] go (الإطلاق). ** وَلا يَحِلُّ لَكُمْ أَنْ تَأْخُذُوا مِمَّا آتَيْتُمُوهُنَ And it is not lawful for you to take anything from what you have given them, of the dowry (المهر), ** شَيْئاً إِلَّا أَنْ يَخافا anything, unless the two fear. This is a shift (التفات) from addressing [the believers] to the third person, and then back to addressing them. Or the address refers to the rulers (الحكام), because the taking and giving only occur by their command. And it was recited with a dammah on the yā' (يُقِيما): that they will not maintain the limits of Allah (أَلَّا يُقِيما حُدُودَ اللهِ) regarding the duties of marriage (وظائف الزوجية) that Allah has obligated upon them both. ** فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ أَلَّا يُقِيما حُدُودَ اللهِ فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِما فِيمَا افْتَدَتْ بِهِ But if you fear that they will not maintain the limits of Allah, there is no sin upon them in what she offers as a ransom. There is no sin (لا جناح) upon the man for taking what she ransoms herself with and seeks divorce through compensation (اختلعت), nor upon the woman for giving it. ** تِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ These are the limits of Allah, A reference to the rulings (الأحكام) that have been defined. ** فَلا تَعْتَدُوها so do not transgress them. By contravening them (بالمخالفة). ** وَمَنْ يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللهِ فَأُولئِكَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ And whoever transgresses the limits of Allah—it is they who are the wrongdoers. This follows the prohibition with a threat (الوعيد), emphasizing the warning (التهديد).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the woman who seeks divorce through compensation (المختلعة). He said: It is not lawful for her to seek divorce through compensation (خلعها) until she says: "By Allah, I will not fulfill any oath for you, nor obey any command of yours, and I will surely tread upon your bed [with others], and I will surely enter upon you without your permission." When she says that, her seeking divorce through compensation (خلعها) becomes lawful, and it becomes lawful for him to take what he took from her dowry (مهرها) and whatever he added. And this is the saying of the Almighty Allah: there is no sin upon them in what she offers as a ransom (فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِما فِيمَا افْتَدَتْ بِهِ). And when he does that, she has become finally separated (بانت) from him by one divorce (تطليقة), and she is the master of her own soul (أملك بنفسها). If she wishes, she may marry him, and if she wishes, she may not. If she marries him, she is with him [having] two [remaining divorces].

And in al-Kafi, there are narrations (اخبار) close to this.

And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): If the woman says to her husband generally, "I will not obey any command of yours," whether specified (مفسراً) or unspecified (غير مفسر), it becomes lawful for him to take what he took from her, and he has no right of reconciliation (رجعة) over her.

(٢٣٠) فَإِنْ طَلَّقَها (230) But if he divorces her after the two (divorces), a third time. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the third divorce. فَلا تَحِلُّ لَهُ she will not be lawful for him marrying her ** مِنْ بَعْدُ afterwards that is, this divorce ** حَتَّى تَنْكِحَ زَوْجاً غَيْرَهُ فَإِنْ طَلَّقَها until she marries another husband. Then if he divorces her, the second husband ** فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِما أَنْ يَتَراجَعا there will be no sin upon them to return to each other meaning, each one of them returns to the other through marriage ** إِنْ ظَنَّا أَنْ يُقِيما حُدُودَ اللهِ if they think that they can maintain the limits of Allah. if it is in their assumption that they will maintain what Allah has limited and legislated concerning the rights of marriage (حقوق الزوجية) ** وَتِلْكَ حُدُودُ اللهِ These are the limits of Allah, meaning, the mentioned rulings (الأحكام) ** يُبَيِّنُها لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ which He clarifies for a people who know. who understand and act according to the requirement of knowledge. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who divorced his wife with a divorce after which she would not be lawful for him until she married another husband, and then a man married her by temporary marriage (متعة). Is it lawful for him (the first husband) to marry her? He said: "No, until she enters into the like of that from which she departed." And al-Ayyashi added: Allah the Almighty said: فَإِنْ طَلَّقَها فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِما أَنْ يَتَراجَعا إِنْ ظَنَّا أَنْ يُقِيما حُدُودَ اللهِ (Then if he divorces her, there will be no sin upon them to return to each other if they think that they can maintain the limits of Allah). And temporary marriage (المتعة) does not involve divorce (طلاق). And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding the man who divorces his wife with the divorce after which she is not lawful for him حَتَّى تَنْكِحَ زَوْجاً غَيْرَهُ (until she marries another husband), and then she marries a man but he does not consummate the marriage (لم يدخل بها). He said: "No, until he tastes her sweetness (عسيلتها)."

(٢٣١) وَإِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَبَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَ (231) When you divorce women, and they complete their term, (They) approached the end of their waiting period (عدّتهن), for al-bulūgh (البلوغ) may be applied to drawing near (الدنو) just as it is applied to reaching (الوصول). And al-ajal (الأجل) may be applied to the termination of the period (منتهى المدة) just as it is applied to the period (itself) (المدة). فَأَمْسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ then retain them honourably Revoke the divorce (راجعوهن) with what is due to her, by fulfilling her rights (القيام بموجبها), without seeking harm (ضرار) through the revocation. أَوْ سَرِّحُوهُنَّ بِمَعْرُوفٍ ** or release them honourably. Leave them (خلّوهن) until their waiting period (عدتهن) expires, so that they become masters of their own selves (أملك بأنفسهن). ** وَلا تُمْسِكُوهُنَّ ضِراراً Do not retain them maliciously And do not revoke the divorce (تراجعوهنّ) intending to harm them (الإضرار بهن), without any desire for them, ** لِتَعْتَدُوا so that you transgress.
