So that you oppress them (تظلموهن) by prolonging the period (تطويل المدة) upon them while they are still bound to you (في حبالكم), or by forcing them (الجائهن) to ransom themselves (الافتداء). In al-Faqih: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about this verse, and He said: "A man divorces (his wife) until her term is almost complete (يخلو أجلها), then he revokes the divorce (راجعها), then he divorces her. He does this three times. So Allah forbade that." وَمَنْ يَفْعَلْ ذلِكَ فَقَدْ ظَلَمَ نَفْسَهُ Whoever does that has certainly wronged himself. By exposing it (نفسه) to punishment (للعقاب). وَلا تَتَّخِذُوا آياتِ اللهِ هُزُواً Do not take the signs of Allah in ridicule. Do not treat His commands and prohibitions lightly (تستخفوا). وَاذْكُرُوا نِعْمَتَ اللهِ عَلَيْكُمْ Remember Allah’s blessing upon you, By what He has made lawful (أباحه) for you of spouses (الأزواج) and wealth (الأموال), ** وَما أَنْزَلَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنَ الْكِتابِ وَالْحِكْمَةِ and what He has sent down to you of the Book and wisdom, Namely, the Quran and the sciences (العلوم) made clear (المبيّنة) to you, ** يَعِظُكُمْ بِهِ admonishing you thereby. So that you may be admonished (لتتّعظوا). وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ Be wary of Allah, and know that Allah is knowing of all things. (This is) an affirmation (تأكيد) and a threat (تهديد).

(٢٣٢) وَإِذا طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ فَبَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَ (232) When you divorce women, and they complete their term, Their waiting period (عدّتهن) has expired. فَلا تَعْضُلُوهُنَّ أَنْ يَنْكِحْنَ أَزْواجَهُنَ do not hinder them from marrying their husbands, Do not prevent them unjustly (ظلماً) from marrying. It is said that this address (خطاب) is either directed to the husbands who unjustly hinder their women after the expiration of the waiting period (العدة), not allowing them to marry whomsoever they wish from among the husbands; or it is an address directed to the guardians (الأولياء) regarding their hindering the women from returning to their husbands; or it is directed to both of them collectively; or it is directed to all people. And al-'Aḍl (العضل) is confinement (الحبس) and restriction (التضييق). إِذا تَراضَوْا بَيْنَهُمْ when they agree among themselves When the suitors (الخطاب) and the women agree بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (in accordance with recognized standards)—that which is good according to the religion (الدين) and chivalry (المروّة) regarding the conditions (الشرائط). ذلِكَ This That which preceded of command and prohibition يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَنْ كانَ مِنْكُمْ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ This is an admonition for those of you who believe in Allah and the Last Day. because He is the one who takes heed (المتعظ) and benefits (المنتفع) from it. ذلِكُمْ (That)—meaning, acting upon what He mentioned ** أَزْكى لَكُمْ That is more decent for you meaning, more beneficial (أنفع) ** وَأَطْهَرُ and purer. from the filth of sins (دنس الآثام). ** وَاللهُ يَعْلَمُ Allah knows, what contains benefit and righteousness (الصلاح) for you ** وَأَنْتُمْ لا تَعْلَمُونَ and you do not know. due to the deficiency of your knowledge (قصور علمكم).

(٢٣٣) وَالْوالِداتُ يُرْضِعْنَ أَوْلادَهُنَ (233) The mothers shall suckle their children This is a statement (خبر) in the meaning of a confirmed command (الأمر المؤكد). Al-Walidat (the mothers) includes divorced women and others. It was said, rather, that it is specific to them (the divorced women) since the discourse is about them. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A free woman (الحرة) is not compelled to breastfeed the child, but a slave woman (أم الولد) is compelled. I say: It is probable that the meaning of the Ayah is that breastfeeding is their right, and they should not be prevented from it if they desire it. Narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: There is no milk better for the child than the milk of his mother. In al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): There is no milk with which a child is suckled that is greater in blessing upon him than the milk of his mother. It was said that it may become obligatory upon them, such as when the child does not suckle except from his mother, or does not live except by her milk, or when no one else is found. حَوْلَيْنِ كامِلَيْنِ (for two full years). He emphasized it with this because it is something that is usually treated leniently. لِمَنْ أَرادَ أَنْ يُتِمَّ الرَّضاعَةَ (for him who desires to complete the suckling). This ruling is for him who desires to complete the suckling, or it is related to يُرْضِعْنَ (shall suckle), meaning: for the sake of their husbands, since the child's maintenance is upon his father. In this is a determination of the maximum period of suckling and a permission to shorten it. وَعَلَى الْمَوْلُودِ لَهُ (And upon him to whom the child is born), who is the father. In this is an indication that the child belongs to the father, and for this reason, he is attributed to him. He did not say 'upon the husband' because he might be other than the husband, such as the divorced man, and to draw attention to the meaning that necessitates the obligation of breastfeeding and the expenses of the nursing mother upon the father. رِزْقُهُنَ (their provision), meaning their food, وَكِسْوَتُهُنَ (and their clothing) if they breastfeed their child بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (in a conventional manner), meaning according to what people of custom recognize. لا تُكَلَّفُ نَفْسٌ إِلَّا وُسْعَها (No soul is tasked beyond its capacity). This is a justification for the obligation of expenses and the restriction by al-ma'ruf (the conventional manner), and what follows is a detailed explanation and confirmation of it. That is, neither of them should task the other with what is beyond his or her capacity, nor should either harm the other because of the child. لا تُضَارَّ والِدَةٌ (A mother shall not be harmed) by her husband بِوَلَدِها (because of her child), by her leaving his breastfeeding out of obstinacy or spite towards his father, especially after the child has become accustomed to her, or by her demanding from him what is not conventional, or by her preoccupying his heart concerning the child's matter, or by her preventing herself from him (intercourse) fearing pregnancy lest it harm the suckling child. وَلا مَوْلُودٌ لَهُ (nor he to whom the child is born) meaning, the one to whom the child is born should also not harm his wife بِوَلَدِهِ (because of his child), by taking him away from her and preventing her from breastfeeding him if she desires it, especially after the child has become accustomed to her, or by compelling her to do it, or by withholding from her something that is obligatory upon him, or by abandoning intercourse with her fearing pregnancy out of compassion for the suckling child.

In al-Kafi: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about this Ayah, and He said: The nursing mothers used to push away the man when he desired intercourse, saying: "I will not let you, for I fear that I might become pregnant and thus kill this child whom I am nursing." And the man, when the woman called him, would say: "I fear that I might have intercourse with you and thus kill my child." So he would leave her and not have intercourse with her. Thus, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, forbade that the man should harm the woman or the woman should harm the man. And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): If a man divorces his wife while she is pregnant, he must spend on her until she delivers her burden. When she delivers it, he gives her her wage, and he should not harm her, unless he finds someone whose wage is cheaper than hers. If she is content with that wage, then she has a greater right to her son until she weans him. I say: It is permissible that لا تُضَارَّ is in the passive voice (البناء للمفعول), meaning لا تُضَارَّ والِدَةٌ (A mother shall not be harmed) by her husband, وَلا مَوْلُودٌ لَهُ (nor he to whom the child is born) by his wife. The meaning does not differ, except that it is reversed upon the two words. It was also read as لا تُضَارُّ (with a ḍamma on the rāʾ), as a substitute for His saying لا تُكَلَّفُ. وَعَلَى الْوارِثِ (And upon the heir), meaning upon the heir of the one to whom the child was born after his death, مِثْلُ ذلِكَ (the like of that), meaning the like of what was obligatory upon the one to whom the child was born. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: The maintenance is upon the heir, the like of what is upon the father. And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: It is not proper for the heir to harm the woman by saying: "I will not let her child come to her," nor should he harm her child if they have something with him, so it is not proper for him to be stingy towards him. In al-Kafi, narrated from Him regarding His saying وَعَلَى الْوارِثِ مِثْلُ ذلِكَ: It is a prohibition against harming the child or harming his mother regarding his suckling, and she is not allowed to take payment for his suckling beyond two full years. In al-Faqih, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He ruled concerning a man who died and left a child, and a wet nurse was sought for him, that the wage for the child's suckling is from what he inherits from his father and mother. فَإِنْ أَرادا فِصالاً (But if they both desire weaning) from suckling before the two years, as stated in al-Majma' narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), عَنْ تَراضٍ مِنْهُما وَتَشاوُرٍ فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْهِما (by mutual consent and consultation, there will be no sin upon them) in that. This is an expansion after the determination (of the period). Their mutual consent was considered out of regard for the child's welfare and caution against either of them undertaking something that would harm him for a specific purpose. وَإِنْ أَرَدْتُمْ أَنْ تَسْتَرْضِعُوا (And if you desire to seek a wet nurse) for the children أَوْلادَكُمْ (for your children). It is said: أرضعت المرأة الطفل (the woman breastfed the child) and استرضعها إياه (he sought her to breastfeed him). The first object (المفعول الأول) was omitted because it is understood. فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ (there will be no sin upon you) in it إِذا سَلَّمْتُمْ (if you deliver) to the wet nurses ما آتَيْتُمْ (what you intended to give them) and stipulated for them. It was also read as ما أتيتم (with a short alif) from أتى إليه إحساناً (he performed a kindness towards him) when he did it بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (in a conventional manner). This is related to سلّمتم (you deliver), meaning in the customary and legally approved manner. In al-Kafi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Do not seek a foolish woman (الحمقاء) or a woman with weak eyesight (العمشاء) as a wet nurse, for the milk transmits (qualities). And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Look carefully at who breastfeeds your children, for the child grows up upon it. I say: Meaning, he becomes a young man (شاباً) upon the suckling. وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ (And be wary of Allah), an emphasis on maintaining what has been legislated concerning the matter of children and wet nurses, وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (and know that Allah is watchful of what you do). This is an encouragement and a warning.

(٢٣٤) وَالَّذِينَ يُتَوَفَّوْنَ مِنْكُمْ وَيَذَرُونَ أَزْواجاً يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنْفُسِهِنَ (234) Those of you who die and leave wives, they shall wait by themselves for four months and ten [days]. after them أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَعَشْراً (four months and ten [days]). The feminization of al-ʿashr (العشر) is due to the consideration of the nights (الليالي), because they are the beginnings (غرر) of the months and the days (الأيّام). And regarding the days (الأيّام), the masculine form (التذكير) is not used in such a context, even if the days are intended. One says: "I fasted ten (عشراً)." It was said that perhaps the reason (المقتضي) for this estimation (التقدير) is that the fetus, in most cases, moves at three months if it is male, and at four months if it is female. So, the longer of the two periods (أقصى الأجلين) was considered, and the ten [days] (العشر) were added to it as a precaution (استظهاراً), since its movement might sometimes be weak in the initial stages, and thus not felt.

In al-ʿIlal, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He made incumbent upon her, when she is afflicted by the death of her husband, the same as He made incumbent upon her during his life when he swore off sexual relations (آلى) with her. And He knew that the limit of a woman's patience in abstaining from intercourse is four months. Therefore, He made it incumbent upon her and for her.

And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Because the anguish (حرقة) of the divorced woman subsides in three months, but the anguish (حرقة) of the woman whose husband has died does not subside except in four months and ten [days] (عشراً).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When this verse was revealed, the women came disputing with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said: "We cannot be patient." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to them: "One of you, when her husband died, would take a piece of dung (بعرة) and throw it behind her in her small enclosure (دويرتها) within her curtained chamber (خدرها), and then she would sit [in mourning]. When that same day of the year arrived, she would take it, crush it, then apply it as kohl (اكتحلت بها), and then she would marry. So Allah has relieved you of eight months."

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding every marriage (النكاح), if the husband dies, the waiting period (العدة) for the woman—whether she is free (حرة) or a slave (أمةً)—and regardless of the form of the marriage, whether it was temporary marriage (متعة) or permanent marriage (تزويجاً) or ownership by the right hand (ملك يمين), the waiting period (العدة) is four months and ten [days] (عشراً).

فَإِذا بَلَغْنَ أَجَلَهُنَ (then, when they have completed their term) meaning: their waiting period (عدتهن) has expired. ** فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ (there is no sin upon you) O guardians (أيها الأولياء). ** فِيما فَعَلْنَ فِي أَنْفُسِهِنَ (in what they may do with themselves) such as exposing themselves to proposals (التعرض للخطاب) and all other things that were forbidden to them due to the waiting period (للعدة). ** بِالْمَعْرُوفِ (in accordance with [Islamic] customary standards) meaning: in a manner (بالوجه) that the Divine Law (الشرع) does not disapprove of. وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ** (And Allah is well aware of what you do) so He will recompense you for it.

(٢٣٥) وَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِيما عَرَّضْتُمْ بِهِ مِنْ خِطْبَةِ النِّساءِ (235) And there is no sin upon you in what you hint at concerning the proposal to [marry] women The women in their waiting period (المعتدات). And hinting (التعريض) is when he says: "You are indeed beautiful," or "pious," or "I love a woman whose description is such-and-such," and he mentions some of her characteristics, and the like of that speech which suggests that he desires to marry her, so that she may restrain herself for him if she desires him, but he does not state the marriage explicitly. أَوْ أَكْنَنْتُمْ فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ or what you conceal in your hearts Or what you hide and keep secret in your hearts, so you do not mention it with your tongues, neither hinting (معرضين) nor stating explicitly (مصرحين). ** عَلِمَ اللهُ أَنَّكُمْ سَتَذْكُرُونَهُنَ Allah knows that you will mention them Inevitable due to your desire for them, along with your fear that someone else might precede you to them, so mention them. ** وَلكِنْ لا تُواعِدُوهُنَّ سِرًّا ** but do not make a secret promise to them Meaning, a private meeting (خلوة), as will follow. إِلَّا أَنْ تَقُولُوا in the private meeting ** قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً unless you speak a known word By hinting at the proposal (الخطبة) and not stating it explicitly. وَلا تَعْزِمُوا عُقْدَةَ النِّكاحِ حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ الْكِتابُ and do not resolve upon the knot of marriage until the Book reaches What has been decreed and obligated concerning the waiting period (العدة), أَجَلَهُ its end (منتهاه).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse: ** وَلكِنْ لا تُواعِدُوهُنَّ سِرًّا إِلَّا أَنْ تَقُولُوا قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً. He said: It is when the man says to the woman before her waiting period (عدّتها) ends, "I promise you the house of so-and-so's family (آل فلان)," intending thereby to hint at the proposal (الخطبة) to her. And His saying إِلَّا أَنْ تَقُولُوا قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً ** means hinting at the proposal (التعريض بالخطبة).

And in a narration: It is when the man says, "Your appointment is the house of so-and-so's family (آل فلان)," then he asks her not to precede him with herself (i.e., marry someone else) when her waiting period (عدتها) ends. And the known word (القول المعروف) is seeking what is lawful (الحلال) without resolving upon the knot of marriage (عقدة النكاح) until the Book reaches its term.

And in another [narration]: It is when he meets her and says, "I am indeed desirous of you, and I am generous to women, so do not precede me with yourself." And the secret (السرّ) is that he does not meet privately (يخلو) with her where he promised her.

I say: These narrations are an interpretation of the promise (المواعدة) which includes the known word (القول المعروف) that is permitted. And the end of the last [narration] is an interpretation of the secret (السر) concerning which the promise is forbidden, meaning the private meeting (الخلوة). He only said "he does not meet privately (لا يخلو)" as an alert that the prohibition refers back to the private meeting (الخلوة), not to hinting at the proposal (التعريض بالخطبة). It is as if they used to speak during it (the private meeting) in a manner deemed objectionable, so they were forbidden from that, as is understood from the narrations that follow. And it is possible that the intent of the secret promise (المواعدة سراً) is hinting at the proposal (التعريض بالخطبة) along with promising lewdness (الرفث) and the like. And that was called a secret (سراً) because it is something that is kept secret (يسر). And the intent of "the house of so-and-so's family (بيت آل فلان)" is specifying the time and place for that.

And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): It is when the man says, "I promise you the house of so-and-so's family (آل فلان)," hinting to her at lewdness (الرفث) and specifying the time. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: (إِلَّا أَنْ تَقُولُوا قَوْلاً مَعْرُوفاً). And the known word (القول المعروف) is hinting at the proposal (التعريض بالخطبة) in its proper and lawful manner (حلها).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said concerning this verse: The woman is in her waiting period (عدتها); you say a beautiful word (قولاً جميلاً) to her, making her desire you, but you do not say, "I will do such-and-such and I will do such-and-such," referring to the ugly matter concerning the private parts (البضع) or any ugly matter. And in another [narration]: You say to her while she is in her waiting period (عدتها): "O you, I love nothing except what pleases you, and if your waiting period (عدتك) has passed, do not let me miss you, if Allah wills, and do not precede me with yourself." And all of this is without resolving upon the knot of marriage (عقدة النكاح).

وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُ ما فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ And know that Allah knows what is in your hearts Such as resolving upon what is not permissible. ** فَاحْذَرُوهُ so beware of Him And do not resolve [upon it]. ** وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ غَفُورٌ and know that Allah is All-forgiving To one who resolved but did not act. ** حَلِيمٌ All-bebearing** He does not hasten the punishment upon you.

(٢٣٦) لا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ (236) There is no sin upon you There is no liability (تبعة) upon you regarding a dowry (مهر) or a sin (وزر).

** إِنْ طَلَّقْتُمُ النِّساءَ ما لَمْ تَمَسُّوهُنَ if you divorce women before you have touched them before you have had intercourse with them (ما لم تجامعوهن). And it was read (تماسوهن) with a dammah on the tā’ and an alif in both instances.

** أَوْ تَفْرِضُوا لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً or fixed a dowry for them unless you have fixed it (الا أن تفرضوه). Fixing the obligation (فرض الفريضة) means naming the dowry (تسمية المهر). This is because the divorced woman with whom intercourse has not occurred (الغير المدخول بها), if a dowry is named for her, she receives half of what was named, as in the forthcoming verse. And if a dowry is not named for her, she receives nothing except the compensation (المتعة), as in this verse. And both rulings (الحكمان) are also narrated; al-Ayyashi narrated them, and they are narrated in al-Kafi from al-Sadiq عليه السلام.

وَمَتِّعُوهُنَ But provide for them meaning, give them from your wealth that by which they may benefit (يتمتعن به).

عَلَى الْمُوسِعِ قَدَرُهُ وَعَلَى الْمُقْتِرِ قَدَرُهُ ** the affluent according to his capacity, and the straitened according to his capacity meaning, upon the wealthy man (الغني) who is in affluence (سعة) due to his richness, according to his condition (على قدر حاله); and upon the poor man (الفقير) who is in straitened circumstances (ضيق), according to his condition. And the meaning of His saying (قدره) is the amount (مقداره) that he can afford (يطيقه). And it was read with a sukūn on the dāl.

مَتاعاً a provision a compensation (تمتيعاً).

** بِالْمَعْرُوفِ in a fair manner in the manner that is deemed good (يستحسنه) by the Law (الشرع) and chivalry (المروة).

حَقًّا عَلَى الْمُحْسِنِينَ. a duty on the virtuous.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi: Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about a man who divorces his wife: Should he compensate her (يمتعها)? He said: "Yes. Does he not wish to be among ** الْمُحْسِنِينَ (the virtuous)? And does he not wish to be among the pious (المتقين)?"

And in al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The compensation (متعة) for the divorced woman is an obligation (فريضة).

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who wishes to divorce his wife before having intercourse with her. He said: "He should compensate her (يمتعها) before he divorces her, for Allah said: ** وَمَتِّعُوهُنَّ عَلَى الْمُوسِعِ قَدَرُهُ وَعَلَى الْمُقْتِرِ قَدَرُهُ** (and provide for them, the affluent according to his capacity, and the straitened according to his capacity)."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the divorced woman: What compensation (المتعة) is due to her? He said: "According to the wealth of her husband."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "So let him compensate her in the manner that women like her are compensated."

I say: Perhaps what is meant is observing the condition of both of them (المراعي حالهما جميعاً).

And in al-Faqih, it is narrated: The wealthy man (الغني) compensates with a house (دار) or a servant (خادم), the man of moderate means (الوسط) compensates with a garment (ثوب), and the poor man (الفقير) compensates with a dirham or a ring (خاتم).

And it is narrated: The minimum of it (أدناه) is a head covering (الخمار) or its like.

And in it (al-Faqih) and in al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying وَمَتِّعُوهُنَ (and provide for them) in Sūrat al-Ahzāb concerning this very ruling (الحكم بعينه), he said: "Meaning, treat them kindly (اجملوهن) according to what you are able to afford of fairness (معروف), for they return with sadness (كآبة), loneliness (وحشة), great grief (همّ عظيم), and the gloating (شماتة) of their enemies. Indeed, Allah is Generous (كريم); He is shy (يستحي) and loves the people of shyness (أهل الحياء). The most honorable of you is the one who is most generous (أشدكم اكراماً) to his wives (حلائلهم)." And the rest of the discussion concerning this will come shortly.

(٢٣٧) وَإِنْ طَلَّقْتُمُوهُنَّ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ تَمَسُّوهُنَّ وَقَدْ فَرَضْتُمْ لَهُنَّ فَرِيضَةً فَنِصْفُ ما فَرَضْتُمْ (237) And if you divorce them before you touch them, and you have already fixed a settlement for them, then [pay] half of what you have fixed.

Then for them is half of what you have fixed. ** إِلَّا أَنْ يَعْفُونَ (except if they remit it) meaning the divorced women (المطلقات), that is, they relinquish what is due to them of the half mahr (نصف المهر) and do not demand it from the husbands. ** أَوْ يَعْفُوَا الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ عُقْدَةُ النِّكاحِ (or he remits in whose hand is the marriage tie.) And He is the guardian (الولي) who manages the contract (عقد) of their marriage (نكاحهن).

In al-Faqih and in al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He means the father (الأب) and the one whom the woman appoints as her agent (توكله) and entrusts with her affair, such as a brother (أخ), a relative (قرابة), or others.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in several reports (اخبار): He is the father (الأب), the brother (الأخ), the man to whom a bequest is made (يوصى إليه), and the man whose command is valid regarding the woman's property (مال المرأة), so he sells and buys for her. And if he remits, it is valid (جاز).

In a narration by al-Ayyashi: Whichever of these remits, it is valid (جاز). It was asked: "What if she says, 'I do not permit what he does'?" He said: "She does not have that right. Does she permit his selling regarding what belongs to her, yet she does not permit this?"

In a narration: If her father (أبوها) remits, it is valid (جاز). And her brother (أخوها), if he resides with her and is responsible for her (القائم عليها), then he is in the position of the father (بمنزلة الأب), and it is permissible for him. But if the brother does not reside with her and is not responsible for her, then his command (أمر) over her is not permissible.

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): the one in whose hand is the marriage tie (الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ عُقْدَةُ النِّكاحِ). He is the guardian (الولي) who performed the marriage (أنكح). He may take some and leave some, but he does not have the right to leave all of it.

In al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): the one in whose hand is the marriage tie (الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ عُقْدَةُ النِّكاحِ) is the guardian (الولي).

And from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He is the husband (الزوج). He said: And the guardian (الولي) according to us is the father (الأب) and the grandfather (الجد)—with the presence of the nearer father (الأب الأدنى)—over the virgin (البكر) who has not reached puberty (غير البالغ). As for those besides them, they have no guardianship (ولاية) except by the appointment (توليتهما) of the two of them. However, the first interpretation (الأول) is more apparent (أظهر), and the school of thought (المذهب) follows it. And the meaning of the husband's (الزوج) remission (عفو) is his not reclaiming it, for they used to permit the mahr (المهر) before consummation (الدخول).

وَأَنْ تَعْفُوا أَقْرَبُ لِلتَّقْوى (and that you remit is nearer to Godwariness.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): A man swore an oath to strike his servant (غلامه) but did not fulfill it. When he (peace be upon him) was asked about it, he said: Does not Allah say: and that you remit is nearer to Godwariness, and do not forget graciousness among yourselves (وَلا تَنْسَوُا الْفَضْلَ بَيْنَكُمْ)? And do not forget that some of you should show favor (يتفضل) to others, and do not be exhaustive in demanding your rights (تستقصوا).

In al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): And do not forget graciousness. ** إِنَّ اللهَ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ.** (Indeed Allah is watchful of what you do.)

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: A time of biting (عضوض) will come upon the people, where every man will bite onto what is in his hands, and they will forget the graciousness (الْفَضْلَ) among themselves. Allah said: وَلا تَنْسَوُا الْفَضْلَ بَيْنَكُمْ (and do not forget graciousness among yourselves.)

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: A time of biting (عضوض) will come upon the people, where the believer (المؤمن) will bite onto what is in his hand, and he will not be safe (يؤمن) with that. The Almighty Allah said: (وَلا تَنْسَوُا الْفَضْلَ بَيْنَكُمْ), the Ayah.

In Nahj al-Balaghah: The wealthy man (الموسر) instead of the believer (المؤمن). And he added: In it, the wicked (الأشرار) will rise up, and the virtuous (الأخيار) will be humiliated, and the distressed (المضطرون) will be sold to. And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم forbade the sale of the distressed (بيع المضطرين).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [A narration] close to it.

(238) حافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَواتِ (Maintain the prayers) Persist in them at their appointed times by performing their pillars (أركانها). وَالصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى (and the middle prayer) among them specifically, or the most virtuous (الفضلى), derived from their saying that the most excellent (الأفضل) is the middle (الأوسط). ** وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ (and stand up for Allah) in the prayer ** قانِتِينَ (obediently). It is said: meaning supplicating (داعين) during the standing (القيام). And al-qunut (القنوت) is also obedience (الطاعة) and humility (الخشوع).

وفي الكافي والتهذيب عن الباقر عليه السلام في ** الصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى قال : هي صلاة الظهر وهي أول صلاة صلّاها رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم وهي وسط النهار ووسط الصلاتين بالنهار صلاة الغداة وصلاة العصر قال عليه السلام وفي بعض القراءات ** حافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَواتِ وَالصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى ** وصلاة العصر وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ قانِتِينَ قال وأنزلت هذه الآية يوم الجمعة ورسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم في سفر فقنت فيها رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم وتركها على حالها في السفر والحضر وأضاف للمقيم ركعتين وإنّما وضعت الركعتان اللتان أضافهما النبيّ صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم يوم الجمعة للمقيم لمكان الخطبتين مع الإمام فمن صلّى يوم الجمعة في غير جماعة فليصلها أربع ركعات كصلاة الظهر في سائر الأيّام. In al-Kafi and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding ** الصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى (the middle prayer), he said: It is the Dhuhr prayer (صلاة الظهر). It is the first prayer that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم performed, and it is the middle of the day and the middle of the two daytime prayers: صلاة الغداة and صلاة العصر. He (peace be upon him) said: And in some recitations (القراءات): ** حافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَواتِ وَالصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى ** (Maintain the prayers and the middle prayer) and صلاة العصر (the Asr prayer) وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ قانِتِينَ (and stand up for Allah obediently). He said: And this verse was revealed on Friday (يوم الجمعة) while the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was on a journey (سفر). So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم performed qunut (قنت) in it, and He left it in its state during travel (السفر) and residence (الحضر), and He added two rak'ahs (ركعتين) for the resident (المقيم). The two rak'ahs that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم added on Friday for the resident were only established due to the two sermons (الخطبتين) with the Imam. So, whoever prays on Friday not in congregation (جماعة) should pray it four rak'ahs, like the Dhuhr prayer (صلاة الظهر) on other days.

والعيّاشيّ عنه عليه السلام : أنه قرئ ** حافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَواتِ وَالصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى وصلاة العصر وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ قانِتِينَ والوسطى هي الظهر قال وكذلك كان يقرؤها رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was recited: ** حافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَواتِ وَالصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى (Maintain the prayers and the middle prayer) and صلاة العصر (the Asr prayer) وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ قانِتِينَ (and stand up for Allah obediently). And the middle (الوسطى) is the Dhuhr prayer (الظهر). He said: And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم used to recite it likewise.

وعن الصادق عليه السلام قال : ** الصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى هي الوسطى من صلاة النهار وهي الظهر وإنّما يحافظ أصحابنا على الزوال من أجلها. And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: ** الصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى (The middle prayer) is the middle one of the daytime prayers, and it is the Dhuhr prayer (الظهر). Our companions (أصحابنا) only maintain the time of al-Zawal (الزوال) [midday] because of it.

وفي المجمع عن عليّ عليه السلام : انها الجمعة يوم الجمعة والظهر سائر الأيّام. And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali (peace be upon him): It is the Friday prayer (الجمعة) on Friday (يوم الجمعة), and the Dhuhr prayer (الظهر) on other days.

والقمّيّ عن الصادق عليه السلام : انه قرأ ** حافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَواتِ وَالصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى وصلاة العصر وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ قانِتِينَ قال اقبال الرجل على صلاته ومحافظته حتّى لا يلهيه ولا يشغله عنها شيء. And in al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He recited: ** حافِظُوا عَلَى الصَّلَواتِ وَالصَّلاةِ الْوُسْطى (Maintain the prayers and the middle prayer) and صلاة العصر (the Asr prayer) وَقُومُوا لِلَّهِ قانِتِينَ (and stand up for Allah obediently). He said: It is the man's turning towards his prayer and maintaining it, such that nothing distracts him or preoccupies him from it.

وفي رواية العيّاشيّ : هو الدعاء. And in a narration by al-Ayyashi: It is supplication (الدعاء).

وفي أخرى له : ** قانِتِينَ مطيعين راغبين. And in another narration by him: ** قانِتِينَ (obediently) means obedient (مطيعين), desiring [His reward] (راغبين).

وفي الكافي عن النبيّ صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم قال : لا يزال الشيطان ذعراً من المؤمن ما حافظ على الصلوات الخمس فإذا ضيّعهن تجرأ عليه وأدخله في العظام. And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "The Devil (الشيطان) remains fearful (ذعراً) of the believer as long as he maintains the five prayers (الصلوات الخمس). But if he neglects them, [the Devil] becomes bold against him and involves him in major sins (العظام)."

وعن الباقر عليه السلام : أن الصلاة إذا ارتفعت في وقتها رجعت إلى صاحبها وهي بيضاء مشرقة تقول حفظتني حفظك الله وإذا ارتفعت في غير وقتها بغير حدودها رجعت إلى صاحبها وهي سوداء مظلمة تقول ضيّعتني ضيّعك الله. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When the prayer ascends at its proper time, it returns to its performer, being white and shining, saying: "You maintained me, may Allah maintain you." And when it ascends outside its proper time, without its limits (حدودها), it returns to its performer, being black and dark, saying: "You neglected me, may Allah neglect you."

وعن الصادق عليه السلام : الصلوات الخمس المفروضات من اقام حدودهن وحافظ على مواقيتهن لقي الله يوم القيامة وله عنده عهد يدخله به الجنة ومن لم يقم حدودهن ولم يحافظ على مواقيتهن لقي الله ولا عهد له إن شاء عذّبه وإن شاء غفر له. And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding the five obligatory prayers (الصلوات الخمس المفروضات): whoever establishes their limits (حدودهن) and maintains their appointed times (مواقيتهن) will meet Allah on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and he will have a covenant (عهد) with Him by which He will admit him to Paradise (الجنة). And whoever does not establish their limits and does not maintain their appointed times will meet Allah, and he will have no covenant. If He wills, He will punish him, and if He wills, He will forgive him.

(٢٣٩) فَإِنْ خِفْتُمْ ** (But if you are afraid) from a thief (لص) or a beast (سبع) or other than that ** فَرِجالاً أَوْ رُكْباناً (then [pray] on foot or mounted) so pray walking (راجلين) or riding (راكبين).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "If he fears a beast (سبع) or a thief (لص), he should pronounce the takbir (يكبّر) and gesture (يؤمي) with a sign (إيماءً)."

And in al-Faqih, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Regarding the prayer of battle (صلاة الزحف), He said: "It is takbir (تكبير) and tahlil (تهليل)," then He recited the verses.

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): "If you are in a fearful land (أرض مخوفة) and you fear a thief (لصاً) or a beast (سبعاً), then pray the obligatory prayer (الفريضة) while you are upon your mount (دابتك)."

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The one who fears thieves (اللصوص) prays by gesturing (إيماء) upon his mount (دابته)."

فَإِذا أَمِنْتُمْ (But when you are safe) and your fear has vanished ** فَاذْكُرُوا اللهَ (remember Allah) It was said: Pray the prayer of safety (صلاة الأمن), or thank Him for the safety كَما عَلَّمَكُمْ (as He taught you) meaning, like what He taught you, or a thankfulness that equals His teaching you ** ما لَمْ تَكُونُوا تَعْلَمُونَ (what you did not know) of the Divine Laws (الشرائع) and the manner of prayer (كيفية الصلاة).

(٢٤٠) وَالَّذِينَ يُتَوَفَّوْنَ مِنْكُمْ وَيَذَرُونَ أَزْواجاً وَصِيَّةً (Those of you who die and leave wives should make a bequest for their wives’ maintenance for a year without their being expelled.)

They make a bequest (وصية) before they are about to die. And it was read with the raf' (الرفع) (nominative case).

لِأَزْواجِهِمْ مَتاعاً إِلَى الْحَوْلِ (for their wives’ maintenance for a year)

Meaning that their wives be provided for (تمتع) after them for a full year, that is, maintenance is spent on them from his estate (تركته).

غَيْرَ إِخْراجٍ (without their being expelled)

And they are not to be expelled from their residences (مساكنهنّ). That was in the beginning of Islam, then it was abrogated (نسخت). When a man died, maintenance was spent on his wife from the principal of the wealth (صلب المال) for a year, then she was expelled without inheritance (ميراث). Then the verse of the quarter (الرّبع) and the eighth (الثّمن) abrogated it, so the woman is maintained from her share (نصيبها). al-Ayyashi narrated it.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in several narrations from him and from al-Baqir (peace be upon them both): It is abrogated (منسوخة).

يَتَرَبَّصْنَ بِأَنْفُسِهِنَّ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَعَشْراً (they shall wait by themselves for four months and ten days.)

The verses of inheritance (آيات الميراث) abrogated it.

I say: Meaning, the duration (المدة) was abrogated by the verse of waiting (آية التربص), and the maintenance (النفقة) was abrogated by the verses of inheritance (آيات الميراث). Although the verse of waiting (آية التربص) precedes it in recitation (التلاوة), it is later in revelation (النزول). A discussion regarding these two verses has already passed in the Sixth Introduction (المقدّمة السادسة).

فَإِنْ خَرَجْنَ (But if they leave)

From the residence of the husbands (منزل الأزواج).

فَلا جُناحَ عَلَيْكُمْ فِي ما فَعَلْنَ فِي أَنْفُسِهِنَ (there is no sin upon you in what they may do with themselves)

Such as adorning themselves (التزين) and offering themselves for marriage (التعرض للأزواج).

مِنْ مَعْرُوفٍ (in accordance with customary good)

Meaning that which the Divine Law (الشرع) does not disapprove of.

وَاللهُ عَزِيزٌ (And Allah is All-mighty)

He takes vengeance upon whoever opposes Him.

حَكِيمٌ (All-wise)

He observes their interests (مصالحهم).

(٢٤١) وَلِلْمُطَلَّقاتِ مَتاعٌ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ حَقًّا عَلَى الْمُتَّقِينَ ** (241) And there shall be a provision for the divorced women, in the fairness, a duty on the Godwary.**

He established the provision (المتعة) for all divorced women (المطلقات) after He made it obligatory for one of them. Narrations have also passed that indicate this generalization (التعميم).

In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said: "The provision (متعة) for women is obligatory, whether he has consummated the marriage with her or not, and he should provide the provision before he divorces her." And he said in al-Tahdhib: "The provision (المتعة) is only obligatory for the woman with whom he has not consummated the marriage. As for the woman with whom he has consummated the marriage, it is recommended (يستحب) to provide her with a provision if she does not have a dowry (مهر) owed to her. The first (provision) is before the divorce, and the second is after the expiration of the waiting period (العدة)."

And in it (i.e., al-Faqih or al-Tahdhib), narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): He was asked about the divorced woman for whom the provision (المتعة) is obligatory upon her husband, and he wrote: "The irrevocably divorced woman (البائنة)." And in a narration: "The woman who seeks divorce through mutual agreement (المختلعة) is not given a provision."

In al-Majma': There is disagreement concerning this. It was said that the provision (المتعة) is only obligatory specifically for the woman for whom no dowry (صداق) was specified, and this is narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them). And it was said: (It is obligatory) for every divorced woman, except the woman who seeks divorce through mutual agreement (المختلعة), the woman who seeks divorce through mutual release (المباراة), and the woman who is divorced through mutual imprecation (الملاعنة). And it was said: (It is obligatory) for every divorced woman except the one for whom a dowry was stipulated, if she is divorced before consummation, for she only receives half the dowry (نصف الصداق) and has no provision (متعة). Our companions (أصحابنا) have also narrated this, and it is interpreted as being recommended (الاستحباب). And he said regarding this verse (الآية): It is specified by that other verse (الآية) if they were revealed together, but if that (other verse) was revealed later, then this verse is abrogated (منسوخة). This is because, according to us, the provision (المتعة) is only obligatory for the divorced woman with whom the marriage was not consummated and for whom no dowry (مهر) was stipulated. As for the woman with whom the marriage was consummated, she receives the dowry appropriate for a woman like her (مهر مثلها) if no dowry was specified for her, and if a dowry was specified for her, then she receives what was specified. And the woman with whom the marriage was not consummated, for whom a dowry was stipulated, receives half the dowry and no provision (متعة) in these circumstances. Therefore, this verse must necessarily be specified (تخصيص).

In al-Kafi, in several narrations from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse (الآية), he said: "Her provision (متاعها) is after her waiting period (عدّتها) expires."

** عَلَى الْمُوسِعِ قَدَرُهُ وَعَلَى الْمُقْتِرِ قَدَرُهُ Upon the affluent according to his means, and upon the straitened according to his means.**

He said: "And how can he provide her with a provision while she is in her waiting period (عدّتها)? She hopes for him and he hopes for her, and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, may bring about whatever He wills between them." And he said: "If the man is affluent (موسّعاً عليه), he provides his wife with a provision of a male slave (العبد) or a female slave (الأمة). And the straitened man (المقترّ) provides a provision of wheat (الحنطة), raisins (الزبيب), a garment (الثوب), or dirhams (الدراهم). Indeed, al-Hasan ibn Ali provided a provision for a wife of his with a female slave (أمة), and he never divorced a woman except that he provided her with a provision."

(٢٤٢) كَذلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمْ آياتِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (242) Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you so that you may apply reason. You understand them and use reason concerning them. (٢٤٣) أَلَمْ تَرَ An expression of astonishment and confirmation of their story إِلَى الَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ وَهُمْ أُلُوفٌ meaning: many thousands حَذَرَ الْمَوْتِ فَقالَ لَهُمُ اللهُ مُوتُوا meaning: Allah caused them to die. And this is like His saying, glory be to Him: ** إِنَّما أَمْرُهُ إِذا أَرادَ شَيْئاً أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ ثُمَّ أَحْياهُمْ**. (Indeed, His command, when He wills something, is only that He says to it, 'Be,' and it is. Then He revived them.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): These were the people of a city among the cities of al-Sham (الشام). When the plague occurred and they sensed it, the wealthy would leave the city due to their strength, and the poor would remain in it due to their weakness. Death would be frequent among those who stayed and infrequent among those who left. Those who left would say, "Had we stayed, death would have been frequent among us." And those who stayed would say, "Had we left, death would have been among us." He said: So, they all agreed that if the plague occurred and they sensed it, they would all leave the city. When they sensed the plague, they all left and withdrew from the plague, wary of death. They traveled through the lands for as long as Allah willed. Then they passed by a ruined city whose inhabitants had departed from it, having been annihilated by the plague. They settled there. When they unloaded their baggage and settled down, Allah, the Almighty, the Majestic, said to them: "Die all of you (مُوتُوا جَمِيعًا)." So, they died instantly and became exposed, decaying bones (رميماً). They were on the path of passersby, so the passersby swept them away, removed them, and gathered them in one place. A Prophet from the Prophets of the Children of Israel, named Hizqil (حزقيل), passed by them. When He saw those bones, he wept and was moved, saying: "My Lord, if You willed, You would revive them now, just as You caused them to die, so they would populate Your lands, bear Your servants, and worship You alongside those of Your creation who worship You." So, Allah revealed to him: "Do you wish for that?" He said: "Yes, O Lord." So, Allah revived them. He said: Then Allah, the Almighty, the Majestic, revealed: "Say such-and-such." So, he said what Allah, the Almighty, the Majestic, commanded him to say. He said—Abu ʿAbdillah (peace be upon him) said—that it was the Greatest Name (الاسم الأعظم). When Hizqil said that, he looked at the bones flying, some joining others, and they returned alive, looking at one another, glorifying Allah, the Almighty, the Majestic, proclaiming His greatness (يكبّرونه), and declaring His oneness (يهلّلونه). Hizqil then said: "I bear witness that Allah is capable of everything."

The narrator said: Abu ʿAbdillah (peace be upon him) said: Concerning them was this Ayah revealed.

In al-Ghawali, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith where he mentions the Nawruz (نيروز) of the Persians, he said: Then a Prophet from the Prophets of the Children of Israel asked his Lord to revive the people ** الَّذِينَ خَرَجُوا مِنْ دِيارِهِمْ وَهُمْ أُلُوفٌ حَذَرَ الْمَوْتِ (who left their homes, and they were thousands, wary of death), whom Allah had caused to die. So, Allah revealed to him to pour water upon their resting places (مضاجعهم). So, he poured water upon them on this day, and they came back to life, and they were thirty thousand. Thus, the pouring of water on the day of Nawruz (النيروز) became an established tradition (سنّة ماضية) whose reason is known only to those firmly grounded in knowledge (الراسخون في العلم).

In al-Majmaʿ: Al-Baqir (peace be upon him) was asked about these people concerning whom ** فَقالَ لَهُمُ اللهُ مُوتُوا ثُمَّ أَحْياهُمْ (Thereupon Allah said to them, 'Die,' and so they died. Then He revived them). He was asked: Did He revive them until people looked at them and then caused them to die again, or did He return them to the world so that they inhabited houses and ate food? He said: No, rather Allah returned them so that they inhabited houses, ate food, and married women, and they remained thus for as long as Allah willed, and then they died at their appointed times (بآجالهم).

إِنَّ اللهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى النَّاسِ (243 cont.) Indeed Allah is gracious to mankind, In that He makes them see what they should take heed from. وَلَكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ but most people are not thankful. They do not take heed.

(244) وَقاتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (And fight in the way of Allah) For fleeing from death will not save one from it. And know that Allah is All-hearing of what the laggards (المخلفون) and the foremost (السابقون) say, عَلِيمٌ (All-knowing) of what they conceal.

(245) مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً (Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan) [A loan] coupled with sincerity (الإخلاص) and good disposition (طيب النفس), from lawful and pure wealth, so He will multiply it (فَيُضاعِفَهُ). (It was also recited with the fā' (الفاء) in the accusative case (بنصب الفاء)). لَهُ أَضْعافاً كَثِيرَةً (manifold increase) which none can estimate save Allah. وَاللهُ يَقْبِضُ وَيَبْصُطُ (and Allah tightens and expands) He withholds and He extends, so do not be stingy towards Him with what He has extended to you. وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (and to Him you shall be brought back) and He will recompense you according to what you have put forth.

In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning giving to the Imam (صلة الامام).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "There is nothing more beloved to Allah than spending a dirham for the Imam. And Allah will surely make that dirham for him in Paradise like Mount Uhud." Then he said: "Indeed, Allah says in His Book: مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً فَيُضاعِفَهُ لَهُ أَضْعافاً كَثِيرَةً (Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan so He will multiply it for him manifold increase)." He said: "By Allah, this is specifically concerning giving to the Imam (صلة الامام)."

And in al-Ma'ali and al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): When this verse was revealed to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْها (Whoever brings a good deed shall receive better than it), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Allah, increase me." So Allah, the Glorified, revealed: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثالِها (Whoever brings a good deed shall receive ten times its like). Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "O Allah, increase me." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يُقْرِضُ اللهَ قَرْضاً حَسَناً فَيُضاعِفَهُ لَهُ أَضْعافاً كَثِيرَةً (Who is it that will lend Allah a good loan so He will multiply it for him manifold increase). Thus, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله knew that the abundance (الكثير) from Allah cannot be counted and has no limit (منتهى).

(٢٤٦) أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الْمَلَإِ مِنْ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ مِنْ بَعْدِ مُوسى (246) Have you not regarded the elite of the Children of Israel after Moses, Has your knowledge not reached, O Muhammad, the group of nobles (جماعة الأشراف) from the Children of Israel after the death of Moses, ** إِذْ قالُوا لِنَبِيٍّ لَهُمُ (when they said to a prophet of theirs)? In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was واشموئيل, and in Arabic, he is إسماعيل (Ishmael). ** ابْعَثْ لَنا مَلِكاً نُقاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (Appoint for us a king, that we may fight in the way of Allah). Set up for us a commander (أميراً) so that we may rise up for fighting alongside him, manage his affairs, and proceed according to his opinion concerning it. In al-Majma' and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The king (الملك) in that time was the one who marched with the armies, and the Prophet (النبيّ) established his command for him and informed him of the news from his Lord. ** قالَ هَلْ عَسَيْتُمْ إِنْ كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْقِتالُ أَلَّا تُقاتِلُوا (He said, 'Is it not likely that if fighting is prescribed for you, you will not fight?') Meaning: that you will not respond and will not fulfill (your promise). This is like taking a covenant from them. ** قالُوا وَما لَنا أَلَّا نُقاتِلَ (They said, 'Why should we not fight) فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ وَقَدْ أُخْرِجْنا مِنْ دِيارِنا وَأَبْنائِنا (in the way of Allah, seeing that we have been expelled from our homes and our children?') By captivity (بالسبي) and coercion (والقهر) over our regions (على نواحينا). ** فَلَمَّا كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقِتالُ تَوَلَّوْا إِلَّا قَلِيلاً مِنْهُمْ وَاللهُ عَلِيمٌ بِالظَّالِمِينَ (But when fighting was prescribed for them, they turned away, all but a few of them, and Allah is aware of the wrongdoers.) This is a threat (تهديد) to those who turned away.

(٢٤٧) وَقالَ لَهُمْ نَبِيُّهُمْ إِنَّ اللهَ قَدْ بَعَثَ لَكُمْ طالُوتَ مَلِكاً قالُوا أَنَّى يَكُونُ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ عَلَيْنا (247) And their prophet said to them, 'Indeed Allah has appointed Saul as king over you.' They said, 'How can he have kingship over us) From where can he have that, and how can he be worthy? وَنَحْنُ أَحَقُّ بِالْمُلْكِ مِنْهُ ** (when we are worthier of kingship than him) by inheritance and capability (مكنة). وَلَمْ يُؤْتَ سَعَةً مِنَ الْمالِ قالَ إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفاهُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَزادَهُ بَسْطَةً (and he has not been given ample wealth?' He said, 'Indeed Allah has chosen him over you, and has increased him in amplitude) in virtue (فضيلة) and breadth (سعةً). فِي الْعِلْمِ وَالْجِسْمِ وَاللهُ يُؤْتِي مُلْكَهُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ واسِعٌ (in knowledge and physique. And Allah grants His kingdom to whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-bounteous,) vast in bounty (واسع الفضل), He expands [provision] upon the poor and enriches him. عَلِيمٌ (all-knowing.') [He is] All-Knowing of who is fit for kingship. When they deemed his kingship unlikely due to his poverty, He refuted them by stating that the foundation (العمدة) of it is Allah's choosing, and He has selected him over you, and He is more knowledgeable of what is beneficial (المصالح). And [He refuted them] by stating that the condition for it is the light of knowledge (نور العلم) so that he may be enabled through it to know political affairs (الأمور السياسية), and the greatness of the body (جسامة البدن) so that he may be of greater significance (خطراً) in the hearts and stronger in resisting the enemy and enduring wars—not what you mentioned. And Allah has increased him in both of these. It was said that when the man stood up, he would extend his hand and reach his head [due to his height]. And [He refuted them] by stating that He, the Exalted, is the absolute Owner of the Kingdom (مالك الملك على الإطلاق), so He has the right to grant it to whomever He wills. And [He refuted them] by stating that He is vast in bounty, He enriches him, and He is All-Knowing of him when He chooses him.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the Children of Israel, after [the time of] Musa, committed acts of disobedience (المعاصي), changed the religion of Allah, and rebelled against the command of their Lord. There was a prophet among them who commanded and forbade them, but they did not obey him. It is narrated that he was the Prophet Irmiya (ارميا). So Allah empowered Jalut (جالوت) over them, and he was from the Copts (القبط). He harmed them, killed their men, expelled them from their homes, seized their wealth, and enslaved their women. So they turned in distress to their prophet and said: "Ask Allah to send forth لَنا مَلِكاً نُقاتِلْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ (for us a king, so that we may fight in the way of Allah.') The prophethood (النبوّة) among the Children of Israel was in one house, and the kingship (الملك) and authority (السلطان) were in another house. Allah did not combine prophethood and kingship for them in one house. Because of this, they said to a prophet of theirs, 'Appoint for us a king, so that we may fight in the way of Allah.' He said, 'Is it not likely that if fighting is prescribed for you, you will not fight?' They said, 'What reason do we have not to fight in the way of Allah, seeing that we have been expelled from our homes and our children?' And it was as Allah the Almighty said: فَلَمَّا كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ الْقِتالُ تَوَلَّوْا إِلَّا قَلِيلاً مِنْهُمْ وَقالَ لَهُمْ نَبِيُّهُمْ إِنَّ اللهَ قَدْ بَعَثَ لَكُمْ طالُوتَ مَلِكاً (Then, when fighting was prescribed for them, they turned away, all but a few of them. And their prophet said to them, 'Indeed Allah has appointed Saul as king over you.') So they became angry at that. They said, 'How can he have kingship over us, when we are worthier of kingship than him, and he has not been given ample wealth?' The prophethood was in the progeny of Lawi (لاوي), and the kingship was in the progeny of Yusuf (يوسف). Talut (طالوت) was from the progeny of Ibn Yamin (ابن يامين), the full brother of Yusuf (يوسف). He was neither from the house of prophethood (بيت النبوّة) nor from the house of kingship (بيت المملكة). Their prophet said to them: إِنَّ اللهَ اصْطَفاهُ عَلَيْكُمْ وَزادَهُ بَسْطَةً فِي الْعِلْمِ وَالْجِسْمِ وَاللهُ يُؤْتِي مُلْكَهُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ واسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (Indeed Allah has chosen him over you, and has increased him in amplitude in knowledge and physique. And Allah grants His kingdom to whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing.') He was the greatest of them in physique, and he was brave and strong, and he was the most knowledgeable of them. However, he was poor, so they criticized him for his poverty and said: لَمْ يُؤْتَ سَعَةً مِنَ الْمالِ. (and he has not been given ample wealth?)

(٢٤٨) وَقالَ لَهُمْ نَبِيُّهُمْ إِنَّ آيَةَ مُلْكِهِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَكُمُ التَّابُوتُ فِيهِ سَكِينَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ آلُ مُوسى وَآلُ هارُونَ تَحْمِلُهُ الْمَلائِكَةُ (248) And their prophet said to them: 'Indeed the sign of his kingship is that the Ark will come to you, carrying a tranquility from your Lord and a remnant of what the house of Moses and the house of Aaron have left behind, borne by the angels.'

He (peace be upon him) said: The Ark (التابوت) was that which Allah sent down to Moses, and his mother placed him in it and cast it into the sea (اليم). It remained among the Children of Israel, and they sought blessings through it (يَتَبَرَّكُونَ بِهِ). When the death of Moses approached, he placed in it the Tablets (الألواح), his shield (درعه), and whatever signs of prophethood (آيات النبوّة) he possessed, and entrusted it to Joshua (يوشع), his successor (وصيّه). The Ark remained among them until they committed sins and treated the Ark lightly (واستخفوا بالتابوت). Allah raised it from them. When they asked the Prophet, and Allah sent Saul (طالوت) to them as a king to fight alongside them, Allah returned the Ark to them, just as Allah the Almighty said: ** إِنَّ آيَةَ مُلْكِهِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَكُمُ التَّابُوتُ فِيهِ سَكِينَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ آلُ مُوسى وَآلُ هارُونَ تَحْمِلُهُ الْمَلائِكَةُ 'Indeed the sign of his kingship is that the Ark will come to you, carrying a tranquility from your Lord and a remnant of what the house of Moses and the house of Aaron have left behind, borne by the angels.' He said: The remnant (البقية) is the progeny (ذريّة) of the Prophets.

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about His saying, the Almighty: ** وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ آلُ مُوسى وَآلُ هارُونَ (and a remnant of what the house of Moses and the house of Aaron have left behind). He said: The progeny (ذريّة) of the Prophets.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: And the fragments (رضراض) (1) of the Tablets (الألواح), in which are knowledge (العلم) and wisdom (الحكمة).

And al-Ayyashi added: Knowledge (العلم) came from the heaven, so it was written on the Tablets (الألواح) and placed in the Ark (التابوت).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him), he said: In it were the Tablets (ألواح) of Moses that were broken, and the basin (الطست) in which the hearts of the Prophets were washed.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The tranquility (السكينة) is a wind (ريح) from Paradise (الجنة) having a face like the face of a human. When the Ark (التابوت) was placed between the Muslims and the disbelievers (الكفّار), if a man advanced toward the Ark, he would not return until he was killed or victorious. And whoever retreated from the Ark committed disbelief (كفر) and the Imam would kill him.

And in al-Ma'ani: Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him) was asked what the Ark (تابوت) of Moses was and what its capacity was. He said: "Three cubits by two cubits." He was asked: "And what was in it?" He said: "The staff of Moses (عصا موسى) and the tranquility (السكينة)." He was asked: "And what is the tranquility (السكينة)?" He said: "A Spirit (روح) of Allah that speaks. When they differed about something, It would speak to them and inform them with the clarification of what they desired."

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The tranquility (السكينة) that was in it was a gentle, rustling wind (ريح هفّافة) from Paradise (الجنة), having a face like the face of a human.

And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The remnant (البقية) is the staff of Moses (عصا موسى) and the fragments (رضراض) of the Tablets (الألواح).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): So the angels came bearing it.

And in a narration: They bore it in the form of a cow (البقرة).

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The likeness of the Weapon (السلاح) among us is only like the likeness of the Ark (التابوت) among the Children of Israel. Whichever household among the Children of Israel found the Ark at their door was granted prophethood (النبوّة). Thus, whoever among us receives the Weapon is granted the Imamate (الإمامة). And in a narration: Wherever the Ark circulated among the Children of Israel, the kingship (الملك) circulated, and wherever the Weapon circulates among us, the kingship (الملك) and knowledge (العلم) circulate.

And in another narration: Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him) was asked: "What is the tranquility (السكينة)?" He said: "A wind (ريح) that emerges from Paradise (الجنة), having a form like the form of a human and a pleasant fragrance. It is the one that descended upon Abraham (إبراهيم) and began circling the corners (أركان) of the House (البيت) while he was constructing the pillars (الأساطين)." He was asked: "Is it the one about which Allah the Almighty said: ** فِيهِ سَكِينَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ وَبَقِيَّةٌ مِمَّا تَرَكَ آلُ مُوسى وَآلُ هارُونَ (carrying a tranquility from your Lord and a remnant of what the house of Moses and the house of Aaron have left behind)?" He said: "Yes, and that tranquility (السكينة) was in the Ark (التابوت). And in it was a basin (طست) in which the hearts of the Prophets were washed. The Ark used to circulate among the Children of Israel with the Prophets." Then he turned to us and said: "What is your Ark (تابوتكم)?" We said: "The Weapon (السلاح)." He said: "You have spoken truly; it is your Ark."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), is that which is close to it, and he added after mentioning the Ayah, he said: "It is from this (هي من هذا)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Ark (التابوت) was in the hands of the enemies of the Children of Israel, the Amalekites (العمالقة), who had overcome them when the affair of the Children of Israel deteriorated and innovations (الأحداث) occurred among them. Then Allah seized it from their hands and returned it to the Children of Israel, borne by the angels. He said: And it was said. And in a narration: The tranquility (السكينة) has two wings and a head like the head of a cat (الهرة), made of chrysolite (الزبرجد) and emerald (الزمرد). This has been narrated in our reports (أخبارنا). He said: And the apparent meaning (الظاهر) is that the tranquility (السكينة) is security (أمنة) and reassurance (طمأنينة) which Allah, glory be to Him, placed in it so that the Children of Israel might find rest in it. And the remnant (البقية) may possibly be a remnant of knowledge (العلم) or something from the signs of the Prophets (علامات الأنبياء), and it may possibly encompass all of them.

(1) Al-Radhrad (الرضراض): The fragments (الفتات) from radhradahu (رضرضه) when He broke it and scattered it. And the fragments of the Tablets (رضراض الألواح) are their broken pieces. And perhaps the Ark (التابوت) is interpreted as the heart (القلب), and the tranquility (السكينة) as knowledge (العلم) and sincerity (الإخلاص), and its coming means making it a repository of knowledge (العلم) and dignity (الوقار) after it was not so. "From him (may Allah have mercy on him)." (منه ره).

(٢٤٩) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (249) There is indeed a sign in that for you, should you be faithful. This is either part of the completion of the speech of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم or a discourse from Allah. فَلَمَّا فَصَلَ طالُوتُ بِالْجُنُودِ When Saul set out with the troops, He separated with them from his town to fight the Amalekites (العمالقة). Its origin (أصله) is that he separated himself from it, but when the object (مفعوله) was frequently omitted, it became like an intransitive verb (كاللازم). قالَ إِنَّ اللهَ مُبْتَلِيكُمْ he said, 'Allah is testing you testing you (مختبركم) بِنَهَرٍ فَمَنْ شَرِبَ مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ مِنِّي with a river. Whoever drinks from it is not of me, then he is not of my company (جملتي) or my followers (أشياعي). وَمَنْ لَمْ يَطْعَمْهُ and whoever does not taste it, did not taste it (لم يذقه). فَإِنَّهُ مِنِّي إِلَّا مَنِ اغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةً بِيَدِهِ he is indeed of me, barring someone who scoops up a handful with his hand.' This is an exception (استثناء) from His saying: فَمَنْ شَرِبَ مِنْهُ (Whoever drinks from it), and its meaning is the permission (الرخصة) to scoop up a handful (الغرفة) with the hand. And ghurfah (غرفة) was read with a fathah (بالفتح). فَشَرِبُوا مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً مِنْهُمْ But they drank from it, all but a few of them. Except for three hundred and thirteen men. Among them were those who scooped up a handful, and among them were those who did not drink at all. This is according to al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). And it is narrated: That whoever restricted himself to the handful (الغرفة) found it sufficient for his drink and his tools (أدواته), and whoever did not restrict himself, his thirst overcame him, his lip blackened, and he was unable to proceed. And such is the world (الدنيا) for the seeker of the Hereafter (الآخرة). فَلَمَّا جاوَزَهُ هُوَ When he crossed it, he Saul (طالوت) crossed the river (تخطّى النهر). وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ and those who had faith with him, meaning the few among his companions (أصحابه), and they saw the great number of Goliath's (جالوت) troops. قالُوا they said, those who had scooped up a handful said: لا طاقَةَ لَنَا الْيَوْمَ بِجالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِ قالَ الَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ 'Today we have no strength to face Goliath and his troops.' Those who were certain Those who were certain (الذين كانوا يتيقّنون) أَنَّهُمْ مُلاقُوا اللهِ that they would meet Allah and they were those who did not scoop up a handful (لم يغترفوا): كَمْ مِنْ فِئَةٍ قَلِيلَةٍ غَلَبَتْ فِئَةً كَثِيرَةً بِإِذْنِ اللهِ وَاللهُ مَعَ الصَّابِرِينَ. said, 'How many a small company has overcome a numerous company by Allah's leave! And Allah is with the patient.'

(٢٥٠) وَلَمَّا بَرَزُوا لِجالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِ قالُوا رَبَّنا أَفْرِغْ (١) عَلَيْنا صَبْراً وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدامَنا وَانْصُرْنا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكافِرِينَ. (250) And when they encountered Goliath and his troops, they said, 'Our Lord, pour down (1) patience upon us, make our feet steady, and help us against the faithless lot.'

(٢٥١) فَهَزَمُوهُمْ بِإِذْنِ اللهِ وَقَتَلَ داوُدُ جالُوتَ وَآتاهُ اللهُ الْمُلْكَ وَالْحِكْمَةَ وَعَلَّمَهُ مِمَّا يَشاءُ (251) So they routed them by Allah's leave, and David killed Goliath, and Allah gave him the kingship and wisdom, and taught him whatever He wished.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Allah revealed to their Prophet that Goliath (جالوت) would be killed by the one upon whom the armor of Moses (peace be upon him) would fit perfectly. He was a man from the progeny of Levi (لاوي) son of Jacob (يعقوب), whose name was David (داود) son of Jesse (اشي) (1). Jesse (اشي) was a shepherd and had ten sons, the youngest of whom was David (داود). When Saul (طالوت) sent for the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل) and gathered them for the war against Goliath (جالوت), he sent word to Jesse (اشي): "Attend, and bring your sons." When they arrived, he called his sons one after the other and dressed them in the armor, the armor of Moses (درع موسى). For some, it was too long, and for others, it was too short. He said to Jesse (اشي): "Have you left behind any of your sons?" He replied: "Yes, the youngest of them; I left him tending the sheep." So, he sent for him, and he brought him. When he was called, he approached, and he had a sling (مقلاع) with him. He said: Three stones (صخرات) called out to him on his way, saying: "O David, take us!" So, he took them and put them in his pouch (مخلاته). He was mighty in strength (شديد البطش), powerful in body, and courageous. When he came to Saul (طالوت), he dressed him in the armor of Moses (درع موسى), and it fit him perfectly. When Saul set out with the troops, their Prophet said to them: "O Children of Israel, 'Allah is going to test you with a river' in this wilderness. Whoever drinks from it is not of the party of Allah, and whoever does not drink is of the party of Allah, 'except someone who scoops up a handful with his hand.'" When they reached the river, Allah permitted every one of them to scoop up one handful. 'But they drank from it, all but a few of them.' Those who drank from it were sixty thousand, and this was a trial (امتحان) by which they were tested, just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has said.

And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: The few (القليل) who neither drank nor scooped up a handful were three hundred and thirteen men (2). When they crossed the river and looked at the troops of Goliath (جالوت), those who had drunk from it said: 'We have no strength today against Goliath and his troops.' And those who had not drunk said: 'Our Lord, pour down patience upon us, make our feet steady, and help us against the faithless lot.' Then David (داود) came and stood opposite Goliath (جالوت). Goliath was upon an elephant, and upon his head was a crown, and on his forehead was a ruby whose light shone brightly, and his troops were before him. David (داود) took a stone from those stones and threw it at Goliath's right flank (ميمنة). It passed through the air and fell upon them, and they were routed. He took another stone and threw it at Goliath's left flank (ميسرة), and they were routed. Then he threw a stone at Goliath, and it struck the ruby on his forehead, reaching his brain, and he fell to the ground dead. This is the Almighty's saying: 'So they routed them by Allah's leave, and David killed Goliath, and Allah gave him the kingship and wisdom.'

And in a narration from al-Ayyashi: When David (داود) entered the camp, he heard them magnifying the matter of Goliath (جالوت). He said to them: "Why do you magnify his matter? By Allah, if I see him, I will surely kill him." News of him spread until he was brought before Saul (طالوت). Saul said: "O youth, what strength do you possess, and what have you tested in yourself?" He replied: "The lion used to attack a sheep from my flock, and I would overtake it, grab its head, pry open its jaws, and take the sheep from its mouth." Saul said: "Then call for a full-length coat of mail (درع سابغة) for me." He said: A coat of mail was brought, and he threw it over his neck, and it filled him completely. Saul (طالوت) said: "By Allah, perhaps Allah will kill him by his hand." He said: When they awoke and returned to Saul (طالوت) and the people met [in battle], David (داود) said: "Show me Goliath (جالوت)." When he saw him, he took the stone, placed it in his sling (مقذافه), and threw it. It struck him between his eyes, crushing his skull, and he fell headlong from his mount. The people said: "David (داود) has killed Goliath (جالوت)." And he ruled the people until no mention of Saul (طالوت) was heard. The Children of Israel (بنو إسرائيل) gathered around David (داود), and Allah sent down the Zabur (الزّبور) upon him, and taught him the craft of iron, and softened it for him.

وَلَوْ لا دَفْعُ اللهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ And were it not for Allah's repelling people, some by means of others,

And it was read difā‘ Allāh (دفاع الله) (Allah's defense). It was said that this means by granting victory to the believers over the disbelievers (الكفّار). And it was said that it means by repelling destruction from the wicked (الفاجر) through the righteous (البر).

And in al-Majma', the second [reading] was narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

لَفَسَدَتِ الْأَرْضُ the earth would surely be corrupted.

meaning, disbelief (الكفر) and destruction (الهلاك) would become widespread.

وَلكِنَّ اللهَ عزّ وجل ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ. But Allah the Mighty and Majestic is gracious to the creatures.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: Indeed, Allah repels [harm] by means of those among our followers (شيعتنا) who pray, from those among our followers who do not pray. If they were to agree upon abandoning the prayer, they would perish. And indeed, Allah repels [harm] by means of those among our followers who pay Zakat (يزكي), from those who do not pay Zakat. If they were to agree upon abandoning Zakat (الزكاة), they would perish. And indeed, Allah repels [harm] by means of those among our followers who perform Hajj (يحج), from those who do not perform Hajj. If they were to agree upon abandoning Hajj (الحجّ), they would perish. This is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَلَوْ لا دَفْعُ اللهِ النَّاسَ بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ لَفَسَدَتِ الْأَرْضُ وَلكِنَّ اللهَ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ By Allah, it was revealed only concerning you, and He did not mean anyone other than you.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Were it not for bowing servants (عباد ركع), suckling infants (صبيان رضع), and grazing animals (بهائم رتع), the punishment would be poured upon you profusely. And narrated from Him (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Indeed, Allah sets things right through the righteousness of the Muslim man, his child, his grandchild, the people of his small house (دويرته), and the small houses around him. They remain under the protection of Allah as long as he is among them.

footnote (١)** أَفْرِغْ** means: pour down patience upon us, that is, grant us success in enduring patience during *Jihad* (*الجهاد*).

footnote (١) In *al-Majma'* and *Tafsir al-Baydawi*, it is *Iysh* (*ايش*) with the *ya'* (*الياء*) followed by the dotted *shin* (*المعجمة*), and it resembles a Hebrew word, and *Asi* (*اسى*) is its Arabicized form [from the author].

footnote (٢) This contradicts what preceded in *al-Kafi* and *al-Ayyashi* that this number was of those who scooped up a handful [from the author].

(٢٥٢) تِلْكَ آياتُ اللهِ (252) These are the signs of Allah [It is] a reference to what was narrated concerning the story of the thousands (الألوف), the appointment of Talut as king, the coming of the Ark, the defeat of the tyrants (الجبابرة), and the killing of Goliath (جالوت) at the hand of a boy. ** نَتْلُوها عَلَيْكَ بِالْحَقِ We recite them to you with the truth In the corresponding manner (بالوجه المطابق) which the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) do not doubt, because it is likewise in their books. ** وَإِنَّكَ لَمِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ and indeed you are one of the apostles. Since you inform about them without prior knowledge (تعرّف) or hearing (استماع).

(٢٥٣) تِلْكَ الرُّسُلُ This is a reference to the group whose stories were mentioned in the Surah. ** فَضَّلْنا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ ** (253) Those are the apostles, some of whom We have preferred over others. by specializing him with a merit (مَنْقَبَة) that is not shared by others. مِنْهُمْ مَنْ كَلَّمَ اللهُ (Of them is he to whom Allah spoke) without travel, like Moses (peace be upon him) on the night of confusion (الحيرة) at the Mount (الطور), and Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم on the night of the Ascension (المعراج) when ** فَكانَ قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى (He was two bows' length or nearer), though there is a vast difference (بَوْن بَعِيد) between the two. ** وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَهُمْ دَرَجاتٍ (and raised some of them in rank) by preferring him over others in multiple aspects (وُجُوه مُتَعَدِّدَة) and by distant ranks (مَرَاتِب مُتَبَاعِدَة). So Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was given what no one else was given, including miracles (المعجزات) ascending to a thousand and more, and He was sent to the jinn and mankind, and He was specialized with the miracle that remains until the Day of Resurrection.

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Allah has not created a creature better than me, nor more honored by Him than me." Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) said: "So I said, 'O Messenger of Allah, are you better, or Gabriel?'" He replied: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty preferred His Messenger-Prophets over His closest angels (ملائكته المقرّبين), and He preferred me over all the Prophets (النبيين) and Messengers (المرسلين). And the preference after me belongs to you, O Ali, and the Imams (الأئمة) from your progeny. And indeed, the angels are our servants and the servants of those who love us."

وَآتَيْنا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ الْبَيِّناتِ and We gave Jesus son of Mary manifest proofs, such as raising the dead and healing the born-blind (الأكمه) and the leper (الأبرص). وَأَيَّدْناهُ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ and We strengthened him with the Holy Spirit, Gabriel (جبرائيل), as passed in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him). وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ مَا اقْتَتَلَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ (Had Allah wished, those who came after them would not have fought) after the Messengers ** مِنْ بَعْدِ ما جاءَتْهُمُ الْبَيِّناتُ (after the manifest proofs had come to them)—the clear miracles (المعجزات الواضحات)—due to their differing in religion and preferring some of them over others. ** وَلكِنِ اخْتَلَفُوا فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ آمَنَ (But they differed. So there were some of them who believed) by adhering to the religion of the Prophets ** وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ كَفَرَ (and some of them who were faithless) due to their turning away from it. ** وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ مَا اقْتَتَلُوا (Had Allah wished, they would not have fought)—He repeated it for emphasis— ** وَلكِنَّ اللهَ يَفْعَلُ ما يُرِيدُ (but Allah does whatever He wills) of abandonment (الخذلان) and protection (العصمة), in justice (عَدْلاً) and grace (فَضْلاً).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "And in this [verse] is that by which one may infer that the Companions (أصحاب) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم differed after Him: ** فَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ آمَنَ وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ كَفَرَ** (So there were some of them who believed and some of them who were faithless)."

And al-Ayyashi [narrated]: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) was asked on the Day of the Camel (يوم الجمل): "The people [on the other side] said Allahu Akbar and we said Allahu Akbar; the people said La ilaha illa Allah and we said La ilaha illa Allah; the people prayed and we prayed. So, based on what are we fighting them?" He recited this verse, then said: "We are those who came after them." And He said: "So we are those who believed, and they are those who were faithless."

And in a narration, He said: "When the difference occurred, we were more worthy of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, and of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and of the Book, and of the Truth (الحق). So we are those who believed, and they are those who were faithless. And Allah willed their fighting by His Will and His Intention (إرادته)."
