(٢٥٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنْفِقُوا مِمَّا رَزَقْناكُمْ مِنْ قَبْلِ أَنْ يَأْتِيَ يَوْمٌ لا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلا خُلَّةٌ وَلا شَفاعَةٌ (254) O you who have faith! Spend out of what We have provided you before the day comes in which there will be neither bargaining, nor friendship, nor intercession.

And it was read with fathah (بالفتح) in all of them. Meaning, before the day comes when you are unable to remedy what you neglected (ما فرطتم) and achieve salvation from His punishment, since there is no bargaining (بيع) therein, whereby you might acquire what you spend and ransom yourselves with it from the punishment. Nor friendship (خُلَّةٌ), so that your friends (اخلّاؤكم) might assist you concerning it, or forgive you for it, because الْأَخِلَّاءُ يَوْمَئِذٍ بَعْضُهُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ إِلَّا الْمُتَّقِينَ (On that day, friends will be enemies of one another, except the Godwary) and وَلِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ شَأْنٌ يُغْنِيهِ (On that day every man among them will have a preoccupation that will suffice him). Nor intercession (شفاعة), except for whom the All-beneficent permits and approves his word, so that you might speak to intercessors (شفعاء) who intercede for you regarding the portion of what is due upon you (ما في ذممكم). And it is probable that what is meant by it is the Day of Death (يوم الموت), as passed previously in His mighty saying: ** وَاتَّقُوا يَوْماً لا تَجْزِي نَفْسٌ عَنْ نَفْسٍ شَيْئاً (And beware of a day when no soul will compensate for another soul in any way). And this is more apparent (أظهر). ** وَالْكافِرُونَ هُمُ الظَّالِمُونَ (And the faithless—it is they who are the wrongdoers.) Since their wrongdoing (ظلمهم) against themselves reached the limit (الغاية), and their deprivation (حرمانهم) of these matters reached the end (النهاية). This is like when it is said, "So-and-so is the jurist (الفقيه) in the town," meaning his precedence (تقدّمه) over others is intended.

(255) اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ (Allah—there is no god except Him) He is the One deserving of worship, and none other. ** الْحَيُ (the Living) the All-Knowing (العليم), the All-Powerful (القدير). ** الْقَيُّومُ (the Self-subsisting) the Everlasting (الدائم), the One Who stands (القائم) maintaining the administration of creation and its preservation (حفظه). It is said of someone that he stands by it (قام به) if he preserves it (حفظه). ** لا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ (Neither drowsiness overtakes Him) Sinah (سِنَةٌ) is drowsiness (نعاس), which is the languor (الفتور) that precedes sleep. ** وَلا نَوْمٌ (nor sleep) by way of priority (بالطريق الأولى). This is an emphasis on the sleep that is implicitly negated. This sentence negates anthropomorphism (التشبيه) and confirms that He is Living (حياً) and Self-subsisting (قيوماً). Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He (the Imam) saw someone sitting mutawarrikan (متوركاً)—resting his leg upon his thigh. It was said to him: "This is a disliked sitting posture (جلسة مكروهة)." He replied: "No. Indeed, the Jews claimed that when the Lord finished creating the heavens and the earth, He sat upon the Throne (الكرسيّ) in this posture to rest. So Allah revealed: اللهُ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ لا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَلا نَوْمٌ لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (Allah—there is no god except Him, the Living, the Self-subsisting. Neither drowsiness overtakes Him nor sleep. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth.) He owns them both and owns their administration (تدبيرهما). This is an affirmation of His Self-subsistence (قيموميّته) and an argument for His uniqueness in divinity (الألوهية). What is meant by "whatever is in them" (بما فيهما) is whatever exists within them, whether it is intrinsic to their reality (داخلاً في حقيقتهما) or external to them (خارجاً عنهما) but established within them. In al-Kafi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He recited ** لَهُ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth) and what is between them and what is beneath the moist earth (الثّرى), the Knower of the unseen and the visible (عالم الغيب والشهادة), the All-beneficent (الرحمن), the All-merciful (الرحيم). ** مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي (Who is it that) [until the end of] the Ayah. مَنْ ذَا الَّذِي يَشْفَعُ عِنْدَهُ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ** (Who is it that may intercede with Him except by His leave?) This is a declaration of the grandeur of His status (كبرياء شأنه) and that no one is equal to Him or approaches Him (يدانيه) such that he could independently repel what He wills, whether through intercession (شفاعة) or submission (استكانة), let alone obstruct Him through defiance (عناداً) or hostility (مناصبة). ** يَعْلَمُ ما بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ (He knows what is before them) meaning what has been (ما كان). وَما خَلْفَهُمْ (and what is behind them) meaning what has not yet been (وما لم يكن بعد). This is how al-Qummi narrated it from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him). وَلا يُحِيطُونَ بِشَيْءٍ مِنْ عِلْمِهِ (and they do not comprehend anything of His knowledge) meaning of His known things (معلوماته). ** إِلَّا بِما شاءَ (except what He wishes). Al-Qummi said: Meaning, except what He reveals (يوحي) to them. I say: Comprehension (الإحاطة) of a thing through knowledge means knowing it exactly as it is in reality (على الحقيقة). The totality of the two sentences indicates His uniqueness in possessing the perfect intrinsic knowledge (العلم الذاتي التام), which points to His Oneness (وحدانيته). وَسِعَ كُرْسِيُّهُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ (His Seat embraces the heavens and the earth) meaning His knowledge (علمه). This is according to al-Tawhid. And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi: He was asked whether the heavens and the earth encompassed the Seat (الكرسيّ) or the Seat encompassed the heavens and the earth. He replied: "Indeed, everything is within the Seat (الكرسيّ)." And al-Qummi narrated: That Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) was asked about this Ayah. He (peace be upon him) said: "The heavens and the earth and whatever creature is between them are within the hollow (جوف) of the Seat (الكرسيّ), and He has four angels (أملاك) who carry it by the leave of Allah." [This is part of] the Hadith. I say: The Seat (الكرسيّ) may sometimes refer to the physical body (الجسم) beneath the Throne (العرش), below which are the heavens and the earth, because it contains the physical world (العالم الجسماني), as if it were its resting place (مستقرّه), and the Throne (العرش) is above it, as if it were its roof (سقفه). And in the Prophetic Hadith: "The seven heavens and the seven earths, compared to the Seat (الكرسيّ), are nothing but like a ring thrown into a desert (فلاة). And the superiority of the Throne (العرش) over the Seat (الكرسيّ) is like the superiority of that desert over that ring." Al-Ayyashi narrated this from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him). It may also refer to the container (وعاء) of the Throne (العرش). In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Throne (العرش) and the Seat (الكرسيّ), what they are. He replied: "The Throne (العرش), in one interpretation (في وجه), is the totality of creation (جملة الخلق), and the Seat (الكرسيّ) is its container (وعاؤه). In another interpretation, the Throne (العرش) is the knowledge (العلم) upon which Allah informed the Prophets (الأنبياء), His Messengers (رسله), and His Proofs (حججه). And the Seat (الكرسيّ) is the knowledge upon which none of His Prophets, His Messengers, or His Proofs were informed." I say: The totality of creation (جملة الخلق) seems to be an expression for the entirety of the physical world (العالم الجسماني), and its container (وعاؤه) refers to the worlds of al-Malakut (الملكوت) and al-Jabarut (الجبروت), due to its establishment upon them and its subsistence through them. It is sometimes said that the Seat (الكرسيّ) being within the Throne (العرش) does not contradict the Throne (العرش) being within the Seat (الكرسيّ), because one of the two existences (الكونين) is in one manner (بنحو) and the other is in another manner. This is because one of them is a comprehensive intellectual existence (كون عقلي إجمالي) and the other is a detailed psychic existence (كون نفساني تفصيلي). The Seat (الكرسيّ) may also be used metaphorically (كناية) for sovereignty (الملك) because it is the resting place of sovereignty (مستقر الملك). It is also sometimes said that it is a depiction of His Almighty greatness (عظمته تعالى) and an imaginative representation through sensory analogy (تمثيل حسِّي), and that there is no actual seat (كرسي), sitting (قعود), or sitter (قاعد), like His saying, glory be to Him: ** وَالْأَرْضُ جَمِيعاً قَبْضَتُهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَالسَّماواتُ مَطْوِيَّاتٌ بِيَمِينِهِ (The entire earth will be in His grasp on the Day of Resurrection, and the heavens rolled up in His right hand.) This is the approach (مسلك) of the literalists (الظاهريين), while what we mentioned first is the approach of those firmly rooted in knowledge (الراسخين في العلم). وَلا يَؤُدُهُ ** (and He is not wearied) meaning, nor does it burden Him (يثقله). ** حِفْظُهُما ** (by their preservation) meaning His preservation of them both. ** وَهُوَ الْعَلِيُ (and He is the All-exalted) above rivals (الأنداد) and likenesses (الأشباه), and no imagination (وهم) can grasp Him. ** الْعَظِيمُ (the All-great) in comparison to Whom everything besides Him is insignificant (المستحقر), and no understanding (فهم) can encompass Him. In al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, the greatest Ayah in the Quran is Ayat al-Kursi (آية الكرسيّ)." In al-Majma' and al-Jawami', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "I heard your Prophet, upon the steps of the pulpit (أعواد المنبر), saying: 'Whoever recites Ayat al-Kursi (آية الكرسيّ) immediately after every obligatory prayer (صلاة مكتوبة), nothing prevents him from entering Paradise except death. And no one persists in reciting it except a truthful person (صديق) or a worshipper (عابد). And whoever recites it when he takes his resting place (مضجعه), Allah grants him security for himself, his neighbor, his neighbor's neighbor, and the houses around him.'"

(٢٥٦) لا إِكْراهَ فِي الدِّينِ قَدْ تَبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِ (There is no compulsion in [accepting] the religion. The right path has certainly become distinct from the wrong path.) Al-Qummi said: Meaning, no one is compelled regarding his religion except after ** تَبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِ (the right path has certainly become distinct from the wrong path). And it was said: It means that compulsion (الإكراه) is, in reality, obligating another person to perform an action in which he sees no good, and thus forcing him to do it. However, قَدْ تَبَيَّنَ الرُّشْدُ مِنَ الْغَيِ (the right path has certainly become distinct from the wrong path); faith (الإيمان) has been distinguished from disbelief (الكفر) by clear signs (الآيات الواضحة), and the proofs (الدلائل) have indicated that faith (الإيمان) is guidance (رشد) that leads to eternal happiness (السعادة الأبدية), and disbelief (الكفر) is error (غي) that leads to everlasting wretchedness (الشقاوة السرمدية). When this becomes clear to the rational person (العاقل), his soul hastens toward faith (الإيمان) seeking to attain happiness and salvation, and he does not need compulsion (الإكراه) or insistence (الإلحاح). And it was said: It is a statement (إخبار) in the meaning of a prohibition (نهي), meaning: Do not compel [anyone] in the religion. This is either general (عام) and abrogated (منسوخ) by His saying: ** جاهِدِ الْكُفَّارَ وَالْمُنافِقِينَ وَاغْلُظْ عَلَيْهِمْ (Contend with the faithless and the hypocrites, and be severe with them), or it is specific (خاصّ) to the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) if they pay the tribute (الجزية).

I say: If what is meant by the religion (الدين) is Shi'ism (التشيّع), as is understood from the forthcoming narration of Ibn Abi Ya'fur, and the entirety of the verse is interpreted by their wilāyah (بولايتهم) (peace be upon them), then it is a statement (إخبار) in the meaning of a prohibition (نهي) without any need for the assertion of abrogation (النسخ) or specification (التخصيص). فَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِالطَّاغُوتِ (So whoever disbelieves in the Tāghūt). The Tāghūt is Satan (الشيطان), as stated in al-Majma' narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

I say: And it includes everything worshipped besides Allah, whether it be an idol (صنم) or one who obstructs the path of Allah (صاد عن سبيل الله), as is understood from other narrations (أخبار أخر). Thus, al-Tāghūt (الطاغوت) is derived from the pattern fa‘alūt (فعلوت) from al-ṭughyān (الطغيان) (transgression). Al-Qummi said: They are those who usurped the right of the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them).

وَيُؤْمِنْ بِاللهِ (and believes in Allah) through monotheism (التوحيد) and the affirmation of the Messengers (تصديق الرسل), ** فَقَدِ اسْتَمْسَكَ بِالْعُرْوَةِ الْوُثْقى (has certainly grasped the firmest handle). He sought the grasping from his soul by means of the strong rope (الحبل الوثيق). This is a metaphor for the rightful adherent (المتمسك المحق) who holds to sound consideration (النظر الصحيح) and the upright religion (الدين القويم). In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is faith (الإيمان) in Allah alone, Who has no partner. And from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is our love (مودتنا), the People of the House (أهل البيت).

لَا انْفِصامَ لَها (there is no breaking for it). Meaning, there is no cutting off (لا انقطاع لها) for it. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever loves to grasp the firmest handle (العروة الوثقى) for which there is no breaking (لا انفصام لها), let him grasp the wilāyah (بولاية) of my brother and successor, Ali ibn Abi Talib, may the blessings of Allah be upon him. For whoever loves him and takes him as a guardian (تولاه) shall not perish, and whoever hates him and shows enmity toward him (عاداه) shall not be saved."

وَاللهُ سَمِيعٌ (and Allah is all-hearing) of the sayings (بالأقوال), ** عَلِيمٌ (all-knowing) of the intentions (بالنيات).

(257) اللهُ وَلِيُّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (257) Allah is the Guardian of the faithful taking charge of their affairs. يُخْرِجُهُمْ He brings them out through His guidance and granting of success (توفيق) مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ of darkness—the darknesses of ignorance and sins—إِلَى النُّورِ into light—the light of guidance and forgiveness.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them), he said: The believer moves within five aspects of light: his entrance is light, and his exit is light, and his knowledge is light, and his speech is light, and his gaze on the Day of Resurrection is toward the Light.

وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَوْلِياؤُهُمُ الطَّاغُوتُ (1) But as for those who are faithless, their guardians are the Taghut In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Their guardians are the Tawaghit (الطواغيت). Al-Qummi said: They are those who wronged the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them). أَوْلِياؤُهُمُ الطَّاغُوتُ their guardians are the Taghut, and they are those who followed those who usurped their right. يُخْرِجُونَهُمْ مِنَ النُّورِ إِلَى الظُّلُماتِ they drive them out of light into darkness. It was said: [They drive them] from the light of innate disposition (الفطرة) to the corruption of preparedness (الاستعداد).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): light (النُّورِ) refers to the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them), and darkness (الظُّلُماتِ) refers to their enemies. And narrated from Ibn Abi Ya'fur, who said: I said to Abu 'Abdillah (peace be upon him): "I mix with people, and I am greatly astonished by some groups who do not follow you (لا يتولونكم) but follow so-and-so and so-and-so, and they possess trustworthiness (أمانة), truthfulness (صدق), and fidelity (وفاء). And [I am astonished by] other groups who follow you (يتولونكم) but do not possess that trustworthiness, fidelity, or truthfulness." He said: So Abu 'Abdillah (peace be upon him) sat up straight and turned to me as if angry, then said: "There is no religion (دين) for one who worships Allah through the guardianship (ولاية) of an unjust Imam who is not from Allah, and there is no blame (عتب) upon one who worships Allah through the guardianship (ولاية) of a just Imam who is from Allah." I said: "No religion for those, and no blame upon these?" He said: "Yes, no religion for those, and no blame upon these." Then he said: "Do you not hear the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: اللهُ وَلِيُّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا يُخْرِجُهُمْ مِنَ الظُّلُماتِ إِلَى النُّورِ (Allah is the Guardian of the faithful; He brings them out of darkness into light)?" Meaning: from the darknesses of sins to the light of repentance and forgiveness, due to their guardianship (ولاية) of every just Imam who is from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And He said: وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَوْلِياؤُهُمُ الطَّاغُوتُ يُخْرِجُونَهُمْ مِنَ النُّورِ إِلَى الظُّلُماتِ (But as for those who are faithless, their guardians are the Taghut; they drive them out of light into darkness). He only meant by this that they were upon the light of Islam, but when they took as guardian (تولوا) every unjust Imam who is not from Allah, they exited, through their guardianship, from the light of Islam into the darknesses of disbelief (الكفر). Thus, Allah made the Fire obligatory for them along with the disbelievers. And Al-'Ayyashi added after His saying إِلَى الظُّلُماتِ (into darkness): He said: I asked: "Did not Allah mean the disbelievers by this when He said وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا (But as for those who are faithless)?" He replied: "What light does the disbeliever have while he is a disbeliever, that he should be brought out of it into darknesses? He only meant by this..." [to the end of the narration].

أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ They are the inmates of the Fire, and they will remain in it [forever]. Al-'Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), at the end of the previous narration, through another chain: "So the enemies of 'Ali, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), are those who will abide eternally in the Fire, even if they were, in their religions (أديانهم), at the peak of piety (الورع), asceticism (الزهد), and worship (العبادة)." Al-Qummi said: هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ they will remain in it [forever]. And praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.

(1) Its origin is طغيوت (طغيوت); its lām is placed before its ʿayn contrary to the rule (خلاف القياس) [from the author].

(258) أَلَمْ تَرَ (1) ** إِلَى الَّذِي حَاجَّ إِبْراهِيمَ فِي رَبِّهِ (Have you not regarded) (1) (him who argued with Abraham about his Lord) You are astonished by the argumentation of Nimrod and his foolishness, ** أَنْ آتاهُ اللهُ الْمُلْكَ (because Allah had given him the kingship?) Because He gave it to him. It is said: meaning, His granting him the kingship made him insolent (أَبْطَرَهُ) and carried him to argumentation, or He placed the argumentation in the place of gratitude for His granting him the kingship.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Barqi, raised (مَرْفُوعاً), he said: "Four ruled the entire earth: two believers and two disbelievers. As for the two believers, they are Sulayman ibn Dawud and Dhul-Qarnayn. As for the two disbelievers, they are Nimrod and Bukht Nassar."

إِذْ قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ رَبِّيَ الَّذِي يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ (When Abraham said, 'My Lord is He who gives life and brings death,') In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that this was after he was thrown into the fire. قالَ أَنَا أُحْيِي وَأُمِيتُ (he said, 'I give life and bring death.') By pardoning from execution and by execution. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): that Abraham said to him, "Give life to one whom I have killed, if you are truthful." قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ فَإِنَّ اللهَ يَأْتِي بِالشَّمْسِ مِنَ الْمَشْرِقِ فَأْتِ بِها مِنَ الْمَغْرِبِ (Abraham said, 'Indeed Allah brings the sun from the east; now you bring it from the west.') Abraham turned away from objecting to his corrupt counter-argument toward arguing with that which he could not manage, such as this deception (التمويه), in order to stop the contention (المشاغبة). In reality, this was a shift from an obscure example to a manifest example of His capabilities, which none besides Him is able to produce, not a shift from one proof to another. ** فَبُهِتَ الَّذِي كَفَرَ (Thereupon the one who was faithless was dumbfounded.) So he became dumbfounded (مَبْهُوتاً). And according to the reading of the active voice (قراءة المعلوم), al-Qummiyy said: "So he was overcome (فَغَلَبَهُ)," meaning he was cut off (silenced). That is because he knew that the sun was older than him. وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي (And Allah does not guide) by the clear proof (مَحَجَّة) of the argumentation, nor the path of salvation, nor the way to Paradise, ** الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ (the wrongdoing lot.) those who wronged themselves by refusing to accept guidance.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Abraham opposed his people and criticized their deities until he was brought before Nimrod, and he argued with them.

(1) Know that both phrases, أَلَمْ تَرَ (Have you not regarded) and أَرَأَيْتَ (Have you seen), are used for the purpose of expressing astonishment (التعجب). However, the first relates to the object of astonishment, so one says: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِي صَنَعَ كَذَا (Have you not regarded him who did such-and-such), meaning: Look at him and be astonished by his state. The second relates to the like of the object of astonishment, so one says: أَرَأَيْتَ مِثْلَ الَّذِي صَنَعَ كَذَا (Have you seen the like of him who did such-and-such), meaning: He is so strange that no like of him is seen, or: Look at the like and be astonished by what he did. Based on this, the conjunction of كَالَّذِي مَرَّ (like him who passed by) upon الَّذِي حَاجَّ (him who argued) is not sound, and it requires interpretation (التأويل). So, it was said that its estimation (تَقْدِيرُهُ) is: أَرَأَيْتَ مِثْلَ الَّذِي (Have you seen the like of him who), which was omitted because أَلَمْ تَرَ indicates it, and its specification with the particle of similitude (حَرْفِ التَّشْبِيهِ) is because those who deny resurrection (الْإِحْيَاء) are numerous, and those ignorant of its manner are too many to count, unlike the claimant of Lordship (الرُّبُوبِيَّة). It was also said that the kāf (الْكَاف) is extra (مَزِيدَة), and the estimation of the speech is: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِي حَاجَّ أَوْ الَّذِي مَرَّ (Have you not regarded him who argued or him who passed by). And it was said that it is a conjunction based on the meaning, as if it were said: أَلَمْ تَرَ كَالَّذِي حَاجَّ أَوْ كَالَّذِي مَرَّ (Have you not regarded like him who argued or like him who passed by). "From him (مِنْهُ)."

(259) أَوْ كَالَّذِي مَرَّ عَلى قَرْيَةٍ (Or like him who passed by a town) He is the Prophet أرميا (Jeremiah), and it is said, the Prophet عزير (Ezra). Narrations concerning this will follow. وَهِيَ خاوِيَةٌ عَلى عُرُوشِها (while it lay desolate on its roofs) —meaning, its walls had fallen upon its ceilings. قالَ أَنَّى يُحْيِي (He said, 'How will Allah revive') —meaning, how will He revive, or when will He revive—هذِهِ اللهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها (this after its death?') —this was an admission of inability to know the method of revival and an expression of awe at the power of the Reviver. He desired to witness the revival of the dead so that his insight might increase. فَأَماتَهُ اللهُ مِائَةَ عامٍ (So Allah made him die for a hundred years). ثُمَّ بَعَثَهُ (Then He raised him up)—meaning, He revived him. قالَ كَمْ لَبِثْتَ قالَ لَبِثْتُ يَوْماً أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ قالَ بَلْ لَبِثْتَ مِائَةَ عامٍ فَانْظُرْ إِلى طَعامِكَ وَشَرابِكَ لَمْ يَتَسَنَّهْ (He said, 'How long have you stayed?' He said, 'I have stayed a day or part of a day.' He said, 'No, you have stayed a hundred years. Now look at your food and drink; they have not spoiled') لَمْ يَتَسَنَّهْ means it did not change despite the passing of years. It was read (وقرئ) by omitting the هاء (الهاء) when connecting (في الوصل). وَانْظُرْ إِلى حِمارِكَ (And look at your ass) —meaning, how its bones scattered, decayed, and crumbled. وَلِنَجْعَلَكَ آيَةً لِلنَّاسِ (and that We may make you a sign for mankind) —meaning, We did that so We might make you a sign. وَانْظُرْ إِلَى الْعِظامِ (And look at the bones)—meaning, your bones—كَيْفَ نُنْشِزُها (how We raise them up)—meaning, how We raise some upon others for assembly. It was read (وقرئ) ننشرها with the راء (الراء) from انشر الله الموتى (Allah revived the dead) when He brought them back to life, and ننشرها with الفتح (the fathah) and the راء (الراء) from نشر (to spread) meaning انشر (to revive). ثُمَّ نَكْسُوها لَحْماً (then clothe them with flesh)—from here and there, as will follow. فَلَمَّا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُ (When it became clear to him) —what became clear—قالَ أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (he said, 'I know that Allah has power over all things'). It was read (وقرئ) اعلم (I know) as an imperative (على الأمر).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Children of Israel committed sins and rebelled against the command of their Lord, Allah intended to empower over them one who would humiliate and kill them. So Allah revealed to أرميا (Jeremiah): "O أرميا! What is the land I chose from among the lands and planted in it noble trees, but it failed and grew خروباً (carob)?" (1) أرميا informed the Children of Israel of this news. They said to him: "Consult your Lord so He may inform us of the meaning of this parable." So أرميا fasted for seven days. Then Allah revealed to him: "O أرميا! As for the land, it is بيت المقدس (Jerusalem). As for what was planted in it, they are the Children of Israel whom I settled therein, but they committed sins, changed My religion, and exchanged My blessing for disbelief (كفراً). By Me, I swear I shall test them with a trial in which the wise man will remain bewildered, and I shall empower over them the worst of My servants in birth, the worst of them in food. He shall be empowered over them with tyranny (بالجبرية), killing their fighters, taking their women captive, destroying their houses which they boast of, and casting their stones, which they pride themselves on before the people, into the garbage dumps for a hundred years." أرميا informed the Children of Israel of this news. They said to him: "Consult your Lord and ask Him: What is the sin of the poor, the needy, and the weak?" So أرميا fasted, then ate a meal, and nothing was revealed to him. Then he fasted seven days, and nothing was revealed to him. Then he fasted seven days, and Allah revealed to him: "O أرميا! You must cease this, or I shall turn your face to the back of your neck." He said: Then Allah revealed to Him: "Tell them: Because you saw the evil (المنكر) and did not forbid it." أرميا said: "My Lord, inform me who he is, so that I may go to him and take security (أماناً) for myself and my household from him." He said: "Go to such-and-such a place, and descend to a youth who is the most wretched of them in time (زماناً), the most wicked of them in birth, the weakest of them in body, and the worst of them in sustenance. He is the one." So أرميا came to that land, and behold, there was a youth in an inn (خان), crippled (زمن), thrown upon a garbage dump in the middle of the inn. And behold, he had a mother who was nursing (تربّى), and she would crumble the bread (الكسرة) in a bowl (القصعة) and milk her sow (خنزيرة) onto it, then bring it near that youth, and he would eat. أرميا said: "If there is anyone in the world whom Allah described, it is this one." So he approached him and asked him: "What is your name?" He replied: "بخت نصّر (Nebuchadnezzar)." So he knew that he was the one. He treated him until he recovered. Then he said to him: "Do you know me?" He said: "No, you are a righteous man." He said: "I am أرميا, the Prophet of the Children of Israel. Allah informed me that He will empower you over the Children of Israel, so you will kill their men and do to them what you will do." The youth became arrogant (فتاه) in his soul at that moment. Then أرميا said: "Write me a letter of security (كتاباً بأمان) from you." So he wrote him a letter. He (بخت نصّر) used to go out at night to the mountain, gather firewood, bring it into the city, and sell it. He called for war against the Children of Israel, whose dwelling was in بيت المقدس (Jerusalem). بخت نصّر advanced with those who answered his call toward بيت المقدس, and a great multitude of people gathered around him. When أرميا learned of his approach toward بيت المقدس, he met him riding his ass, carrying the security letter that بخت نصّر had written for him. أرميا could not reach him due to the multitude of his soldiers and companions, so he placed the security letter on a piece of wood and raised it. بخت نصّر asked: "Who are you?" He replied: "I am أرميا, the Prophet who gave you the good news that you would be empowered over the Children of Israel, and this is your security for me." He said: "As for you, I have granted you security. But as for your household, I shall shoot an arrow from here toward بيت المقدس. If my shot reaches بيت المقدس, they have no security with me. If it does not reach, they are safe." He drew his bow and shot toward بيت المقدس. The wind carried the arrow (النشابة) until it lodged in بيت المقدس. He said: "They have no security with me." When he arrived, he looked at a mound of earth in the middle of the city, and behold, blood was boiling in its center. Whenever earth was thrown upon it, it came out boiling. He asked: "What is this?" They said: "This was a Prophet of Allah whom the kings of the Children of Israel killed, and his blood boils. Whenever we throw earth upon it, it comes out boiling." بخت نصّر said: "I shall kill the Children of Israel forever until this blood settles." That blood was the blood of يحيى بن زكريا (John son of Zechariah). In his time, there was a tyrannical king who committed adultery with the women of the Children of Israel. He used to pass by يحيى بن زكريا, and يحيى said to him: "Fear Allah, O King! This is not lawful for you." Then a woman, one of those with whom the king committed adultery, said to him when he was drunk: "O King, kill يحيى!" So he ordered his head to be brought. The head of يحيى was brought in a basin (الطست), and the head spoke to him, saying: "O man, fear Allah! This is not lawful for you." Then the blood boiled in the basin until it overflowed onto the ground, and it came out boiling and would not settle. There were a hundred years between the killing of يحيى and the emergence of بخت نصّر. بخت نصّر continued killing them. He would enter village after village, killing men, women, children, and every animal, while the blood boiled, until he annihilated those who remained of them. Then he asked: "Is anyone left in this land?" They said: "An old woman in such-and-such a place." So he sent for her and struck her neck over that blood, and it settled. She was the last one remaining. Then he came to بابل (Babylon), built a city there, settled, and dug a well. He threw دانيال (Daniel) into it and threw a lioness with him. The lioness ate the clay of the well, and دانيال drank her milk. He remained like that for a time. Then Allah revealed to the Prophet who was in بيت المقدس: "Go with this food and drink to دانيال and convey My greetings to him." He asked: "And where is He, O Lord?" He said: "In a well in بابل, in such-and-such a place." So he came to him, looked into the well, and said: "O دانيال!" He replied: "At your service (لبيك), a strange voice!" He said: "Your Lord conveys greetings to you and has sent food and drink to you." So he lowered it to him. دانيال said: "Praise be to Allah, Who does not forget him who remembers Him. Praise be to Allah, Who does not disappoint him who calls upon Him. Praise be to Allah, Who suffices him who relies upon Him. Praise be to Allah, Who does not entrust him who trusts Him to anyone else. Praise be to Allah, Who rewards good with good. Praise be to Allah, Who rewards patience with salvation. Praise be to Allah, Who removes our harm during our distress. Praise be to Allah, Who is our trust when the rope is cut off from us. Praise be to Allah, Who is our hope when our opinion of our deeds is poor." He said: Then بخت نصّر saw in his sleep that his head was made of iron, his legs of copper, and his chest of gold. He summoned the astrologers (المنجّمين) and asked them what he had seen. They said: "We do not know, but relate to us what you saw in the dream." He said: "I have been providing you with sustenance for such-and-such a time, yet you do not know what I saw in the dream!" So he ordered them to be killed. Then some of those who were with him said: "If anyone knows anything, it is the man in the pit (صاحب الجبّ), for the lioness did not harm him; she eats the clay and nurses him." So he sent for دانيال and asked: "What did I see in the dream?" He replied: "You saw that your head was made of such-and-such, your legs of such-and-such, and your chest of such-and-such." He said: "That is exactly what I saw. What does that mean?" He said: "Your kingdom has gone, and you will be killed within three days by a man from the progeny of فارس (Persia)." He said to him: "I have seven cities, and at the gate of every city there is a guard. I was not satisfied with that until I placed a copper duck (بطة) at the gate of every city, which cries out if a stranger enters, so that he may be seized." He said to him: "The matter is as I told you." He then dispatched the cavalry and commanded: "Do not meet anyone of creation except that you kill him, whoever he may be." دانيال was sitting with him, and he said: "Do not leave me these three days, and if they pass, I shall kill you." When it was evening on the third day, grief overcame him, so he went out. He was met by a youth whom he had adopted as his son, who was from the people of فارس, though he did not know he was from the people of فارس. He handed him his sword and said: "O youth, do not meet anyone of creation except that you kill him, and if you meet me, kill me." The youth took his sword and struck بخت نصّر a blow, killing him. أرميا went out on his ass, carrying figs (تين) he had provisioned and some juice (عصير). He looked at the beasts of the land, the beasts of the sea, and the beasts of the air eating those corpses. He pondered for an hour, then said: "How will Allah revive these when the beasts have eaten them?" So Allah caused him to die right there. This is the saying of Allah the Almighty: أَوْ كَالَّذِي مَرَّ عَلى قَرْيَةٍ وَهِيَ خاوِيَةٌ عَلى عُرُوشِها قالَ أَنَّى يُحْيِي هذِهِ اللهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها فَأَماتَهُ اللهُ مِائَةَ عامٍ ثُمَّ بَعَثَهُ (Or like him who passed by a town while it lay desolate on its roofs. He said, 'How will Allah revive this after its death?' So Allah made him die for a hundred years. Then He raised him up)—meaning, He revived him. When Allah had mercy on the Children of Israel and destroyed بخت نصّر, He returned the Children of Israel to the world. As for عزير, when Allah empowered بخت نصّر over the Children of Israel, he fled and entered a spring (عين) and disappeared therein. أرميا remained dead for a hundred years, then Allah revived him. The first thing Allah revived of him were his eyes, which were like the shells of eggs (غرقي البيض). He looked, and Allah revealed to him: كَمْ لَبِثْتَ قالَ لَبِثْتُ يَوْماً (How long have you stayed?' He said, 'I have stayed a day'). Then he looked at the sun, which had risen high, and said: أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ (or part of a day'). Then Allah the Almighty said: بَلْ لَبِثْتَ مِائَةَ عامٍ فَانْظُرْ إِلى طَعامِكَ وَشَرابِكَ لَمْ يَتَسَنَّهْ (No, you have stayed a hundred years. Now look at your food and drink; they have not spoiled)—meaning, they had not changed—وَانْظُرْ إِلى حِمارِكَ وَلِنَجْعَلَكَ آيَةً لِلنَّاسِ وَانْظُرْ إِلَى الْعِظامِ كَيْفَ نُنْشِزُها ثُمَّ نَكْسُوها لَحْماً (And look at your ass, and that We may make you a sign for mankind. And look at the bones, how We raise them up, then clothe them with flesh). So he began to look at the decayed, scattered bones gathering together, and the flesh that the beasts had eaten assembling onto the bones from here and there and adhering to them until he stood up, and his ass stood up. Then he said: أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (I know that Allah has power over all things).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) what is close to the beginning and end of this narration concerning the story of أرميا, but he did not mention the blood of يحيى nor the pit of دانيال. Rather, he summarized the story of بخت نصّر, saying: So Allah empowered بخت نصّر over them, and he did to them what has reached you. Then he sent for the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) and said: "You have been informed by your Lord and have told them what I shall do to them. If you wish, stay with me among those you choose, and if you wish, leave." He said: "No, rather I shall leave." So he provisioned himself with juice (عصيراً) and figs (تيناً) and departed. When he was out of sight, as far as the eye could see, he turned to it and said: أَنَّى يُحْيِي هذِهِ اللهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها (How will Allah revive this after its death?). So Allah caused him to die for a hundred years. He caused him to die in the morning and revived him in the evening before the sun set. The first thing He created of him were his eyes, which were like the shells of eggs (غرقي البيض). Then it was said to him: كَمْ لَبِثْتَ قالَ لَبِثْتُ يَوْماً (How long have you stayed?' He said, 'I have stayed a day'). When he looked at the sun, which had not set, he said: أَوْ بَعْضَ يَوْمٍ قالَ بَلْ لَبِثْتَ مِائَةَ عامٍ فَانْظُرْ إِلى طَعامِكَ وَشَرابِكَ لَمْ يَتَسَنَّهْ (or part of a day.' He said, 'No, you have stayed a hundred years. Now look at your food and drink; they have not spoiled'). وَانْظُرْ إِلى حِمارِكَ وَلِنَجْعَلَكَ آيَةً لِلنَّاسِ وَانْظُرْ إِلَى الْعِظامِ كَيْفَ نُنْشِزُها ثُمَّ نَكْسُوها لَحْماً (And look at your ass, and that We may make you a sign for mankind. And look at the bones, how We raise them up, then clothe them with flesh). He said: So he began to look at his bones, how some joined to others, and he saw how the veins flowed. When he stood upright, قالَ أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (he said, 'I know that Allah has power over all things').

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: And Allah caused the Prophet أرميا (peace be upon him) to die, who looked at the destruction of بيت المقدس and what was around it when بخت نصّر invaded them, so قالَ أَنَّى يُحْيِي هذِهِ اللهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها فَأَماتَهُ اللهُ مِائَةَ عامٍ (He said, 'How will Allah revive this after its death?' So Allah made him die for a hundred years). Then He revived him, and he looked at his limbs, how they rejoined and how they were clothed with flesh, and at his joints and veins, how they were connected. He said: When he sat upright, قالَ أَعْلَمُ أَنَّ اللهَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (he said, 'I know that Allah has power over all things').

In al-Ikmal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: And the confirmation of this from the Book of Allah is that the signs (الآيات) are the proofs (الحجج), the saying of Allah the Mighty and Majestic: وَجَعَلْنَا ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ وَأُمَّهُ آيَةً (and We made the son of Mary and his mother a sign)—meaning, a proof (حجّة). And His saying, the Mighty and Majestic, to أرميا when Allah revived him after He had caused him to die: وَانْظُرْ إِلى حِمارِكَ وَلِنَجْعَلَكَ آيَةً لِلنَّاسِ (And look at your ass, and that We may make you a sign for mankind)—meaning, a proof (حجّة).

And narrated from the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) in a حديث (narration) in which he mentioned بخت نصّر and his killing of those Jews he killed over the blood of يحيى بن زكريا in the forty-seventh year of his reign, he said: Then Allah the Almighty sent العزير (Ezra) as a Prophet to the people of the villages whose inhabitants Allah the Mighty and Majestic had caused to die and then revived them for him. They were from various villages, and they had fled in fear of death, but they were killed near عزير. They were believers, and he used to visit them and hear their speech and their faith, and he loved them for that and befriended them upon it. He was absent from them for one day, then he came to them and found them lying dead, all of them. He grieved for them and قالَ أَنَّى يُحْيِي هذِهِ اللهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها (He said, 'How will Allah revive this after its death?')—expressing astonishment that this had befallen them, as they had all died on the same day. So Allah the Mighty and Majestic caused him to die at that moment for a hundred years. He remained among them for a hundred years, then He revived them. They were one hundred thousand fighters. Then Allah killed them all; none escaped at the hands of بخت نصّر.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a حديث in which he mentioned بخت نصّر's domination over the Children of Israel, his killing of them, and his taking their offspring captive, and his selection of دانيال and عزير from the captives while they were young. دانيال was a prisoner in his hand for ninety years. Then he mentioned his throwing him into the pit (الجبّ), then his removal from it after a time, in a manner different from the narration of al-Qummi. Then he said: And he entrusted the supervision of his kingdom's affairs and judging among the people to him (دانيال). He (بخت نصّر) did not remain long before he died, and the authority passed after him to عزير. They used to gather around him, feel comfortable with him, and take the landmarks of their religion from him. Then Allah concealed his person from them for a hundred years, then He revived him.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): عزير left his family while his wife was pregnant and he was fifty years old. Allah caused him to die for a hundred years, then revived him. He returned to his family at fifty, and he had a son who was a hundred years old. Thus, his son was older than him. That is one of the signs of Allah.

Al-Ayyashi: Ibn al-Kawwa said to Ali (peace be upon him): "O Commander of the Faithful, who is a son older than his father among the people of the world?" He said: "Yes, those are the children of عزير, when he passed by a ruined village. He had come from his farm, riding his ass, and with him was a basket containing figs (تين) and a jug containing juice (عصير). He passed by a ruined village and قالَ أَنَّى يُحْيِي هذِهِ اللهُ بَعْدَ مَوْتِها فَأَماتَهُ اللهُ مِائَةَ عامٍ (He said, 'How will Allah revive this after its death?' So Allah made him die for a hundred years). His children were born and multiplied. Then Allah sent him back to them, reviving him in the place where He had caused him to die. Thus, his children were older than their father."

It is narrated: That he came to his people riding his ass and said: "I am عزير." But they disbelieved him. So he recited the التوراة (Torah) from memory, and no one before him had memorized it. They recognized him by that, and they said: "He is the son of God." It is said that when he returned to his dwelling, he was a youth, and his children were old men. When he related a حديث to them, they would say: "A حديث of a hundred years."

I say: It is possible to reconcile these narrations by saying that this event occurred twice: once for أرميا in his astonishment at the revival of those killed by بخت نصّر, and another time for عزير in his astonishment at the revival of his companions who died on a single day. However, the event was expressed for أرميا as death, and for عزير sometimes as absence (الغيبة) and sometimes as death. The only contradiction is between the narration of al-Qummi regarding the story of دانيال and the narration of al-Ikmal concerning it, and between the two narrations of al-Ikmal, where in one it is said that the killing by بخت نصّر was over the blood of يحيى بن زكريا, which agrees with al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi, and in the other it is said that the birth of يحيى occurred years after those events. The knowledge is with Allah.

(1) خروب (kharūb) is said [to be pronounced] like تنور (tannūr), [meaning] a wild, thorny tree. "From him [the author/source]."

(٢٦٠) وَإِذْ قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ رَبِّ أَرِنِي كَيْفَ تُحْيِ الْمَوْتى (260) And when Abraham said, 'My Lord, show me how You revive the dead.' He only asked that so his knowledge might become direct observation (عياناً). He said, 'Do you not believe?' meaning: [Do you not believe] that I am capable of reviving [the dead] by restoring the composition (التركيب) and life? He said that to him, though He knew that he (Abraham) was the most deeply rooted of people in faith and the firmest of them, so that he might answer as he did, and thus the listeners would know his purpose. ** قالَ بَلى وَلكِنْ He said, 'Yes, but لِيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِي so that my heart may be at rest.' Meaning: Yes, I have believed, but I asked so that I might increase in insight and the tranquility of my heart by joining direct observation (العيان) to revelation (الوحي) and clear explanation (البيان). In al-Mahasin and al-Ayyashi, it is narrated: Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) was asked: Was there doubt in his heart? He said: "No, he was certain (على يقين), but he desired an increase in his certainty from Allah." قالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةً مِنَ الطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَ He said, 'Take four birds, and tame them meaning: tame them (فاملهن). It was also read with a kasra on the ṣād (فَصِرْهُنَّ), meaning: gather them (واضممهنَّ). ** إِلَيْكَ to yourself.' So that you may contemplate them and know their nature, lest they become confused for you after the revival. ** ثُمَّ اجْعَلْ عَلى كُلِّ جَبَلٍ مِنْهُنَّ جُزْءاً Then put a part of them on every mountain. Cut them up, mix them, and scatter the parts upon the mountains. It was also read جُزْءاً (with tashdid and hamza). ** ثُمَّ ادْعُهُنَ Then call them; Say to them: "Come, by the permission of Allah." ** يَأْتِينَكَ سَعْياً they will come to you swiftly. running quickly (ساعيات مسرعات). ** وَاعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ And know that Allah is All-mighty, He is not incapable of what He wills, حَكِيمٌ All-wise, possessing profound wisdom (حكمة بالغة) in everything He does and manages.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Abraham (peace be upon him) saw the dominion (ملكوت) of the heavens and the earth, he turned and saw a carcass on the seashore, half of it in the water and half on the land. The beasts of the sea would come and eat what was in the water, then they would return, and some would attack others, eating one another. And the beasts of the land would come and eat from it, then some would attack others, eating one another. It was then that Abraham was astonished by what he saw and said: ** رَبِّ أَرِنِي كَيْفَ تُحْيِ الْمَوْتى 'My Lord, show me how You revive the dead.' He (Abraham) said: How do You bring forth what has been reproduced from that which ate one another? He (Allah) said: (أَوَلَمْ تُؤْمِنْ) (Do you not believe?) He said: بَلى وَلكِنْ لِيَطْمَئِنَّ قَلْبِي 'Yes, but so that my heart may be at rest,' meaning: until I see this, just as I have seen all other things. ** قالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةً مِنَ الطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَّ إِلَيْكَ ثُمَّ اجْعَلْ عَلى كُلِّ جَبَلٍ مِنْهُنَّ جُزْءاً He said, 'Take four birds, and tame them to yourself. Then put a part of them on every mountain.' So he cut them up and mixed them, just as that carcass had been mixed within those beasts that ate one another. So he mixed them, ثُمَّ اجْعَلْ عَلى كُلِّ جَبَلٍ مِنْهُنَّ جُزْءاً ثُمَّ ادْعُهُنَّ يَأْتِينَكَ سَعْياً 'Then put a part of them on every mountain. Then call them; they will come to you swiftly.' When he called them, they answered him. And the mountains were ten.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Allah Almighty revealed to Abraham: "I am taking a friend (خليلاً) from My servants; if he asks Me to revive the dead, I shall answer him." It occurred to Abraham that he was that friend (الخليل), so he said: "My Lord, show me how You revive the dead." He said: "Do you not believe?" He said: "Yes, but so that my heart may be at rest concerning the friendship (الخُلَّة)." قالَ فَخُذْ أَرْبَعَةً مِنَ الطَّيْرِ فَصُرْهُنَّ إِلَيْكَ ثُمَّ اجْعَلْ عَلى كُلِّ جَبَلٍ مِنْهُنَّ جُزْءاً ثُمَّ ادْعُهُنَّ يَأْتِينَكَ سَعْياً وَاعْلَمْ أَنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ He said, 'Take four birds, and tame them to yourself. Then put a part of them on every mountain. Then call them; they will come to you swiftly. And know that Allah is All-mighty, All-wise.' So Abraham took an eagle (نسراً), a duck (بطّاً), a peacock (طاووساً), and a rooster (ديكاً). He cut them up and mixed them, then he placed a part of them on every mountain among the mountains surrounding him—and they were ten. He placed their beaks between his fingers, then called them by their names. He placed grain (حباً) and water near him. Those parts flew towards one another until the bodies were complete. Every body came until it joined its neck and head. Abraham released their beaks, and they flew, then landed, drank from that water, and picked up some of that grain. They said: "O Prophet of Allah, you have revived us, may Allah revive you!" Abraham said: "Rather, Allah revives and causes death, and He is powerful over everything."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a narration: And Abraham called for a mortar (مهراس) and pounded all the birds in it, keeping the heads near him. Then he called out as he had been commanded. He began to look at how the feathers emerged, and at the veins, vein by vein, until its wing came out complete. It flew towards Abraham. Abraham inclined some of the heads and met it with one, but the head he met it with was not for that body, until another head moved towards it and matched the body. Thus, the number was completed, and the bodies were completed.

In al-Khisal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He took the hoopoe (الهدهد), the shrike (الصرد), the peacock (الطاووس), and the crow (الغراب). He slaughtered them and separated their heads. Then he ground their bodies in the mortar (المنخار)—their feathers, flesh, and bones—until they were mixed. Then he divided them into ten parts upon ten mountains. Then he placed grain (حباً) and water near him, and placed their beaks between his fingers. Then he said: "Come swiftly by the permission of Allah." The flesh, feathers, and bones flew towards one another until the bodies were complete as they had been. Every body came until it attached itself to its neck, which held its head and beak. Abraham released their beaks, and they landed, drank from that water, and picked up some of that grain. Then they said: "O Prophet of Allah, you have revived us, may Allah revive you!" Abraham (peace be upon him) said: "Rather, Allah revives and causes death." He said: This is its interpretation (تفسيره) in the apparent sense (في الظاهر). And its interpretation in the esoteric sense (في الباطن) is: Take four of those who are capable of speech (ممن يحتمل الكلام), entrust your knowledge to them, then send them to the ends of the earth as proofs (حججاً) against the people. And if you wish them to come to you, you call them by the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر), and they will come to you swiftly by the permission of Allah Almighty.

In al-Ilal and al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): The birds were the rooster (الدّيك), the pigeon (الحمامة), the peacock (الطاووس), and the crow (الغراب).

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The like of it.

And in one narration: the crow (الغراب) was replaced by the hoopoe (الهدهد), and in another, by the goose (الوزة), and the pigeon (الحمامة) by the ostrich (النعامة). And in this story, there is an indication that the revival of the soul (النفس) with eternal life is only achieved by causing the death (اماتة) of the bodily powers that incite the love of desires (الشهوات), adornments (الزخارف), greed (الحرص), long hope (طول الأمل), baseness of the soul (خسّة النفس), and rushing towards caprice (الهوى)—which are the characteristics attributed to the aforementioned birds—and mixing some of them with others until their intensity (سورتها) is broken, so they become obedient and swift whenever they are called by the caller of intellect (العقل) and Divine Law (الشرع). And the birds were specified because they are closer to man and encompass more of the characteristics of animals.

footnote (١) What supports this is what is found in the *Tafsir*, narrated also in *al-Ikmal* in another place, from the Prophet (prayers and peace of Allah be upon him and his family) in a long narration: after mentioning that Allah entrusted His knowledge and wisdom after Jesus to Simon son of Hamun al-Safa (*شمعون بن حمون الصفا*), and after him to John son of Zechariah (*يحيى بن زكريا*), and after him to the son of Simon, and after him to the progeny of Jacob son of Simon, he said: And at that time, Nebuchadnezzar (*بخت نصر*) reigned for one hundred and eighty-seven years, and he killed seventy thousand fighters from the Jews over the blood of John son of Zechariah (*يحيى بن زكريا*), and he destroyed the Holy House (*بيت المقدس*), and the Jews scattered throughout the lands. [End of quote from the narration].

(٢٦١) مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوالَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ كَمَثَلِ (261) The parable of those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah is like [the parable of] a sower of a seed (حَبَّةٍ) which grew seven ears (سَنابِلَ) due to its stalk branching into seven shoots (شعب), with an ear (سنبلة) in each one. فِي كُلِّ سُنْبُلَةٍ مِائَةُ حَبَّةٍ وَاللهُ يُضاعِفُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ in every ear a hundred grains. And Allah multiplies for whomever He wishes, by His grace, according to the condition of the spender (المنفق)—from his sincerity (إخلاصه) and effort (تعبه), and the condition of the recipient (المصرف), and other factors.

al-Qummi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: [This is] for the one who spends his wealth seeking the pleasure of Allah.

And in Thawab al-A'mal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him عليه السلام: When the believing servant perfects his deed, Allah multiplies his deed for him by seven hundred times for every good deed (حسنة). And that is the saying of Allah, the Almighty: وَاللهُ يُضاعِفُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ And Allah multiplies for whomever He wishes, And in another narration by al-Ayyashi, He added at the end of it: "So perfect your deeds which you perform for the reward of Allah." It was asked: "And what is perfection (الإحسان)?" He said: "When you pray, perfect your bowing (ركوعك) and prostration (سجودك). And when you fast, beware of that which corrupts your fast (صومك). And when you perform Hajj, beware of everything that is forbidden to you in your Hajj (حجتك) and Umrah (عمرتك)." He said: "And every deed you perform, let it be pure (نقياً) from impurity (الدنس)." وَاللهُ واسِعٌ And Allah is expansive, it is not difficult for Him what He bestows of increase [in reward] by His grace. عَلِيمٌ All-knowing, of the intention (نيّة) of the spender (المنفق) and the measure of his spending (إنفاقه).

(262) **الَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوالَهُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ثُمَّ لا يُتْبِعُونَ ما أَنْفَقُوا مَنًّا وَلا أَذىً لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ ** (Those who spend their wealth in the way of Allah, and then do not follow up what they have spent with reproach or annoyance, they shall have their reward near their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve.)

Al-Mann (المنّ) is that one counts his favor upon the one to whom he has shown favor, and al-Adha (الأذى) is that one behaves arrogantly toward him because of what he has bestowed upon him. And thumma (ثمّ) [then] is for indicating the difference (التفاوت) between the spending (الإنفاق) and the abandonment of al-Mann (المنّ) and al-Adha (الأذى).

It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in several reports (أخبار): that Allah disliked several characteristics (خصال), and He counted among them al-Mann (المنّ) after charity (الصدقة).

In al-Majma' (المجمع) and al-Qummi (القمّيّ), narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Whoever confers a favor (معروفاً) upon a believer, then annoys him (أذاه) with words (بالكلام) or counts the favor upon him (منّ عليه), Allah has certainly nullified his charity (صدقته)."

(٢٦٣) قَوْلٌ مَعْرُوفٌ (263) A good word (A kind word) جميل وَمَغْفِرَةٌ وتجاوز عن السائل الحاجة أو نيل مغفرة من الله بسبب الردّ الجميل and forgiveness and overlooking the petitioner of the need, or attaining forgiveness from Allah due to the kind response. ** خَيْرٌ مِنْ صَدَقَةٍ يَتْبَعُها أَذىً is better than a charity followed by injury. ** وَاللهُ غَنِيٌ لا حاجة له إلى المنفق يمنّ ويؤذي And Allah is all-sufficient, He has no need for the spender who reminds of the favor (يَمُنّ) and causes injury (يُؤْذِي). ** حَلِيمٌ عن المعاجَلَة بالعقوبة. all-forbearing,** regarding hastening the punishment.

(٢٦٤) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لا تُبْطِلُوا صَدَقاتِكُمْ بِالْمَنِّ وَالْأَذى O you who have faith! Do not render your charities void by reproaches and affronts. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): It was revealed concerning Uthman and applied to Mu'awiyah and their followers. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: ** بِالْمَنِّ وَالْأَذى (by reproaches and affronts) towards Muhammad and the Household of Muhammad (آل محمّد). He said: This is its interpretation (تأويله). كَالَّذِي يُنْفِقُ مالَهُ رِئاءَ النَّاسِ وَلا يُؤْمِنُ بِاللهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ like him who spends his wealth to be seen by people and does not have faith in Allah and the Last Day. He does not intend thereby the pleasure of Allah nor the reward of the Hereafter. ** فَمَثَلُهُ (So his parable) in his spending ** كَمَثَلِ صَفْوانٍ (is that of a smooth rock) — a smooth stone (حجر أملس) — ** عَلَيْهِ تُرابٌ فَأَصابَهُ وابِلٌ (on which there is some soil, then a downpour hits it) — rain of great drops (مطر عظيم القطر) — ** فَتَرَكَهُ صَلْداً (and leaves it bare) — smooth, clean of soil (أملس نقياً من التراب). ** لا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلى شَيْءٍ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا (They have no power over anything of what they have earned) — they do not benefit from what they did, nor do they find its reward (ثوابه). ** وَاللهُ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الْكافِرِينَ (And Allah does not guide the faithless lot) — to goodness (الخير) and guidance (الرشاد). And in this is an allusion that hypocrisy (الرياء), reproach (المنّ), and affront (الأذى) regarding spending are characteristics (صفة) of the faithless (الكفّار), and the believer must avoid them.

(٢٦٥) وَمَثَلُ الَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوالَهُمُ ابْتِغاءَ مَرْضاتِ اللهِ وَتَثْبِيتاً مِنْ أَنْفُسِهِمْ (265) The parable of those who spend their wealth seeking the pleasure of Allah and to make their souls firm القمّيّ : عن المنّ والأذى. Al-Qummi (reports): regarding (المنّ) (reproach) and (الأذى) (harm). أقول : يعني يوطّنون أنفسهم على حفظ هذه الطاعة وترك اتباعها ممّا يفسدها من المنّ والأذى والسمعة والرياء والعجب ونحوها بعد إتيانهم بها ابتغاء مرضات الله. I say: It means they settle their souls upon preserving this act of obedience and abandoning the pursuit of that which corrupts it, such as (المنّ) (reproach), (الأذى) (harm), seeking reputation (السمعة), hypocrisy (الرياء), conceit (العجب), and the like, after having performed it seeking the pleasure of Allah. العيّاشيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام : انها نزلت في عليّ عليه السلام. Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrates from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning Ali (peace be upon him). كَمَثَلِ جَنَّةٍ أي مثل نفقتهم في الزكاة كمثل بستان is like a garden (meaning: the parable of their spending in zakat (الزكاة) is like a garden) بِرَبْوَةٍ أي في موضع مرتفع فان شجره يكون أحسن منظراً وأزكى ثمراً وأمنع من أن يفسده السيل بالوابل ونحوه on a hill (meaning: in an elevated place, for its trees are better in appearance, purer in fruit, and more protected from being ruined by the torrent through heavy rain (الوابل) or the like) أَصابَها وابِلٌ فَآتَتْ أُكُلَها ثمرتها وقرئ بالتخفيف ثمرتها which is caught by a downpour, and yields its produce (its fruit (ثمرتها). It was read with takhfif (التخفيف) [meaning] its fruit (ثمرتها)) ضِعْفَيْنِ مثلي ما كانت تثمر بسبب الوابل. twofold. (twice what it used to yield because of the heavy rain (الوابل).) وفي المجمع عن الصادق عليه السلام : معناه يتضاعف ثمرتها كما يتضاعف أجر من أنفق ماله ابتغاء مرضات الله In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Its meaning is that its fruit is multiplied, just as the reward of one who spends his wealth seeking the pleasure of Allah is multiplied. فَإِنْ لَمْ يُصِبْها وابِلٌ فَطَلٌ فمطر صغير القطر يكفيها لكرم منبتها And if it is not caught by a downpour, then a drizzle (meaning: a rain of small drops suffices it due to the nobility of its place of growth) والطل يقال لما يقع بالليل على الشجر والنبات ، قيل إن المعنى ان نفقات هؤلاء زاكية عند الله تعالى لا تضيع بحال وإن كانت تتفاوت باعتبار ما ينضم إليها من الأحوال And al-tall (الطل) is said of what falls upon trees and plants at night. It is said that the meaning is that the expenditures of these people are pure (زاكية) in the sight of Allah, the Exalted, and are not lost under any circumstance, even if they vary according to the circumstances that accompany them. ويجوز أن يكون التمثيل لحالهم عند الله تعالى بالجنة على الربوة ونفقاتهم الكثيرة والقليلة الزائدتين في زلفاهم بالوابل والطل And it is permissible that the similitude for their state in the sight of Allah, the Exalted, is the garden on the hill, and their abundant and scarce expenditures—which increase their proximity (زلفاهم)—are likened to the heavy rain (الوابل) and the drizzle (الطل). وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ تحذير عن رياء وترغيب في الإخلاص. and Allah is watchful of what you do. (This is a warning against hypocrisy (رياء) and an encouragement toward sincerity (الإخلاص).)

(٢٦٦) أَيَوَدُّ أَحَدُكُمْ The hamzah in it is for denial (الإنكار). ** أَنْ تَكُونَ لَهُ جَنَّةٌ مِنْ نَخِيلٍ وَأَعْنابٍ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهَا الْأَنْهارُ لَهُ فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ الثَّمَراتِ (266) Does any of you like that he should have a garden of date-palms and vines, with streams running in it, and he has in it all kinds of fruits, He made the garden (الجنة) to be composed of these (date-palms and vines), despite it containing other trees, due to giving precedence (تغليباً) to them because of their nobility (شرفهما) and the abundance of their benefits (منافعهما). Then He mentioned that it contains all kinds of fruits (الثمرات) to indicate its inclusion of all other types of trees. It is also permissible that what is meant by fruits (الثمرات) is benefits (المنافع). ** وَأَصابَهُ الْكِبَرُ and old age overtakes him, Meaning, old age (كبر السن), for poverty (الفاقة) and destitution (العالة) are harder in old age (الشيخوخة). ** وَلَهُ ذُرِّيَّةٌ ضُعَفاءُ while he has weak offspring, Meaning, young children (صغار) who have no ability (قدرة) to earn a living (الكسب). فَأَصابَها إِعْصارٌ فِيهِ نارٌ فَاحْتَرَقَتْ then a whirlwind, in which there is fire, smites it, and it is burnt down? The whirlwind (الإعصار) is a violent wind (ريح عاصف) that spirals from the earth to the sky, circular like a column (كعمود).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever spends his wealth seeking the pleasure (مرضات) of Allah, then reminds the recipient of the favor (امتن) upon whom he gave charity (تصدّق), is like the one who had a garden (جنة) with abundant fruits (كثيرة الثمار), and he is a weak old man (شيخ ضعيف) who has weak young children (أولاد صغار ضعفاء), then a wind (ريح) or fire (نار) comes and burns all his wealth (ماله كله)." كَذلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ اللهُ لَكُمُ الْآياتِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَفَكَّرُونَ Thus does Allah clarify the signs for you, so that you may reflect. upon them, so that you may take heed (تعتبرون) by them.

(٢٦٧) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنْفِقُوا مِنْ طَيِّباتِ ما كَسَبْتُمْ (267) (O you who have faith! Spend out of the good things you have earned) from its lawful (حلاله) and best parts, وَمِمَّا أَخْرَجْنا لَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ (and out of what We have brought forth for you from the earth), and from the good things (طيبات) We have brought forth, such as grains, fruits, and minerals. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The people had earned evil earnings (مكاسب سوء) during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية). When they embraced Islam, they wanted to take those earnings out of their wealth to give them in charity (يتصدقوا بها). But Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, refused, except that they should spend from the good (طيِّب) of what they had earned." وَلا تَيَمَّمُوا الْخَبِيثَ (and do not aim for the bad) Do not aim for the poor quality (الرديء) of it, whether from the wealth or from the bad (الخبيث), تُنْفِقُونَ (to spend) which you single out for spending (الإنفاق), وَلَسْتُمْ بِآخِذِيهِ (when you yourselves would not take it) while your condition is that you would not take it for your own rights due to its poor quality (لرداءته), إِلَّا أَنْ تُغْمِضُوا فِيهِ (unless you overlook it), meaning, unless you tolerate it (تتسامحوا فيه). Thus, it is permissible for one who closes his eye (أغمض بصره) to some of his right when he lowers it (i.e., accepts less). In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded that the date palms be assessed for zakat (يزكّى), some people would bring varieties of dates that were among the worst quality of dates, offering them as their zakat. These dates were called al-Ja'rud (الجعرود) and al-Mu'afarah (المعافارة), having little flesh (اللحا) (with a kasrah on the lām) and large pits (عظيمة النوى). Some of them would bring these instead of good dates. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'Do not estimate (تخرصوا) these two types of dates, and do not bring anything from them.' And concerning this, the verse وَلا تَيَمَّمُوا الْخَبِيثَ (and do not aim for the bad) was revealed. He said: And al-Ighmād (الإغماض) (overlooking) is that you take these two types of dates." And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "The people of Medina used to bring Sadaqat al-Fitr (صدقة الفطر) to the mosque of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. In it were two clusters (عذق), one called al-Ja'rud (الجعرود) and one called al-Mu'afarah (المعافارة). Both had large pits (عظيم نواهما) and thin flesh (رقيق لحاهما), and there was bitterness (مرارة) in their taste. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to the assessor (الخارص): 'Do not assess (تخرص) these two varieties for them, perhaps they will be ashamed and will not bring them.' Then Allah revealed يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَنْفِقُوا (O you who have faith! Spend) up to His saying تُنْفِقُونَ (to spend)." And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "It was revealed concerning a people who used to bring al-hashaf (الحشف) and mix it into the dates of charity (تمر الصدقة)." I say: Al-Hashaf (الحشف) is poor quality dates (رديّ التمر). And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He said: "Indeed, Allah accepts charities (الصدقات) and accepts nothing from them except the good (الطيّب)." وَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّ اللهَ غَنِيٌ حَمِيدٌ (267) (and know that Allah is Self-sufficient, All-laudable.) He is Self-sufficient (غَنِيٌ) from your spending (إنفاقكم); He only commands you to do it for your own benefit. He is All-laudable (حَمِيدٌ) for accepting it and confirming it.

(٢٦٨) الشَّيْطانُ يَعِدُكُمُ الْفَقْرَ (268) Satan promises you poverty regarding spending (الإنفاق) in the ways of righteousness (وجوه البر) and regarding spending the good (الجيّد) part of the wealth. And the promise (الوعد) is used for both good (الخير) and evil (الشر).

وَيَأْمُرُكُمْ بِالْفَحْشاءِ and bids you to indecency. And He tempts you towards stinginess (البخل) and withholding the Zakat (منع الزكاة), a temptation (إغراء) that is like the command (الأمر) given to the commanded (المأمور). And the Arabs call the stingy person (البخيل) indecent (فاحشاً).

وَاللهُ يَعِدُكُمْ But Allah promises you regarding spending (الإنفاق).

مَغْفِرَةً مِنْهُ His forgiveness for your sins (ذنوبكم) and an expiation (كفّارة) for them.

وَفَضْلاً and grace. And a better replacement (خلَفاً أفضل) than what you spent, either in this world (الدنيا) or in the Hereafter (الآخرة) or both.

وَاللهُ واسِعٌ And Allah is all-bounteous, Vast (واسع) in grace (الفضل) for whoever spends.

عَلِيمٌ all-knowing. aware of his spending (بإنفاقه).

(٢٦٩) يُؤْتِي الْحِكْمَةَ تحقيق العلم وإتقان العمل ** مَنْ يَشاءُ وَمَنْ يُؤْتَ الْحِكْمَةَ فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْراً كَثِيراً وَما يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ (269) He grants wisdom to whomever He wishes, and whoever is granted wisdom is certainly granted an abundant good, and none takes admonition except those who possess intellect.

يُؤْتِي الْحِكْمَةَ (He grants wisdom) is the realization of knowledge and the mastery of action. ** وَما يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ (and none takes admonition except those who possess intellect) refers to those who possess intellects purified from the impurities (شوائب) of illusion (الوهم) and caprice (الهوى).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "Obedience to Allah and the recognition (معرفة) of the Imam."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "The recognition (معرفة) of the Imam and the avoidance (اجتناب) of the major sins (الكبائر) for which Allah has ordained the Fire."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): ** الْحِكْمَةَ (Wisdom) is recognition (المعرفة) and understanding (الفقه) in the religion (الدين). So whoever among you gains understanding (فقّه) is wise (حكيم). And no one among the believers dies who is more beloved to Iblis than a jurist (فقيه).

And al-Qummi said: "The abundant good (الخير الكثير) is the recognition (معرفة) of the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) and the Imams (الأئمة)."

And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from him (peace be upon him): ** الْحِكْمَةَ (Wisdom) is the light (ضياء) of recognition (المعرفة), the inheritance (ميراث) of piety (التقوى), and the fruit (ثمرة) of truthfulness (الصدق). If I were to say that Allah has not bestowed upon His servants a blessing more pleasant (أنعم), greater (أعظم), higher (أرفع), more generous (أجزل), or more splendid (أبهى) than wisdom, I would have spoken truly. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: يُؤْتِي الْحِكْمَةَ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَمَنْ يُؤْتَ الْحِكْمَةَ فَقَدْ أُوتِيَ خَيْراً كَثِيراً وَما يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّا أُولُوا الْأَلْبابِ (He grants wisdom to whomever He wishes, and whoever is granted wisdom is certainly granted an abundant good, and none takes admonition except those who possess intellect.) meaning: No one knows what I have deposited (أودعت) and prepared (هيّأت) within wisdom except the one whom I have chosen (استخلصته) for Myself and singled out (خصصته) with it. And الْحِكْمَةَ (Wisdom) is the Book (الكتاب). And the characteristic (صفة) of the Wise (الحكيم) is steadfastness (الثبات) at the beginning of matters and pausing (الوقوف) at their consequences. And he is the guide (هادي) of Allah's creation to Allah.

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty granted me the Quran and granted me wisdom similar to the Quran. And there is no house in which there is nothing of wisdom except that it is ruined (خراباً). Beware, therefore, gain understanding (تفقهوا) and learn, and do not die ignorant." And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "The head (رأس) of wisdom is the fear (مخافة) of Allah."

And in it (al-Khisal) and in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): One day, while He was on one of His journeys, a group of riders (ركب) met Him. They said: "Peace be upon you, O Messenger of Allah." He turned to them and said: "Who are you?" They replied: "Believers (مؤمنون)." He said: "Then what is the reality (حقيقة) of your faith (إيمانكم)?" They said: "Contentment (الرضا) with Allah's decree (قضاء الله), submission (التسليم) to Allah's command (أمر الله), and delegation (التفويض) to Allah." The Messenger of Allah said: "Scholars (علماء), wise ones (حكماء), you are almost prophets due to this wisdom (كادوا أن يكونوا من الحكمة أنبياء). If you are truthful, then do not build what you will not inhabit, and do not gather what you will not eat, and fear Allah to Whom you shall return."

(270) وَما أَنْفَقْتُمْ مِنْ نَفَقَةٍ (270) Whatever expenditure you may make, (whether) small or large, secretly or openly, in truth (في حقّ) or falsehood (أو باطل), أَوْ نَذَرْتُمْ مِنْ نَذْرٍ or whatever vow you vow, in obedience (في طاعة) or disobedience (أو معصية), ** فَإِنَّ اللهَ يَعْلَمُهُ Allah indeed knows it. and He will recompense you for it. ** وَما لِلظَّالِمِينَ The wrongdoers will have (meaning) those who spend in acts of disobedience (في المعاصي) and vow therein, or withhold the obligatory charities (الصدقات) and do not fulfill the vow (بالنذر), ** مِنْ أَنْصارٍ no helpers who can help them against Allah and prevent the punishment (العقاب) from them.

(٢٧١) إِنْ تُبْدُوا الصَّدَقاتِ فَنِعِمَّا هِيَ ** (271) If you disclose your charities, that is well for excellent is the disclosure of them as a thing. ** وَإِنْ تُخْفُوها وَتُؤْتُوهَا (meaning) you give them while concealing them. ** الْفُقَراءَ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ but if you hide them and give them to the poor, that is better for you In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: ** وَإِنْ تُخْفُوها He said: "This is other than al-Zakah (الزكاة), for al-Zakah (الزكاة) is public, not secret." And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Everything that Allah has made obligatory upon you, its declaration is better than its concealment, and whatever is voluntary (تَطَوُّعاً), its concealment is better than its declaration. If a man were to carry the Zakah (الزكاة) of his wealth upon his shoulder and distribute it publicly, that would be good and beautiful." And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of the Mighty and Majestic: ** إِنْ تُبْدُوا الصَّدَقاتِ فَنِعِمَّا هِيَ He said: "It means the obligatory Zakah (الزكاة المفروضة)." He said: I asked, ** وَإِنْ تُخْفُوها وَتُؤْتُوهَا الْفُقَراءَ? He said: "It means the supererogatory (النافلة). They used to prefer the manifestation of the obligatory duties (الفرائض) and the concealment of the supererogatory ones (النوافل)." وَيُكَفِّرُ and He will absolve you of some of your misdeeds. meaning, Allah will absolve, or the concealment (الإخفاء) [will absolve]. And it was recited with the nūn (بالنون), either in the nominative (مرفوعاً) or the jussive (مجزوماً). ** عَنْكُمْ مِنْ سَيِّئاتِكُمْ وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ And Allah is well aware of what you do. This is an encouragement toward secrecy (الاسرار) and avoidance of showing off (الرياء).

(٢٧٢) لَيْسَ عَلَيْكَ هُداهُمْ (272) (It is not your duty to guide them) It is not incumbent upon you to make them guided to cease what they were forbidden from, such as reminding of the favor (المنّ), harm (الأذى), spending from the foul (الخبيث), and other things. Your duty is only the conveyance (البلاغ). وَلكِنَّ اللهَ يَهْدِي مَنْ يَشاءُ (but Allah guides whomever He wishes.) He shows kindness (يلطف) to whomever He knows that kindness (اللطف) will benefit, so that person ceases what He has forbidden. وَما تُنْفِقُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ (Whatever good you spend) of wealth (مال) فَلِأَنْفُسِكُمْ (is for your own souls) then it is for your own souls; no one else benefits from it, so do not remind of the favor (تمنّوا) regarding it to the one upon whom you spend it, nor harm him (تؤذوه). وَما تُنْفِقُونَ (and you do not spend) And your spending is not إِلَّا ابْتِغاءَ وَجْهِ اللهِ (except seeking the Face of Allah.) except for seeking what is with Him. So what is wrong with you that you remind of the favor (تمنّون) regarding it, and spend the foul (الخبيث) which is not directed towards Allah with the like of it? وَما تُنْفِقُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ يُوَفَّ إِلَيْكُمْ (Whatever good you spend shall be repaid to you in full) its reward, multiplied manifold. And there is no excuse for you to turn away from spending in the best and most beautiful ways. وَأَنْتُمْ لا تُظْلَمُونَ (and you will not be wronged.) You will not be diminished the reward of your spending.

(273) لِلْفُقَراءِ Direct yourselves toward the poor, or your charities are for the poor. ** الَّذِينَ أُحْصِرُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** (for the poor) who are confined in the way of Allah. al-Jihad confined them. ** لا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ due to their preoccupation with it (the Jihad), ** ضَرْباً فِي الْأَرْضِ they cannot move about in the land, traveling therein for earning a livelihood.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It (the verse) was revealed concerning the People of the Bench (أصحاب الصفة). It is said they were about four hundred poor Muhajirun (المهاجرين) who resided in the bench (صفة) of the Mosque, dedicating their time entirely to learning and worship. They used to go out in every detachment (سرية) sent by the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. ** يَحْسَبُهُمُ الْجاهِلُ بِحالِهِمْ ** the ignorant person supposes them to be wealthy because of their restraint. And it was read (وقرئ) with the fathah on the sin (السين) wherever it occurs in the conjugations of the future tense (المستقبل). ** أَغْنِياءَ مِنَ التَّعَفُّفِ wealthy because of their restraint, because of their restraint (تعفّفهم) from asking. ** تَعْرِفُهُمْ بِسِيماهُمْ ** You recognize them by their mark, from the paleness of the face and the shabbiness of the condition. ** لا يَسْئَلُونَ النَّاسَ إِلْحافاً They do not ask people importunately, meaning importunity (إلحاحاً), which is that the asker clings to the one being asked until he gives him. ** وَما تُنْفِقُوا مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَإِنَّ اللهَ بِهِ عَلِيمٌ And whatever good you spend, Allah indeed knows it. This is an encouragement for spending (الإنفاق), especially upon these (people).

(٢٧٤) الَّذِينَ يُنْفِقُونَ أَمْوالَهُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ سِرًّا وَعَلانِيَةً فَلَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ. (274) Those who spend their wealth by night and day, secretly and openly, they shall have their reward near their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve.

In al-Majma' and al-Jawami', narrated from Ibn 'Abbas: It was revealed concerning 'Ali (peace be upon him). He had four دراهم with him, so he gave one درهم in charity at night, one درهم during the day, one درهم secretly, and one درهم openly. He said: And this was narrated from al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (prayers of Allah be upon them both).

And in al-'Ayyashi, narrated from Abu Ishaq, who said: 'Ali ibn Abi Talib had four دراهم and owned nothing else besides them. So he gave one درهم in charity at night, one درهم during the day, one درهم secretly, and one درهم openly. This reached the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so He said: "O 'Ali, what motivated you to do what you did?" He replied: "Fulfilling the promise of Allah." Then Allah revealed the آية (verse).
