And in al-Faqih, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: that it was revealed concerning spending on horses (الخيل). He said: And it was narrated: that it was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful, 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (prayers of Allah be upon him). And the reason for its revelation was that he had four دراهم with him, so he gave one درهم in charity at night, one درهم during the day, one درهم secretly, and one درهم openly, so this آية (verse) was revealed concerning him. He said: And when the آية (verse) is revealed concerning something, it applies to everything that falls under its ruling. And the belief (الاعتقاد) regarding its interpretation is that it was revealed concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and applies to spending on horses (الخيل) and similar things.

And in al-Kafi and al-'Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that it is not concerning الزكاة (obligatory charity).

(٢٧٥) الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبا لا يَقُومُونَ (Those who consume usury will not stand) when they are resurrected from their graves إِلَّا كَما يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطانُ (except as stands the one whom Satan has confounded) except like the standing of the epileptic (المصروع) مِنَ الْمَسِ ** (by the touch) meaning, madness (الجنون).

In al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: When I was taken on the Night Journey (أُسرى بي) to the heaven, I saw a people, one of whom desired to stand but could not stand due to the size of his belly. I asked: "Who are these, O Gabriel?" He said: "These are الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبا لا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَما يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطانُ مِنَ الْمَسِ (Those who consume usury will not stand except as stands the one whom Satan has confounded by the touch)." And behold, they were on the path of Pharaoh's folk, being exposed to the Fire morning and evening, saying: "Our Lord, when will the Hour commence?"

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The consumer of usury (آكل الربا) does not depart from the world until Satan confounds him. That punishment (is) بِأَنَّهُمْ قالُوا إِنَّمَا الْبَيْعُ مِثْلُ الرِّبا (That is because they said, 'Trade is just like usury') They analogized (قاسوا) one to the other. وَأَحَلَّ اللهُ الْبَيْعَ وَحَرَّمَ الرِّبا (whereas Allah has allowed trade and forbidden usury) This is a rejection of their equalization and an invalidation of the analogy (القياس).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah only forbade usury (الربوا) so that people would not refrain from performing good deeds (اصطناع المعروف).

I say: By "good deed" (المعروف), He means the goodly loan (القرض الحسن), as will come when interpreting (لا خَيْرَ فِي كَثِيرٍ مِنْ نَجْواهُمْ). So whoever receives مَوْعِظَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ (an admonition from his Lord) a deterrent through the prohibition, فَانْتَهى (and quits it) so he takes heed and refrains from it, فَلَهُ ما سَلَفَ (what is past is his) He will not be held accountable for what has passed of it, nor will it be reclaimed from him.

In al-Kafi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both), and in al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in al-Ayyashi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them both), he said: The admonition (الموعظة) is repentance (التوبة).

And in al-Kafi and al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Every usury (ربا) that people consumed out of ignorance (بجهالة) and then repented, it is accepted from them if their repentance is known. And He said: If a man inherited wealth from his father and knew that there was usury (ربا) in that wealth, but it had become mixed in trade with non-lawful wealth (بغير حلال), then it is lawful (حلال) and pure (طيّباً), so let him consume it. But if he knew of a specific isolated amount (شيئاً معزولاً) that it was usury (ربا), then let him take his principal (رأس ماله) and return the usury (الربوا). And whichever man acquired (1) a large amount of wealth in which he had accumulated much usury (الربوا), and he established that, then knew of it afterwards, and desired to remove it (ينزعه), then what has passed is his, and he should abandon it in what he undertakes anew. There are many narrations with this meaning.

وَأَمْرُهُ إِلَى اللهِ (and his affair rests with Allah) He judges concerning his matter. وَمَنْ عادَ (But whoever reverts) to deeming usury (الربوا) lawful (تحليل) and treating it lightly (الاستخفاف به) after its prohibition has become clear to him, فَأُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ النَّارِ هُمْ فِيها خالِدُونَ. (—they shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they will remain in it [forever])

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who consumes usury (الربوا) while believing that it is lawful (حلال). He said: "It does not harm him until he commits it intentionally (متعمّداً). If he commits it intentionally, then he is in the position that Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has described."

And in al-Faqih and al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): It is a major sin (كبيرة) after the clarification. He said: And treating that lightly (الاستخفاف بذلك) is entering into disbelief (الكفر). Some of the Gnostics (العارفين) said: The consumer of usury (أكل الربوا) is in a worse state than all perpetrators of major sins (مرتكبي الكبائر), for every earner has reliance (توكل) regarding what he has earned, whether little or much, such as the merchant (التاجر), the farmer (الزارع), and the professional (المحترف). They did not specify their provisions (أرزاقهم) by their intellects, nor were they specified for them before earning. Thus, they are truly upon the unknown, just as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Allah refuses to provide the believer except from where he does not know." As for the consumer of usury (أكل الربوا), he has specified his earning and his provision, and he is veiled from his Lord by his own self, and from his provision by his specification. He has no reliance (توكّل) whatsoever. So, Allah entrusts him to his own self and his intellect, and removes him from His protection (حفظه) and guardianship (كلائته). Thus, the Jinn snatch him and confuse him (خبّلته). He will stand on the Day of Resurrection with no connection (رابطة) between him and Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, unlike the rest of the people who are connected to Him through reliance (التوكل). Thus, he will be like the epileptic (المصروع) whom Satan has touched and confounded (يتخبّطه), unable to find his destination (مقصده).

(1) أفاد (acquired) is in the meaning of استفاد (benefited/gained). In al-Faqih, أراد (desired) is used instead of أفاد. That is an indication of the prohibition of usury (الربوا). The prominent meaning in ينزعه (to remove it) refers back to الربوا (usury) in the sense of the excess (الزائد). In al-Faqih, it is نزع ذلك المال (removed that wealth), which is clearer "than it."

(٢٧٦) يَمْحَقُ اللهُ الرِّبا (Allah brings the usury to naught) He removes its blessing and destroys the wealth into which it enters. In al-Faqih and al-Kafi: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked about this Ayah. It was said: "I see someone who consumes usury, yet his wealth increases." He said: "What destruction (محق) is more destructive (أمحق) than a single dirham of usury? It destroys the religion (الدين), and even if he repents from it, his wealth departs and he becomes poor." وَيُرْبِي الصَّدَقاتِ (and He enhances the charities). He multiplies their reward and blesses that from which they were extracted. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "There is nothing except that an angel has been entrusted (1) with it, other than charity (الصدقة), for Allah takes it with His Hand and nurtures it just as one of you nurtures his child, until you meet it on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be like Mount Uhud." And there are many narrations with this meaning. And in the Prophetic Hadith: "Wealth is not diminished by charity (صدقة)." وَاللهُ لا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ (and Allah does not love any ungrateful), insistent upon making lawful (تحليل) what is forbidden (المحرمات), أَثِيمٍ (sinful), immersed in committing it.

(٢٧٧) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَأَقامُوا الصَّلاةَ وَآتَوُا الزَّكاةَ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ وَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ. (277) Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds and maintain the prayer and give the zakat, their reward is with their Lord, and they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (٢٧٨) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَذَرُوا ما بَقِيَ مِنَ الرِّبا (278) O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and abandon what remains of the usury, And abandon the remnants of what you stipulated upon the people from the usury (الربوا) ** إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ if you are faithful. in your hearts, for its proof is the compliance with what you have been commanded. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah used to practice usury (يُرْبِي) during the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية), and remnants of it remained due to him from Thaqif. Khalid ibn al-Walid intended to demand them after he embraced Islam, so [the verse] was revealed. And al-Qummiyy [narrated]: When the verse ** الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبا (Those who consume usury) was revealed, Khalid ibn al-Walid stood up and said: "O Messenger of Allah, the usury (ربا) of my father is with Thaqif, and he instructed me upon his death to take it." So Allah revealed [this verse].

footnote (١) وفي رواية : ان الله يقول ليس شيء الا وكلته به ان يقضيه غيري الا الصدقة فأنا أتلقفها حتّى الرجل والمرأة يتصدق بتمرة وشق تمرة اربيها كما يربى الرجل فلوه وفصيله فيلقى يوم القيامة وهي مثل أحد وأعظم من احد. والفلو ولد الفرس والفصيل ولد الناقة إذا فصل عن أمه «منه». (1) And in a narration: Indeed, Allah says: "There is nothing except that I have entrusted someone else to fulfill it, except for charity (الصدقة). I Myself receive it, even if a man or a woman gives charity (يَتَصَدَّق) with a date or half a date. I nurture it (أُرَبِّيهَا) just as a man nurtures his foal (فَلُو) and his young camel (فَصِيل). He will meet it on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be like Mount Uhud, or greater than Uhud." The al-Falu (الفلو) is the offspring of the horse, and the al-Fasil (الفصيل) is the offspring of the she-camel when it is weaned from its mother. "From him (منه)."

(٢٧٩) فَإِنْ لَمْ تَفْعَلُوا فَأْذَنُوا بِحَرْبٍ مِنَ اللهِ وَرَسُولِهِ (279) But if you do not do it, then be informed of a war from Allah and His Apostle.

So know of it (the war), derived from (أذن بالشيء) when one knows of it (إذا علم به). And it was recited with the lengthening of the alif and the kasrah on the dhāl (فَآذِنُوا) from (الإيذان), meaning notification (الاعلام). For when they know (علموا), they notify (أعلموا), but not the reverse, so this is more emphatic (آكد). And the indefiniteness (التنكير) is for magnification (التعظيم).

In al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him): "One dirham of usury (رباً) is more severe in the sight of Allah than seventy acts of fornication (زنية), all of them committed with a prohibited relative (بذات محرم)."

And al-Qummi added: "in the Sacred House of Allah (في بيت الله الحرام)." And he said: "Usury (الرّبوا) has seventy parts, the least of which is like a man having intercourse with his mother in the Sacred House of Allah (في بيت الله الحرام)."

And in al-Faqīh and al-Tahdhīb, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) cursed usury (الرّبوا), its consumer (آكله), its seller (بايعه), its buyer (مشتريه), its scribe (كاتبه), and its two witnesses (شاهديه)."

وَإِنْ تُبْتُمْ And if you repent from dealing in usury (الارتباء) and believing it to be lawful (اعتقاد حلّه)

فَلَكُمْ رُؤُوسُ أَمْوالِكُمْ لا تَظْلِمُونَ then you shall have your principal sums. You do not wrong the debtors (المديونين) by taking the increase (بأخذ الزيادة)

وَلا تُظْلَمُونَ nor will you be wronged by delay (بالمطل) or reduction (والنقصان) from them (the principal sums).

وَإِنْ كانَ ذُو عُسْرَةٍ If one who is in hardship (إعسار) occurs among your debtors, فَنَظِرَةٌ then a respite, meaning: then a granting of respite, i.e., then grant him respite, إِلى مَيْسَرَةٍ until ease. And it was recited with the dammah on the sīn (مَيْسُرَةٍ), meaning until the time of ease (يسار). وَأَنْ تَصَدَّقُوا And that you remit (the debt). And it was recited with the lightening of the ṣād (تَتَصَدَّقُوا), meaning that you give charity by absolving (the debt), خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ is better for you, meaning: greater in reward than granting respite, إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ وَإِنْ كانَ ذُو عُسْرَةٍ فَنَظِرَةٌ إِلى مَيْسَرَةٍ وَأَنْ تَصَدَّقُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (280) If he is a person in hardship, then [grant him] a respite until a time of ease. And that you remit [the debt] is better for you, should you know.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم ascended the pulpit (المنبر) one day, praised Allah, glorified Him, and invoked blessings upon His Prophets. Then He said: "O people! Let the present among you inform the absent. Behold! Whoever among you grants respite to one in hardship (معسراً), he shall have from Allah, for every day, a charity equivalent to his wealth until he recovers it." Then Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) recited: وَإِنْ كانَ ذُو عُسْرَةٍ فَنَظِرَةٌ إِلى مَيْسَرَةٍ وَأَنْ تَصَدَّقُوا خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (If he is a person in hardship, then [grant him] a respite until a time of ease. And that you remit [the debt] is better for you, should you know), [meaning] that he is in hardship (معسر), so give charity to him with what you are owed by him.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever desires that Allah shade him on a Day when there is no shade except His shade"—He said this three times, and the people were in awe of Him and refrained from asking Him—He then said: "Then let him grant respite to one in hardship (معسراً) (1) or let him waive a portion of his right for him."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Leave the path of the one in hardship (المعسر) free, just as Allah has left it free."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): A man came to him and said: "O Abu Abdillah, a loan until a time of ease (ميسرة)." Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said to him: "Until a harvest (غلّة) that is realized?" The man said: "No, by Allah." He said: "Then until a trade (تجارة) that returns?" He said: "No, by Allah." He said: "Then until a property (عقدة) that is sold?" He said: "No, by Allah." Then Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: "Then you are one of those for whom Allah has established a right in our wealth." Then He called for a pouch containing dirhams, put His hand into it, and handed him a handful from it.

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this respite (النظرة) which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned in His Book: Does it have a known limit, when this person in hardship (المعسر) must be granted respite, and he has taken this man's wealth and spent it on his dependents, and he has no harvest (غلّة) whose realization he awaits, nor a debt (دين) whose due time he awaits, nor absent wealth (مال غائب) whose arrival he awaits? He said: "Yes, he is granted respite for the duration it takes for his news to reach the Imam (الامام), so that He may settle what is upon him from the share of the debtors (سهم الغارمين), provided he spent it in obedience to Allah. But if he spent it in disobedience to Allah, then he has no claim upon the Imam (الامام)." It was asked: "What about this man who trusted him, while he does not know whether he spent it in obedience to Allah or in disobedience to Allah?" He said: "He strives for what is his, and he returns it while he is humbled (صاغر)."

Al-Qummi narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who said: "There is no creditor (غريم) who takes his debtor (غريمه) to a governor (والٍ) among the governors (ولاة) of the Muslims, and the debtor seeks the governor's help regarding his hardship (عسرته), except that this person in hardship (المعسر) is absolved of his debt, and his debt falls upon the governor (والي المسلمين) from the wealth of the Muslims that is in his hands."

(1) He intended to clarify that when the one in hardship (المعسر) reaches the point where he must be granted respite, does his respite have a known duration if he has nothing expected [to come] to him? (منه).

(٢٨١) وَاتَّقُوا يَوْماً تُرْجَعُونَ فِيهِ إِلَى اللهِ (And be wary of a day wherein you shall be brought back to Allah) Prepare yourselves for your return (مصيركم) to Him. And it was read with the fatḥah (فتح) on the tā' (التاء) and the kasrah (كسر) on the jīm (الجيم). ** ثُمَّ تُوَفَّى كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ما كَسَبَتْ (then every soul shall be paid in full for what it has earned) whether good or evil, وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ (and they shall not be wronged) by the reduction of reward (ثواب) or the multiplication (تضعيف) of punishment (عقاب). In al-Majma', narrated from Ibn 'Abbās: It is the last verse with which Gabriel (جبرائيل) descended.

(٢٨٢) يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذا تَدايَنْتُمْ بِدَيْنٍ (282) O you who have faith! When you contract a debt إذا تعاملتم نسيئة When you transact on credit إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى for a specified term, معلوم known فَاكْتُبُوهُ write it down. لأنّه أوثق وادفع للنزاع. because it is more reliable (أوثق) and prevents dispute.

في العلل عن الباقر عليه السلام : ان الله عزّ وجلّ عرض على آدم أسماء الأنبياء وأعمارهم قال فمرّ بآدم اسم داود النبيّ فإذا عمره في العالم أربعون سنة فقال آدم يا ربّ ما أقل عمر داود وما أكثر عمري يا ربّ ان انا ازددت داود ثلاثين سنة أتثبت ذلك له قال نعم يا آدم قال فانّي قد زدته من عمري ثلاثين سنة فأنفذ ذلك وأثبتها له عندك واطرحها من عمري قال أبو جعفر عليه السلام فأثبت الله عزّ وجلّ لداود في عمره ثلاثين سنة وكانت له عند الله مثبتة فذلك قوله عزّ وجلّ يَمْحُوا اللهُ ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ ، قال فمحا الله ما كان عنده مثبتاً لآدم وأثبت لداود ما لم يكن عنده مثبتاً قال فمضى عمر آدم فهبط ملك الموت لقبض روحه فقال له آدم يا ملك الموت انه قد بقي من عمري ثلاثون سنة فقال له ملك الموت يا آدم ألم تجعلها لابنك داود النبيّ وطرحتها من عمرك حين عرض عليك أسماء الأنبياء من ذرّيتك وعرضت عليك أعمارهم وأنت يومئذ بوادي الدّخياء فقال له آدم ما اذكر هذا قال فقال له ملك الموت يا آدم لا تجحد ألم تسأل الله عزّ وجلّ أن يثبته لداود ويمحوها من عمرك فأثبتها لداود في الزّبور ومحاها من عمرك في الذكر قال آدم حتّى أعلم ذلك ، قال أبو جعفر عليه السلام : وكان آدم صادقاً قال لم يذكر ولم يجحد فمن ذلك اليوم أمر الله تبارك وتعالى العباد أن يكتبوا بينهم إذا تداينوا وتعاملوا إلى أجل لأجل نسيان آدم وجحوده ما على نفسه. In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, presented to Adam the names of the Prophets and their lifespans. He said: The name of the Prophet Dawud passed by Adam, and behold, his lifespan in the world was forty years. Adam said: "O Lord, how short is the lifespan of Dawud and and how long is my lifespan! O Lord, if I increase Dawud by thirty years, will You confirm that for him?" He said: "Yes, O Adam." He said: "Then I have increased him by thirty years from my lifespan, so execute that and confirm it for him with You, and deduct it from my lifespan." Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, confirmed thirty years for Dawud in his lifespan, and it was confirmed for him with Allah. That is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: يَمْحُوا اللهُ ما يَشاءُ وَيُثْبِتُ وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (Allah effaces and confirms whatever He wishes, and with Him is the Mother of the Book.) He said: So Allah effaced what was confirmed with Him for Adam and confirmed for Dawud what was not confirmed with Him. He said: Then Adam’s lifespan passed, and the Angel of Death descended to seize his soul. Adam said to him: "O Angel of Death, thirty years still remain of my lifespan." The Angel of Death said to him: "O Adam, did you not grant them to your son, the Prophet Dawud, and deduct them from your lifespan when the names of the Prophets from your progeny were presented to you, and their lifespans were presented to you, while you were then in the Valley of al-Dukhya (وادي الدّخياء)?" Adam said to him: "I do not recall this." He said: Then the Angel of Death said to him: "O Adam, do not deny! Did you not ask Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, to confirm it for Dawud and efface it from your lifespan? So He confirmed it for Dawud in the Zabur (الزّبور) and effaced it from your lifespan in the Dhikr (الذكر)." Adam said: "Until I know that." Abu Ja‘far (peace be upon him) said: Adam was truthful; he said he did not recall and did not deny. From that day, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, commanded the servants to write down [their transactions] among themselves when they contract debts and transact for a term, due to Adam’s forgetfulness and his denial of what was incumbent upon himself.

وفي الكافي ما يقرب منه في روايتين على اختلاف في عدد ما يزيد على عمر داود : وزاد شهادة جبرائيل وميكائيل على آدم. In al-Kafi there is what is close to this in two narrations, with a difference in the number of years added to Dawud’s lifespan. And it added the testimony of Jibra’il (جبرائيل) and Mika’il (ميكائيل) against Adam.

وَلْيَكْتُبْ بَيْنَكُمْ كاتِبٌ بِالْعَدْلِ and let a scribe write [it] between you justly. لا يزيد على ما يجب ولا ينقص He should not add to what is required nor decrease it. وَلا يَأْبَ كاتِبٌ and no scribe should refuse لا يمتنع احد من الكتّاب no scribe should refrain أَنْ يَكْتُبَ كَما عَلَّمَهُ اللهُ to write as Allah has taught him. مثل ما علّمه الله من كتبه الوثائق ولا يأب أن ينفع الناس بكتابته كما نفعه الله بتعليمها كقوله [He should write] like what Allah taught him regarding writing documents, and he should not refuse to benefit people with his writing, just as Allah benefited him by teaching it, like His saying: ** وَأَحْسِنْ كَما أَحْسَنَ اللهُ إِلَيْكَ فَلْيَكْتُبْ and be good as Allah has been good to you. So let him write. تأكيداً ومتعلق بكما علّمه الله [This is] for emphasis, and it relates to "as Allah has taught him." وَلْيُمْلِلِ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الْحَقُ And let the one who incurs the debt dictate, لأنّه المقرّ المشهود عليه والإملال والأملاء واحد because he is the one who acknowledges [the debt] and against whom testimony is borne. And al-imlāl (الإملال) and al-imlā’ (الأملاء) are the same. ** وَلْيَتَّقِ اللهَ رَبَّهُ ** and let him be wary of Allah, his Lord, أي المملي أو الكاتب meaning the dictator or the scribe ** وَلا يَبْخَسْ and not diminish ولا ينقص and not decrease مِنْهُ anything from it. من الحق أو ممّا أملى عليه from the right (الحق) or from what he dictated. ** شَيْئاً فَإِنْ كانَ الَّذِي عَلَيْهِ الْحَقُّ سَفِيهاً If the debtor is senseless, ناقص العقل أو مبذّراً deficient in intellect (ناقص العقل) or extravagant (مبذّراً) ** أَوْ ضَعِيفاً أَوْ لا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يُمِلَّ هُوَ or weak, or unable to dictate himself, في تفسير الإمام : يعني ** ضَعِيفاً في بدنه لا يقدر أن يملّ أو ضعيفاً في فهمه وعلمه لا يقدر أن يمل ويميز الألفاظ التي هي عدل عليه وله من الألفاظ التي هي جور عليه أو على حميمه أَوْ لا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يُمِلَّ هُوَ بمعنى أن يكون مشغولاً في مرّمة لمعاش أو تزوّد لمعاد أو لذة في غير محرّم فان تلك الأشغال التي لا ينبغي للعاقل أن يشرع في غيرها. In Tafsir al-Imam: It means weak (ضَعِيفاً) in his body, unable to dictate, or weak in his understanding and knowledge, unable to dictate and distinguish the phrases that are just for him and against him from the phrases that are oppressive (جور) against him or against his close relative (حميمه). Or unable to dictate himself (أَوْ لا يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يُمِلَّ هُوَ) means that he is preoccupied with repairing his livelihood (مرّمة لمعاش) or preparing for the Hereafter (تزوّد لمعاد) or pleasure in what is not forbidden, for those are the preoccupations that the sensible person should not engage in other than them.

وفي التهذيب عن الصادق عليه السلام : السفيه الذي يشتري الدرهم بأضعافه والضعيف الأبله. In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The senseless person (السفيه) is he who buys a dirham (الدرهم) for many times its value, and the weak person (الضعيف) is the simpleton (الأبله). والعيّاشيّ عنه : السفيه الشارب الخمر والضعيف الذي يأخذ واحداً باثنين And al-Ayyashi narrated from him: The senseless person (السفيه) is the wine drinker (الشارب الخمر), and the weak person (الضعيف) is he who takes one for two. فَلْيُمْلِلْ وَلِيُّهُ then let his guardian dictate النائب عنه والقيّم بأمره the one acting on his behalf and managing his affairs بِالْعَدْلِ justly, بأن لا يحيف على المكتوب له ولا المكتوب عليه by not being unjust to the one for whom the writing is made nor the one against whom the writing is made. ** وَاسْتَشْهِدُوا ** and take as witness على الدَّين over the debt شَهِيدَيْنِ مِنْ رِجالِكُمْ two witnesses from your men, أحراركم دون عبيدكم فان الله قد شغل العبيد بخدمة مواليهم عن تحمل الشهادات وعن أدائها وليكونوا من المسلمين منكم فان الله شرف المسلمين العُدول بقبول شهاداتهم وجعل ذلك من الشرف العاجل لهم ومن ثواب دنياهم قبل أن يصلوا إلى الآخرة كذا في تفسير الإمام عن النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله وسلم. :. your free men, not your slaves, for Allah has preoccupied the slaves with serving their masters, preventing them from bearing testimony and from rendering it. And let them be from the Muslims among you, for Allah has honored the upright Muslims (المسلمين العُدول) by accepting their testimonies, and He made that part of the immediate honor (الشرف العاجل) for them and part of the reward of their worldly life before they reach the Hereafter. Thus is it stated in Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family). أقول : لا ينافيه تقييد الاستشهاد بالأحرار لاشتغال العبيد بالخدمة قبول شهادة العبيد إذا استشهدوا وكانوا عدولاً كما يثبت عن أهل البيت عليهم السلام I say: The restriction of seeking testimony to free men due to the slaves' preoccupation with service does not negate the acceptance of the testimony of slaves if they are called upon as witnesses and are upright (عدول), as is established from the People of the House (peace be upon them). ** فَإِنْ لَمْ يَكُونا And if the two are not يعني الشهيدين meaning the two witnesses رَجُلَيْنِ فَرَجُلٌ وَامْرَأَتانِ مِمَّنْ تَرْضَوْنَ مِنَ الشُّهَداءِ two men, then one man and two women from those whom you approve as witnesses, قال عليه السلام : يعني ممن ترضون دينه وأمانته وصلاحه وعفّته وتيقّظه فيما يشهد به وتحصيله وتميّزه فما كل صالح مميّز ولا محصّل ولا كل محصّل مميّز صالح وإنّ من عباد الله لمن هو أهل لصلاحه وعفّته لو شهد لم يقبل شهادته لقلّة تميّزه فإذا كان صالحاً عفيفاً مميّزاً محصّلاً مجانباً للمعصية والهوى والميل والتحامل فذلك الرجل الفاضل فيه فتمسّكوا وبهداه فاقتدوا وان انقطع عنكم المطر فاستمطروا به وان امتنع نبات فاستخرجوا به النبات وان تعذّر عليكم الرزق فاستدروا به الرزق فان ذلك ممن لا يخيّب طلبه ولا ترد مسألته He (peace be upon him) said: Meaning those whose religion (دينه), trustworthiness (أمانته), righteousness (صلاحه), chastity (عفّته), alertness (تيقّظه) in what he testifies to, comprehension (تحصيله), and discernment (تميّزه) you approve. For not every righteous person is discerning or comprehending, nor is every comprehending person discerning and righteous. Indeed, among the servants of Allah are those who, despite their righteousness and chastity, if they were to testify, their testimony would not be accepted due to their lack of discernment (قلّة تميّزه). So, if he is righteous, chaste, discerning, comprehending, and avoids disobedience (المعصية), caprice (الهوى), inclination (الميل), and bias (التحامل), then that is the virtuous man (الرجل الفاضل) among them. So adhere to him, follow his guidance, and if the rain is cut off from you, seek rain through him; if vegetation is withheld, extract vegetation through him; and if provision (الرزق) becomes difficult for you, seek provision through him, for such a person is one whose request is not disappointed and whose plea is not rejected. أَنْ تَضِلَّ إِحْداهُما so that if one of them errs, وقرئ بكسر الهمزة And it was read with the kasra on the hamza (أَنْ). فَتُذَكِّرَ the other may remind her. وقرئ مرفوعاً وبالتخفيف والنصب من الاذكار And it was read with the rafa' (مرفوعاً), and with takhfif (التخفيف) and nasb (النصب) from al-idhkār (الاذكار). إِحْداهُمَا الْأُخْرى the other may remind her. في تفسير الإمام عن أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام : إذا ضلت إحداهما عن الشهادة ونسيتها ذكرتها الأخرى فاستقامتا في أداء الشهادة. In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): If one of them errs concerning the testimony and forgets it, the other reminds her, and thus they become upright in rendering the testimony. أقول : وهو من قولهم ضلّ الطريق إذا لم يهتد وهذه علّة لاعتبار العدد قال عليه السلام : عدل الله شهادة امرأتين بشهادة رجل لنقصان عقولهن ودينهن. I say: This is derived from their saying, "He erred (ضلّ) concerning the path" when he was not guided. And this is the reason (علّة) for considering the number [of witnesses]. He (peace be upon him) said: Allah equated the testimony of two women with the testimony of one man due to the deficiency of their intellects (عقولهن) and their religion (دينهن).

وفي الكافي عن الصادق عليه السلام في عدة أخبار : أربعة لا يستجاب لهم دعوة أحدهم رجل كان له مال فأدانه بغير بينة يقول الله عزّ وجلّ ألم آمرك بالشهادة وعنه عليه السلام : من ذهب حقه على غير بيّنة لم يؤجر. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in several narrations: Four people whose supplication is not answered, one of them is a man who had wealth and loaned it without evidence (بغير بينة). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: "Did I not command you concerning testimony?" And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Whoever loses his right (حقه) without evidence (بيّنة) will not be rewarded.

وَلا يَأْبَ الشُّهَداءُ إِذا ما دُعُوا And the witnesses must not refuse when they are summoned. في الكافي والعيّاشيّ عن الصادق عليه السلام في عدة أخبار في هذه الآية قال : لا ينبغي لأحد إذا ما دعي إلى الشهادة ليشهد عليها أن يقول لا أشهد لكم وفي بعضها قال في آخره : فذلك قبل الكتاب وفي بعضها هي قبل الشهادة ومن يكتمها بعد الشهادة. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in several narrations concerning this Ayah, he said: It is not proper for anyone, when he is summoned to bear witness to it, to say, "I will not testify for you." And in some of them, he said at the end: That is before the writing (الكتاب). And in some of them: It is before the testimony (الشهادة) and [applies to] whoever conceals it after the testimony. وعن الكاظم عليه السلام فيها : إذا ما دعاك الرجل تشهد له على دين أو حقّ لم ينبغ لك أن تقاعس عنه. And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) concerning it: If a man calls upon you to testify for him regarding a debt or a right (حقّ), it is not proper for you to hold back from him. وفي تفسير الإمام عن أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام في هذه الآية : من كان في عنقه شهادة فلا يأب إذا دعي لإقامتها وليقمها ولينصح فيها ولا تأخذه فيها لومة لائم وليأمر بالمعروف ولينه عن المنكر قال في خبر آخر : وَلا يَأْبَ الشُّهَداءُ إِذا ما دُعُوا أنزلت فيمن إذا دعى لاقامة إسماع الشهادة فأبى ونزلت فيمن امتنع عن أداء الشهادة إذا كانت عنده In Tafsir al-Imam, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) concerning this Ayah: Whoever has a testimony incumbent upon him should not refuse when he is summoned to render it. Let him render it and be sincere in it, and let him not be taken by the blame of any blamer. Let him enjoin the good (المعروف) and forbid the evil (المنكر). He said in another narration: And the witnesses must not refuse when they are summoned (وَلا يَأْبَ الشُّهَداءُ إِذا ما دُعُوا) was revealed concerning whoever refuses when summoned to establish the hearing of the testimony, and it was revealed concerning whoever refrains from rendering the testimony when he possesses it. وَلا تَسْأَمُوا Do not be weary ولا تملوا and do not tire أَنْ تَكْتُبُوهُ صَغِيراً of writing it down, whether small كان الحق the right (الحق) was أَوْ كَبِيراً إِلى أَجَلِهِ or big, along with its term. إلى وقت حلوله الذي أقرّ به المديون until the time of its maturity which the debtor acknowledged. ذلِكُمْ أَقْسَطُ عِنْدَ اللهِ That is more just with Allah أعدل more equitable ** وَأَقْوَمُ لِلشَّهادَةِ and more upright for testimony, وأثبت لها وأعون على إقامتها and firmer for it, and more helpful in establishing it. وَأَدْنى أَلَّا تَرْتابُوا and likelier to keep you from doubt, وأقرب في أن لا تشكّوا في جنس الدين وقدره وأجله والشهود ونحو ذلك and closer to preventing you from doubting the type of debt, its amount, its term, the witnesses, and the like. ** إِلَّا أَنْ تَكُونَ تِجارَةً حاضِرَةً تُدِيرُونَها بَيْنَكُمْ unless it is a spot transaction you effect among yourselves, إلا أن تتبايعوا يداً بيد unless you trade hand-to-hand. فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْكُمْ جُناحٌ أَلَّا تَكْتُبُوها in which case there is no sin upon you if you do not write it down. لبعده عن التنازع والنسيان because it is far removed from dispute and forgetfulness. ** وَأَشْهِدُوا إِذا تَبايَعْتُمْ Take witnesses when you trade, لأنّه أحوط because it is more cautious (أحوط). ** وَلا يُضَارَّ كاتِبٌ وَلا شَهِيدٌ and let no scribe or witness be harmed. يحتمل البنائين وهو نهى لهما عن ترك الإجابة والتحريف والتغيير في الكتب والشهادة أو نهي عن الضرار بهما مثل أن يعجّلا عن مهمّ ويكلّفا الخروج عما حدّ لهما أو لا يعطي الكاتب جعله والشهيد مؤنة مجيئه حيث كان It admits both constructions (البنائين): either it is a prohibition for them [the scribe and witness] against refusing to respond, or against distortion (التحريف) and alteration (التغيير) in the writing and testimony; or it is a prohibition against causing harm (الضرار) to them, such as rushing them away from an important matter, or obligating them to deviate from what was defined for them, or failing to give the scribe his fee (جعله) and the witness the expense (مؤنة) of his travel wherever he may be. وَإِنْ تَفْعَلُوا If you do الضرار وما نهيتم عنه the harm (الضرار) and what you have been forbidden from فَإِنَّهُ فُسُوقٌ بِكُمْ [that is] transgression on your part. خروج عن الطاعة لا حقّ بكم a departure from obedience (خروج عن الطاعة) that attaches to you. ** وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ Be wary of Allah, في مخالفة أمره ونهيه regarding disobeying His command and prohibition. وَيُعَلِّمُكُمُ اللهُ and Allah teaches you, أحكامه المتضمنّة لمصالحكم His rulings (أحكامه) which contain your interests. وَاللهُ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ and Allah is all-knowing. قيل كرر لفظة الله في الجمل الثلاث لاستقلالها فان الأولى حثّ على التقوى والثانية وعد بانعامه والثالثة تعظيم لشأنه ولأنه ادخل في التعظيم من الكناية. It is said that the word Allah was repeated in the three sentences because they are independent [clauses]: the first is an encouragement toward piety (التقوى), the second is a promise of His favor (انعامه), and the third is an exaltation of His status, and because it is more conducive to exaltation than using a pronoun (الكناية). القمّيّ في البقرة خمسمائة حكم وفي هذه الآية خاصّة خمسة عشر حكماً. Al-Qummi stated: In Surat al-Baqarah there are five hundred rulings, and in this Ayah specifically, there are fifteen rulings.

(٢٨٣) وَإِنْ كُنْتُمْ عَلى سَفَرٍ ** (283) If you are on a journey meaning: travelers (مسافرين). ** وَلَمْ تَجِدُوا كاتِباً فَرِهانٌ ** and do not find a scribe, then a security deposit is to be taken. So, that by which security is established is a pledge (رهان). And it was recited (وقرئ) furuhun (فُرُهُنٌ) with two ḍammahs (بضمتين), and both (كلاهما) are the plural of rahn (رهن), which is in the meaning of marhūn (مرهون) (pledged). ** مَقْبُوضَةٌ is to be taken. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "There is no pledge (رهن) except one that is taken (مقبوضاً)." I say: The purpose is not to restrict the act of pledging (الارتهان) specifically to the state of travel. Rather, since travel is a place where scribes (الكتب) and witnesses (الاشهاد) are likely to be scarce (مظنّة لإِعواز), the traveler was commanded to establish the pledge (الارتهان) in place of writing and witnessing, by way of guidance (الإرشاد) toward the preservation of wealth. فَإِنْ أَمِنَ بَعْضُكُمْ بَعْضاً If one of you trusts another, meaning: some of the creditors trust some of the debtors due to their good opinion of him. ** فَلْيُؤَدِّ الَّذِي اؤْتُمِنَ ** let him who is trusted deliver and he is the one upon whom the right (الحق) rests, ** أَمانَتَهُ his trust, He named the debt a trust (أمانة) because the creditor entrusted him with it by abandoning the requirement of a pledge (الارتهان) from him. ** وَلْيَتَّقِ اللهَ رَبَّهُ and let him be wary of Allah, his Lord. regarding betrayal (الخيانة) and the denial of the right (إنكار الحق). And in this, there are exaggerations (المبالغات) that are not hidden. ** وَلا تَكْتُمُوا الشَّهادَةَ Do not conceal the testimony, is an address to the witnesses (للشهود). وَمَنْ يَكْتُمْها and whoever conceals it, despite his knowledge of that which is testified to (بالمشهود به) and his ability to deliver it (أدائها), ** فَإِنَّهُ آثِمٌ قَلْبُهُ his heart is indeed sinful. This means that concealing testimony is among the sins of the hearts (آثام القلوب) and among the greatest of sins (معاظم الذّنوب). In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), He said: "His heart is an unbeliever (كافر قلبه)." And in the ḥadīth of the Prohibitions (مناهي) of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): He forbade the concealment of testimony and said: "Whoever conceals it, Allah will feed him his own flesh before the heads of creation (على رؤوس الخلائق)." And this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: ** وَلا تَكْتُمُوا الشَّهادَةَ وَمَنْ يَكْتُمْها فَإِنَّهُ آثِمٌ قَلْبُهُ (Do not conceal the testimony, and whoever conceals it, his heart is indeed sinful.) وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ عَلِيمٌ and Allah is knowing of what you do. This is a threat (تهديد).

(٢٨٤) لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ (To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth.) as creation and dominion ** وَإِنْ تُبْدُوا ما فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ (Whether you disclose what is in your hearts) whether good or evil ** أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ يُحاسِبْكُمْ بِهِ اللهُ (or conceal it, Allah will call you to account for it.) In Nahj al-Balaghah: And He recompenses the servants based on what is in the breasts (الصدور).

I say: The whispers (الوساوس) and self-talk (حديث النفس) do not enter into what a person conceals, because it is not within his capacity to be free from them. Rather, [what is included is] what he believes and resolves upon (عزم عليه).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Nine characteristics have been lifted from my Ummah: error (الخطأ), forgetfulness (النسيان), what they do not know, what they are unable to bear (لا يطيقون), what they are compelled to do (اضطروا إليه), what they are forced to do (استكرهوا عليه), ill-omen (الطّيرة), the whispers (الوسوسة) regarding reflection upon creation, and envy (الحسد) so long as it is not manifested by the tongue or the hand."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "It is incumbent upon Allah that He does not admit into Paradise anyone who has in his heart the weight of a mustard seed of love for the two of them (حبّهما)."

فَيَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ (Then He forgives whomever He wishes) His forgiveness ** وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشاءُ (and punishes whomever He wishes.) His punishment وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (And Allah is capable of everything.) so He is capable of calling to account (المحاسبة).

(٢٨٥) آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ (The Messenger has faith in what has been sent down to him) This is a testimony and explicit declaration from Allah affirming the validity of his faith. وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ (and the faithful.) It is said that either it is grammatically conjoined (عطف) to الرَّسُولُ (the Messenger), and what follows is a new sentence (استئناف); or it is a new sentence (استئناف) by singling out the Messenger and singling out his faith, in order to magnify his status and the status of his faith. أقول : And there is another aspect to this singling out (للافراد) which will come in the narrative (الحديث). كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللهِ وَمَلائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ (Each of them has faith in Allah, His angels, His Books, and His apostles.) And it was recited (وقرئ) as وكتابه (and His Book, singular). In al-Ghaybah, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "On the night I was taken up to the heaven (الإسراء), the Almighty, Glorious is His praise, said: The Messenger has faith in what has been sent down to him from his Lord. I said: and the faithful. He said: 'You have spoken the truth, O Muhammad.'" لا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْ رُسُلِهِ (‘We do not differentiate between any of His apostles.’) They say this, and the intent is to negate differentiation in affirmation (التصديق). And it was recited (وقرئ) as لا يفرق (he does not differentiate) with the yā’ (الياء), where the singular is used in the meaning of the plural because it occurs in the context of negation (سياق النفي), and this is why the word بين (between) is applied to it. وَقالُوا سَمِعْنا (And they say, ‘We hear) We respond. وَأَطَعْنا ** (and obey.) Your command. غُفْرانَكَ (Our Lord, Your forgiveness!) Forgive Your forgiveness (اغفر غفرانك), or we seek Your forgiveness. رَبَّنا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ (and toward You is the ultimate return.’) The return after death, and this is an acknowledgment (إقرار) from them of the Resurrection (البعث). لا يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْساً (Allah does not task any soul) regarding what Allah has made obligatory upon it. Al-Ayyashi narrated this from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them). إِلَّا وُسْعَها (beyond its capacity.) Except what its ability (قدرتها) can encompass, out of grace (فضلاً) and mercy (رحمة). In al-Tawhid, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (may the blessings of Allah be upon him): "He did not command the servants except with what is less than their capacity (سعتهم). Everything that people are commanded to undertake, they have the capacity (متسعون) for it. What they do not have the capacity for is lifted from them. But people have no good in them." لَها ما كَسَبَتْ (To its benefit will be whatever good it earns,) of good. وَعَلَيْها مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ (and to its detriment will be whatever evil it incurs.) of evil. No one else benefits from its obedience or is harmed by its disobedience. رَبَّنا لا تُؤاخِذْنا إِنْ نَسِينا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنا (‘Our Lord, do not take us to task if we forget or make a mistake.) Meaning: Do not take us to task for what led us to forgetfulness (نسيان) or error (خطأ), whether due to negligence (تفريط) or lack of concern (قلّة مبالاة). رَبَّنا وَلا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنا إِصْراً (Our Lord, do not lay upon us a burden) A heavy load (حملاً ثقيلاً) that confines (يأصر) its bearer, meaning, holds him in his place. This refers to difficult obligations (التكاليف الشاقة). كَما حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنا (like You laid on those who were before us.) This refers to what the Children of Israel were obligated with, such as killing their own souls (قتل الأنفس), cutting off the place of impurity (قطع موضع النجاسة), and other things. رَبَّنا وَلا تُحَمِّلْنا ما لا طاقَةَ لَنا بِهِ (Our Lord, do not charge us with that for which we have no strength.) Such as the punishments (العقوبات) that descended upon those before us. وَاعْفُ عَنَّا (And excuse us,) and wipe away our sins. وَ اغْفِرْ لَنا (and forgive us,) and conceal our faults (عيوبنا), and do not expose us through accountability (بالمؤاخذة). وَارْحَمْنا (and have mercy on us.) Be compassionate toward us and bestow Your favor upon us. أَنْتَ مَوْلانا (You are our Master,) our Lord (سيّدنا), and we are Your servants. فَانْصُرْنا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكافِرِينَ (so help us against the faithless lot.’) By overpowering them (بالقهر لهم) and prevailing over them with proof (بالحجة عليهم), for it is the right of the Master (المولى) to help His dependents (مواليه) against the enemies. Al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them) regarding the end of al-Baqarah: He said: "When they supplicated (دعوا), they were answered (أُجيبوا)." Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): This verse is Allah's direct address (مشافهة) to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم when He took him up to the heaven (الإسراء). The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "When I reached the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary (سدرة المنتهى), behold, a single leaf from it overshadowed an entire nation (أمّة) of nations. And I was near my Lord like قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنى (the measure of two bows or closer), as Allah Almighty recounted. Then my Lord, blessed and exalted is He, called out to me: آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَبِّهِ (The Messenger has faith in what has been sent down to him from his Lord). So I replied, answering on my behalf and on behalf of my nation: وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللهِ وَمَلائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْ رُسُلِهِ (and the faithful. Each of them has faith in Allah, His angels, His Books, and His apostles. ‘We do not differentiate between any of His apostles.’) Then I said: سَمِعْنا وَأَطَعْنا غُفْرانَكَ رَبَّنا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ (‘We hear and obey. Our Lord, Your forgiveness! and toward You is the ultimate return.’) Then Allah said: لا يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْساً إِلَّا وُسْعَها لَها ما كَسَبَتْ وَعَلَيْها مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ (Allah does not task any soul beyond its capacity. To its benefit will be whatever good it earns, and to its detriment will be whatever evil it incurs.) So I said: رَبَّنا لا تُؤاخِذْنا إِنْ نَسِينا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنا (‘Our Lord, do not take us to task if we forget or make a mistake.’) Then Allah said: 'I shall not take you to task.' So I said: رَبَّنا وَلا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنا إِصْراً كَما حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنا (Our Lord, do not lay upon us a burden like You laid on those who were before us.) Then Allah said: 'I shall not lay it upon you.' So I said: رَبَّنا وَلا تُحَمِّلْنا ما لا طاقَةَ لَنا بِهِ وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنا وَارْحَمْنا أَنْتَ مَوْلانا فَانْصُرْنا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكافِرِينَ (Our Lord, do not charge us with that for which we have no strength. And excuse us, and forgive us, and have mercy on us. You are our Master, so help us against the faithless lot.’) Then Allah, blessed and exalted is He, said: 'I have granted that to you and to your nation.' Then Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "No one who came before Allah Almighty was more honored than the Messenger of Allah when he asked these qualities (الخصال) for his nation." And al-Ayyashi narrated what is similar in meaning in a narrative (حديث), without the saying of Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) until the end of the narrative. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him), from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them), in a narrative mentioning the merits (مناقب) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he said: "When He was taken on the Night Journey (الإسراء) from the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) to the Farthest Mosque (المسجد الأقصى), a journey of a month, and He was ascended into the dominion (ملكوت) of the heavens, a journey of fifty thousand years, in less than a third of a night, until He reached the base of the Throne (ساق العرش). He drew near with knowledge (بالعلم) and then descended (فتدلّى). A green cushion (رفرف أخضر) was lowered for Him from Paradise, and the light covered His sight. He saw the greatness of His Lord, Almighty and Majestic, with His heart (بفؤاده) and did not see it with His eye. فَكانَ (So He was) like قابَ قَوْسَيْنِ (the measure of two bows) between Him and Him أَوْ أَدْنى فَأَوْحى إِلى عَبْدِهِ ما أَوْحى (or closer. Then He revealed to His servant what He revealed). Among what He revealed to Him was the verse in Surat al-Baqarah, the Almighty's saying: لِلَّهِ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَما فِي الْأَرْضِ وَإِنْ تُبْدُوا ما فِي أَنْفُسِكُمْ أَوْ تُخْفُوهُ يُحاسِبْكُمْ بِهِ اللهُ فَيَغْفِرُ لِمَنْ يَشاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. Whether you disclose what is in your hearts or conceal it, Allah will bring you to account for it. Then He will forgive whomever He wishes, and punish whomever He wishes, and Allah has power over all things). This verse had been presented to the Prophets, from Adam (peace be upon him and our Prophet) until Allah, blessed is His Name, sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. It was also presented to the nations, but they refused to accept it due to its heaviness (ثقلها). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم accepted it and presented it to His nation, and they accepted it. When Allah Almighty saw their acceptance, despite the fact that they could not bear it, when He proceeded to the base of the Throne, He repeated the speech to Him so that He might understand. So He said: آمَنَ الرَّسُولُ بِما أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ رَبِّهِ (The Messenger has faith in what has been sent down to him from his Lord). So He replied, answering on His behalf and on behalf of His nation, saying: وَالْمُؤْمِنُونَ كُلٌّ آمَنَ بِاللهِ وَمَلائِكَتِهِ وَكُتُبِهِ وَرُسُلِهِ لا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِنْ رُسُلِهِ (and the faithful. Each of them has faith in Allah, His angels, His Books, and His apostles. ‘We do not differentiate between any of His apostles.’) Then, Glorious is His mention, He said: 'For them are Paradise and forgiveness, provided they do that.' Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'Since You have done that for us, then غُفْرانَكَ رَبَّنا وَإِلَيْكَ الْمَصِيرُ (Our Lord, Your forgiveness! and toward You is the ultimate return),' meaning the return (المرجع) in the Hereafter. He said: Then Allah, Almighty and Glorious is His praise, answered Him: 'I have already done that for you and for your nation.' Then He, Almighty and Majestic, said: 'Since you have accepted the verse with its severity (بتشديدها) and the greatness of what is in it, and I presented it to the nations and they refused to accept it, but your nation accepted it, it is incumbent upon Me to lift it from your nation.' And He said: لا يُكَلِّفُ اللهُ نَفْساً إِلَّا وُسْعَها لَها ما كَسَبَتْ (Allah does not task any soul beyond its capacity. To its benefit will be whatever good it earns,) of good, وَعَلَيْها مَا اكْتَسَبَتْ (and to its detriment will be whatever evil it incurs,) of evil. When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم heard that, He said: 'Since You have done that for me and for my nation, grant me more.' He said: 'Ask.' He said: رَبَّنا لا تُؤاخِذْنا إِنْ نَسِينا أَوْ أَخْطَأْنا (‘Our Lord, do not take us to task if we forget or make a mistake.’) Allah Almighty said: 'I shall not take your nation to task for forgetfulness (النسيان) or error (الخطأ) due to your honor with Me. The previous nations, when they forgot what they were reminded of, the doors of punishment were opened upon them, but I have lifted that from your nation. The previous nations, when they erred, they were taken to task for the error and punished for it, but I have lifted that from your nation due to your honor with Me.' Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'O Allah, since You have granted me that, grant me more.' Allah Almighty said to Him: 'Ask.' He said: (رَبَّنا وَلا تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْنا إِصْراً كَما حَمَلْتَهُ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِنا) (‘Our Lord, do not lay upon us a burden like You laid on those who were before us.’) He meant by the burden (الإصر) the hardships (الشدائد) that were upon those before us. So Allah answered Him regarding that, and blessed and exalted is His Name, He said: 'I have lifted from your nation the burdens (الآصار) that were upon the previous nations. I used not to accept their prayers (صلواتهم) except in specific, known places (بقاع) on the earth that I chose for them, even if they were far away. But I have made the entire earth a place of prayer (مسجداً) and purification (طهوراً) for your nation. This is one of the burdens that were upon the nations before you, and I have lifted it from your nation. The previous nations, when they were afflicted by impurity (نجاسة), they would cut it away from their bodies. But I have made water a purifier (طهوراً) for your nation. This is one of the burdens that were upon them, and I have lifted it from your nation. The previous nations used to carry their sacrifices (قرابينها) (1) to the Holy House (بيت المقدس). Whoever I accepted it from, I would send a fire to consume it, and he would return joyful. And whoever I did not accept it from, he would return ruined (مثبوراً) (2). But I have made the sacrifice (قربان) of your nation in the stomachs of their poor and needy. Whoever I accept it from, I multiply it for him manifold. And whoever I do not accept it from, I lift the punishments of the world from him. I have lifted that from your nation, and it is one of the burdens that were upon the nations before you. The previous nations had their prayers obligated upon them in the darkness of the night and the middle of the day, and this was among the hardships that were upon them. I have lifted that from your nation and obligated their prayers upon them at the edges of the night and the day, and during their times of activity (نشاطهم). The previous nations had fifty prayers obligated upon them in fifty times, and this was among the burdens that were upon them. I have lifted that from your nation and made them five in five times, which are fifty-one rak'ahs, and I have granted them the reward of fifty prayers. The previous nations had their good deed counted as one good deed and their evil deed as one evil deed, and this was among the burdens that were upon them. I have lifted that from your nation and made the good deed count as ten and the evil deed as one. The previous nations, if one of them intended a good deed but did not perform it, it was not written for him, but if he performed it, one good deed was written for him. But your nation, if one of them intends a good deed and does not perform it, one good deed is written for him, and if he performs it, ten are written for him. This is one of the burdens that were upon them, and I have lifted it from your nation. The previous nations, if one of them intended an evil deed but did not perform it, it was not written against him, but if he performed it, one evil deed was written against him. But your nation, if one of them intends an evil deed and does not perform it, a good deed is written for him. This is one of the burdens that were upon them, and I have lifted that from your nation. The previous nations, when they sinned, their sins were written on their doors, and their repentance from sins was that I would forbid them their favorite food after repentance. I have lifted that from your nation and made their sins between Me and them, and I have placed thick veils (ستوراً كثيفة) over them, and I accept their repentance without punishment, and I do not punish them by forbidding them their favorite food. The previous nations, one of them would repent from a single sin for a hundred years, or eighty years, or fifty years, and then I would not accept his repentance without punishing him with a worldly punishment, and this was one of the burdens that were upon them. I have lifted that from your nation. Indeed, a man from your nation may sin for twenty years, or thirty years, or forty years, or a hundred years, then he repents and regrets for the blink of an eye, and I forgive all of that for him.' Then the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: 'O Allah, since You have granted me all of that, grant me more.' He said: 'Ask.' He said: رَبَّنا وَلا تُحَمِّلْنا ما لا طاقَةَ لَنا بِهِ (Our Lord, do not charge us with that for which we have no strength.) Blessed is His Name, He said: 'I have done that for you and for your nation. I have lifted from them the great afflictions (بلايا) of the nations. And that is My judgment (حكمي) concerning all nations: that I do not task a creation beyond their capacity.' The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: وَاعْفُ عَنَّا وَاغْفِرْ لَنا وَارْحَمْنا أَنْتَ مَوْلانا (And excuse us, and forgive us, and have mercy on us. You are our Master.) Allah Almighty said: 'I have done that for the repentant ones (تائبي) of your nation.' He said: فَانْصُرْنا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكافِرِينَ (so help us against the faithless lot.’) Allah, Glorious is His Name, said: 'Your nation on earth is like the white beauty spot (الشّامة البيضاء) on the black bull. They are the capable ones (القادرون) and the dominant ones (القاهرون). They employ others and are not employed, due to your honor with Me. And it is incumbent upon Me to make your religion prevail over all religions, until no religion remains in the East or West of the earth except your religion, or they pay the tribute (الجزية) to the followers of your religion." In Thawab al-A’mal, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever recites four verses from the beginning of al-Baqarah, the Verse of the Throne (Ayat al-Kursi), and two verses after it, and three verses from its end, he will not see anything hateful concerning himself or his wealth, nor will the Devil approach him, nor will he forget the Quran." And narrated from Jabir, from Him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, in a narrative, he said: Allah, blessed and exalted is He, said: "I have given you and your nation a treasure from the treasures of My Throne: Fatihat al-Kitab (فاتحة الكتاب) and the conclusion of Surat al-Baqarah." And it is narrated from Him صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Allah sent down two verses from the treasures of Paradise, which the All-beneficent wrote with His Hand two thousand years before He created creation. Whoever recites them after the last evening prayer (العشاء الآخرة), they will suffice him instead of standing in prayer during the night (قيام الليل)." And in a narration: "Whoever recites the two verses from the end of Surat al-Baqarah, they will suffice him (كفتَاه)." In Thawab al-A’mal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surat al-Baqarah and Al Imran, they will come on the Day of Resurrection overshadowing him above his head like two clouds (الغمامتين) or like two shades (الغيابتين), meaning two canopies (المظلتين)."

(1) Al-Qurbān (القربان), with a ḍamma (الضم), is that by which one seeks nearness to Allah Almighty. Plural: Qarābīn (قرابين) (Qāmūs).

(2) The Almighty's saying مَثْبُوراً means destroyed (مهلكاً), and it is also said: cursed (ملعوناً), banished (مطروداً) (Majma’).
